observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant and GSO to complete and update the PPELC and PC, respectively, in compliance with the aforementioned provisions or, due to the time-intensive nature of this process, designate personnel from respective offices to focus exclusively on the task, to facilitate the reconciliation of both records and ensure fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Management has not yet completed the updating of the PPELCs and PCs.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to reconcile the Accounting and GSO records

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

through the concerned offices, after updating the PPELC and PC, immediately conduct the reconciliation of records to identify the cause of the discrepancies between the balances of the records being maintained by the Accounting Office and the GSO or Inventory Committee;

instruct the Inventory Committee to immediately complete the physical count of all PPE of the Municipality and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the Audit Team; and

study and consider the applicability of COA Circular No. 2020-006 which provides the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of properties for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances, to come-up with a complete RPCPPE that will help both the Accounting Office and the GSO in updating and reconciling their records; thus, establishing the fair presentation of the PPE account in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction:
Reconciliation of records was not yet conducted.

The physical count of the  PPE was not yet completed.

The Management studied the COA Circular and intends to request for the one-time cleansing of the PPE, once the physical count is completed.
observations.reason: The updating of the Accounting and GSO records is still on-going.

observations.managementAction:
The updating of the RPTAR was not yet completed.

The RPTAR was not yet submitted to the Accounting Office, thus, the balances per books remained unadjusted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

exert best effort to locate pertinent documents to substantiate the dormant accounts and assess collectability of the dormant accounts;

if collection is no longer feasible, undertake the appropriate actions for the proper disposition of these accounts, following the procedures outlined in COA Circular 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023; and 

consider the recognition of impairment losses for those dormant accounts with no potential collection or recovery pursuant to Item 7.3 of COA Circular No.  2016-005.
observations.reason: Lack of supporting documents to support the inactive or dormant accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Assessor to submit the lacking documents for the acquisition of lot, to facilitate the conduct of review and evaluation with the objective of determining whether the disbursements are valid, legal and appropriate; and

enjoin all concerned officials not to approve the transactions and disbursements of government funds without the availability of basic documentary requirements outlined in the aforementioned Circular.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to require the Collecting Officers to remit all collections on a daily basis and ensure that all collections remitted to him are deposited daily/regularly and intact to the authorized depository bank, in accordance with the pertinent provisions of the Local Treasury Manual.
observations.managementAction: Revenue collections were still not remitted and deposited daily/regularly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct the BAC to comply with the requirement on the submission of APPs, PMRs, and APCPI Results to the GPPB pursuant to applicable provisions of GPPB Circular No. 02-2020 dated May 20, 2020, in order to enable the LGU to achieve its performance targets and to avert any risk of possible sanctions due to such non-compliance.
observations.managementAction: The Management was not able to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to (a) cause the reconstitution of the Inventory Committee; (b) instruct the Inventory Committee to (i) conduct physical inventory of all PPE of the Municipality; (ii) submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team on or before January 31 of each year; and (iii) reconcile the inventory reports with the accounting records; (c) Acting Municipal Treasurer to (i) maintain PC and PAR for the proper control and monitoring of the LGU’s property and equipment; (ii) ensure that all vehicles in active use are registered with the LTO and covered with property insurance; and (iii) submit to the Audit Team copies of OR/CR of all motor vehicles and heavy equipment for verification of its ownership; (d) Municipal Accountant to maintain PPELC; (e) Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer to (i) consider adopting the one-time cleansing option for PPE accounts; and (ii) in coordination with the accountable officers, prepare IIRUP to report all unserviceable properties; and (f) Disposal Committee to facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable PPEs in accordance with the prescribed guidelines and procedures.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the: a. City Planning and Development Coordinator to expedite the conduct of comprehensive assessment to identify the needed units of Motor Vehicle for immediate procurement; and b. City Engineer to follow up with Urban Settlement Development Office, the status of the possible relocation site of informal settlers’ families in order to proceed with the immediate implementation of the planned infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: As of December 31, 2024, the City Planning and Development Coordinator was able to determine what the PPAs are to be reverted and which PPAs will push through except only non-infra projects. According to Planning and Development Coordinator, eight Motor Vehicles/Heavy Equipment and seven Medical Equipment were not yet implemented. Three infrastructure projects totaling P4,356,706.33 were already bidded in the 1st quarter of CY 2025. As of December 31, 2024, the 24 projects intended for the relocation site of informal settlers’ families, funded out of the Continuing Appropriation of the 20 percent DF amounting to P61,872,532.23 remained unimplemented/unobligated as at year-end due to Protected Area Management Board (PAMB) clearance issues.
observations.reason: verification and assessment are being conducted by the City Planning and Development Coordinator as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: a. conduct One-Time Cleansing of PPE account, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006; and b. strictly comply with Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2024, the City Government already hired and trained staff for the One-Time Cleansing of its PPE account balances. Reconciliation of unaccounted balances and variances of the City between the Accounting records and RPCPPE is still ongoing as of year-end.
observations.reason: One-Time Cleansing was not conducted as of December 31, 2024 due to the voluminous PPE account balances of the City.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the CDRRMO to: a. ensure full utilization of the LDRRMF for the targeted projects pursuant to the City’s disaster risk reduction management plans; b. coordinate with the City Engineer in ensuring that all programmed infrastructure projects under the LDRRMF are monitored and completely implemented; and c. strictly comply with Section 21 of RA No. 10121.
observations.managementAction: As of December 31, 2024, only P15,827,000.00 out of the LDRRMF from Continuing Appropriation was utilized as of December 31, 2024. The Sangguniang Panlungsod authorized the reversion of the identified projects under the Continuing Appropriation of LDRRMF totaling P48,306,853.25 through Ordinance No. 2024-1115 enacted on May 6, 2024. The CDRRMO closely coordinated with the City Engineer and prioritized the implementation of the projects funded under LDRRMF. Implementation of some projects funded under the LDRRMF from current year appropriation were given priority by the City. Reiterated with modifications in Observation No. 7, Page Nos. 49-51, Part II of CY 2023 AAR.
observations.reason: There are PPAs that were funded by the Provincial Government and National Government Agencies; some projects remained unimplemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to: a. coordinate with the City Cooperative and Livelihood Office, and issue demand letters to those existing organizations, and demand immediate settlement of their payables to the City; b. consider and/or initiate a request for write-off for those balances which remained dormant for 10 years or more; and c. conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts, to ensure collection when these become due and demandable, in conformity with COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: The City through the City Government collected a total of P355,963.57 from three Non-Government Organizations (NGOs). The City requested for dormant receivables write-off; the City Government will prepare the request for write-off for those receivables which were difficult to retrieve. The City Accounting Office conducted a regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts. Some cooperatives were already dissolved and the City could no longer send demand letters. The City Government is still in the process of retrieving needed documents for the request for write-off.
observations.reason: Various cooperative were already dissolved and documents were difficult to retrieve, making it difficult to process write-off requests.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to follow the recording and reporting guidelines for LDRRMF as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and the provisions of Paragraph 27 of the IPSAS.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: For CY 2024, the Management had already submitted the related accounts with the correct recording and reporting guidelines for LDRRMF. However, as of this writing, no proof of compliance was submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: a. Immediately secure the relevant supporting documents/records as required under Annex 8 of COA Circular No. 2023-008; b. File through the Municipal Mayor, with/through the Audit Team Leader and/or Supervising Auditor, depending on the jurisdictional amount, the request for the approval of COA to write-off/derecognize from the books the dormant unliquidated cash advances amounting to P526,565.64; and c. Strictly comply with the guidelines prescribed in COA Circular No. 2023-008 on the proper disposition of the dormant unliquidated cash advances for the fair presentation of the Advances accounts in the Municipality’s financial statements.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has yet to prepare the necessary documents to support their request for the approval of the authority to write-off. As of this writing, no records of compliance were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to adopt a complete ATM payroll system for the Municipality to eliminate the risk of loss or misapplication of funds aside from promoting greater convenience, transparency, and accountability in consonance with the provisions of Section 2.1 of IRR of EO No. 170, S. 2022, Section 124 of PD No. 1445 and DBM Circular No. 2008-8 dated October 23, 2008.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: Management had added an additional manpower for compliance on the prompt submission of necessary payroll documents but proof of full implementation was not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Mayor and the members of the Local School Board ensure a more strategic and efficient utilization, including the maximize use thereof, of the appropriated funds for priority programs/projects/activities under the Special Education Fund.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: Management still has discrepancies in their records due to absence of proper coordination between the concerned Offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to submit the required Liquidation Reports for the various fund transfers from DOH-CHD; and b. Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to reconcile the Municipality’s records with the DOH-CHD.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: The Management is still in the process of reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Health Officer to determine the reason for the non-utilization of the P1,511,396.79 funds for the Construction/repair of BHS at Valley Golf, Cainta, and if feasible, address the need to implement the program in accordance with the guidelines governing the DOH’s HFEP; and b. Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer to return the unutilized funds, if determined that the project is not anymore feasible for implementation.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: The Management had re-aligned the use of funds and fully utilized the said funds for the purchase of medical equipment. As of this writing, no proof of compliance was submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. OIC-GSO in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to list all insurable properties in the PIF as required under COA Circular No. 2018-002 and submit said PIF to the Supervising Auditor/Audit Team Leader and the GIF, GSIS, not later than April 30 of each year; b. Municipal Budget Officer to prioritize the inclusion in the Municipal Government’s annual budget the amount of premiums necessary to cover the required insurance for all insurable properties; and c. Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 656 as amended by PD No. 245 and COA Circular No. 2018-002.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: Management has partially complied by insuring some of the Municipality’s insurable properties. The Municipal Accountant has yet to prioritize the inclusion in the Municipal Government’s annual budget the amount of premiums necessary to cover the required insurance for all insurable properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to prepare a catch-up action plan on the programs/projects/activities to fast track the implementation of the developmental projects charged to the 20 percent DF Capital Outlay and monitor the effective and efficient implementation of these projects; b. Ensure that development projects for inclusion in the 20 percent DF are properly planned and are procurement-implementation-ready; and c. Henceforth, ensure to maximize the utilization of the 20 percent DF in compliance with DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 and Section 2 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason:
Management still has unimplemented PPAs due to the reprogramming of identified PPAs during the year and the lack of monitoring of its implementation.
Reiterated with modifications in Observation No. 14, Page Nos. 56 to 59, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to submit the lacking documentary requirements relative to the procurement of infrastructure projects in the amount of P26,861,371.15 to establish the regularity, propriety and validity of the disbursements.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: Management submitted the lacking documents in October 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Require the GAD Focal Person to ensure that all PPAs are properly planned, and that implementation thereof be in accordance with the approved GAD Plan and Budget to optimize the use of allocated funds, and that all issues on gender mainstreaming are duly addressed by the prepared plan, for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries; and b. Henceforth, maximize the utilization/implementation of the GAD Plan and Budget to attain its objective in accordance with Item 2.3 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by JMC No. 2016-01.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: For CY 2024, the Management was able to implement 90.69 percent utilization rate for the PPAs and substantially implemented the GAD PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO to: a. Submit justification why the expenditures of P3,935,506.34 which are non-disaster preparedness related PPAs charged to LDRRMF should not be disallowed in audit; b. Refrain from appropriating/utilizing the LDRRMF to projects and activities not pertaining to disaster risk reduction and management; and c. Henceforth, strictly abide with the guidelines set forth under NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint JMC No. 2014-01 dated April 4, 2014, COA Circular No. 2012-002 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: Management reverted the amount of P3,935,506.34 in the LDRRMF and ensures that charges to LDRRMF are aligned with pertinent provisions in making disbursements under the LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned implementing offices to optimize the utilization of its LDRRMF, to lessen the socio-economic and environmental impacts of disasters in the community and disaster risks, including projected climate risks, and to enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the community.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: No comment was provided by the Management, moreover, as of this writing, no records of compliance were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: a) Municipal Accountant to verify and evaluate the accuracy of the reconciling items reflected in the BRS and effect the necessary adjustments in the books to come up with the adjusted balance of the Cash in Bank- LCCA account; b) Municipal Treasurer to request from the Manager of the Municipality’s depository bank the copies of the debit/credit memos as basis for the adjustment of the valid identified reconciling items without details in the books; c) Municipal Treasurer to exert extra effort to locate and immediately submit to the Municipal Accounting Office the disbursement vouchers for proper recording in the Municipality’s books; and henceforth, d) strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: The Management had already requested copies of debit/credit memos from Land Bank of the Philippines. However, they are still not able to adjust their books as of year-end. Reiterated with modifications in Observation No. 1, Page Nos. 30 to 32 and Observation No. 6, Page Nos. 40 to 41, CY 2024 AAR. Hence, the Audit Team recommends that these audit recommendations be closed in the Recommendations Tracking Sheet as of December 31, 2024, which was approved by the COA IV-A Regional Director as per Memo dated September 2, 2025.

observations.recommendation: a) use the duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names of the taxpayers and amount due and collectible form each of them provided by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer in setting-up the RPT/SET Receivables at the start of year; b) reconcile the balances of the RPT/SET Receivable accounts to reflect their correct balances in the financial statements; and c) strictly comply with the provisions of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, on the recording of RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: For preparation of request to the RD for the approval of the RTS for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications.

observations.recommendation: a) reconcile the net variance of P1,806,281,464.72 between the Detailed Statement of Financial Position and RPCPPE; b) conduct One-Time Cleansing of PPE account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006; c) prepare JEVs to effect the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account; and henceforth, d) strictly comply with Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still on its process of preparing for the preliminary activities relative to the conduct of the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra efforts to conduct periodic reconciliation of their records in order to identify the cause/s of the differences between their respective records and thereafter, make the necessary adjustments to reconcile their books.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: For preparation of request to the RD for the approval of the RTS for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications.

observations.recommendation: a) analyze and ascertain the validity of outstanding obligations of the Municipality, those found valid should be paid immediately in order to clear the payable accounts; b) request for an authority for the reversion of those payables without valid documentation that have been outstanding for two years or more, to the Sangguniang Bayan. Thereafter, make the necessary journal entry to revert the said long outstanding obligations to Government Equity so that the Municipality can use the available funds for other priority projects that would uplift the living condition of its constituents; and c) strictly comply with the provisions of GAAM, Volume I on the proper recognition of Accounts Payable.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: For preparation of request to the RD for the approval of the RTS for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications.

observations.recommendation: a) direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the OIC-Provincial Accountant and Provincial Treasurer to verify the net differences of P8,360,287.52 and P6,725,113.92, between the recorded balances of Due from and Due to LGUs accounts of the Municipality and their reciprocal accounts Due to and Due from LGUs reflected in the books of the Province; b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to subsequently prepare the required adjusting journal entries to present the correct balances of the said accounts in the Municipality’s financial statements; and c) require the Municipal Accountant to conduct a periodic reconciliation of accounts Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs in the Municipality books with the corresponding reciprocal accounts in the books of the Province.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: The Management has yet to formally conduct a reconciliation of records with the Provincial Accountant to determine the established and correct balance of the account, however, as of this writing, no records of compliance were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to continue exerting extra efforts in retrieving the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) and/or other related documents, conduct thorough analysis of the accounts to determine the cause/s of the difference in the reciprocal accounts and thereafter, make the necessary adjusting entry to correct the records.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: For preparation of request to the RD for the approval of the RTS for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor file the request for authority to write-off the outstanding cash advances of the deceased AOs to the COA ATL and/or SA in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant did not complete the necessary documents to support their request for authority to write-off. Reiterated with modifications in Observation No. 11, Page Nos. 52 to 54, CY 2023 AAR. Hence, the Audit Team recommends that these audit recommendations be closed in the Recommendations Tracking Sheet as of December 31, 2024, which was approved by the COA IV-A Regional Director as per Memo dated September 2, 2025.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to reconcile the above subject accounts and remit whatever balance due to the agencies concerned to avoid incurrence of penalties, surcharges and inconveniences to the employees concerned. Strictly remit in full the withheld taxes, premium contributions and loan amortizations to the respective government agencies in accordance with the above-mentioned regulations.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: Reiterated with modifications in Observation Nos. 11 and 12, Page Nos. 48 to 53, CY 2024 AAR. Hence, the Audit Team recommends that these audit recommendations be closed in the Recommendations Tracking Sheet as of December 31, 2024, which was approved by the COA IV-A Regional Director as per Memo dated September 2, 2025.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to strictly comply with the guidelines set out in COA Circular No. 2013-004 on the notification to the public or publicity relating to the project.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: The Management is still on the process of ensuring that all designated project billboards show all relevant information of the projects being implemented by the Municipality. As of this writing, no records of compliance were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor: a) require the Municipal Engineer to expedite the submission of the complete description and cost segregation of the road components for road projects of the Municipality; b) instruct the Municipal Accountant and OIC-GSO to maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components and Local Road Network Property Card to substantiate the correctness of the general ledger balance for Infrastructure Assets - Road Networks account; c) direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the total road network system of the Municipality at year-end; and d) henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer did not submit a complete description and cost segregation needed. The Municipal Accountant and the OIC-GSO have failed to maintain a complete subsidiary records for roads and road components and Local Road Network Property Card. As of this writing, no records of compliance were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Chairperson and BAC Secretariat to: a) furnish the Audit Team copies of perfected contracts and PO together with the supporting documents within the prescribed period; and b) strictly adhere with the above-cited provision of COA Circular No. 2009-01 for timely and objective evaluation of contracts and purchase orders by the Audit Team, so that the Municipality could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: Lack of proper coordination among concerned officials. As of this writing, no records of compliance were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: a. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete inventory of all supplies and materials for every semester and submit a report thereon to the Audit Team in proper form, summarized according to type of inventory and properly accomplished in all particulars, in accordance with Section 124 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; b. Municipal Accountant and the OIC-General Services Officer to maintain a complete and updated SLCs and stock cards for each type of Inventories for proper monitoring and conduct periodic reconciliation of accounting and property records; and c. Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to trace back the details of non-moving account balances of Food Supplies for Distribution and Property and Equipment for Distribution under Inventory Held for Distribution; and Accountable Forms, Plates and Stickers and Construction Materials Inventory under Inventory Held for Consumption for fair presentation of the Inventories accounts.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: The Inventory Committee did not conduct the physical count of inventory of all supplies and materials for the Municipality for CY 2023. The Municipal Accountant and OIC-GSO failed to maintain a complete and updated SLCs and stock cards for each type of Inventories. Management has yet to conduct the physical count and to prepare the necessary documents to support their request for the approval to write-off. As of this writing, no records of compliance were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, at the earliest and practicable period, initiate the one-time cleansing of PPE balances of the Municipality by following the guidelines and procedures on the six activities prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006, to address the noted discrepancy amounting to P154,804,590.31 as of December 31, 2023 and to come up with reliable PPE balances; and direct the Supply/Property Officer to come up with a reliable Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment and updated property cards.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had already attended the Training/Seminar on One-Time Cleansing of PPE Accounts led by Center for Global Best Practices and will plan for the initiation of the latter. Furthermore, the Supply/Property Officer had already come up with a reliable RPCPPE and continue to update the property cards.
observations.reason: One-time cleansing of PPE balances is still not yet performed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to: a. Strictly comply with the IRR of RA No. 8291, RA No. 9679 and HDMF Circular No. 275 and RA No. 7875 by ensuring that the amounts withheld from employees are remitted in full within the prescribed period to avoid payment of penalties for the late remittance; and b. Cause the immediate remittance of the amount of P1,144,895.96, P31,569.77, and P57,861.00 to the GSIS, PAG-IBIG, and PhilHealth respectively.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue to ensure that the amounts withheld from employees are remitted in full within the prescribed period to avoid payment of penalties for the late remittance; coordinated with the agencies and continue the settlement of the remittances thereof as compliance to the said audit observations. The Municipal Accountant stated that the Municipality had already submitted the unpaid premiums to GSIS amounting to P945,215.23 which pertains to various years from CY 2007 to 2024 and attached supporting documents; and they continue to coordinate with GSIS for billing from November 2024 onwards and with Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth for dormant accounts.
observations.reason: Still with unremitted balances as of April 2025 under the GF for Due to GSIS, Pag-ibig and Philhealth accounts submitted to the Audit Team on July 21, 2025 were as follows: P1,122,917.04, P83,628.72 and P187,720.23, respectively, still indicating non-remittance within the prescribed period of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Inventory Committee to prepare the RPCPPE in the prescribed form, filled up completely with the necessary data; b. Reconcile the variance of P320,199,351.95 between the Statement of Financial Position and RPCPPE; c. Conduct one time cleansing of PPE accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to effect the same and henceforth; and d. Strictly comply with Section 6.3 of COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee had already prepared the RPCPPE for CY 2022 in prescribed form and filled up with necessary data and submitted to COA Office. Upon back tracing of the Inventory Committee's record, certain discrepancies were noted and necessary adjustments were done for CY 2022. The Municipality had already established a Disposal Committee in preparation for the conduct of one-time cleansing of PPE accounts. The Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant will strictly comply with Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.reason: One-time cleansing of PPE balances is still not yet performed and net discrepancies between the book balances of PPE and actual physical inventory as reflected in the RPCPPE were still observed.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to continue exerting efforts to identify the cause/s of the remaining differences between their respective records and thereafter, make the necessary adjustments to reconcile their books.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2024, the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to continue exerting efforts to identify the cause/s of the remaining differences between their respective records and thereafter, make the necessary adjustments to reconcile their books. For preparation of request to the RD for the approval of the Recommendations Tracking Sheet (RTS) for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications.
observations.reason: Management is still on the process of reconciling the variance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to: a. Identify the cause/s of the P427,267.23 differences between their respective records and thereafter, make the necessary adjustments to reconcile their books. b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort in identifying/ locating the related documents for the P135,016.85 variance found in the BRS and thereafter, draw journal entry vouchers to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustments and correction in the books for a fair presentation of the balance of the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements. c. Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit to the Accounting Office the schedule of unclaimed/unreleased checks every end of the year to serve as basis in setting up of liability account.
observations.managementAction: The Management had reduced the unreconciled balance by P292,250.38, leaving an unreconciled balance of P135,016.85. The Municipal Accountant was able to prepare the necessary adjusting entry No. dated October 31, 2024 for the stale check and the Municipal Treasurer was able to furnish the Accounting Office the schedule of unclaimed/unreleased checks.
observations.reason: Management is still on the process of reconciling the variance. For preparation of request to the RD for the approval of the Recommendations Tracking Sheet (RTS) for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Direct the Inventory Committee, in coordination with the Officer in Charge (OIC)-Municipal General Service Officer (MGSO) to submit to the Audit Team the duly approved Physical Inventory Plan (PIP), containing at least, the specific assignments/duties of the members, the cut-off date, and a schedule specifying the dates and locations of the inventory taking activities from start up to the targeted completion of the physical inventory; and b. Monitor compliance by the Inventory Committee, the OIC-MGSO and the Municipal Accountant with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 on the procedural guidelines for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances, including the reconciliation of the inventory count per RPCPPE with property and accounting records.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management. For preparation of request to the RD for the approval of the Recommendations Tracking Sheet (RTS) for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications.
observations.reason: Management is still on the process of preparing for the activities relative to the conduct of the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-MPDC to prepare a catch-up action plan on the programs/projects/activities to fast track the implementation of the developmental projects charged to the 20 percent DF Capital Outlay and monitor the effective and efficient implementation of these projects. Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor in coordination with the members of the Sangguniang Bayan: a. Ensure that development projects for inclusion in the 20 percent DF are properly planned, procurement-implementation-ready, and properly provided with timely enacted appropriation ordinance; and b. Henceforth, maximize the utilization of the 20 percent DF in compliance with DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 and Section 2 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management. For preparation of request to the RD for the approval of the Recommendations Tracking Sheet (RTS) for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications.
observations.reason: Management was unable to fully implement 11 out of 22 projects funded under 20 percent DF. For preparation of request to the RD for the approval of the RTS for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor: a. Strictly require the GAD Focal Person to ensure that all programs, plans and activities are properly planned, and that implementation thereof be in accordance with the approved GAD Plan and Budget to optimize the use of allocated funds, and that all issues on gender mainstreaming are duly addressed by the prepared plan, for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries; and b. Henceforth, maximize the utilization/implementation of the GPB to attain its objective in accordance with Item 2.3 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA-JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by JMC No. 2016-01.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: In CY 2024, the Municipality failed to secure a DILG-endorsed GPB as requirement to ensure that all issues on gender mainstreaming are duly addressed by the prepared plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra efforts to conduct periodic reconciliation of their records in order to identify the cause/s of the differences between their respective records and thereafter, make the necessary adjustments to reconcile their books.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2024, the amount of discrepancy between the General Ledger and Cash Book amounts to P47,347.20. For preparation of request to the RD for the approval of the Recommendations Tracking Sheet (RTS) for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications.
observations.reason: Management is still on the process of reconciling the variance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor file the request for authority to write-off duly supported with complete relevant documents of dormant Other Receivables account balance of P1,127,095.01 as at year-end to the Commission on Audit through the Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA). The request shall be supported by the following documents: a. Schedule of dormant accounts by accountable officer/debtor/government entity and by account, certified by the Accountant and approved by the Head of the government entity; b. Certification by the responsible officials of the entity to the effect that there are no records/documents available to validate claim; c. Proof of exhaustion of all remedies to collect the receivables; and d. Other justifications, like in the case of request for write-off due to loss of documents, the circumstances of the loss should be stated in the letter-request.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has yet to prepare the necessary documents to support their request for write-off.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal General Services Officer to: a. Reconcile the net variance of P19,205,065.70 between the Detailed Statement of Financial Position and RPCPPE; b. Conduct One-Time Cleansing of PPE account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006; c. Prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to effect the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account; and d. Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management. For preparation of request to the RD for the approval of the Recommendations Tracking Sheet (RTS) for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications.
observations.reason: Management is still on the process of preparing for the activities relative to the conduct of the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) to: a. Reconcile the Due to GSIS account and remit whatever balance due to the GSIS to avoid inconveniences to the employees concerned and make representation with the GSIS regarding the prior years’ unremitted balance; and b. Promptly remit in full the withheld GSIS premiums/contributions/loan amortization in compliance with the provisions of RA No. 8291.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: The Municipality still has a recorded balance of undocumented amounts (Due to GSIS under Trust Fund) still not remitted back as of year-end. The balance pertains to amounts without details and dates as of year-end, some from CY 2003 and prior years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Require the GAD Focal Person to ensure that all programs, plans and activities are properly planned, and that implementation thereof be in accordance with the approved GPB to optimize the use of allocated funds, and that all issues on gender mainstreaming are duly addressed by the prepared plan, for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries; and b. Henceforth, maximize the utilization/implementation of the GPB to attain its objective in accordance with Item 2.3 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA-JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by JMC No. 2016-01.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management. For preparation of request to the RD for the approval of the Recommendations Tracking Sheet (RTS) for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications.
observations.reason: In CY 2024, the Municipality failed to secure a DILG-endorsed GPB as requirement to ensure that all issues on gender mainstreaming are duly addressed by the prepared plan.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor: a. Direct the Inventory Committee to expedite the completion of the inventory of PPE, indicate whether the items counted are under General Fund, Special Education Fund or Trust Fund and submit thereon to the Audit Team; b. Instruct the GSO to update Property Cards for all PPE accounts and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain PPE Ledger Cards for each category of assets and Real Property Ledger Cards for land to substantiate the correctness of the general ledger balances for PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: The submitted RPCPPE still does not indicate whether the items counted are charged and recorded according to the funds wherein these were recorded. Updating of PCs and PPE Ledger Cards (PPELCs) is still ongoing.
observations.reason: Lack of familiarity with the rules on the necessity of identifying the inventoried items according to the funds charged and recorded; lack of manpower for complete updating of PCs and PPELCs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor
direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to effect the immediate adjustment of valid
reconciling items in the books of accounts to ensure the correctness of
the account balances appearing in the Municipality’s financial
statements at any given time; and,
b. Municipal Treasurer to revert any unreleased checks as at year-end to
Cash in Bank account and set up its corresponding liability account to
ensure the proper representation of account balances in the financial
statements; and
c. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to closely coordinate
with each other to promptly resolve and adjust in their respective
records any unrecorded deposits, collections and disbursements
appearing in the reconciliation and ensure they are recorded
accordingly; and
d. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provision of COA Circular No. 96-011
dated October 2, 1996 on the preparation of the bank reconciliation
statements.
observations.managementAction:
The Management has provided justification for the noted reconciling
items. The unrecorded disbursement was identified in the BRS but
remained unadjusted at year-end to properly report the correct balance
of CIB-LCCA. The Municipal Accountant took immediate action to
adjust all valid reconciling items identified in the BRS to ensure the
correctness of the account balances appearing in the Municipality’s
financial statements at any given time.
The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant were advised to
exert all best effort to comply with the recommendations and to
promptly revert any unreleased checks at year-end to the Cash in Bank
account and to their liability account to ensure the accuracy and
proper representation of the account balances in the financial
statements. The Municipal Treasurer has been instructed to ensure the
disbursement vouchers and supporting documents are submitted to
the Accounting Office in a timely manner.
observations.reason:
Five unrecorded disbursement was identified in the BRS noted
reconciling items. The unadjusted reconciling items remained
unadjusted at year-end to properly report the correct balance of CIB-
LCCA. Various unreleased checks were not revered back to the Cash in
Bank account and were treated as outstanding checks at year-end. The
reconciliation items identified in the BRS remained unadjusted on the
cashbook and accounting books.

observations.managementAction: The OIC-General Services Officer (GSO) submitted the Report on Physical Count of PPE and coordinated with the Municipal Accountant on the reconciliation of the inventory. The Municipal Accountant disclosed that semi-expendable properties with acquisition cost below P50,000 purchased during the year 2024 were already treated as inventory and that identification and finalization of adjustments are in process. The Municipal Accountant and OIC-GSO were instructed to give top priority to the completion of the One-Time Cleansing for non-existing PPE in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.reason: Request for One-Time Cleansing was still not completed and submitted. PPE with acquisition cost of less than P50,000.00 per unit remained unadjusted. Inventory of the Municipality's PPE and reconciliation thereof were still incomplete.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and the Human Resources Management Officer to:
a. Adopt a complete ATM payroll system for the payment of JO Personnel and CoS Employees to eliminate the risk of loss or misapplication of funds and to promote greater convenience, transparency, and accountability in consonance with the provisions of Section 2.1 of IRR of EO No. 170, S. 2022 and provisions of PD No. 1445;
b. Submit the duly verified/approved DTRs and Accomplishment Reports of the CoS personnel hired for the Emergency Employment Program (EEP) to facilitate the post audit of the disbursement made, otherwise, the said transactions shall be suspended in audit; and
c. Henceforth, strictly comply with the documentary requirements for the payment of JO and CoS personnel as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-01 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Management coordinated with the Landbank of the Philippines – Tanay, Rizal for the facilitation of ATM accounts for JO personnel. The DTRs and Accomplishment Reports of the CoS Personnel hired under the Emergency Employment Program (EEP) were submitted and the transactions were recorded. The Municipal Accountant and HRMO were instructed to adhere strictly to the documentary requirements and payroll process.
observations.reason: Payroll system for JO personnel and CoS employees will be implemented on July 2025. The DTRs and Accomplishment Reports were properly attached in some transactions; however, training and complete adoption of ATM payroll system is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require immediate action with the GSIS and the concerned Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) to make representation with the GSIS on the updating and/or settlement of the delinquent loan accounts to avoid further accumulation of penalties and surcharges by the concerned municipal employees and unnecessary tying up of funds of the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor directed the Municipal Accountant (ERF Handler) and the HRMO (AAO) to coordinate with the GSIS for reconciliation. On February 7, 2025, the Municipality sent a letter to GSIS expressing willingness to settle unremitted premium dues subject to condonation of penalty and interest. The Municipality remitted unpaid premiums amounting to P588,298.73 after final reconciliation.
observations.reason: Pending final reconciliation and approval with GSIS. Continued monthly GSIS notices on unremitted premiums were still being received.

observations.recommendation:
We strongly reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to:
a. Fully implement the GAD Plan and Budget so that the intention of GAD on promoting gender-responsive governance, protecting and fulfilling women’s rights, and promoting women’s empowerment as mandated could be fully achieved;
b. Strictly monitor the implementation of the planned GAD PPAs to ensure that GAD objectives are fully attained and the actual expenditures will not exceed the approved budget per PPAs; and
c. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the guidelines of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by JMC No. 2016-01, on the localization of the Magna Carta for Women.
observations.managementAction: The Management accepted the findings and recommendations and undertook to strengthen collaboration between the GAD Focal Person, the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant, ensure functionality of the GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team, and remind concerned officials on laws governing GAD programs.
observations.reason: GAD Fund was still not fully utilized and various projects exceeded their appropriated budget. The GAD Plan and Budget were not fully implemented as required.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. effect the immediate recording of valid reconciling items in the books of accounts to ensure the correctness of the account balances appearing in the Municipality’s financial statements at any given time; and
b. closely coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to cause the timely submission of the disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents of the checks issued and released to the payee/suppliers; and henceforth, for recording of all checks released on the preceding day including the related supporting disbursement voucher, and as well as, the immediate release of all the checks have been drawn for release remain unclaimed by the payees.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor directed the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to have a close coordination on the timely/daily submission or transmission of the RCI to the Accounting Office and to record all checks released on the preceding day accordingly. The Municipal Accountant was instructed to effect the immediate recording of valid reconciling items.
observations.reason: Reconciliation items presented in the BRS for CYs 2023 and 2024 were not properly adjusted in the books. Several unreleased checks and delayed submission of RCI from the Treasury Office caused the unrecorded transactions and unadjusted reconciling items.

observations.managementAction: The Physical Inventory Team started conducting physical count of all PPEs on April 14, 2024. A new Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) is being prepared and will be submitted to the Audit Team. The OIC-GSO was instructed to exert best efforts to comply with the required records and the Municipal Accountant to avail the One-Time Cleansing under COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.reason: Inventory of the Municipality’s PPEs was still incomplete and reconciliation with the accounting records was not yet completed. Procedures for One-Time Cleansing were not fully executed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant for the reconciliation of Due from LGUs to the Due to LGUs accounts so that appropriate adjustments could be made on the affected accounts for fair presentation in the Municipality’s financial statements; and
b. Conduct periodic reconciliation of the account balances of Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs in the Municipality’s books against their reciprocal accounts reflected in the books of the Province to avoid the accumulation of discrepancies between the two records.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office was instructed to have constant follow-up to the Provincial Government of Rizal on the status of the request for reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts reflected in the books of the Province to avoid accumulation of discrepancies.
observations.reason: Pending approval of request to write-off old balances of Reciprocal accounts in the books of Rizal Province for proper reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts. The Office of the Municipal Accountant is following up on the status.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the provisions of Sections 6.03 and 6.04 of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 to ensure timely submission of the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents for recording in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer was instructed to ensure the timely submission of DVs and supporting documents to the Accounting Office. The Municipal Accountant was instructed to assure timely recording in the books.
observations.reason: Submission of disbursement vouchers and supporting documents to the accounting office was still late for some transactions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to:
a. fully implement the GAD Plan and Budget so that the intention of the GAD on promoting gender-responsive governance, protecting and fulfilling women’s rights, and promoting women’s empowerment as mandated could be fully achieved;
b. strictly monitor the implementation of the planned GAD PPAs to ensure that GAD objectives are fully attained and the actual expenditures will not exceed the approved budget per PPAs; and
c. strictly adhere to the guidelines of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 as amended by JMC No. 2016-01 on the localization of the Magna Carta for Women.
observations.managementAction: The Management accepted the findings and recommendations and undertakes to strengthen collaboration between the GAD Focal Person, the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant. The Management ensures functionality of the GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team and to constantly remind concerned officials on the laws governing GAD programs.
observations.reason: The GAD Plan and Budget was not fully implemented and some disbursements exceeded appropriated amounts. Monitoring and full utilization remain works-in-progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to trace back and substantiate the old balances of the PPE accounts to provide the correct depreciation for each type of property accounts to provide the correct depreciation for each type of property of accounts and thereafter recognize the depreciation accordingly to avoid the overstatement of assets and government equity.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is tracing back and attempting to substantiate the old balances of the PPE accounts. Plans were made to provide correct depreciation by type of property and adjust the books accordingly.
observations.reason: Procedures for One-Time Cleansing and tracing of old balances were not fully implemented. The process is ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO-Designate to:
a. cause the immediate disposal of all the accounted unserviceable properties following the guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 92-386; 
b. coordinate to trace back and determine the details of the 320 unaccounted unserviceable properties; and
c. draw Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) for the derecognition of all unserviceable properties in the Municipality’s books once disposed of and record accordingly for the income that could be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed and efforts are being undertaken to comply with the request for disposal of the unserviceable properties. The Municipal Accountant will avail the One-Time Cleansing provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 to address the unaccounted unserviceable properties and prepare the necessary JEVs upon disposal.
observations.reason: Pending submission of request for One-Time Cleansing and completion of required procedures. Disposal process not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursement and collection reports and their supporting documents are submitted within the prescribed period as required under the cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer were instructed to ensure timely submission of DVs and supporting documents and accounting entries. Transmittals of DVs were submitted to the Audit Team for reference.
observations.reason: Submission of outstanding DVs and supporting documents remained delayed for some months; some older items were submitted after report date but some records still awaited.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-General Services Officer to ensure compliance with the Perpetual Inventory Method and that the required forms, subsidiary records and periodic reports are prepared, maintained and submitted and that Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and original copies of Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) from end-users are used.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-GSO was instructed to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to comply with the required forms, subsidiary records and periodic reports as prescribed in the MNGAS for LGUs and to conduct periodic physical counts for appropriate adjustments and recognition of the expended inventory.
observations.reason: Recording of issuance of supplies and materials were made without the required SSMI and original RIS from end-users. Perpetual inventory method adoption was still in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GSO to maintain Local Road Inventory and Road Map, Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPC) and Report on Local Road Network (RLRN) in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-008; and the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the total road network system of the Municipality as required by COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: GSO was directed to maintain the related records regarding the Road Network System. The Notes to the Financial Statements for CY 2024 included disclosure of the Local Road Network in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.reason: Property records for Road Networks were still in the process of preparation and reconciliation; GSO records were incomplete at report date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Conduct thorough analysis and prepare journal entries for the identified valid book reconciling items reflected in the bank reconciliation and the same should be done before closing the books to ensure the accuracy of the cash at the end of each reporting period and copy furnish this Office for the drawn journal entries for validation and evaluation; and b. Gather documents for analysis and reconciliation and to trace the accounting entries made in previous years starting from the point the subsidiary ledgers on the PNB account balance has become abnormal and the recorded series of transactions that occurred to determine the root cause and draw adjusting entries, if warranted, to correct the account balance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already provided the Municipal Treasurer the bank reconciling items in order for the latter to locate the checks not taken up by the books.
observations.reason: The identified bank reconciling items are not yet taken up/recorded in the books up to date. Reiterated with modification in Audit Observation No. 2, Page Nos. 47 to 52.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the guidelines in RA No. 11032 within the prescribed period and the corresponding MOA entered into between by the MGT and BFP. Otherwise, any unjustified failure on the part of the official concerned to submit the required reports shall be considered a ground for the automatic suspension of payment of his salaries until he shall have complied with the requirement without prejudice to any disciplinary action which may be instituted against him, pursuant to Section 122 of PD No. 1445; and determine cause and find solutions thereto.
observations.managementAction: The Management is committed to adhere with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: The Management still did not submit all the required documents and reports as the MOA. Noted as 'Not Implemented' in follow-up.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to verify and evaluate the accuracy of the reconciling items reflected in the BRS and effect the necessary adjustments in the books to come up with the adjusted balance of the CIB-LCCA accounts; He will also send a letter to Land Bank of the Philippines regarding the deposits not yet recorded due to insufficient information which form part of the reconciling items. b. OIC-Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to locate and immediately submit the disbursement vouchers (DVs) and collection reports amounting to P8,985,079.13 and P3,594,411.25, respectively, for proper recording in the MGT's books; and c. Strictly comply with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already provided the Municipal Treasurer the bank reconciling items in order for the latter to locate the checks not taken up by the books. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer are in coordination to reconcile the reciprocal accounts of Due to and Due From accounts. The Management formulated a system for monitoring of vouchers and checks. The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to locate and immediately submit the DVs and collections reports to facilitate recording in the MGT’s books.
observations.reason: The identified bank reconciling items are not yet taken up/recorded in the books. Abnormal balance reflected in the BRS still existed and not yet adjusted by the Municipal Accountant. Reiterated with modification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. OIC-Municipal Accountant and the GSO to conduct One-Time Cleansing of PPE account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006; b. OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary Journal Entry Vouchers; and c. Strictly comply with Section 6.3 of COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction: There were continuous coordination made between GSO and Municipal Accountant as well as on-going reconciliation of their respective records. Moreover, an ongoing preparation of the necessary documents such as notices for lost/missing properties, order of replacement for lost/missing property with equal or higher specifications, deduction from the terminal leave of the end-user based on their market value of the property at the time of lost and conduct public auction of unserviceable motor vehicles subject for disposal and writing-off.
observations.reason: Procedural guidelines for One-Time Cleansing of PPE is yet to be implemented by the Management. The program of works of some completed projects cannot be located which resulted to the non-completion of the detailed cost segregation of road components of completed road projects for years before 2015. For preparation of request to the RD for the approval of the RTS for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal OIC-Municipal Accountant with the list of complete description and detailed cost segregation of road components of completed road projects of the MGT for years before 2015, to facilitate the conduct of inventory, reconciliation and proper recognition of the same in the books of accounts; b. Municipal GSO to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to prepare the Report on the Local Road Network (RLRN) in proper format as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015, and a Road Map of the MGT to facilitate the preparation of a proper inventory and report of the MGT's road network system; and c. OIC-Municipal Accountant, upon receipt of the corresponding required reports from the Municipal General Service and Engineering Offices, to make the necessary adjustments to recognize the cost of the previous years’ Local Road Network to present the correct balance of the Local Road Networks account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal GSO furnished the Municipal OIC-Municipal Accountant a copy of the road projects of the MGT. The Municipal Engineer will furnish the GSO the copy of the detailed cost segregation of Local Road Network. Afterwards, necessary adjustment will be effected once the office of the Municipal Accountant receive the report due to the inability of the Municipal Engineer and GSO to submit to the former the required reports pursuant to the pertinent provisions of the Circular.
observations.reason: Non-preparation of RLRN by the GSO due to non-submission of the detailed cost segregation by the Municipal Engineer. The GSO is yet to prepare the RLRN based on the actual inventory and coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Office for the Road ID No. and furnish the same to Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. OIC-Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Accounting Office to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and Provincial Treasurer to verify the net differences of P2,544,129.30 and P7,248,958.75, recorded balances of Due from and Due to LGUs between the books of the Municipality and their reciprocal accounts Due from/Due to LGUs of Due to and Due from accounts of the Province; and b. OIC-Municipal Accountant to subsequently prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to present the correct balances of the said accounts in the Municipality’s financial statements; and henceforth, OIC-Municipal Accountant to conduct a periodic reconciliation of accounts from LGUs and Due to LGUs in the Municipality books with the corresponding reciprocal accounts in the books of the Province.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office and the Provincial Accountant has been continuously coordinating to reconcile the reciprocal accounts of Due to and from LGUs.
observations.reason: There was still variance between the books of the Municipality and the Province of Rizal regarding the Due from/Due to LGU accounts.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned officials to exert efforts in finding relevant documents such as report of the farmers/ beneficiaries regarding the death of the cattle, if any, the report of the provincial veterinarian and certification from the concerned offices and conducted were not necessary to be filed to COA as to the deaths of the cattle for relief of accountability.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Agriculturist already have the documents and reports as a result of investigation and actions conducted were not yet submitted to the Audit Team to support the request for the relief of accountability.
observations.reason: Relevant documents and reports as a result of investigation and actions conducted were not yet submitted to the Audit Team to support the request for relief of accountability.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to: a. require the Officer-in-Charge (OIC)-Supply Officer and the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular reconciliation of their respective records/reports to achieve an accurate balance of the account as reported in the Financial Statements and Property Records and prepare all the necessary activities, documents and reports in order to take advantage of the opportunity for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; b. instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the PPELCs for the unrecorded PPE; and OIC-Supply Officer to issue Property Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR) for all PPE items to the concerned accountable officers and maintain and update the related PCs as necessary; c. direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and keep required records and reports for Local Road Networks and disclose the total road network system in the Notes to the Financial Statements as required and the OIC-Supply Officer to render a Report on Local Road Network, road map and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; d. instruct the Municipal Engineer to assist the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Supply Officer with data needed such as complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects and assists and provide the latter all information/data in preparing the records/reports as required; e. instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all local road assets, prepare Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System and submit to the Audit Team immediately; f. create a Municipal Disposal Committee in accordance with COA and Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Joint Circular (JC) No. 2024-1 dated January 30, 2024; g. instruct the OIC-Supply Officer to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Inventory and Inspection Reports of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and Waste Material Report (WMR), whichever is applicable, and the Municipal Disposal Committee to cause the disposal of the identified unserviceable properties to prevent its further deterioration through proper disposal procedures and applicable mode of disposition as required by the COA and DBM JC No. 2024-01 dated January 30, 2024; and h. Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction: Not Management is still in the process of initial preparation of the one-time cleansing of PPE, as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 and other deficiencies observed such as: a) non-maintenance of PPELCs and PCs by the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Supply Officer, respectively, and lapses in the property management, contrary to the provisions of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume 1; b) non-compliance with the guidelines on the Local Road Assets Management System, contrary to COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015; and c) non-disposal of unserviceable PPE which runs counter to Section 79 of PD No. 1445 and Section 125 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.
observations.reason: Management is still in the process of initial preparation of the one-time cleansing of PPE

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-related PAPs as planned, in order to attain the objective for which funds are provided for, in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2011-01, as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01.
observations.managementAction: No activities were implemented. The recommendation is reiterated.
observations.reason: Election ban (Barangay and SK Elections) affected implementation of GAD-related PAPs

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to communicate with the bank and obtain bank statements from year 1997 that could provide additional information on the investigation of such unaccounted reconciling items reported in the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) and ultimately determine the cause of the unreconciled balance.
observations.managementAction: Efforts have been exerted by Management in the past but still they cannot determine the unreconciled balance. The Municipal Accountant communicated with the bank to obtain available statements.
observations.reason: Unreconciled balance still being investigated.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated that the Municipal Officials concerned obtain the approval of COA for the sale and submit the required documents, to facilitate the review and determination of the validity/propriety of the sale.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management. Request for complete document relative to the sale.
observations.reason: Management still in the process retrieving the complete document relative to the sale.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the: (a) GSO – Designate and Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all its Inventories and PPEs of the Municipality, prepare and submit the corresponding RPCI and RPCPPE in accordance with the prescribed format and with complete details; (b) GSO – Designate and Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare and maintain complete perpetual inventory records; and (ii) analyze the remaining items included in PPE accounts and reclassify the PPE items that do not meet the P50,000.00 capitalization threshold pursuant to COA Circular No. 2022-004; and (c) GSO – Designate to: (i) include all unserviceable PPEs reported in the RPCPPE in the IIRUP; and (ii) submit IIRUP with complete details, such as appraised value, and supported with photos of all unserviceable PPEs to be disposed to facilitate proper inspection and disposal of unserviceable items.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The Inventory Committee was not able to conduct a complete physical count of inventory and property items since inventory items granted by source agencies to the municipality did not pass through the General Services Office, instead these were directly issued/delivered to the end-users, such as the Municipal Agriculture Office and Municipal Health Office. Moreover, discrepancies on the remaining inventory accounts were also noted which can be attributed to the current practice of the municipality that the requested and procured inventories were immediately issued by the GSO to the respective end-users/departments after inspection and acceptance of the delivered inventories due to lack of storage. The GSO-Designate was able to submit the RPCI as of December 31, 2024, on March 20, 2025 and the revised RPCI on May 27, 2024.
