review; and

b. Ensure complete transmittal of the vouchers and its supporting documents
and other financial reports with transmittal letter that contain chronological
and sequential order including the unclaimed/cancelled/voided checks and

proper monitoring of transactions and strict compliance with the provisions of
COA Circular No. 95-006 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 on the submission of
DVs, ORs, and other mandatory financial reports to the Audit Team within the
reglementary period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to:

a. Record the issued checks in chronological order in the RCI whether or not the
checks issued are yet to be claimed by the payees and include
cancelled/voided checks on the said reports; and

b. Strictly comply with the guidelines provided in the Manual on the National
Government Accounting System for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and
Development Office to:

a. Closely monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different development
programs, projects and activities to prevent delays; and

b. Assess and update Annual Investment Plan (AIP) to ensure that only those
considered crucial to the efficient discharge of government functions for the
attainment of the desired socio-economic development and its environmental
outcomes are included in the AIP.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD focal person to
conduct a thorough and careful review and assessment to determine whether the
GAD-related programs or activities effectively address the identified gender issues
in compliance with the guidelines set forth in the MCW and its IRR and PCW-DILG-
DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Service Officer to
ensure that all insurable properties of the Municipality are insured and likewise
make the necessary steps for the proper implementation of the Section 5 of the
Property Insurance Law in order not to deprive of the opportunity to be
compensated in case of losses of damages brought about by any circumstances
or unforeseen events.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

a. Municipal Accountant to conduct thorough review on the affected accounts
and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the erroneous recording
and ensure the accuracy of the account balances; and

b. Municipal Accountant and Head of the Human Resource Management Office to
address the causes of discrepancies including the untimely remittances and
come up with appropriate measures to prevent its recurrence.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal Treasurer to:

a. Verify the composition of the Due to GSIS account and immediately remit to
the GSIS the amount due for premium contributions; and

b. Make an appropriate action to reconcile the delinquent accounts of 169
Municipal employees totaling P175,584.49 with the GSIS in accordance with the
Revised IRR of RA No. 8291, to avoid further accumulation of interests.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer, in
coordination with the Municipal Accountant to immediately remit to the Bureau of
Internal Revenue the withheld taxes amounting to P228,403.18 and remit succeeding
withheld taxes in full and within ten (10) days following the month in which the
withholding was made to avoid unnecessary payment of penalties and/or
surcharges by the withholding officer of the Municipality pursuant to Revenue
Memorandum Order No. 08-2003 dated March 3, 2003.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to:
a. determine the details of the noted difference of P54,660,867.55 for the CIB-LCCA between their respective records, and subsequently prepare the necessary adjustments to present the correct balance of the CIB-LCCA, duly supported by certified reconciliation statements of Accounting and Treasury's records; and
b. conduct periodic reconciliation of their records for particularly for the CIB-LCCA at least quarterly as required under Section 181(c) of the GAAM, Volume I and Section 63(F) of the LTOM.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. prepare and submit immediately the lacking BRS for Bank Account No. LBP 003112-1000-90 under General Fund to determine the reconciling items for adjustment in the books of accounts in consonance with PD No. 1445 and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011;
b. prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to revert the amount of Stale Checks by debiting Cash in Bank - LCCA and by crediting Accounts Payable both accounts in the amount of P126,909.06; and
c. effect the immediate adjustment of valid reconciling items in the books of accounts, as reflected in the BRS for LBP 3112-1000-74, LBP 3112-1000-66, LBP 3122-1000-18 and LBP 3112-1015-00, with net reconciling items amounting to (P92,532.70), P6,221.02, P2,940.00 and (P37,353.60), respectively, to ensure the accuracy of the account balances appearing in the Municipalitys financial statements.

We reiterated the previous years recommendation that the Municipal Mayor, at the earliest and practicable period, to initiate the one-time cleansing of PPE balances of the Municipality by following the guidelines and procedures on the six activities prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006, to eliminate the noted discrepancy amounting to P2,171,147,917.63 as of December 31, 2024 to come up with reliable PPE balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant the certified list of the names of the taxpayers and the corresponding amount of taxes due and collectible excluding the non-taxable RPUs, in the manner prescribed in Section of 20 of MNGAS, Volume I, for LGUs; and
b. direct the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to evaluate the discrepancy between their records and make the necessary adjustments to come up with a reliable RPT/SET Receivable and Deferred RPT/SET balances.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to revert the payables without valid documentation and proper authority to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund totaling P11,774,832.42, pursuant to Section 98 of PD No. 1445, subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan with a condition that appropriation shall be made available in the event that claim arises in the future. Thereafter, draw the necessary ournal entry voucher to revert the subject long outstanding obligations to the Government Equity account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to draw JEV by debiting Accounts Payable and crediting Prior Period Adjustments both in the amount of P1,809,949.41 to reflect the correct balances of affected accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Municipalitys records with the DOH and prepare the necessary adjusting entry to revert to the Due to NGAs account the unsupported Liquidation Reports for fund transfers; and
b. Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the OIC-Municipal Treasurer, to submit the required Liquidation Reports for fund transfers from DOH within the reglementary period in compliance with COA Circular No. 94-013.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to take up the unexpired portion of insurance as at year-end by debiting Prepaid Insurance and crediting Prior Year Adjustment both accounts in the amount of P3,702,034.52, to reflect the correct balances of affected accounts; and
b. Henceforth, strictly observe the application of accrual basis in the recognition of expenses to ensure fair presentation of the financial statements in compliance with PPSAS 1 and Section 4.b of GAM for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct that:
a. Inventory Committee to prepare a complete inventory of all supplies and materials at least once a year by type of inventory and submit a report thereon to the Auditor in proper form, summarized according to the type of inventory and properly accomplished in all particulars, in accordance with Sections 194 and 206 of GAM for LGUs, Volume I; and
b. Municipal Accountant to maintain complete and updated SLCs for each type of Inventories for proper monitoring and conduct periodic reconciliation of accounting and property records, in compliance with Section 202 of the same Manual, and if possible, initiate the integration of the generation of SLC in their computerized accounting system.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Property Officer to actively assist the members of the Inventory Committee
before, during, and after the conduct of the physical count for the purpose of
the PPE One-Time Cleansing, to ensure completeness and accuracy of the
inventory count and related documentation;

b. PPE One-Time Cleansing Inventory Committee (IC) to immediately convene and
proceed with the procedural guidelines and prepare a revised Physical
Inventory Plan (PIP) and submit it to the Audit Team, in accordance with the COA
Circular No. 2020-006 and ensures the compliance therewith for the proper and
smooth conduct of One-Time Cleansing of non-existing/missing PPE of the
Municipality; and

c. Municipal Accountant to draw necessary Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to
properly account for the results of the PPE One-Time Cleansing undertaken by the
Municipality;

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the:

● Supply Officer to:

a) Immediately implement measures to secure both the storage and delivery
areas by installing appropriate security features, such as durable locks,
physical barriers, and monitoring systems, to safeguard government
assets. Management should also regularly assess and monitor these
facilities to ensure continuous adherence to asset protection policies;

b) Immediately secure the fidelity bond of the Supply Custodian in compliance
with Section 101(2) of PD No. 1445 and Treasury Circular No. 02-2009;

c) Ensure that the Supply Custodian formally reports to and functions under
the supervision of the designated Supply Officer;

● Municipal Administrator to:

d) Create an Inventory Committee, as authorized by the Municipal Mayor, to
properly oversee the physical inventory-taking and the reconciliation of
inventory records with accounting records to ensure the accuracy,
existence and completeness of properties;

e) Issue appropriate office orders or memoranda clearly defining the roles,
responsibilities, and reporting arrangements between in the Supply Office
the to segregate the key functions in accordance with internal control
principles; and

f) Monitor the actions of all concerned departments to ensure that audit
recommendations are prioritized and implemented promptly, and
consistently remind concerned officials and employees of the laws, rules,
and regulations pertaining to the management of inventories;

● Inventory Committee to:

g) Conduct a complete and timely physical count of all inventories at the end
of every semester, and prepare and submit the corresponding Report of
Physical Count of Inventories (RCPI); and

h) Ensure the timely notification and participation of a COA representative
during future physical inventory activities to enhance the credibility of the
inventory results and to comply with applicable rules and regulations;

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor
require/instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to (i) prioritize the reconciliation of the deposits in transit, and henceforth ascertain that deposits are recorded in the books at the time these were actually deposited to the AGDB; and (ii) prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to account for the stale checks for fair and accurate presentation of the Cash in Bank – LCCA and the related Payable account in the Statement of Financial Position as at year-end; and
b. Municipal Treasurer to (i) send written notices to the payees one month before the check becomes stale; (ii) for stale checks already issued to the payees, immediately inform them to return the same to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, for cancellation and issuance of the necessary check replacements thereof; (iii) for unclaimed stale checks still in his possession, immediately cancel the same and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the related original DVs and supporting documents, to facilitate adjustment in the books; and (iv) henceforth ensure that all checks issued are properly monitored, to avoid accumulation of stale checks.

In view thereof, we reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that the Municipal Mayor ensure strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) strictly monitor the grant of cash advance by issuing certification that previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books to prevent the grant of additional cash advances to those officers and employees with outstanding cash advances; and (ii) ensure that cash advances granted to AOs are within their maximum cash accountability.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the: (a) GSO-Designate and Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all its Inventories and PPEs of the Municipality, prepare and submit the corresponding RPCI and RPCPPE in accordance with the prescribed format and with complete details; (b) GSO-Designate and Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare and maintain complete perpetual inventory records; and (ii) analyze the remaining items included in PPE accounts and reclassify the PPE items that do not meet the P50,000.00 capitalization threshold pursuant to COA Circular No. 2022-004; and (c) GSO-Designate to prepare and submit IIRUP for the remaining identified unserviceable PPEs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to (i) conduct a review and evaluation of the projects recorded under the CIP account to determine their current status; (ii) reclassify completed and accepted projects to the appropriate PPE accounts, supported by relevant documents, such as Certificates of Completion and Inspection Reports, in accordance with Section 50 of the Manual on NGAS; (iii) conduct a thorough reconciliation between the total billings and project costs for all ongoing and completed projects; and (iv) prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the actual balance of the CIP accounts in accordance with the description of the account set forth in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the Management (i) require the NGOs/POs to submit a complete and appropriate liquidation documents set under COA Circular Nos. 2012-001 and 2007-001 before recognizing fund transfers as liquidated; (ii) require the NGOs/POs to submit the original sales invoices and other necessary documents to support their fund liquidation; (iii) conduct periodic validation and monitoring of fund utilization by NGOs/POs; and (iv) require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the books of accounts, if necessary, to reflect the correct status of unliquidated fund transfers.

We also recommended that the Management ensure strict compliance with COA Circular Nos. 2004-005 and 2021-001 by limiting the use of RERs to transactions not exceeding P1,000.00. Transactions beyond this threshold should follow the prescribed procurement procedures, including the use of appropriate supporting documents such as official receipts and disbursement vouchers.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:
• institute appropriate legal actions/charges against the contractor who abandoned the projects amounting to P4,800,000.00;
• make representations with the source agency for the possible reprogramming of the unexpended balances to another PPAs otherwise, cause the immediate refund of said balances; and
• direct the project implementers/end-users to fast track the utilization of funds granted by various SAs to ensure that benefits from the PPAs are immediately realized by the constituents.
We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments and conduct regular monitoring of the status on the individual subsidiary ledger accounts composing the Due to NGAs account to avoid misrepresentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: (i) review the composition of the Accounts payable and ensure that all recorded payables are duly supported with the required and necessary documents to determine the validity and enforceability of claims; and (ii) revert to the unappropriated surplus all accounts payable which have been outstanding for two years or more and for which no valid claims have been established in compliance with Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Heads of the Municipal Offices to review the DTRs and accomplishment reports of the contractual and/or job order personnel under their respective offices before approving the same to ensure that pertinent supporting documents were duly accomplished; and
b. Human Resource Management Officer to carefully monitor and check the actual services rendered in the DTRs and reconcile the same with the payrolls, to avoid overpayments.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that payments to the contractual and/or job order personnel are supported with documents with all the required information duly indicated in the prescribed form, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 before processing claims, to establish the propriety, regularity and validity of the disbursements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Concerned contractor to correct and rectify the aforementioned deficiency at his/her own expense, and provide pictures and/or proofs of compliance to the Municipal Accountant for the eventual submission to the Audit Team.
Otherwise, require the refund of the equivalent cost of the defect/deficiency; and
b. Municipal Engineer to closely monitor the project implementation and ensure that projects are implemented in accordance with the approved plans and specifications.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant, upon submission of the required technical documents from the Municipal Engineer, immediately submit the same to the Audit Team to facilitate further review and evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: (i) prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to account for the errors noted in recording the transfer of the unexpended/unobligated balance of DRRMF-CO for proper monitoring; and (ii) transfer only the unutilized balances of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE from Trust Liability-DRRM in the General Fund books to the account Trust Liability-DRRM in the Trust Fund books in accordance with Items 5.1.10 and 5.1.11 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the requirements set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002 specifically the guidelines on the accounting and reporting of LDRRMF to ascertain that financial information on the LDRRMF are taken up appropriately in the books.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor, through the MSWMB, to: (i); fast track the preparation of the updated Solid Waste Management Plan and have it approved by the NSWMC to ensure that priority projects concerning Solid Waste Management are in accordance with the National Solid Waste Management Framework; (ii) prioritize projects, programs and activities concerning solid waste management and (iii) enforce proper collection, sorting, segregation, composting, recycling and disposal of waste materials to efficiently address solid waste concerns within the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, with the assistance of the Local Development Council, the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Municipal Engineer:
• judiciously plan and evaluate target PPAs to facilitate effective and efficient implementation thereof for the benefit of the Municipality. Institutionalize robust project identification and evaluation process, requiring the conduct of feasibility studies, pre-investment assessments and stakeholder consultations before the projects be included in the AIP and Annual Budget; and
• maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to spur development, provide jobs to constituents and to achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU so that the intended users/beneficiaries may enjoy the immediate benefits from said development projects.

We recommended that the:

a. Municipal Accountant exert all reasonable efforts to reconstruct the SLs
pertaining to the cash accountability of the Municipal Treasurer, based on
the availability of reliable records, in compliance with Sections 111(1) and
114(2) of PD No. 1445. The reconstructed SLs should be submitted to the
Audit Team for verification and audit; and

b. Office of the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal
Accountant, review and reconcile the available cashbooks with the
accounting records. A reconciliation report, along with complete and
updated copies of the cashbooks, should be submitted to the Audit Team.

We recommended that:

a. Management (i) direct the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and other
accountable officers (AOs) concerned to exert all reasonable efforts to
retrieve the source documents supporting various book reconciling items
and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant; (ii) pursue previous
requests with the depository bank to obtain copies of debit and credit
memoranda related to the said reconciling items for proper recording in
the books of accounts; and (iii) initiate the closure of dormant and unused
bank accounts; and

b. Office of the Municipal Treasurer (i) perform reconciliation of all Cash in
Bank cashbooks with the corresponding accounting records; (ii) submit a
detailed reconciliation report to the Audit Team; and (iii) ensure strict
compliance with the monthly reconciliation of cashbook balances with
subsidiary accounting records, in accordance with Section 63(F) of the
LTOM.

We recommended that the:

a. Municipal Accountant (i) continue sending regular written notices to all AOs
concerned at least quarterly to demand the settlement of their outstanding
cash advances; (ii) ensure that no new cash advance is granted unless
previous ones have been fully liquidated; and (iii) investigate the reasons for
unreconciled cash advance balances and make appropriate adjustments in
the books to rectify discrepancies; and

b. Management (i) initiate the preparation and consolidation of necessary
documents in support of the request for write-off of deceased employees and
those dormant unliquidated cash advances; and (ii) consider the filing of
appropriate legal or administrative action against AOs who have deliberately
failed to liquidate their advances after proper demand, if warranted.

We recommended that Management:

a. Instruct the members of the Inventory Committee to promptly conduct
physical count of inventories and prepare the corresponding RPCI in
compliance with existing rules;

b. Facilitate the reconciliation of the results of the physical count with the
accounting records to enable the recording of necessary adjustments and
ensure the accurate valuation of inventories in the books of accounts; and

c. Institute measures to ensure the timely conduct of annual physical inventory
and submission of the corresponding report.

We recommended that the relevant offices (i) revisit the provisions of the
aforementioned Circulars; and (ii) ensure compliance with preparation and
maintenance of the prescribed forms, registry, and reports related to Semi-
Expendable Property.

We recommended that Management:

a. Consider reorganizing the Inventory Committee by designating members
who are capable of completing the physical inventory, reconciling results
with relevant records, and preparing and submitting the required reports, in
accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-
time cleansing of PPE accounts;

b. Explore viable options to augment the manpower needs of the Inventory
Committee, in order to improve operational efficiency and provide adequate
support for the conduct of inventory-taking activities; and

c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to review and reinforce the
observance of proper accounting and reporting procedures for all PPE-
related transactions (e.g., acquisition, receipt, issuance, transfer, and
disposal), in line with applicable laws and regulations. The Municipal Mayor
is likewise encouraged to regularly monitor and ensure that annual physical
inventory is conducted and the corresponding reports are properly
submitted on time.

We recommended that Management:

a. Render an explanation/justification for the identified lapses for further
review and evaluation;

b. Submit the aforementioned lacking contract documents to the Audit Team;

c. Evaluate the implications of the noted discrepancies and institute necessary
corrective measures, if deemed appropriate, copy furnished the Audit Team
of the actions taken for monitoring purposes; and

d. Ensure that the BAC, Secretariat, and TWG strictly adhere to all applicable
procurement laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management:

a. Direct the responsible office to compute the negative slippage of delayed
projects and promptly issue notices to contractors, informing them of their
delays and requiring the submission of necessary documents in accordance
with GPPB Circular No. 03-2019 dated March 8, 2019;

b. Instruct the implementing unit to intensify regular on-site supervision and
evaluation of project progress, and prepare contingency plans, if warranted;

c. Reevaluate the proposed projects intended for funding through the LBP loan
to determine their continued relevance and necessity for public benefit; and

d. Strengthen its planning and monitoring mechanisms by conducting
comprehensive feasibility studies, risk assessments, and resource
evaluations before project execution to ensure efficient and timely
implementation.

We recommended that Management consider the potential benefits of COA
Circular No. 2023-006 by transitioning to the digital submission of ERDs to
enhance document security and accessibility.

To recognize in the books of accounts the unrecorded leave benefits payable and subsequently, the leave benefits which are expected to be settled within 12 months after the end of the reporting period, subject to certification by the Municipal Budget Officer and Treasurer of available appropriations and cash back-up. Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed that:
The Municipal Accountant:
Ensures that all receipts of goods, except those paid thru petty cash fund, are recognized as inventory upon receipt; and
Record the issuances in the books of accounts, upon receipt of the SSMI  together with the RIS, Fuel Consumption Report(FCR) and other documents from the Municipal Treasurer.
The Municipal Treasurer consolidates the RIS, prepare the SSMI, prepare FCR and timely submit the same to the MA for recording in the books of accounts; and
The Inventory Committee conduct physical count of inventories semestrally and submit the RPCI to the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer for reconciliation of their records, copy furnished the COA.

Recommendations
We reiterated our recommendation, and the MGSO agreed to request the disposal of unserviceable properties by proceeding with disposal procedures in accordance with  COA DBM Joint Circular No. 2024-01, and to submit to the Municipal Accountant the duly approved IIRUP and disposal documents for recording in the books of accounts,  after the completion of the disposal process.
Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant agreed to recognize impairment loss by  P0.730 million on the unserviceable PPEs from the books of accounts while disposal thereof is yet to be executed.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare JEV and reclassify the balance of Due to  PhilHealth account to Due to GOCC account:
Due to PhilHealth 		 P656,602.38
Due to GOCC 		P656,602.38

Recommendation
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to draw a JEV to adjust the trust liability account and recognize the income earned in the GF books, as follows:
Unreconciled PPE per books and Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) – P189.257 million

Recommendations
We  recommended, and the management agreed to the following:
The Inventory Committee to:
Prepare the prescribed RPCPPE complete with remarks for missing  items, unserviceable items, and found at station items, etc; and
Tag the PPE items with new property stickers.
The Municipal Treasurer/GSO to prepare the List of PPEs Found at Station and  List of PPEs Non-Existing/Missing PPEs based on the RPCPPE prepared by the Inventory Committee, and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books, if necessary.
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer/GSO to:
Reconcile their records with the updated RPCPPE of the Inventory  Committee; and
Prepare and update their property and ledger cards.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to properly disclose the following in the Notes to FS:
Aggregate remuneration of key management and those who are already retired for the year and those that are retiring in the next reporting period, as well as the Municipality’s adoption of IPSAS 39 in accordance with COA Circular No. 2024-009; and provide disclosure in the Notes to the FS on the nature of the leave benefits payable and its expected payment for at least the following year;
Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/(Deficit) in the Notes to the FS as provided for in the aforementioned regulations; and
Accurate reported balances of LDRRMF.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the GFPS agreed to:
Include in the Annual GPB the attendance to seminars on GeRL tool to capacitate themself in the conduct of gender audit; and
Conduct gender audit on its agency thru the use of GeRL tool.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the BAC to properly examine the bids/quotations and supporting documents submitted by prospective  suppliers/contractors to ensure that eligibility requirements are complete and valid prior to award of contract or purchase order (PO).

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management, through the BAC, agreed to ensure that observers are invited at least five calendar days before each procurement activity and that invitation to observers encompass all stages, including those for alternative modes of procurement, not solely in the opening of bids.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to stop from granting any additional allowances, incentives, or other financial benefits to officials and employees unless expressly authorized by law.

Recommendation
We recommended, and LDRRM Officer agreed to prepare the LDRRMFIP for the current year appropriation for LDRRMF, and incorporate therein the PPAs charged to the unexpended LDRRMF in previous years or special trust fund.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to fully institutionalize the LDRRM Office by filling up the mandatory positions to perform its duties and responsibilities including in administration and training, research and planning, and operations and warning as well as the execution of LDRRMF PPAs.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the Municipal Budget Officer agreed to ensure correct reporting of the amounts in the SAAOB.
We also recommend, and the MA agreed to conduct robust review of the reported amounts in the SAAOB filled-up by the Municipal Budget Officer and reconcile their record for accurate reporting.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure complete  documentation of every transaction and adhere to the rules and regulations as provided  above before payment is made to the payee in consonance with pertinent rules and  regulations.

Recommendations
We reiterated our recommendation, and Management agreed that:
The Municipal Engineer to immediately conduct regular review and evaluation of the unimplemented project and expedite the preparation of necessary documents to facilitate the request for procurement and implementation;
The MPDC to re-assess the projects funded under the 20 per cent current appropriations as to viability, feasibility, and relevance, and endorse the results thereof to the Municipal Development Council for appropriate action; and
The Municipal Budget Officer to provide the unobligated balances of 20 per cent DF to the Municipal Development Council for reprogramming to other development projects.

Recommendation
We recommended and the LSB agreed to ensure that:
A copy of the approved SEF Budget is furnish to the Municipal Budget Officer, the Municipal Accountant, and the Municipal Treasurer not later than seven days after the approval of the SEF Budget; and
SEF utilization reports are submitted quarterly not later than the 20th day after the end of the quarter and annually not later than the 15th day of February of the following year, to the DepEd CO, through appropriate channels, and copy furnish the local sanggunian concerned as well as the regional offices of the DBM and DILG.

Recommendation
Moving forward, we recommended, and Management agreed to closely supervise and monitor project progress and implement remedial measures and interventions to ensure timely completion of PRDPs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) Municipal Engineer to (i) submit the Status Report of Completion, Advance copies of Final Inspection Report, Certificates of Completion, along with advance copies of the Final Inspection Report, Certificates of Completion and Acceptance to the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the recording of the transfer of completed projects to appropriate asset accounts; and (ii) report the status of projects in the Quarterly Report on Accomplishment of Government PPAs; and (b) Municipal Accountant to use Final Inspection Report, Certificates of Completion and Acceptance as basis in recording the transfer of completed projects from CIP to proper asset accounts for unpaid completed projects.
observations.managementAction: Recorded the transfer of completed CIP projects to proper asset accounts through General Journal JEV No. 2024-01-032 dated January 31, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to return the unutilized funds to the respective source agencies together with the requisite report of disbursement in observance of COA Circular No. 94-013; and (b) Municipal Accountant to draw journal entry to correct the year-end balances of Due to GOCCs and Due to NGAs accounts.
observations.managementAction:
Returned the unutilized funds to the respective source agencies in August 2024 under Check Nos. 1194087, 1194088, and 1194094, along with the requisite report of disbursement.
Correcting entry was made through General Journal JEV No. 2024-06-001 dated June 28, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and BAC Secretariat to (i) ensure compliance with the required notifications and posting requirements and other pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its RIRR in all procurements; (ii) henceforth submit proof or screenshots of any error that may prevent the uploading in the PhilGEPS website of upcoming procurement activities; and (iii) cause the submission of a DOLE-approved construction and safety health programs and PERT/CPM of winning bidder in every infrastructure project; and (b) Local Finance Committee to submit Certification and evidence of posting of LGSF-SBDP quarterly reports on its website and in three conspicuous places pertaining to (i) the three completed projects; and (ii) any relatedly-funded projects in the future.
observations.managementAction: (i) ensure compliance with the required notifications and posting requirements and other pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its RIRR in all procurements; and (ii) henceforth submit proof or screenshots of any error that may prevent the uploading in the PhilGEPS website of upcoming procurement activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer to (i) regularly monitor/ supervise project implementation to allow timely interventions on concerns that may arise therefrom; (ii) expedite the installation of transformer and water system in the Super Health Center so that intended beneficiaries can immediately benefit therefrom; (b) optimally utilized 20% DF within the budget year; (c) BAC to submit to the Audit Team the bidding documents that form part of the contract for all bidded projects; and (d) Municipal Accountant to submit all noted procurement-related documents required in COA Circular No. 2012-001 and R.A. No. 9184 and its RIRR to support the regularity of the payments made.
observations.managementAction:
Regular monitoring of project implementation was done.
The Super Health Center is now operational.
All projects were implemented.
The documents have been submitted to the Audit Team.
The Municipal Accountant submitted the noted procurement-related documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) LDRRM Officer to (i) in coordination with the Municipal Engineer and other officials concerned, maximize the use of LDRRMF through facilitated implementation of the programmed PPAs within the budget year; (ii) expedite the implementation of the two projects that had remained unimplemented in CY 2023; (iii) submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to DILG-LGOO as required by Section 5.1.6 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and (b) Municipal Accountant to submit/attach in the DVs copies of APP and ITR of winning bidders as required in COA Circular No. 2012-001 and R.A. No. 9184 and its RIRR to support the regularity of the payments made.
observations.managementAction:
In adherence to recommendations, the two projects were completed as of yearend.
Timely submission of monthly reports was complied with.
Municipal Accountant submitted copies of APP and ITR of winning bidders as required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the BAC and BAC Secretariat to (a) ensure compliance with the required notifications and posting requirements and other pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its RIRR in all procurements; and (b) henceforth submit proof or screenshots of any error that may prevent the uploading in the PhilGEPS website of upcoming procurement activities.
observations.managementAction: Ensure compliance with the required notifications and posting requirements and other pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its RIRR in all procurements; and henceforth submit proof or screenshots of any error that may prevent the uploading in the PhilGEPS website of upcoming procurement activities.
observations.reason: Complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the MENRO to (a) assess the feasibility of implementing the planned PPAs mentioned in the Municipality’s Ten-Year SWMP, otherwise consider implementing other programs that will improve further the waste reduction, segregation and diversion processes; (b) in coordination with MSWMB, review and update every year the plan as required by Section 16 of R.A. No. 9003; (c) in coordination with Barangay Officials, consider the inclusion of composting operation in every Barangay MRF and ensure that they are fully operating and functioning to help in the diversion of biodegradable wastes as required in Section 1, Rule XI of the IRR of R.A. No. 9003; and (d) expedite the operation of rotary composter and mixer to help in waste reduction and diversion.
observations.managementAction: In coordination with Barangay Officials, consider the inclusion of composting operation in every Barangay MRF and ensure that they are fully operating and functioning to help in the diversion of biodegradable wastes as required in Section 1, Rule XI of the IRR of R.A. No. 9003; and expedite the operation of rotary composter and mixer to help in waste reduction and diversion.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) BPLO to (i) enforce the submission of business taxpayers’ certified ITR copy on or before April 30 of each year as required in Section 2D.04 of the Revenue Code; (ii) impose fines on businesses that are operating but failed to secure or renew their business permits in accordance with Section 9.01 of the same Code; and (iii) henceforth ensure that all businesses operating in the locality have valid business permits; and (b) Municipal Treasurer to make representation with the proper office for the hiring of additional personnel or assignment of staff who will be trained and assigned in the examination of books of accounts of businesses.
observations.managementAction:
Two identified businesses in CY 2023 already renewed their business permits in CY 2024 and settled the corresponding taxes, surcharges, and interests.
The Municipal Treasurer sent a letter to the LCE requesting the creation of a team from BPLO, MTO, and the Market Office to examine the books of accounts of business establishments every 2nd quarter of the year.
observations.reason: Some actions implemented (renewal and collection for identified businesses); full enforcement and staffing for examinations pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the GFPS through the GAD Focal Person to (a) administer the HGDG test when attributing costs to GAD PPAs; (b) seek expert assistance and arrange HGDG training to adequately equip the GFPS and other stakeholders involved in GAD planning; (c) create a multi-year GAD Agenda as basis for the annual GAD Plans and Budget by using the templates provided in PCW Memorandum Circular No. 2018-04 dated September 19, 2018; (d) timely submit to the Audit Team the GAD Accomplishment Report in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-001; and (e) review the expenses reported in the GAD Accomplishment Report prior to its submission to DILG to identify any inconsistencies or occurrences of duplicated charges.
observations.managementAction:
Timely submission of the GAD Accomplishment Report was implemented.
Review of expenses reported in the GAD Accomplishment Report prior to submission was implemented.
observations.reason: Creation of multi-year GAD Agenda, HGDG training and administration of HGDG test not yet implemented due to lack of available gender analysis specialist / training provider during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continuously comply with the BIR rules and regulations on the withholding and timely remittance of taxes on compensation, value added taxes, percentage taxes, and expanded taxes from government money payments.
observations.managementAction: All taxes withheld by the LGU are remitted in full within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer sustain the timely withholding and remittance of GSIS premiums and loan amortizations of members in accordance with Section 6(b) of R.A. No. 8291.
observations.managementAction: All withheld contributions and loan amortizations are remitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue ensuring the timely withholding and remittance of PhilHealth premiums from members, together with the government’s share, in accordance with R.A. No. 7875 as amended by R.A. Nos. 9241 and 10606 or the National Health Insurance Act of 2013.
observations.managementAction: All withheld contributions are remitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue the timely withholding and remittance to HDMF of the members’ contributions and loan amortization, together with the LGU’s share, in accordance with R.A. No. 9679.
observations.managementAction: All withheld contributions and loan amortization are remitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to require the City Accountant to recompute the year-end balance of all inventories using the moving average method and draw Journal Entry Voucher to record the understatement/s for forwarded beginning balance in CY 2024.
observations.managementAction: The necessary adjustment was posted in the books of accounts per GJ No. 056-03-24-100 dated 3/27/2024. As of December 31, 2024, proper costing of inventories on the basis of weighted average on the date of issue was implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant to: a. enforce the immediate settlement of CAs of officers and employees who are still employed with the City Government; and b. request for write-off/derecognition from the books all dormant CAs following the procedures prescribed in COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023. We also recommended to enjoin all officers and employees to strictly follow the timelines of liquidation of CAs.
observations.managementAction: Out of the P1,538,668.65 unliquidated CAs amounting to P180,320.00 and P32,176,653.58 as of March 31, 2025. As of December 31, 2024, the City requested for dormant CAs write-off/derecognition from the books of accounts the dormant CAs totaling P706,510.21. As of December 31, 2024, only P180,320.00 was unliquidated as of March 31, 2025. Only P92,733.56 out of unliquidated CAs of P1,538,668.65 amounting to P180,320.00 was unliquidated as of March 31, 2025. As of December 31, 2024, the City requested for dormant CAs write-off/derecognition from the books of accounts the dormant CAs totaling P706,510.21 were derecognized from the books of accounts as of year-end. For CY 2024, liquidation of CAs were submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to: a. make the necessary journal entry to revert the said obligations without valid supporting documents totaling P1,928,502.63 to Accounts Payable; and b. ensure that accruals of expenses are supported with valid supporting documents pursuant to Section 46 of PD No. 1177.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Panlungsod has authorized the reversion of the Accounts Payable totaling P1,928,502.63 henceforth. Subsequently, adjustment was taken up in the books of accounts with said amount through GJ No. 140-24-07-100 dated July 19, 2024. As of December 31, 2024 expenses accrued were supported with valid supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to strategize/distribute the loads evenly to the staff to ensure that the FSIF collections through online payment platform are remitted to the BFP not later than two working days upon receipt.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2024, two additional personnel were assigned to the City Treasurer’s Office to ensure that the FSIF collections through online payment platform are remitted to the BFP within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: a. require the personnel assigned in the preparation of the PPMP to attend the capacity development program/training conducted by the Government Procurement Policy Board-Technical Support Office or an in-house training to enhance their knowledge regarding the preparation of PPMPs and other related reports; thereafter.
observations.managementAction: The City personnel assigned in the preparation of the PPMP have attended the capacity development program/training conducted by the Government Procurement Policy Board-Technical Support Office and/or an in-house training to enhance their knowledge regarding the preparation of PPMPs and other related reports; thereafter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the CDRRMO to: a. execute/implement the 105 projects totaling P123,630,966.74 from the Continuing Appropriation; and b. coordinate with the City Engineer and OIC-City Budget Officer in ensuring that all programmed infrastructure projects under the LDRRMF are monitored and completely implemented and that any savings from such completed projects shall be made available for use in the disaster risk reduction.
observations.managementAction: The City was able to determine what the PPAs are to be reverted and what will push through except only non-infra projects. According to CDRRMO, the non-infra projects are still subject for review of the Office. Once evaluation is completed, projects deemed suitable for implementation will be executed. The Sangguniang Panlungsod authorized the reversion of the identified projects under the Continuing Appropriation of LDRRMF totaling P48,306,853.25 through Ordinance No. 2024-1115 enacted on May 6, 2024.
observations.reason: Out of the Continuing Appropriation of LDRRMF of P104,840,326.74 a total of P15,827,000.00 was utilized as of December 31, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the: a. Appraisal Committee to expedite the conduct of appraisal of insurable PPEs, giving priority to PPEs acquired in CY 2023 and submit the required report to the concerned offices; b. Head of GSO in coordination with the City Accountant to list all insurable properties in the Property Inventory Form (PIF) with the latest appraised values/valuation, appraisal date and other information as required under COA Circular No. 2018-002 and submit the PIF to the Supervising Auditor/Audit Team Leader and the GIF, GSIS, not later than April 30 of each year; and c. OIC-City Budget Officer to prioritize the inclusion in the City Government’s budget the amount of premiums for the general insurance covering all insurable properties acquired in CY 2023 due to budgetary constraint.
observations.managementAction: In Calendar Year 2024, the Appraisal Committee already appraised insurable PPEs acquired in CY 2023 and already submitted the report to concerned offices. PIF was already submitted to Audit Team Leader on April 29, 2024. The Sangguniang Panlunsod passed the Supplemental Budget No.1 which includes additional appropriation for premiums for insurance amounting P10,000,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the coordinating/focal point officer to: a. ensure immediate and proper implementation of projects funded by transfers from SAs; b. coordinate with the City Accounting Office on the implementation and return of any unused balance of the fund transfers to the Source Agencies and in case of the completion of the project, pursuant to the aforecited regulations; and c. strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 in implementing projects funded by SAs so as to ensure that funds are utilized and liquidated in a timely manner, and that the services and benefits due to the public are not hampered.
observations.managementAction: As of December 31, 2024, the City was able to implement 19 out of 43 projects funded by Source Agencies; 24 projects were not implemented by the City. Unused fund transfers totaling P309,400.00 were returned to the Source Agencies and were properly recorded in the books per Disbursement Voucher Nos. 2023-01-0046, 2023-03-0145, 2023-08-0324 dated January 31, 2023, April 20, 2023 and August 14, 2023, respectively. Management will closely monitor the implementation of projects funded by Source Agencies and noted difficulties in implementation of projects funded by different National Government Agencies.
observations.reason: Some projects remained unimplemented due to documentary issues encountered with the Source Agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant, the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) and the City Treasurer to: a. adopt a complete ATM payroll system for the City to eliminate the risk of loss or misapplication of funds aside from promoting greater convenience, transparency, and accountability; and b. strictly observe the provisions of Section 124 of PD No. 1445 and DBM Circular Letter No. 2008-8 dated October 23, 2008.
observations.managementAction: As of December 31, 2024, employees and JO personnel totaling 3,247 were registered with ATM Payroll System. The City complied with the related provisions of PD No. 1445 and DBM Circular No. 2008-8.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee to: a. ensure the optimal utilization of the City’s GAD funds; b. monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs to ensure maximum implementation thereof; and c. strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 4.1 C.1(1) of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2024, regular assessment of utilization was conducted to ensure optimal utilization of GAD funds. As of December 31, 2024, only 85.03 percent of GAD PPAs were implemented by the City. The City adhered the related provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.reason: There are projects that were funded by different National Government Agencies and were implemented leaving the corresponding budget of such projects unutilized. There were three projects that were not implemented due to time constraints.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to include the FS of PRDP in the Consolidated FS of the Municipality to properly present the account pertaining to this fund;
b. Municipal Accountant to properly record the bank accounts for Mun. Gov't of Rizal Laguna - For Trust Liabilities by drawing a JEV with a debit to CIB and credit to Trust Liabilities account by same amount of P150,854.53, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 to ensure that accounts are accurately presented in the financial statements; and
c. Municipal Treasurer to evaluate the necessity of the dormant accounts and if proven not needed, secure SB resolution authorizing the closing of dormant accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the FS of PRDP in the Consolidated FS. The Municipal Accountant prepared the Consolidated FS including the PRDP. The Municipal Treasurer evaluated the dormant accounts and found it still needed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to draw JEV to correct the misclassification of the PPE items with the following proposed adjusting journal entry:

We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the recording of the Municipality’s transactions in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant recorded the transactions pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the misclassified accounts by debiting Due to NGAs (P12,760.23), Due to GOCCs (P230,909.40), and Due from Officers and Employees (P1,046.75), and crediting Trust Liabilities (P12,760.23) and Prior Year Adjustment (P231,956.15). The Municipal Accountant shall ensure future compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 to maintain accurate and properly classified financial records.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the necessary JEV to reclassify the misclassified accounts and now strictly complies with COA Circular No. 2015-009 to ensure the accurate presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly record the RPT/SET collections and recognize the income from RPT/SET using the sample journal entries below, in compliance with Section 22, Chapter 3 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, and COA Circular No. 2015-009:

Account Title Debit Credit
Cash – Local Treasury XX
Discount on Real Property Tax – Basic XX
Real Property Tax – Basic Receivables XX
To record the receipt of prompt payment for RPT

Deferred Real Property Tax – Basic XX
Real Property Tax – Basic XX
To recognize the income from RPT

Cash – Local Treasury XX
Discount on Special Education Tax XX
Special Education Tax Receivables XX
To record the receipt of prompt payment for SET

Deferred Special Education Tax XX
Special Education Tax XX
To recognize the income from SET
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant recorded the necessary JEV to reclassify the misclassified accounts, by debiting Due to NGAs (₱12,760.23), Due to GOCCs (₱230,909.40), and Due from Officers and Employees (₱1,046.75), crediting Trust Liabilities (₱12,760.23) and Prior Year Adjustment (₱231,956.15). The Municipal Accountant shall ensure future compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 to maintain accurate and properly classified financial records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete subsidiary ledgers and supporting schedules as basis in the disclosing of the required information in the NTFS, in accordance with Paragraph 127(c) of the IPSAS I, Section 9.2 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 and Item 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to assist the users in understanding the financial statements and to provide them with the additional information value.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant provided additional disclosure in the NTFS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local Finance Committee to:

a. Request from the lending financial institution copies of the amortization of all the existing loans of the Municipality to determine the schedule and amount of loan repayment; and
b. Strictly implement a plan on allocating the available cash by closely monitoring the utilization of funds, impose cost-cutting measures, determine the amount which has reasonably assurance of collectivity and intensify collection to increase the Municipality’s cash balance.
observations.managementAction: The Local Finance Committee requested copies of the amortization schedules from the concerned financial institutions. The Local Finance Committee closely monitored the cash flows of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of granting additional or new cash advances to the AOs with outstanding cash advances unless their previous cash advances are fully liquidated and propose doable and effective measures to resolve delays in liquidation; otherwise, designate additional AO who can handle the cash advances of the Municipality.

We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the recording of the Municipality’s transactions, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant ensured the proper recording of the Municipality’s transactions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

a. Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to exert extra effort to locate the details and supporting documents and determine the person/s accountable for the missing PPE items and prepare the List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs for items described as "non-existing" or "missing" in the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), as required under Sections 6.3.1 and 7.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006, otherwise, comply the procedures provided under Section 7.11 of the same Circular if accountability could not be established despite exhaustion of all diligent efforts;

b. Municipal Accountant to provide proper disclosures on the Non-Existing/Missing PPEs, in accordance with Section 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2020-006; and

c. MGSO, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant and Inventory Committee, to strictly comply with the disposition, derecognition and disposal procedures of all missing and unserviceable PPEs, as required under COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.managementAction: The MGSO has coordinated with the Local School Board (LSB) Chairperson for identification and investigation of the missing PPE items. The Municipal Accountant provided additional disclosure in the NTFS. MGSO in coordination with Municipal Accountant and Inventory Committee shall also ensure proper disposition, derecognition, and disposal procedures of all missing and unserviceable PPEs.
observations.reason: The other recommended actions were not taken on the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. MGSO to prepare the corresponding IIRUP by filling-out the inventory portion and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor and to the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to initiate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties, in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445 and the rules and procedures provided in COA Circular No. 92-386 and COA-DBM JC No. 2024-01; and

b. Appraisal and Disposal Committee to conduct an appraisal of the unserviceable properties and submit the appraisal report to the Office of the Auditor; and

c. Municipal Accountant to derecognize the unserviceable properties from the books of accounts after the completion of the disposal.
observations.managementAction: The concerned officials will prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property to comply with AOM NO. 24-017 (No. 10, Page Nos. 45 to 47) and COA-DBM JC No. 2024-01. The Municipal Appraisal and Disposal Committee of Rizal, Laguna. The Municipal Accountant will derecognize the unserviceable properties upon the completion of the disposal.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to:

a. Furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectable for the year which will serve as basis to draw a JEV in recording the receivables;

b. Reconcile the RPT/SET Receivables accounts with the Municipal Assessor’s records based on the updated market values and assessed value of the existing real property units; and

c. Generate/prepare Certified Statement of RPT/SET Delinquencies as of given date and reconcile with the Municipal Accountant to ascertain the balances of the RPT Receivables, SET Receivables and the corresponding Deferred RPT/SET Income.
observations.managementAction: The Certified Statement of RPT/SET Delinquencies were generated and the two offices reconciled their reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MGSO to conduct periodic physical count of supplies and other inventories and prepare the RPCI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the 1st and 2nd semester, respectively, and submit the same to the Audit Team, in compliance with Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, and Section 56, Chapter 2 of the same Manual, Volume II, to ensure reliability of the presented inventory amount in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The RPCI was prepared and submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to account all payments of interest and bank charges for the contracted bank loans pertaining to construction of qualifying assets by drawing JEV to capitalize the borrowing costs as part of the acquisition cost of qualifying assets and recompute the accumulated depreciation for fair presentation of the accounts in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted copies of JEV (JEV No. 05-42 dated May 29, 2024) for the capitalization of borrowing costs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the BAC Secretariat and other concerned personnel to furnish the Audit Team the copies of perfected contracts together with its supporting documents within five working days for technical review and evaluation, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat submitted copies of perfected contracts and its supporting documents to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct to:

a. Municipal Assessor to reevaluate or reassess the appraised values of the properties under the Municipality’s jurisdiction based on the prevailing market rates and thereafter make necessary revisions every three years, pursuant to Sections 212 and 219 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted to the Bureau of Local Government and Finance the proposed Schedule of Market Value for General Revision of Real Property Tax.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. MPDC to maintain the monthly monitoring report on the implementation of all Municipality’s PPAs for the preparation and submission within the prescribed period a comprehensive list of all on-going and to be implemented government PPAs, as mandated by Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004; and

b. Municipal Engineer to notify the Audit Team within ten days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are posted, pursuant to Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC submitted the Monitoring Report on the implementation of all Municipality’s PPAs. The Municipal Engineer ensured that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are posted for all their on-going projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF duly reconciled with the accounting records and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, as required under Section 5.1.5 of the COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO prepared and submitted the required Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer, in coordination with the GAD Focal Person, to provide the necessary appropriation for GAD representing at least five percent of the annual budget and otherwise, follow the required procedures in the attribution of GAD into the regular activities of the Municipality, using the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) tool, pursuant to Section 30 of the General Provisions of RA No. 10964 and Items 4.1.C.3.1 and 4.1.C.4.1(a) of the PCW, DILG, NEDA and DBM JMC Nos. 2013-01 and 2016-01.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has properly allocated the total estimated income for CY 2024 equivalent to five percent of its total estimated annual revenue.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person and all other concerned officials to furnish the Audit Team a copy of the GAD Accomplishment Report as supported by brief summary of the reported program or project; copies of reported policy issuances; results of HGDG tests; and actions taken by the LGU on the audit findings and recommendations, if any, pursuant to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 and Section 4.1.C.8.5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 for the review and comprehensive evaluation of the effectiveness of GAD PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The copy of the GAD Accomplishment Report for CYs 2022 and 2023 were submitted on June 21, 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to request for copies of the Bank’s debit memos and credit memos and other reconciling items for submission to the Municipal Accountant to support the adjustments to the related asset and government equity accounts.

We also recommended the Municipal Accountant to submit the duplicate and quadruplicate copies of the BRS including the paid checks, original copies of debit/credit memos to the Audit Team for verification within 10 days for receipt after the end of each month, as prescribed under Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011.
observations.managementAction: The Management communicated and inquired with the LBP for the details and nature of the reconciling items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the MGSO, to trace the period or date of acquisition of the PPEs and recompute for the depreciation based on the estimated useful life of assets and make necessary adjusting entries to take up the prior years’ and current year’s depreciation as Prior Year Adjustment to be closed to Government Equity.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had traced the period or date of acquisition of the PPEs and computed for the depreciation based on the estimated useful life of assets and made necessary entries to take up the prior year’s depreciation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectable for the year which will serve as basis to draw a JEV in recording of the receivables; reconcile the RPT/SET Receivables accounts with the Municipal Assessor’s records; and generate/prepare Certified Statement of RPT/SET Delinquencies as of given date and reconcile with the Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: The Certified Statement of RPT/SET Delinquencies were generated and the two offices reconciled their reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MGSO to conduct periodic physical count of supplies and prepare the RPCI and submit to the Audit Team not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the 1st and 2nd semester, respectively.
observations.managementAction: The RPCI was prepared and submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to account all payments of interest and bank charges for the contracted bank loans pertaining to construction of qualifying assets by drawing JEV to capitalize the borrowing costs as part of the acquisition cost of qualifying assets and recompute the accumulated depreciation for fair presentation of the accounts in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted copies of JEV for the capitalization of borrowing costs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Secretariat and concerned personnel to furnish the Audit Team the copies of perfected contracts together with its supporting documents within five working days for technical review and evaluation, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat submitted copies of perfected contracts and its supporting documents to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Assessor to reevaluate or reassess the appraised values of the properties under the Municipality’s jurisdiction based on the prevailing market rates and thereafter make necessary revisions every three years, pursuant to Sections 212 and 219 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted to the Bureau of Local Government and Finance the proposed Schedule of Market Value for General Revision of Real Property Tax.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDC to maintain the monthly monitoring report on the implementation of all Municipality’s PPAs and submit comprehensive list and the Municipal Engineer to notify the Audit Team that project signboards/public notices are posted.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC submitted the Monitoring Report on the implementation of all Municipality’s PPAs. The Municipal Engineer ensured that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are posted for all their on-going projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF duly reconciled with the accounting records and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO prepared and submitted the required Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer, in coordination with GAD Focal Person, to provide appropriation for GAD representing at least five percent of the annual budget using HGDG tool, or follow required procedures for attribution of GAD into regular activities.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality properly allocated the total estimated income for CY 2024 equivalent to five percent of its total estimated annual revenue.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that SSMI, distribution reports and other pertinent documents were attached to the JEV in recording issuances of supplies and materials and regularly conduct reconciliation of inventory records with GSO.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant ensured that SSMI, distribution reports, and other supporting documents are attached to the JEV. Regular reconciliation between the Accounting Office and the General Services Office are being conducted.
observations.reason: The process of preparation and submission of SSMIs were still not executed since their staff were not trained on the procedures.
