observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Make the necessary journal entry to revert to the said obligations without valid claim to Government Equity by debiting Accounts Payable in the amount of P17,787,573.31 and crediting the Prior Period Adjustment by same amount so that the Municipality can use the available funds for other priority projects that would uplift the living condition of its constituents; and b. Henceforth, strictly comply with the provision of PD Nos. 1445 and 1177 on the proper recognition of Accounts Payable.
observations.managementAction: Make the necessary journal entry to revert the said obligations without valid claim to Government Equity and ensure proper recognition of Accounts Payable.
observations.reason: Partial reversion was only made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. OIC-Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Social Welfare and Development (MSWD) Head, to coordinate and reconcile the Municipality’s records with the DSWD; and b. OIC-Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to submit the required Liquidation Reports for various fund transfers from DSWD within the reglementary period.
observations.managementAction: Coordinate with MSWD for the reconciliation of records with DSWD.
observations.reason: Submission of Liquidation Report to DSWD was on time, however recording in the books was delayed, hence discrepancy still exists.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman to: a. Submit immediately the Supplemental APP and PMR for Second Semester 2023 to the GPPB and submit proof of submission to the Audit Team; and b. Comply with the posting requirement of the APP and PMR in the Municipality’s website as required under Section 12.2 of the 2016 R-IRR of RA No. 9184 and in consonance with EO No. 662. s. 2007.
observations.managementAction: Submit supplemental APP and PMR and post in website.
observations.reason: Management complied with posting requirement and submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to fully implement the ATM payroll system to eliminate the risk of loss or misapplication of funds aside from promoting greater convenience, transparency, and accountability in consonance with the provisions of Section 2.1 of IRR of Executive Order No. 170, S. 2022; and b. Human Resource Management Officer to develop a written policy on how the payment of salary of Job Order personnel should be made.
observations.managementAction: Implemented ATM payroll system for the salary of Job Order personnel and developed guidelines.
observations.reason: Implemented; HRMO followed the policy on payment of Job Order personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant in coordination with BAC Secretariat and GAD Focal person to: a. submit the lacking documentary requirements for the procurement of various goods charged to the GAD to establish the regularity, propriety and validity of the disbursements; and b. ensure strict compliance with documentary requirements in the procurement processes as provided in the 2016 R-IRR of RA No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the lacking documentary requirements for the procurement of various goods charged to the GAD.
observations.reason: Management submitted required documents and committed to comply with procurement laws.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. LDRRM Officer to prepare and submit the proposed LDRRMFIP to incorporate the programs and projects to be charged to the STF in accordance with Section 4.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and b. Fund the unutilized STF from CY 2017 in the amount of P1,174,996.59 and any unutilized amount after five years in accordance with Section 5.1.13 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: LDRRM Officer to prepare and submit LDRRMFIP and to revert unutilized fund.
observations.reason: Reverted unutilized fund to unappropriated surplus; committed to incorporate funds into LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant in coordination with BAC Secretariat and Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office to: a. submit the lacking documentary requirements for the procurement of various goods charged to the LDRRMF to establish the regularity, propriety and validity of the disbursements; and b. ensure strict compliance with documentary requirements in the procurement of infrastructure projects as provided in the 2016 R-IRR of RA No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the lacking documentary requirements for the procurement of various goods charged to the LDRRMF.
observations.reason: Management submitted required documents; compliance being monitored.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant in coordination with BAC Secretariat to: a. submit the lacking documentary requirements for the procurement of various goods charged to the SEF to establish the regularity, propriety and validity of the disbursements; and b. ensure strict compliance with documentary requirements in the procurement of infrastructure projects as provided in the 2016 R-IRR of RA No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the lacking documentary requirements for the procurement of various goods charged to the SEF.
observations.reason: Management submitted required documents; compliance being monitored.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to: a. Ensure that the project billboard is posted on the project site and in conspicuous places within the agency premises showing all relevant information of the said project being implemented by the Municipality to promote public awareness and concern over the government infrastructure projects; b. Assign a specific person who will regularly update the relevant information of the projects on the posted signboards to guarantee transparency in all projects undertaken by the Municipality; and c. Strictly comply with the guidelines set out in COA Circular No. 2013-004 on the notification to the public or publicity relating to the project.
observations.managementAction: Ensure posting of project billboards and assign personnel to update them.
observations.reason: Management assigned responsible person and ensured posting for projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough review and reconciliation of all reported transactions related to the Cash Local Treasury in all funds; b. OIC-Municipal Accountant to draw a journal entry to adjust the Cash Local Treasury account with the net discrepancy of P415,204.62, duly supported by certified reconciliation statements of Accounting and Treasury’s records; and c. Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with Section 181(c) of GAAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Reconciled records and prepared JEVs; adjustments taken-up in the books supported with JEV #100-23-01-01033S.
observations.reason: Discrepancies still exist between the records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to: a. Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the discrepancy between the balances of RPT and SET Receivables accounts against the total amount of tax delinquencies; b. Prepare the necessary adjustments in the books to recognize the correct balance of the RPT and SET Receivables accounts as at year-end; and c. Strictly comply with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS I.
observations.managementAction: Coordinate with Treasurer for reconciliation; prepared necessary adjusting entries to present correct balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to: a. Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to present the correct balance of the said accounts in the financial statements; and c. Strictly comply with Sections 111 and 112 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Coordinated with the Province to reconcile and prepare necessary adjusting entries; partial adjustments made.
observations.reason: Reconciliation in process; some partial adjustments only.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to adjust the recorded PPE with acquisition cost below the capitalization threshold of P50,000.00 by debiting Prior Period Adjustment and corresponding Accumulated Depreciation of PPE accounts by P2,429,780.81 and P10,927,818.19, respectively, and crediting the corresponding PPE accounts by P13,357,599.00 in consonance with Sections 4.1 and 4.2 of COA Circular No. 2022-004 and Sections 3.6 and 3.7 of COA Circular No. 2024-006.
observations.managementAction: A copy of the recommended JEV No. 2024-06-1286 dated June 3, 2024 was submitted to the Audit Team on August 20, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to: a. Determine the details of the noted difference of P364,438.86 between their respective records, and subsequently prepare the necessary adjustments to present the correct balance of the Cash in Bank-LCCA; b. Continue the conduct of periodic reconciliation of their records at least quarterly as required under Section 181(c) of the GAAM, Volume I and Section 63 (F) of the LTOM; and c. Check meticulously the accuracy of the details in the DVs, Check Register, and other reports, ensure that DVs and Check Register were completely filled-up, and prepared the RCI in chronological order including cancelled/voided checks in accordance with Volume II, Chapter 2 of NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant are now checking meticulously the accuracy of the details in their respective office’s records, reports and documents. They performed periodic reconciliation of their records in CY 2024.
observations.reason: Upon validation of submitted DVs and check registers as of July 31, 2025, there were still signed but undated line items in the check registers of the Treasury Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Chairman to: a. Determine the root cause of the delayed submission of APP to the GPPB and cure the cause therein by enforcing strategies and setting deadlines to address these issues; b. Prepare and submit the required procurement reports to the GPPB within the deadline set by Sections 7.3.5 and 12.2 IRR of RA No. 9184 of the Government Procurement Reform Act, GPPB Circular No. 02-2020 dated May 20, 2020 and GPPB Advisory No. 03-2022 dated December 29, 2022; and c. Afterwards, comply with the posting requirement of the PMR in the Municipality’s website in consonance with EO No. 662, s. 2007 as required under Section 12.2 of the IRR of RA No. 9184 of the Government Procurement Reform Act.
observations.managementAction: The Audit Team was informed of the preliminary findings which indicated gaps in the Municipality's internal processes and communication channels that prompted the implementing of strategies such as informing the Budget Officer to strictly adhere with the prescribed timelines of the GPPB, setting specific deadlines, establishing a tracking system and conducting regular training sessions. The BAC Chairman is now also preparing and submitting the required procurement reports to the GPPB within the set deadline.
observations.reason: The first submission of CY 2024 APP and 1st semester of CY 2024 PMR were submitted to the GPPB on February 12 and July 16, 2024, respectively which still showed delays ranging from two to 12 days.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: a. The current GSIS Agency Authorized Officer to coordinate with Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the subject loan repayments will be remitted; and b. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to verify the composition of the Due to GSIS account and immediately remit to the GSIS the amount due for loan repayments/amortizations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer had already verified the composition of Due to GSIS by coordinating with GSIS, and together with the current GSIS Agency Authorized Officer, they will continue to follow-up the involved municipal employees regarding their unremitted loan repayments/amortizations to settle the latter as compliance to the recommendations and guidelines of the said audit observations. A list of the municipal employees with unremitted loan repayments/amortizations was provided in the submitted AAPSI on August 20, 2024.
observations.reason: Still with unremitted balance as of December 31, 2024 under the GF for Due to GSIS account amounting to P1,034,134.59.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to: a. Determine the details of the noted difference of P18,622,724.04 between their respective records, and subsequently prepare the necessary adjustment to present the correct balances of the Cash in Bank – LCCA in the cashbook and financial statements; b. Continue the conduct of periodic reconciliation of their records at least quarterly as required; and c. Check meticulously the accuracy of the details in the DVs, Check Register, and other reports.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office and Treasury performed periodic reconciliation of their records in CY 2024 and the Cash in Bank-LCCA balances of both offices were reconciled in the stated year under audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Human Resource Management Officer to assess the existing organization structure and staffing pattern of the Municipality so that the ideal number of plantilla items/positions could be established and develop a written policy guideline on the hiring of individuals under JO, clearly defining the conditions whereby employment of such JO personnel may be availed of.
observations.managementAction: Copy of the LGU's Policy Guidelines for the hiring and rehiring of personnel under JO/Contract of Service was submitted to the Audit Team on August 13, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to: a. Prepare the required reports on time and provide the assigned Supervising Auditor and/or Audit Team Leader with an updated list of all ongoing PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year, for audit validation and verification; and b. Strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2013-004 for the timely submission of the notices of posting of PPAs signboard and the required Monthly Monitoring Report.
observations.managementAction: The LGU complied in the submission of the required reports and was complying in the submission of the required Monthly Monitoring Report; proof/copy of the continuous remittance and submission of financial, statistical and other supporting reports to the PFDA for the period January - June 2024 and July to December 2024 were provided to the Audit Team on August 20, 2024 and May 14, 2025, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point System to: a. Obtain the details/breakdown of the reported costs/expenditures in the CY 2023 GAD Accomplishment Report and reconcile it with the Accounting Office to identify the cost spent/charged to each GAD PPAs and come up with the actual total GAD costs/expenditure that should have been reported in the GAD AR; and b. Strictly conduct regular monitoring and checking of the actual cost of GAD PPAs in consonance with Section 4.1.C.7 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013 as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016.
observations.managementAction: GAD related supporting documents were provided to the Audit Team on May 21, 2024. The GAD Focal Person conducted her best efforts to monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator assisted in the effort.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Direct the Local Finance Committee to prepare Monthly Report of Fund Utilization and Status of PPAs until the completion of the program reflecting the correct information regarding the utilization and disbursements and submit the same to the Audit Team and to other offices pursuant to Item 4.4 of LBC No. 136; and b. Instruct the concerned official to post the said reports on the Municipality's website in order to properly disclose the related information regarding the utilization and disbursements of the aforesaid financial assistance in public.
observations.managementAction: The said report was posted in the Municipality’s website on August 29, 2024 as per the Audit Team's verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit within the prescribed period all their accounts and financial reports to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to the above-mentioned regulations, to facilitate timely review/post audit of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office are exerting extra efforts up to the extent of adopting multi-tasking and implementing overtime services to enforce the timely submission of all the accounts and financial reports to the Office of the Auditor as compliance to the said audit observations.
observations.reason: Delays were still incurred in the submission of CY 2024 Accounting Reports to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the P2,124,362.54 in accordance with COA Circular No. 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022.
observations.managementAction: Management belatedly submitted the required documents. The Municipal Accountant was able to reclassify the identified semi-expandable items amounting to P2,124,362.54.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant remit to the BIR all taxes withheld within the prescribed period. Otherwise, any surcharges, interests and penalties that may be incurred shall be the personal liability of the municipal officials concerned who are responsible for the non-remittance of the taxes.
observations.managementAction: The Management was able to fully remit the fund withheld Due to BIR for the Calendar Year 2024 as reflected in the subsequent remittances made by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Accountant and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to: a. Submit the lacking documentary requirements for the procurement of various goods charged to the SEF to establish the regularity, propriety and validity of the disbursements; b. Henceforth, ensure strict compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Management belatedly submitted the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Direct the Municipal Engineer to strictly monitor the project implementation taking into consideration the target period of implementation; b. Submit to the Audit Team the approved suspension or extension order for the project to determine the validity of its new estimated completion date; and c. Henceforth, strictly comply with the terms of agreement embodied in the MOA with DOH- Center for Health Development of CALABARZON (CHD 4A) as regards project completion date.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer had monitored the project implementation in accordance with revised target period of implementation. Management furnished the Audit Team the two approved Contract Time Extension. Management, now ensures timely compliance with terms embodied in the MOA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor in coordination with the members of the Sangguniang Bayan: a. Ensure that disaster risks reduction program, projects and activities for inclusion in the LDRRMF 70 percent Mitigation Fund are properly planned, procurement-implementation-ready, and properly provided with timely enacted appropriation ordinance; and b. Henceforth, maximize the utilization of the Municipality’s LDRRMF - 70 percent Mitigation Fund.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2024, the Management has substantially implemented the LDRRMF 70 percent Mitigation Fund.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Provincial Accountant and Provincial Treasurer to verify the net difference of (P71,077.78), between the recorded balance of Due from LGUs account of the Municipality and its reciprocal account Due to LGUs reflected in the books of the Province; b. Municipal Accountant to subsequently prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to present the correct balance of the said account in the Municipality’s financial statements; and c. Thereafter, conduct a periodic reconciliation of Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs accounts in the Municipality’s books with the corresponding reciprocal accounts in the Province’s books.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2024, the recorded balance of Due to LGUs account of the Municipality and its reciprocal account reflected in the books of the Province are tie up in the amount of P1,223,630.14.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Government adopt and implement austerity measures in its market and slaughterhouse operation in order to minimize its deficit/losses. Also, we reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Municipal officials concerned immediately consider other alternatives on how to fully utilized the market slaughterhouse to maximize its earning capacity like leasing it out to other entities for warehouse or other purposes.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to furnish a Statement of Financial Performance of the Market as proof that the Market generated income of P199,448.97 in CY 2024. Also, Management has implemented measures as earlier recommended.

The Management furnished the Audit Team with SLs showing the details of the transactions. Corrected aging of Accounts Payable were submitted to the Audit Team. Proper recording entries were taken-up by the Municipal Accountant. The Management adhered on the cited provision of PD No. 1445 on keeping accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Chairman to:
a. Submit immediately the Supplemental APP to the GPPB and submit proof of the submission to the Audit Team; and
b. Comply with the posting requirement of the APP and PMR in the Municipality’s website as required under Section 12.2 of the 2016 R-IRR of RA No. 9184 and in consonance with EO No. 662, s. 2007.
observations.managementAction: The Supplemental APP and APP were submitted to the GPPB and the Audit Team. The APP and PMR were posted in the Municipality's website as required.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to:
a. Ensure that the project billboard is posted on the project site and in conspicuous places within the agency premises showing all relevant information of the said project being implemented by the Municipality to promote public awareness and concern over the infrastructure projects;
b. Submit notices to the Audit Team that signboards were posted within 10 days after the award of contract pursuant to Item 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004; and
c. Assign a specific person who will regularly update the relevant information of the projects on the posted signboards to guarantee timely transparency in all projects undertaken by the Municipality; and
d. Strictly comply with the guidelines set out in the same COA Circular on the notification to the public or publicity relating to the project.
observations.managementAction: Issued Executive Order No. 15 designating one personnel for the monitoring and placement of signboards. The Bid and Award Committee Technical Working Group was instructed to monitor compliance with the posting of signboards and submission of notices to the Audit Team.

The LDRRMC has been directed to review and update the LDRRMFIP and on May 6, 2025 the council convened and identified, reprogrammed and approved DRR projects amounting to P6,643,000.00. The council identified and prioritized DRRM projects during their 2025 2nd Quarter Meeting and will conduct quarterly reviews of the LDRRMF utilization.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LDRRMO to prepare and submit the proposed LDRRMFIP incorporating the programs and projects to be charged to STF in accordance with Section 4.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The projects to be funded by the STF were incorporated in the LDRRMFIP for CY 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local School Board (LSB) to:
a. Furnish the Audit Team with details of the PPAs and their corresponding budget in reference charged to SEF for audit; and
b. Ensure compliance with the planning, budgeting and utilization requirements for SEF in line with the DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017 and s. 2020.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted to the Audit Team the corresponding details of PPAs charged to SEF for CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LSB to ensure that resolutions being issued were approved or signed by all or majority of its member specially those involving the realignment of funds, henceforth ensure full compliance with the requirements set by the RA No. 7160 on the realignment of funds.
observations.managementAction: The LSB ensured that all budget realignment were monitored by the board and were supported with resolutions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Budget Officer to prepare and maintain the required RAAOs for SEF to ensure that proper control and monitoring of utilization of the SEF and the implementation of the approved budget are observed.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted the RAAO for CY 2023 and ensured continued compliance with the proper maintenance of the record.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal General Services Officer to strictly adhere to Article 47(c) of Administrative Order No. 270 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 to ensure that all real properties owned are titled in the name of the Municipality and to submit copies of the titles to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Management provided copies of the titles and the concerned properties were already transferred in the name of the Municipality. Two parcels of land pertaining to the Public Market were already expropriated and the title transferred in the name of the Municipality. The parcel of land where a school was located is subject to DepEd Memorandum No. 28, s. 2015.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to:
a. Prepare the required reports on time and provide the assigned Supervising Auditor and/or Audit Team Leader with an updated list of all ongoing PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year, for audit validation and verification; and
b. Strictly comply with the provisions of the cited regulations for the timely submission of the notices of posting of PPAs and Monthly Monitoring Reports.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted the investment plan as the list of all PPAs to be implemented for CY 2024 to the Audit Team. Issued Executive Order No. 15 designating one personnel to monitor the placement of signboards and timely submission of notices to Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
a. direct the responsible officials to conduct a re-study and re-evaluation of the operations of the MRCF to optimize its income generating capacity; and
b. direct the MRCF/MENRO personnel to submit/maintain reports/records on the operations of the facility for proper monitoring; and
c. exhaust all available remedies to collect the long outstanding receivable from EXPERT Inc.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-MENRO and MRF Supervisor were directed to re-study and re-evaluate the operation to optimize income generating capacity and were regularly instructed to submit monthly reports of the operation of MRCF. Demand letters were sent to the lessee but were returned due to insufficient address and the SEC registration was found suspended; hence management requested write-off under COA Circular No. 2023-008.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the OIC-GSO and Municipal Accountant to conduct regular reconciliation of their respective records/reports to achieve an accurate balance of the accounts as reported in the Financial Statements and Property Records and prepare all the necessary activities, documents and reports in order to take advantage of the opportunity for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balance as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31,2020. The procedural guidelines on the six activities for one-time cleansing of PPE balances namely the preliminary activities prior to the conduct of inventory taking, reconciliation of inventory count per Report of Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) with property and accounting records, disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs, procedures in the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability, and accounting entries and disclosures in the notes to financial statements are enumerated under Items 6.0, 7.0, 8.0, and 9.0 of the above Circular.
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the PPELCs, and if the GAAMS cannot provide, prepare PPELC manually; and OIC-GSO to issue PAR for all PPE items to the concerned accountable officers and maintain and update the related PCs as necessary pursuant to the Property Records provisions of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.
c. Create a Property Unit under the GSO or designate a Property Officer with a plantilla position, which shall be supervised by the General Services Officer, to focus on property management;
d. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and OIC-GSO with complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects as basis in the preparation of records/reports and disclosure of total road network system in the Notes to the Financial Statements as required pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008; and
e. Instruct the OIC-GSO to prepare and submit the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and Waste Material Report (WMR) to the Audit Team, whichever is applicable, and the Municipal Appraisal and Disposal Committee to cause the disposal of the identified unserviceable properties to prevent its further deterioration through proper disposal procedures and applicable mode of disposition as required by the COA and Department of Budget and Management (DBM) JC No. 2024-01 dated January 30, 2024.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor issued Office Order No. OMM-2024-309 dated May 8, 2024, achieved an accurate balance of the accounts as reported in the Financial Statements and Property Records and prepare all the necessary documents and reports in order to take advantage of the opportunity for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balance as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020. PPELCs are now being updated and manually maintained by the Accounting Office. PARs are now being issued as the Property Officer regularly approach and communicate with the Stockroom Unit to transmit the Purchase Order for the PPE items delivered. Office Order No. OMM-2024-308 dated April 26, 2024, was issued by the HRMO designating an acting Property Officer under GSO who will perform duties and functions of taking custody and accountability of PPEs of the Municipality. The Municipality has submitted the IIRUP and WMR to the Audit Team on October 1, 2024, for the initial inspection of the items for disposal. However, the Gymnasium inspection and appraisal were given priority due to its scheduled demolition. The Municipality regularly approach and communicate with the Stockroom Unit to transmit the Purchase Orders for the PPE items delivered. PPELCs are now being updated and manually maintained by the Accounting Office. The Municipality has prepared and submitted required documents and initiated disposal actions as necessary.
observations.reason: Management is still in the process of initial preparation for creating a team composed of members from the General Services Office (GSO) and Municipal Accounting Office who will undertake the necessary activities, procedures for one-time cleansing of PPE account balances. For preparation of RTS for recommendation s considered closed for having been reiterated with modification.

observations.managementAction: A thorough review and analysis of the accounts payable as of year-end has been made by the Municipal Accountant in compliance with the recommendation. The Municipal Mayor issued an SB Ordinance No. 11 s. 2024 authorizing the adjustments. The total amount of P70,840,282.27 has been warranted by the SB to be reverted to the unappropriated surplus last June 5, 2024, during its 21st Regular Session. The adjusting reversing entries has already been recorded pursuant to the transactions warranted and authorized by the SB’s Ordinance No. 11 s. 2024 authored by Con. Mario Roderick C. Lazarte. In coordination with the Budget Office, the Accounting Office ensures the completeness of supporting documents (e.g., obligation request (OBR), invoices, contracts, etc.) and accuracy of amount prior to the recording/ set up of accounts payable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require immediate action from the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer on the net book reconciling items totaling P18,155,557.59 reflected in the BRS to fully identify the nature of such items, and cause appropriate adjustments so as to reflect correct and accurate CIB-LCCA, in adherence to the provisions of Sections 3.2 and 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 and Paragraph 27 of IPSAS I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer have identified the reconciling items and the corresponding journal entries were recorded in the books. A thorough review and analysis of the transactions related to Bank Reconciliations was conducted and the necessary adjustments were made. Some reconciling items remained unadjusted as of year-end but the offices committed to expedite reconciliation and adjustment of residual items.
observations.reason: For preparation of RTS for recommendation s considered closed for having been reiterated with modification.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Re-evaluate the necessity for the granting of PCF to the 33 PCF Custodians to establish adequate control over the fund; 
b. Impose that PCF shall be strictly for petty operating expenses to cover recurring expenses for one month duly supported by an estimate of costs in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002 and 2023-004; and
c. Assess whether there is a need to reduce the said fund based on historical and/or projected monthly expenses of the Municipality and return the excess funds to the General Fund, if necessary, to mitigate the risk of loss or misuse and unnecessary tying up of funds that could have been used for other purposes.
observations.managementAction: The Finance Committee conducted a thorough evaluation of the functions of each office/unit requesting PCF to assess the necessity of their requests and determine a reasonable amount to be granted. As a result, the number of PCF Custodians was reduced from 33 to only 10. The departments/offices requesting for PCF are now required to submit an approved estimate of their monthly petty expenses. The Finance Committee agreed to limit cash advances for PCF at Twenty-Five Thousand Pesos (P25,000.00) and any request above this amount should be justified and approved by the Municipal Mayor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to investigate the noted discrepancy of P362,000.00, reconcile their respective records and thereafter, monitor the collections therein, on compliance with Section 2 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office already made the necessary correcting entries to record the set-up of the remaining receivables for the lease of Tondominium thru Journal Entry Voucher No. GJ-100-2404-0088 dated April 30, 2024 amounting to P44,332,000.00. The CTS issued to the beneficiaries were rescinded and the CTS issued to the beneficiaries were rescinded. The Accounting Office implemented actions to reconcile the discrepancy and set up the lease receivables accordingly.
observations.reason: The CTS issued to the beneficiaries were rescinded. The reconciliation of balances and set-up of receivables were undertaken.

observations.managementAction: On February 7 and 8, 2024, the LGU conducted a capacity building seminar/training on the preparation of AIP and PPMP in partnership with the System and Plan Integrator and Development Corporation (SPIDC). The said seminar/training was attended by the department/office heads as well as the staff in charge in the AIP and PPMP preparation. The Municipal Administrator instructed all the units to submit their PPMP on time and the Municipal Budget Office has been requested to oversee the review, consolidation, and finalization of the APP. The APP for CY2024 submitted to the Audit Team already indicated the mode of procurement. The BAC Secretariat has finalized the modes of procurement under the proposed APP where applicable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require immediate action from its Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) to make representation with the GSIS on the updating and/or settlement of the delinquent loan accounts of 46 municipal employees totaling P1,661,536.56 in accordance with the IRR of RA No. 8291, to avoid further accumulation of penalties and surcharges by the concerned municipal employees and the unnecessary tying up of funds of the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-HRMO made the necessary inquiries to GSIS to shed light on the matter on the update of their loan status for clarification purposes and to make the necessary deductions. A series of communications were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-MDRRMO to prepare and submit to the MDRRMC the proposed LDRRMFIP incorporating the programs and projects to be charged to the STF in accordance with Section 4.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMFIP for 2024 was already prepared. The MDRRMC meeting was conducted to present, finalize and approve the use of STF and the PPA to be funded as indicated in the LDRRMFIP through an MDRRMC Resolution. Requested the office of the Municipal Mayor to convene the Municipal Development Council (MDC) to adopt the LDRRMFIP as approved by the MDRRMC in order to amend the current AIP. Requested the Municipal Mayor to endorse the passage of a SB resolution approving the AIP amendment and enactment of a supplemental budget to use the STF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-MDRRMO, in coordination with other concerned offices, to ensure full utilization of the MDRRM fund by implementing the identified PPAs in compliance with RA 10121 that is aimed to strengthen the disaster resilience of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO in coordination with the concerned LGU offices has implemented the various PPAs lined up for 2024. The LDRRMFIP for CY 2024 was prepared and submitted with identified programs, projects and activities (PPA) to be funded by the STF with a total amount of PhP 228,128,894.02. As of December 31, 2024, specific PPAs were implemented as programmed and funds utilized accordingly.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the GSO in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to list all insurable properties in the Property Inventory Form (PIF) with the latest appraised values/valuation, appraisal date and other information as required under COA Circular No. 2018-002 and submit the PIF to the Supervising Auditor/Audit Team Leader and the GIF, GSIS, not later than April 30 of each year;
b. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to prioritize the inclusion in the Municipality’s annual budget of the premiums for the general realignment to provide a budget for the premiums for the general insurance covering all insurable properties; and
c. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure the payment of the insurance premiums to the GSIS; and
d. Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 656 as amended by PD No. 245 and COA Circular No. 2018-002, to protect the interest of the Municipality and be indemnified in the event of damage or loss due to casualty or fortuitous events.
observations.managementAction: GSO prepared the Property Inventory Form for insurable properties limited to buildings erected within the specified appraisal. Budget Office prepared the realignment to provide a budget for the premiums for the general insurance covering all insurable properties under the Insurable Expense Account. A check payable to GSIS was issued on August 1, 2024 in the amount of P186,743.05 to cover insurance premiums. The Municipal Treasurer ensures timely renewal and payment of insurance premiums.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to adopt the payroll system to eliminate the risk of loss or misapplication of funds aside from promoting greater convenience, transparency, and accountability in consonance with the provisions of Section 124 of PD No. 1445, DBM Circular No. 2008-08 and the IRR of EO No. 170, s. 2022; and
b. Municipal HRMO to formulate policy requiring each JO personnel to open a payroll account with the servicing bank immediately upon assumption of office.
observations.managementAction: The Human Resource Management Office ensures that all onboarding Job Order Personnel thoroughly complete their Veterans Applications Forms and submit all necessary documents before their assumption of office as part of the hiring process. An executive order No. 19, Series of 2024, titled 'An Order Amending Executive Order No. 32, Series of 2023, on the Requirements for Hiring and Rehiring JO Personnel in the Municipality of Rodriguez, Rizal', was issued by the Municipal Mayor on March 13, 2024. The order directs the HRMO to include, among other requirements, the submission of photocopy of the Veterans Bank ATM card, along with photocopies of one valid government-issued ID and three (3) specimen signatures. As of May 2, 2025, the updated status of payroll disbursement shows improvement in ATM coverage and account openings for employees.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. The GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-related PAPs as planned, in order to attain the objective for which funds are provided for, in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2011-01, as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01;
b. The Municipal Budget Officer to strengthen collaboration with the GAD Focal Person to ensure that the budget reflects a more realistic and practical allocation of resources, taking into account both financial constraints and gender and development priorities; and
c. The Municipal Administrator to constantly remind concerned officials and employees on the pertinent laws, rules and regulations governing GAD programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Office conducted numerous seminars, trainings and livelihood programs. The Office commits to enhance planning processes and to develop Annual GAD Plan and Budget based on a comprehensive analysis of the department’s actual expenditures over the past three years to allow for more accurate budget forecasting and allocation. The Directive of the Municipal Mayor to the GFPS to maximize the utilization of GAD funds through the implementation of planned GAD-related PPAs is being observed.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor thoroughly investigate the cause of the difference of P167,651,860.75 between their respective records and subsequently prepare the necessary adjustments to present the correct balance of the CIB-LCCA in the Accounting and Treasury Offices’ records.
observations.managementAction: The reconciliation of the balances of its Cash in Bank – LCCA was still ongoing. The Municipal Treasury Office has identified an amount of P4,555,106.54 and P34,566,823.51 in the Special Education Fund and Trust Fund, respectively. The Municipal Accountant continues to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to determine the details of discrepancies in related accounts and to prepare adjustments as necessary.
observations.reason: Out of the total amount, the Provincial Accountant revealed discrepancies of P4,555,106.54 and P34,566,823.51 in the Special Education Fund and Trust Fund, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to determine the details of the discrepancies between the balances of the Due from/Due to LGUs accounts recorded in the Municipality and Province’s records so that appropriate adjustments can be made for fair presentation of the affected accounts in the Municipality’s financial statements; and henceforth, to conduct periodic reconciliation of the Municipality’s accounts with the Provincial Accounting records to avoid the accumulation of discrepancies between the two records.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant awaits instructions from the Provincial Accountant regarding the unreconciled balances of the Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs accounts. Coordination efforts were made and transactions are under review with the Provincial Accounting records.
observations.reason: There are still discrepancies in balances of Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs accounts as of December 31, 2024 and awaiting reconciliation with the Provincial Accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from granting additional cash advances unless the previous ones had been already settled/liquidated; and henceforth, strictly observe the guidelines on the granting and liquidation of cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Eleven (11) demand letters were sent to concerned accountable officers as of October 9, 2023. The Municipal Accounting Office enforces the guidelines on cash advances and refrains from granting additional cash advances unless the previous ones had been liquidated. The Accounting Office will continue to issue demand letters on a monthly basis and endorse to HRMO for appropriate administrative action when necessary.
observations.reason: For preparation of RTS for recommendation s considered closed for having been reiterated with modification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-HRMO to consider those qualified JO personnel for appointment to casual or plantilla positions in the various regular plantilla positions in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: As of December 31, 2024, the Municipality has considered JO personnel for appointment to casual, coterminous and permanent positions. The Municipality converted several JO positions to Casual – 23; JO to Coterminous – 21; JO to Permanent – 11; Casual to Coterminous – 21. The HRMO processed actions to regularize qualified personnel and to fill vacancies where applicable.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Exert efforts to locate and retrieve the relevant documents pertaining to the long outstanding receivable accounts to establish its validity and legality; and
b. Closely coordinate with the concerned National Government Agencies to immediately return the unexpended balances of fund transfers to its source agencies as required under COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.
observations.managementAction: Unused fund of P71,500.00 was returned to the Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD) on May 26, 2023 per Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 400-2305-0080 and Official Receipt (OR) No. 9290793. The Accounting Office continues to coordinate with concerned agencies for the return of the remaining balances amounting to P4,090,433.20 which remain on the books as of December 31, 2024 pending formal request from the concerned agencies to return the unutilized funds.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO to prepare a comprehensive and integrated LDRRMFIP in accordance with the national, regional, provincial framework, and policies in Disaster Risk Reduction in close coordination with the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, and Municipal Development Council, which will be reviewed and updated annually in compliance with the above-mentioned regulation.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMFIP for CY 2024 was already prepared. The MDRRMO coordinated with concerned offices to prepare the LDRRMFIP and the MDRRMC resolved for its approval. The MDRRMO continues to conduct the regular updates and coordination with the MDC for its adoption and for the timely submission of reports.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to strictly monitor and ensure the efficient implementation of the planned GAD program, projects and activities to ensure GAD objectives are fully attained and the actual expenditures equate the amount of the approved budget per PPA.
observations.managementAction: The change in administration affected the implementation of GAD PPAs which included the shortage of personnel and discrepancies in the allocated budget per PPA. However, the GAD Office implemented measures to enhance planning and monitoring. The GAD Office commits to enhancing its planning processes. Moving forward, the Annual GAD Plan and Budget will be developed based on a comprehensive analysis of the department’s actual expenditures over the past three years for better forecasting and allocation.
observations.reason: The change in administration affected the implementation of the GAD PPAs which included the shortage of personnel and discrepancies in the allocated budget per PPA.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to carefully analyze the transactions related to the Loans Payable account to determine if there were errors committed in recording these transactions. Thereafter, draw necessary journal entry to establish the correct balance of the said account.
observations.managementAction: Analysis of transactions is in progress. All loan payments pertaining to Philippine Veterans Bank accounts were accounted for. As of December 31, 2024, an unaccounted balance still exists between the records of the Municipal Accountant and the Philippine Veterans Bank accounts. The Accounting Office continues to coordinate with the bank for full reconciliation.
observations.reason: Analysis of transactions is in progress. All loan payments pertaining to Philippine Veterans Bank accounts were accounted for. Unreconciled balance remains and coordination with the bank is ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Continue exerting efforts to locate and retrieve the relevant documents pertaining to the long outstanding receivable accounts to establish its validity and legality;
b. Exhaust all possible means to enforce payment from the debtor for those receivables proven to be valid; and
c. Request for write-off of the Dormant Receivable Accounts to the Commission on Audit, duly supported with the documentary requirements prescribed in Item 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by Management to request for write-off. The Accounting Office continues to exert efforts to locate and retrieve the relevant documents and to exhaust all possible means to enforce payment from debtors. Analysis and validation of dormant receivables are ongoing.
observations.reason: No action was taken to request for write-off to the Audit Team. Validation and analysis of cash advances and receivables are ongoing and being reiterated with modifications.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the: a. Inventory Committee (IC) to- • Prepare, in coordination with General Services Office (GSO), Office of the Municipal Accountant (OMA), and submit the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) for approval of the Municipal Mayor to ensure that the conduct of inventory is properly and adequately planned pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006; • Submit a copy of the approved PIP to COA at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities; • Use temporary control stickers in the conduct of physical inventory and attach the same to the prominent and secured portion of the inventoried PPE for proper and easy identification; • Prepare the List of PPEs Found at Station to facilitate the recognition of PPE items not recorded in the books to establish reasonable basis to consider the same as property of the Municipality; • Prepare the List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs to facilitate the conduct of required procedures in the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without established accountability; and • Strictly implement COA Circular No. 2020-006 in the conduct of inventory of PPE. b. GSO to- • Use pre-numbered property tags or control stickers to all PPE containing the information required under Section 5.7 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 and to attach the same to the prominent and secured portions of the inventoried PPE for proper and easy identification; • Ensure that all issuances of PPE items are covered with PARs as prescribed under Section 5.6 of COA Circular No. 2020-006; • Renew/update issued PARs every three years after issuance or whenever there is a change in custodianship to establish proper accountability; • Ensure that all PPE acquisitions and disposal are timely and completely recorded in the PC; • Ensure submission of RPCPPE on or before January 31 of the following year in compliance with the prescribed guidelines; and • Keep a complete and updated record of all PPE items. c. OMA to: • Record adjustments after completion of proper reconciliation with GSO to establish the correct balances of PPE and other affected accounts, with the presence of valid supporting documents; • Prepare and maintain PPELC for each PPE items and continually update the same to account for additions to and subtractions from PPE; and • Strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2020-006, and Section 491, GAAM, Vol. I. d. OMA, GSO and IC to, henceforth, coordinate and conduct reconciliation of the results of physical inventory and GSO records/RCPPE; identify and address the nature and causes of variances; and hold responsible/ accountable the designated supply officers/custodians pursuant to Section 491 of GAAM, Vol. I, and other existing laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The PIP prepared by the GSO and OMA was approved by the Municipal Mayor to support the conduct of inventory in a proper manner provided in COA Circular. There were continuous coordination made between GSO and Municipal Accountant as well as on-going reconciliation of their respective records such as retrieval of records to determine the concerned accountable officers to whom the PPE items were issued. Moreover, an on-going preparation of temporary control stickers to be used in the conduct of physical inventory for One-Time Cleansing of PPE accounts. The IC, in coordination with the GSO and OMA, already prepared the List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs awaiting other procedures set forth in the COA guidelines.
observations.reason: Not considered closed for having been reiterated with modification in Audit Observation No. 1, Page Nos. 38 to 47. Not all properties subjected in the physical counts were provided with pre-numbered property tags.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the: a. Municipal Accountant to: • Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the balance of the affected accounts for the fair presentation in the financial statements, copy furnish the Office of the Auditor for evaluation; • Strictly observe the application of accrual basis and monitor all the liabilities due the suppliers/creditors by merely recording all valid claims or liabilities in the books of accounts in order to avoid/prevent conflict claims to be filed by the latter; and b. General Services Officer to copy furnish the Municipal Accountant regularly with the accomplished required Acceptance and Inspection Report duly supported with PO/contract and delivery receipt every after completed delivery of procured items to facilitate timely recording by the latter in the books.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already reflected the adjustment in the books by drawing journal entry vouchers. The Municipal GSO consistently furnishing the Municipal Accountant copies of the necessary documents to be recorded by the latter in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Prepare an updated and detailed schedule of the guaranty deposits payable account for monitoring; b. Inform the identified suppliers/contractors who have valid outstanding claims against the Municipality to file their requests for refund and thereafter cause the release of their guaranty/security deposits; and c. Revert to the unappropriated surplus those identified as long outstanding wherein the obligation to pay had prescribed or could no longer be established due to the lack of details or wherein the guaranty/security deposit had been forfeited.
observations.managementAction: The list or schedule of the account was already updated and detailed manner was undertaken by the Municipal Accountant reflecting sufficient information to determine the extent of liability by the MGT into the suppliers/contractors. The Municipal Accountant informed 16 suppliers and contractors, in a letter dated May 8, 2025, regarding the outstanding claims from the MGT totaling P1,057,653.17.
observations.reason: On-going reconciliation of records to determine the amounts to be reverted to the unappropriated surplus. There were long outstanding amounts recorded in the account that has still lack of details. Reiterated with modification in Audit Observation No. 4, Page Nos. 56 to 58.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman/ Secretariat to furnish the Auditor with copies of approved contracts/Purchase Orders (POs) together with the complete supporting documents within five working days from the execution or issuance in compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2024, the Bids through the GSO consistently submitting the contracts together with the supporting documents within the prescribed period to the Audit Team pertaining to the MGT's transactions aside from the contracts related to the LGSF – SBDP of the NTF – ELCAC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to: a. Conduct a thorough assessment and evaluation of existing manpower complement to re-assign JO workers whose functions are part of the job description of the agency's existing regular employees as defined in COA-DBM JC No. 2, s. 2020; or remove subject JO workers if determined unnecessary and excessive to avoid inefficient bureaucracy and wasteful government expenditure; and b. Fill-up vacant plantilla positions to minimize the hiring of JO workers.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO conducted initial assessment and evaluation on the existing manpower. Personnel evaluation was conducted by HRMO. Afterwhich, 19 JO workers were given regular plantilla positions and 14 regular employees were promoted per personnel movement report dated May 15, 2025.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2024, JO workers are still the main workforce of the Municipality as this consist of 439 JO out of 673 total personnel. The measures were ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the GSO to: a. Ensure that all insurable properties of the Municipality are insured and likewise make the necessary steps for the proper implementation of the Section 5 of RA No. 656; and b. Prepare the Property Inventory Form (PIF) based on the RPCPPE prepared in accordance with the provisions of the COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018.
observations.managementAction: The GSO listed all the properties that are insurable in the PIF and likewise submitted to the Government Service Insurance System for (GSIS), in accordance the discrepancies of insurance noted in audit observations. The GSO prepared the PIF.
observations.reason: Not all insurable properties were insured; some portions of the amount of insurance applied were disapproved by GSIS thus, discrepancies noted in audit observations. Reiterated with modification in Audit Observation No. 9, Page Nos. 68 to 70.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the: a. MDRRMO to maximize the use of LDRRMF of the MGT and fully implement the planned Programs, Projects and Activities (PPAs) to attain the MGT’s preparedness, response, and recovery from the impacts of natural and man-made disasters efficiently and effectively; and b. Review and update annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) indicating the PPAs to be charged against the LDRRMF trust fund pursuant to section 5.1.15 of the COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2002.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2024, the Municipality implemented 22 out of 23 PPAs and the remaining 1 PPA was implemented in February 2025. The LDRRMFIP was reviewed and updated annually by the MDRRMO with the special approval of the Municipal Mayor including the 5-year fund transferred in the special trust fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to: a. Review the amount appearing as unremitted and cause the immediate remittance to the GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth; ensure that the latter of remittances correspond to the exact amount withheld; remit the withheld balances and repayments within the prescribed period to avoid penalties and guarantee that Municipal personnel/employees enjoy the full benefits. b. HRMO to update immediately the data/information of new recruits/newly promoted personnel so that withheld deductions shall be duly accepted/billed by the systems of GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth; and issue memorandum to concerned employees to immediately update/report changes regarding member’s information with government agencies to avoid delays in remittance or non-remittance of contributions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, already remitted in full the withheld taxes, premium contributions and loan amortizations for CY 2022 to the respective government agencies. The Municipal Welfare and Development Officer exerted effort to collect remained balances of the account of the Municipality and yet returned to the concerned Source Agency.
observations.reason: Variances are due to the renewal of loans of employees and are under reconciliation with concerned agencies. Preparation of working papers for withholding and remittance was made by the Municipal Accountant; adjustments in the books will be reflected once reconciled. Noted as reiterated with modification in AAR CY 2023 Audit Observation No. 10, Page Nos. 66 to 69.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to: a. prepare the necessary journal entry to revert the said outstanding obligations to Government Equity pursuant to Section 98 of PD No. 1445 so that the Municipality can use the available funds for other priority projects; and b. initiate action to request authority to write-off the said dormant account in accordance with requirements and procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: The Management could not revert the identified outstanding obligations to the unappropriated surplus by virtue of SB resolution as the SB, in the letter dated May 8, 2025, commented that the revertion of the same is not within their mandate.
observations.reason: No adjustment was made by the Municipality as no authority given by the Sangguniang Bayan to revert the outstanding balance into government equity.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to immediately return to the Source Agency the unexpended balance of fund amounting to P609,685.00 which was received during the term of former Congresswoman. Otherwise, initiate action to request for an authority to write-off the said dormant account in accordance with the requirements and procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer exerted effort to collect the balances of the unspent Self Employment Assistance Kaunlaran (SEA-K) loans and returned to the Source Agency. The Municipality is still in the process of determining the status of beneficiaries as to their current location, financial status and vital records.
observations.reason: The unexpended balance due to Source Agency remained in the account of the Municipality and yet to be remitted to the concerned source agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to: a. Immediately remit whatever balance due to the agencies to avoid incurrence of penalties, surcharges and inconveniences to the employees concerned; and b. Strictly remit in full the withheld taxes, premium contributions and loan amortizations to the respective government agencies in accordance with the cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer already remitted in full the withheld taxes, premium payments and loan amortizations for CY 2022 to the respective government agencies. The HRMO instructed the Administrator to update the records; HRMO updated the data/information of 16 newly hired personnel, retired, separated and promoted personnel of the MGT.
observations.reason: There were withheld amounts due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth still unremitted as of December 31, 2023. Preparation of request to the RD for the approval of the RTS for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO to monitor the extent of the implementation of the planned PPAs to ensure that its constituents would receive the benefits that may be derived therefrom at the soonest.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2024, the Municipality implemented 22 out of 23 PPAs and the remaining 1 PPA was implemented in February 2025.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the OIC-Municipal Accountant to remit to the Bureau of Treasury/Source Agencies all the unused balance of funds pursuant to the cited regulations, so that the amount could be immediately used for other projects and to relieve the MGT from any accountability/liability for the said unused funds. Should there be a need for the unspent amounts, the MGT should request the source agencies for authority/approval to use the said amounts for other purposes or projects.
observations.managementAction: The Management has coordinated with the concerned Source Agencies for the implementation of the projects and the actions to be taken regarding the unutilized funds.
observations.reason: Some subject amounts are still not remitted to the concerned Source Agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Dispose the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445 to avoid further deterioration; and b. Instruct the GSO to submit the necessary documents such as list of unserviceable documents, pictures of disposed equipment, deed of donation if donated to other agency or the Official Receipt from sale of the unserviceable PPE item/s to the Accounting Office, as basis of the latter to drop the unserviceable properties from books of accounts in accordance with the existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Only disposal for unserviceable motor vehicles was prepared by the Municipality. The Office prepared necessary documents for disposal for unserviceable motor vehicles. The GSO prepared documents as basis for the Accounting Office for dropping these unserviceable properties from books after reconciliation between GSO and the OMA.
observations.reason: Not all PPE other than motor vehicles disposal documents were submitted to the Accounting Office due to on-going reconciliation with the GSO and OMA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to: a. Ensure that all financial reports and their supporting documents are submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed period as required under COA Circular Nos. 95-006 and 2009-006. Otherwise, any unjustified failure on the part of the official concerned to submit the required reports shall be considered a ground for the automatic suspension of payment of his salaries until he shall have complied with the requirement, and b. Determine the cause/s of the delay in the submission of the accounts of the Municipality to the Audit Team and find solutions thereto.
observations.managementAction: The Management is committed to adhere with the audit recommendations. The Management effected an internal policy as solution for the fast recording of transactions in the books of accounts in compliance with the existing guidelines. However, there were instances that cannot be addressed by the employed internal control that causes delay in the submission of the accounts of the MGT due to unavoidable risks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, nonetheless, issue the corresponding demand letter or billing statement to enforce collection thereon and to exhaust all possible means to enforce payment by the debtors, and for the Management to avail of writing-off of the account subject to the provisions stated in Annex 8 of COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality exhausted efforts to collect these accounts. Request for write-off was submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Government Department Head (MGDH) - Municipal Human Resource Management Office to: a. request a copy of billing statements or its equivalent and records from the respective agencies to facilitate the reconciliation of account balances; b. trace to prior years’ accounting entries to ascertain the cause of negative balances in the Due to Pag-IBIG and GSIS accounts; and c. review and analyze records pertaining to the unreconciled balances in the Subsidiary Ledgers (SL) of the Due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth accounts and immediately adjust the unreconciled and abnormal account balances to correct and update SL balances in accordance with the provisions in Sections 123 and 124 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Human Resource Office has already obtained the record. The Municipal Accountant was able to trace the prior years’ accounting entries and adjusted the balances accordingly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to: a. instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to closely monitor the implementation of the identified priority development projects to achieve its full implementation based on the timeline set; and b. direct the concerned officials to ensure that the projects under the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready in accordance with Item No. 3.2.3 of the JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 to assure strict compliance on the proper utilization of the fund.
observations.managementAction: The Management confirms implementing the recommendations and takes full responsibility for the delayed or not implementing the 13 out of 20 percent DF. The OIC-MPDC and Municipal Engineer intensified Local Monitoring Committee (LPMC) to be more proactive in monitoring the implementation of projects funded under the 20 percent DF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to: a. process the release of the guaranty deposits in a total amount of P1,295,223.39 subject to the attested Certification of the Procurement Office that the delivered goods were free from manufacturing defects; and b. process the release of the guaranty deposits after the lapse of the warranty period and within reasonable time allowed under Section 62.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, to present fairly the liability account in the financial statements as of a given period.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer have processed and released a total amount of P1,027,909.03 and the amount of P13,131.70 was reversed out of P1,295,223.89 balances of the Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Mayor to require immediate action from its Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) to make representation with the GSIS on the updating and/or settlement of the delinquent loan accounts of 29 municipal employees totaling P1,547,056.17 in accordance with the Revised IRR of RA No. 8291, to avoid further accumulation of penalties and surcharges by the concerned municipal employees and the unnecessary tying up of funds of the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Authorized Officer (AAO) under the Office of the Municipal Accountant have coordinated with the GSIS on January 31, 2024, thus, resulting to updating of records of said 29 employees. The extracted report from GSIS system showed remarks as reconciled, fully paid, and with Multi-purpose loan (MPL) availment.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the MGDH-HRMO to: a. adopt policies and internal controls to monitor loan accommodations and other payroll deductions such that monthly net take-home pay of any employee does not fall below the prescribed ceiling of P5,000.00; b. direct those in charge of payroll preparation to exercise diligence in monitoring net take home pay of employees to comply with the requirements of existing law. Remedial measures should be taken to rectify the situation of the 15 employees receiving below the required net take home pay which includes not allowing them to apply for the new loan unless their net take-home pay will not fall below P5,000.00; and c. stop the current practice and strictly implement Section 56 of the General Provisions of RA No. 11936 on the mandatory monthly net take home pay of P5,000.00.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has adopted policies and internal controls to monitor loan accommodations and other payroll deductions such that monthly net take-home pay of any employee does not fall below the prescribed ceiling of P5,000.00. The Municipal HRMO will ensure diligence in monitoring net take home pay and remedial measures shall be taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to conduct tax campaign initiatives to boost the collection of Real Property Tax and other taxes imposed by the Municipality. Additional personnel handling year-round issuance of notice of tax delinquencies and tax campaign. Notice of tax delinquencies were being posted in all nine (9) barangays and entrance of the Municipal Hall.
observations.managementAction: The Citizen’s Charter of the Municipality was reviewed including the tax payment process. Additional personnel were assigned handling year-round issuance of notice of tax delinquencies and tax campaign. Notice of tax delinquencies were being posted in all nine (9) barangays and entrance of the Municipal Hall.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to determine the details of the discrepancies between the balances of the reciprocal accounts Due from/Due to LGUs recorded in the Municipality’s and the Province’s record so that appropriate adjustment can be made for fair presentation of the affected accounts in the Municipality’s financial statements; and b. henceforth, conduct periodic reconciliation of The Municipality’s account with the Provincial accounting records to avoid the accumulation of discrepancies between the two records.
