observations.managementAction: Coordination with the Provincial Accounting Office to reconcile accounts has been made for the balance of Due to LGU as of December 31, 2022 based on the Certification made by the former dated May 8, 2024 for the balance of Due to LGU account as of December 31, 2023. The reconciliation has been made for balances as of June 30 and September 21, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Human Resource and Management Officer (HRMO) to consider those qualified JO personnel for appointment to casual or vacant regular plantilla positions in the agency.
observations.managementAction: Policy guidelines in hiring JO personnel and contract of service were submitted to the Audit Team on May 22, 2024, duly approved by the Local Chief Executive (LCE) on April 3, 2023. Of the 15 qualified JO personnel, 10 have already been appointed as casual employees and five as permanent employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal person to regularly monitor and ensure the efficient implementation of GAD PPAs in accordance with the DILG endorsed GPB.
observations.managementAction: Certificate of Review and Endorsement (CRE) for the GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2023 was issued by the DILG dated November 2, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the PPE including the Road Network System to visually check the existence and condition of the properties of the Municipality. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Office Head to request from the Engineering Office the detailed cost of Local Road Networks and other related information to be included in the RPCRNS.
observations.managementAction: The Property/Supply Officer closely coordinated with the Municipal Engineering Office for the complete Physical Count of Local Road Network as well as the complete Physical Count of the Road Network and submitted the RPCPPE to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the: (i) all the officials concerned to discontinue the over-the-counter arrangement for loan repayments; and (ii) the Human Resource Management Officer to (a) conduct thorough evaluation on the employees’ capacity to pay before certifying that the monthly net pay of loan applicant is sufficient to cover all authorized deductions, without falling below the statutory net pay of P5,000.00 per month as required in the GAA. In the event that total authorized deductions shall reduced net take home pay to less than P5,000.00, deductions shall be made in accordance with the order of preference as required in Section 56 of FY 2023 GAA; and (b) be strict in the approval of loans of LGU personnel to prevent occurrence of similar transaction as these may also affect the efficiency of their delivery of public service. We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to conduct proper monitoring and periodic reconciliation of accounts to ensure that correct amounts are withheld as well as remitted to the concerned National Government Agencies for reliable and correct financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: The amount of over remittance of employee’s loan amortization to GSIS had been recovered and collected from the concerned employees. Moreover, balances of the Inter-Agency Payables reflected the actual normal balances of payables due for remittance to other government agencies as at December 31, 2024 which will be remitted in the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the portion of the long-term loan which is due for settlement within 12 months from reporting date as current liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: The Management had effected the adjustment on June 27, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the: (i) Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant, at the beginning of the year, a certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year for the proper establishment of Real Property and Special Education Taxes Receivables based on the Real Property Account Register/Taxpayers’ Index Card; and (ii) Municipal Accountant to: (a) regularly take up in the books of accounts the total RPT/SET tax dues based on the certified list of tax due and collectible furnished by the Municipal Treasurer; (b) coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for proper reconciliation of the discrepancy in the recorded balances of RPT and SET Receivables as against the total amount of tax delinquencies indicated in the Real Property Tax Owners Delinquency Listing and prepare the necessary adjusting entry to present the correct and reliable balances of RPT and SET Receivable accounts in the financial statements as at year-end; (c) ensure that RPT and SET Receivables are properly recognized only in the year they are earned; and (d) analyze the net effect of the foregoing observations in the financial statements and draw the necessary adjusting entries in the General and Special Education Fund books to reflect the correct balance of the affected accounts; and (iii) Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to analyze and determine the cause of the deficiencies between their records, prepare reconciliation and draw the necessary adjusting journal entry, and submit to this Office the actions taken to establish the correct balances of the subject receivables.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: Management had begun implementing the recommended accounting entries in the CY 2024 transactions. The Municipal Accountant effected the necessary adjusting journal entries under JEV Nos. (General): 2024-01-18A and 2024-01-20A; (SEF): 2024-01-01A and 2024-01-01A. Non-reconciliation with the MAccO and Municipal Assessor’s Office and lack of personnel to focus on RPT-related tasks were cited as reasons for prior delays; similar discrepancies were reiterated in CY 2024 audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to ensure strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to avoid accumulation of outstanding cash advances. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly monitor the grant of cash advance by issuing certification that previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books to prevent the grant of additional cash advances to those officers and employees with outstanding cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: This had been monitored by the Municipal Accountant; however, there are still instances when cash advances were granted even though the previous cash advance was not yet liquidated. Thus, some outstanding advances persisted and similar issues were reiterated in the CY 2024 audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to: (i) exert more effort and undertake effective, efficient and aggressive tax collection strategies to improve collection by conducting massive tax campaigns and consistent preparation and issuance of Notices of Delinquency for all delinquent Real Property Units; (ii) monitor the payment of real property taxes due for the current year and impose the available remedies and procedures as authorized under the Local Government Code; (iii) for the delinquent real property taxes with NDs already served, recommend to the Provincial Treasurer the imposition of the legal remedies either by administrative auction thru levy on real property or judicial action, following the procedures authorized in the LGC, to enforce collection and to undertake the same expeditiously on a yearly basis to reduce amounts accumulated as receivables for delinquent taxes; and (iv) conduct dialogue with owners different from the records on file to address concerns on the collection of taxes. If warranted, request the presence of the Provincial Treasurer and Provincial Legal Officer to assist in such undertaking.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: Management continues its tax campaign strategies, such as posting reminders at Municipal Hall and Barangay Halls and sending Notices of Delinquency once a year; requested assistance of Barangay Officials and coordinates with Provincial Treasurer’s Association. However, collections remain constrained due to factors beyond LGU control (e.g., adverse events affecting taxpayers, unknown whereabouts of owners, remote locations, insufficient revenue personnel). Reconciliation and additional staffing/efforts remain necessary; similar issues were reiterated in subsequent audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the: (a) Heads of the Municipal Offices to review the DTRs and accomplishment reports of the contractual and/or job order personnel under their respective offices before approving the same to ensure that pertinent supporting documents were duly accomplished; and (b) Human Resource Management Officer to carefully monitor and check the actual services rendered in the DTRs and reconcile the same with the payrolls, to avoid overpayments. We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant committed to ensure that payments to the contractual and/or job order personnel are supported with documents with all the required information duly indicated in the prescribed form, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 before processing claims, to establish the propriety, regularity and validity of the disbursements.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: Management commented that deficiencies may have been overlooked; they committed to submit lacking supporting documents and employ the recommendations to prevent recurrence. Some corrective actions were effected and prior year recommendations addressed; however, similar deficiencies were observed in CY 2024 transactions and reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: (a) BAC and its TWG to: (i) revisit the updated guidelines and provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and GPPB Resolutions to ensure that all procurement activities undertaken by the Municipality are strictly in accordance with the provisions of the updated 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; (ii) be cautious and exercise prudence in the examination of eligibility and technical documents submitted by the prospective bidders in faithful adherence to relevant provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to prevent the award of contract to ineligible contractors/suppliers; (iii) immediately submit the lacking bidding documents to the Audit Team to arrive at a decision in audit; (iv) ensure strict compliance with the provisions set forth in Rule No. 36 of the IRR of Republic Act No. 9502 and EO No. 49 regarding the procurement of drugs and medicines; and (v) strictly comply with the prescribed period of furnishing the COA Auditor copies of contracts including the documentary requirements to facilitate timely review and evaluation as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 as amended; (b) Municipal Engineer to prepare the ABC in the format prescribed under DPWH Department Order No. 197 s. 2016; (c) Municipal Accountant to (i) provide explanation on the noted deficiencies in the supporting documents; and (ii) immediately submit the lacking supporting documents in the claims for payment, and henceforth ensure that all payments are supported with complete and proper documents before processing for payment; (d) Municipal Treasurer to ensure that claimants other than the authorized representative of contractor are duly supported with SPA and/or authorization from the supplier/contractor and with proofs of identification; and (e) General Services Officer to have the odometer/speedometer repaired or replaced at once.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: The BAC and Management submitted several lacking supporting documents on August 08, 2024, and committed to submit additional documents and justifications; some documentation deficiencies remained outstanding. Some corrective actions were effected by the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant, and some payments were adjusted; however, numerous procurement and documentation deficiencies persisted and were reiterated in CY 2024 audit findings.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the: (i) LDRRM Council to prepare and submit annually an LDRRMFIP that includes under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years, to ensure the attainment of the objectives of LDRRMF in strengthening the capacity to build disaster-resilient communities and to enhance disaster preparedness at all levels; and (ii) Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from charging expenditures against the 30% QRF without the required supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: Audit recommendation had been acted upon by Management; utilization reports updated and reclassification/adjusting entries were undertaken. However, prior deficiencies such as transfer of unutilized DRRMF-CO to Trust Fund which was contrary to COA Circular requirements were identified and adjustments recommended; some JEVs and transfers were to be effected for proper monitoring.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the: (a) MPDC for the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund to spur development, provide jobs to constituents and to achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU so that the intended users/beneficiaries may enjoy the immediate benefits derived from said development projects; and (b) Engineering Office to hasten and timely prepare the complete design plans/revised design plans and other required documents to ensure that the PPAs funded under the 20% Development Fund are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: Several previously unimplemented PPAs in CY 2023 were implemented or on-going in CY 2024 (e.g., Construction of DepEd Office Building, Acquisition of 22 units service vehicle etc.). For CY 2024 a total appropriation of P111,879,427.68 (current and continuing) resulted in P54,852,388.67 disbursed (49.03% financial delivery rate). Non-implementation reasons cited included conflicts in land ownership, lack of licensed professionals for standard plans, site disputes with barangays, donor contestation, delayed approvals (e.g., GSLUP), lack of suppliers, and missing design plans and POWs. MPDC engaged a licensed engineer to prepare design plans and POWs; Management committed to realign funds if projects not implemented by Dec 2025. Some non-allowable charges to 20% DF (meals) were noted and reiterated for correction.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant exert all reasonable efforts to reconstruct the SLs pertaining to the cash accountability of the Municipal Treasurer, based on the availability of reliable records, in compliance with Sections 111(1) and 114(2) of PD No. 1445. The reconstructed SLs should be submitted to the Audit Team for verification and audit; and
b. Office of the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, review and reconcile the available cashbooks with the accounting records. A reconciliation report, along with complete and updated copies of the cashbooks, should be submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Mun. Accountant exerted efforts to reconstruct the SLs. The OIC- Municipal Treasurer undertook efforts to reconcile the records.
observations.reason: Not Implemented for applicable laws, rules, and regulations on the reconciliation of cashbooks with the accounting records. Not Implemented Same as above.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Treasurer (i) strictly observe the provisions of Section 63(F) of the LTOM on the monthly reconciliation of cashbook balances with subsidiary accounting records as this is an important control tool for cash; (ii) monitor long outstanding checks on a regular basis and notify payees concerned to return the stale checks; and (iii) furnish the Municipal Accountant with copies of relevant records, such as check registers, to ensure recording of disbursements in the proper accounting period;
b. Municipal Accountant (i) make the necessary adjustments in the books for the long outstanding/stale checks; and (ii) ensure that the recording of check disbursements is based only on issued checks for the current period, and the unclaimed checks are reported as Accounts Payable at year-end; and
c. Municipal Mayor (i) require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to immediately reconcile their records and submit a reconciliation statement thereon and take-up the necessary adjusting entries in their respective records; (ii) demand from/require the Municipal Treasurer and other accountable officers (AOs) concerned to immediately submit to the Municipal Accountant the source documents of various book reconciling items, after which, the same shall be submitted to the Audit Team for audit purposes; (iii) make representations with the bank to furnish the Municipality with copies of debit and credit memos of said reconciling items for recording in the books of accounts; and (iv) cause the account closure of dormant and unused accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Mun. Treasurer complied with the regular monitoring of long outstanding checks. The Mun. Accountant ensured that the recording of check disbursements is based only on issued checks. Management has already requested their depository banks to furnish them with the needed documents. The Municipal Mayor will require immediate reconciliation and submission of reconciliation statements.
observations.reason: The payees concerned failed to return the stale checks for adjustment in the books. Records were furnished to the Mun. Accountant for recording in the proper accounting period. Regular monitoring of long outstanding checks was carried out.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant (i) continue sending regular written notices to all AOs concerned at least quarterly to demand the settlement of their outstanding cash advances; (ii) ensure that no new cash advance is granted unless previous ones have been fully liquidated; and (iii) investigate the reasons for unreconciled cash advance balances and make appropriate adjustments in the books to rectify discrepancies; and
b. Management (i) initiate the preparation and consolidation of necessary documents in support of the request for write-off of deceased employees and those dormant unliquidated cash advances; and (ii) consider the filing of appropriate legal or administrative action against AOs who have deliberately failed to liquidate their advances after proper demand, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The Mun. Accountant complied with the regular issuance of withholding of salaries of AOs, enforcing strict internal controls over grants, and identifying the causes of unreconciled balances. The Municipal Mayor undertook the request for write-off of accounts of deceased employees and those dormant unliquidated cash advances and initiated appropriate actions.
observations.reason: Investigation is still in progress. Unreconciled balances belong to years prior to 2015 with no available supporting records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Mayor ensure the timely submission of the required documents on the grant of CF to the Audit Team and that the same is contained in a sealed envelope; and
b. Municipal Accountant (i) cause the submission of the lacking supporting documents; and (ii) ensure the completeness of documentation on the grant of CF and its confidentiality.
observations.managementAction: The officers concerned submitted their response on the said audit observation. The Municipal Accountant made a follow-up on the submission of the lacking requirements and ensured completeness of documentation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Verify the validity of dormant and unsubstantiated payables and confirm the existence of actual claimants. Legitimate claims should be settled promptly, while unresolved payables may be subject to reversal if found to lack substantiation; and
b. Investigate the reasons for negative balances and make appropriate adjustments in the books of accounts to rectify discrepancies.
observations.managementAction: The Mun. Accountant verified the validity of dormant and unsubstantiated payables. Investigation is still in progress to determine reasons for negative balances and appropriate adjustments were made where possible.
observations.reason: Investigation is still in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of all dormant fund transfers; (ii) effect adjustment in the books for the correction of identified errors/misstatements in the said account; (iii) cause the submission of liquidation reports to the source agency for all fully implemented projects, programs and activities (PPAs). We also recommended that Management (i) cause the return of unexpended balances of completed PPAs to its source agency; (ii) prioritize the completion of PPAs with delayed or postponed implementation; and (iii) consider filing a request for authority to write-off dormant fund transfers to this office following the guidelines and procedures under COA Circular No. 2023-008.
observations.managementAction: Unexpended balances of completed PPAs were already returned to their source agencies. Regular monitoring of project implementation was conducted. The Mun. Accountant conducted analysis of existence of dormant fund transfers, recorded corrections in the books, and made a follow-up on the submission of reports.
observations.reason: No action taken so far as reported in the AAPSI for auditor’s validation regarding the request for write-off; pending.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
a. Requisitioning Units of drugs and medicines ensure that their PRs are accompanied by a certification that the items being requisitioned conform with the PNF;
b. Requisitioning Units and the BAC (i) provide justification for their non-reference in the DPRI as the ceiling price in the PRs and POs, and reply on the said audit observation. (ii) ensure compliance with RA No. 9502 and DOH AO No. 2019-0040; and
c. Municipal Accountant (i) coordinate with the offices concerned relative to the submission of the lacking documentary requirements, (ii) ensure that disbursements are supported with complete documentations, and (iii) strictly observe the perpetual inventory method of accounting for inventories.
observations.managementAction: The Requisitioning Units ensured PRs are supported with the required certifications. The officers submitted their reply on the said audit observation. The Mun. Accountant made a follow-up on the submission of the lacking requirements and ensured completeness of documentation. The Mun. Accountant implemented the perpetual inventory method for “Drugs and Medicines” for proper recording and monitoring of inventories.
observations.reason: Appropriation activities were made in expense category “Drugs and Medicines.” Some supporting documents still to be completed; process ongoing.

observations.managementAction: The Personnel Selection Board has screened applicants for 12 positions which has been successfully filled up. The proposed revised organizational structure has been submitted to the Sangguniang Bayan Office for adoption. Management initiated the process of collecting the manpower/COS requirements per office and requested the CSC Palawan Field Office for an increase in allocation slots for the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Cause the immediate formulation and execution of a comprehensive and detailed catch-up plan for the full implementation of all priority development projects at the soonest possible time and consider realignment or reprogramming of the unimplemented PPAs to other feasible development projects;
b. Require the Mun. Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC), in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to re-assess the selected mode of implementation of projects in the AIP to identify the most efficient and appropriate mode for the Municipality; and
c. Ensure that the status report of government PPAs is completely provided with the required information and reconciled with the records of the offices concerned.
observations.managementAction: Management ensured the regular update on the status of project implementation for better monitoring and reporting. The MPDC and Project Monitoring Team for local development projects intensified monitoring. Management ensured the update and reconciliation of project reports with concerned offices.
observations.reason: As at December 31, 2024, 43 PPAs are still ongoing and 22 PPAs remained unimplemented. Lack of updated project reports and limited manpower involved in monitoring.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (i) ensure the balanced implementation of activities addressing the four thematic areas of DRRM by making sure that implementation of prevention and mitigation projects and ensure the timely delivery of items chargeable under the DRRM Fund; and (ii) expedite the prompt implementation and completion of DRRM projects, in coordination with the MDRRM Council and other offices concerned.
observations.managementAction: Management fully implemented procurement monitoring to ensure the timely delivery of items chargeable under the DRRM Fund. Reconciliation has been completed while unimplemented projects from CYs 2016 to 2021 have been terminated, reprogrammed, and utilized. The SAOB will be revised.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, in coordination with the members of the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) and other stakeholders, establish effective monitoring and reporting systems to oversee the progress of GAD PPAs by conducting regular review and evaluation to assess performance against objectives, identify improvement opportunities, and ensure the successful completion of GAD initiatives.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person monitored the procurement, and the person in-charge of particular GAD program made a follow-up and initiated procurement of capital outlay items. The GAD Focal Person is addressing the recommendations; strategies are in place and re-orientation will be conducted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the following:

a. Municipal Accountant

8. To adjust the entries made by reversing the previously recorded accounts. The following entries given hereunder:	
Accounts Payable   2,493,438.87
Other Payables          164,584.00                      
    Prior Period Errors      2,658,022.87		
Prior Period Errors 2,658,022.87
    Government Equity     2,658,022.87 	
	
9. Strictly follow the accrual basis of accounting in recording transactions and ensure that only those obligated covered by Obligation Request/CAFOA but delivered items and services rendered are recognized and recorded as assets/expense and payable as at year-end. For obligated and undelivered items, disclosure in the Notes to FS shall be made; and 

10. In coordination with the Municipal Treasurer/ MGSO to review the transactions and determine the payables without POs and deliveries to date and prepare a certification thereof.

b. Municipal Budget Officer to:

11. Refrain from obligating transactions on the basis only of PRs and ensure that Obligation Requests/ CAFOA are supported by POs and/or contracts; and

12. Upon receipt of the certifications from the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer/ MGSO, to revert to GF the accounts payable and other payables.

We recommended, and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

23. Recalculate the accumulated depreciation, disregarding the residual value on its computation as outlined in item no. 14 of COA Circular No. 2015-008, in order to derive the true value of annual depreciation related to the components of Local Road Networks; and

24. Adjust the affected accounts related to the above-mentioned understatement with the following suggest adjusting entries:

Debit:
Government Equity P113,880.72

Credit: 
Accumulated Depreciation - Road Networks P113,880.72

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct:

25. The Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV supported by appropriate documents to record and adjust the reconciling items in the books and ensure that reconciling items are timely adjusted to generate accurate financial reporting as at a given period.

The Municipal Treasurer to:

26. Send written notices to the payees to confirm the existence of the stale checks and advise the payees to submit the stale check for replacement following the procedure prescribed in Section 59 of Chapter 3 of the MNGAS for LGUs Volume I; and

27. Regularly monitor unclaimed checks and practice the sending of written notice to the payee/s at least one month before the check becomes stale.

28. Regularly submit a List of Stale Checks to the Municipal Accountant for proper action in the books of accounts.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct:

The BAC to:

29. Stop preparing a consolidated PR, as well as the preparation of POs, AIR, CAFOA and DVs

30. Stop preparing two different ITBs, instead adopt a strategy to ensure that similar items/nature of goods to be procured be consolidated in the ITB with reference to approve PRs or may opt to form these PRs into Lots in the ITB; and

31. Strictly conduct the procurement of goods in accordance with the RIRR of RA No. 9184. 

32. The MGSO, BAC and other department heads to come up with an efficient strategy in preparing and processing of PRs for the procurement of goods in accordance with RIRR of RA No. 9184 while ensuring that goods of similar nature or items and needed at about the same time are procured thru public bidding save for any exceptions.

We recommended, and the management agreed to direct the BAC to:

33. Implement a rigorous document verification process during post-qualification to validate the authenticity, veracity, and validity of all submitted documents. This process should be conducted using non-discretionary criteria, as outlined in the bidding documents, to ensure fairness and transparency;

34. Ensure prompt and written notification to the concerned bidder, detailing the grounds for disqualification upon determination of a bidder's failure to meet the post-qualification criteria. This notification should be issued in accordance with Section 34.5 of the RIRR to uphold transparency and accountability; and

35. Refrain from recommending to the Head of Procuring Entity for the award of a contract when there has been no proper conduct of the post-qualification process during the procurement of goods. This ensures a fair and competitive bidding process among all other bidders and adherence to the non-negotiable bidding requirements.

40. We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Budget Officer, Accountant and Treasurer to transfer the unexpended current balance of the LDDRMF in the GF to TF as at year-end and not merely as set-up of obligation, moving forward.

We recommended that management direct the Accounting Office, the General Service Office and the Assessor’s Office to coordinate with each other to reconcile the records of both offices and provide complete description of the property to reflect the true and correct balance of the Land account.

We further recommended that management identify the exact location of all the lots owned by the LGU and secure the property by putting fence. Facilitate the titling or transfer of title of the parcels of land owned by the LGU to avoid possible risk/disputes or claims of outside parties.

We recommended and Management agreed to comply with the provision of Property Number and other vital information on all government properties in accordance with COA Circular No. 80-124 dated June 18, 1980, and COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit the lacking supporting documents to strictly ensure the validity, propriety, necessity and regularity on the purchase of the two lots in the amount of P18,000,000.00.
observations.managementAction: The Management was not able to submit all the lacking supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

require the MHO and the concerned departments to henceforth furnish the Accounting Office with complete documents relative to the donated COVID-19 vaccines, medicines and other donations-in kind for proper accounting thereof; and 

perform an inventory of all the undistributed/undisposed vaccines, if there is any, and recognize as inventories in the books of accounts of the Municipality for fair presentation of account balances in the financial statements in keeping with Paragraphs 27 and 96 of IPSAS 1 and 23, respectively and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.
observations.managementAction: The MHO and the concerned departments were instructed to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: No documents submitted to the accounting office for proper recording.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Inventory Team/Committee to complete the conduct of physical count of property, plant and equipment in order to finalize the preparation of the RPCPPE pursuant to Section 66 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in Observation No. 3, Part II of this Report
observations.reason: Management was not able to complete the physical count of its PPE.

observations.managementAction: The certified list of taxpayers was not submitted to the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasury Office is still in the process of updating its record.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify those cash advances granted to those officers and employees who are already dead, resigned, no longer connected with the LGU and those whereabouts are not known to “Other Receivables” account in the total amount of P4,172,123.84;

Require the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to the officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances and demand the immediate settlement thereof; and
	
Request for write-off of dormant accounts where collection is nil following the procedures set under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant issued demand letters to the concerned employees.
observations.reason: Supporting documents for the request for write-off are not yet available.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strictly use funds for the specific purpose for which they have been appropriated pursuant to Section 336 of RA 7160 or the Local Government Code of 1991.

(a) together with the Local Development Council to engage in thorough planning to determine which PPAs should be included in the 20% Local Development Plan, with a view of preventing the need for reprogramming; (b) regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions can be introduced at the earliest time; and (c) prioritize the full implementation of all targeted priority projects for the year and ensure its timely completion.

agreed to instruct the GFPS to

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
a. conduct One-Time Cleansing of PPE account, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006; and henceforth,
b. strictly comply with Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The Asset Committee had prioritized the completion of the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account. Henceforth, reconciliation of the PPE account is still incomplete.
observations.reason: The City Government is still waiting for the completion of the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the coordinating/focal point officer to:
a. ensure immediate and proper implementation of projects funded by the transfers from SAs; 
b. coordinate with the City Accounting Office on the immediate return of any unused balance of the fund transfers to the SAs, upon verification of the status of the funds and in case of the completion of the project, pursuant to the aforecited regulations; and
c. strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 in the implementing projects funded by SAs so as to ensure that funds are utilized and liquidated in a timely manner, and that the services and benefits due to the public are not hampered.
observations.managementAction: Some of the funds for the projects funded by the transfers from SAs were used in CY 2023. Some of the unused fund transfers to the Source Agencies were returned to the Source Agencies and were properly recorded in the books per Disbursement Voucher Nos. 2023-01-0046, 2023-03-0145, 2023-08-0324 dated January 31, 2023, April 20, 2023 and August 14, 2023, respectively.
observations.reason: Funds to some projects remained unutilized due to documentary issues and difficulties in completing required documents for the Source Agencies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant, the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) and the City Treasurer to:
a. adopt a complete ATM payroll system for the City to eliminate the risk of loss aside from promoting greater convenience, transparency, and accountability; and henceforth, to register the newly hired JOs and Casual personnel with ATM cards of the ATM Payroll System; and
b. strictly observe the provisions of Section 124 of PD No. 1445 and DBM Circular No. 2008-8 dated October 23, 2008.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO has taken action to register the newly hired JOs and Casual personnel with ATM cards of the ATM Payroll System. However, only 84.29 percent of the total hired JOs and Casual personnel were registered as at year-end.
observations.reason: Newly hired workers encountered difficulties in processing the application for ATM cards by the bank and failure to comply with requirements of the ATM Payroll System.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the City GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee to:
a. ensure the optimal utilization of the City’s GAD funds; 
b. monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs to ensure maximum implementation thereof; and henceforth,
c. strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 4.1 C.1(1) of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01.
observations.managementAction: The City GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee conducted monthly assessment of GAD utilization to strengthen the implementation of GAD Fund. Some of the PPAs in the GAD Plan were hampered during the year.
observations.reason: The public spending ban hindered the implementation of GAD PPAs for CY 2023; as of December 31, 2023, 16 GAD PPAs remained unimplemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the CDRRMO to:
a. ensure full utilization of the LDRRMF for the targeted PPAs, pursuant to the City’s disaster risk reduction management plans; 
b. coordinate with the City Engineer in ensuring that all programmed infrastructure projects under the LDRRMF are monitored and completely implemented; and henceforth,
c. strictly comply with Section 21 of RA No. 10121.
observations.managementAction: For preparation of request to the Regional Director (RD) for the approval of Recommendation Tracking Sheet (RTS) for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications. The City regularly assesses the implementation and is coordinating with the City Engineer. The City Government has a low utilization of LDRRMF from continuing appropriation as at year-end.
observations.reason: Due to implementation of PPAs by the Provincial Government and National Government Agencies on their proposed site for their own PPAs and other conflicts and issues encountered during different stages of planning and procurement process which hampered the implementation of PPAs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to:
a. Coordinate with the City Cooperative and Livelihood Office, and issue demand letters to those existing organizations, and demand immediate settlement of their payables to the City; 
b. Consider and/or initiate a request for write-off for those balances which remained dormant for 10 years or more; and henceforth, 
c. Conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts, to ensure collection when these become due and demandable, in conformity with COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: The City Cooperative and Livelihood Office has issued demand letters to organizations with unsettled balances. The City is in the process of preparing the request for write-off for those dormant receivables. Regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts was conducted but the Accounting Office encountered difficulty in retrieving the necessary documents.
observations.reason: Some receivables remained dormant and the City encountered difficulty in retrieving the necessary documents for the request for write-off.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Account to:
a. Closely coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the immediate submission of the subject disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents for the unrecorded check disbursements, and subsequently draw the necessary journal entries to record the same;
b. Exert extra efforts to locate and retrieve the necessary supporting documents for the subject unrecorded debit memos, stale checks and book errors, and subsequently draw the necessary journal entries to record/adjust the same; and henceforth,
c. Ensure that all book reconciling items in the BRS are immediately recorded/adjusted in the book of accounts of the Municipality pursuant to item 3.3 of COA Circular No.96-011.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer submitted already the subject disbursement vouchers and its supporting document to the Municipal Accounting Office.
observations.reason:
Unrecorded check disbursements and credit memos were presented as reconciling items in the BRS for SEF and TF.
Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 4, Pages Nos. 44 to 45

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, OIC-Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to:
a. Reconcile the noted difference of P362,223,545.92 between the recorded balances of Due from Receivables, SEF Receivables, and Delinquent Accounts Receivable respectively.
b. Follow the guidelines and conduct a periodic reconciliation of the balances of Basic and Special Education Tax dues and RPT and SET Receivable accounts to immediately detect and rectify any discrepancies that may arise.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant recognized expense and revenue in the Trust Fund books of the Municipality for CY 2023.
The Municipal Accountant, OIC-Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor periodically reconciled their records. Period reconciliation and strict monitoring of respective records of the Accounting and Treasury Offices is being implemented.
observations.reason: No action from the Municipal Accounting Office. No result of reconciliation was submitted to the Audit Team to verify the same. Also, discrepancy still exist on CY 2022.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to:
a. Reconcile the variance of P52,07,537.76 between the Post-Closing Trial Balance and RPCPPE;
b. Conduct one-time cleansing of PPE accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to effect the same; and
c. Strictly comply with Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction:
The GSO and Municipal Accounting Office is still reconciling their record.
The GSO and Accounting Office will look for/ attend One-Time Cleansing. The Municipal Mayor directed the subject officers to conduct One-time Cleansing in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006, once they had attended the said seminar.
observations.reason: A discrepancy of P6,199,337,581.05 between the PPE balances of the Accounting and GSO were still noted during the audit of CY 2023. One-time cleansing of PPE accounts was still not availed by the Municipality as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Determine the details of the noted difference between their respective records and subsequently prepare the necessary adjustments to present the correct balances of the Cash in Bank-LCCA in the cashbooks and financial statements; and
b. Conduct periodic reconciliation of their records as required under Section 63(F) of the LTOM.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting and Municipal Treasurer Offices are still reconciling their record. Periodic reconciliation from January to July 2023 were conducted. The Municipal Treasurer will coordinate and initiate reconciliation once she received from the Municipal Accountant the BRS for the months of August to December 2023.
observations.reason: No periodic reconciliation was conducted from August to December 2023, hence discrepancy of P5,175,044.09 between accounting and treasury records was still noted as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC- Municipal Accountant to investigate the noted discrepancy between the available balance of the LDRRMF under the Special Trust Fund of the Municipality in their CY 2022 Notes to FS disclosure and the ending balance of the Trust Liability – LDRRMF account in the CY 2021 FS, and thereafter, make the necessary adjustment/corrections accordingly.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant investigated and corrected the noted discrepancy due to the errors in disclosures in Notes to FS in CY 2022.
observations.reason: No action was taken by the Municipality. Necessary documents are being processed and will be submitted to COA once all documents have been notarized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (MDRRMO) to prepare a comprehensive and integrated LDRRMFIP including the list and activities to be funded out of the previous years’ unexpended balances of the LDRRMF in accordance with the above-mentioned regulations. Likewise, prepare and submit required reports to the LDRRM Council and properly incorporate all activities in the Annual Investment Plan including those in the LDRRMFIP as approved by the LDRRM Council.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO has already incorporated LDRRM activities in the projects the LDRRMF in CY 2023.
observations.reason: The LDRRMFIP for CY 2023 still did not include the list of PPAs to be funded out of the previous years’ unexpended balances under a separate caption, contrary to rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the OIC-Provincial Accountant to verify the net differences of P(623,264.33) and P(1,665,030.52) between the recorded balances of Due from and Due to LGUs accounts of the Municipality and their reciprocal accounts in the books of the Province; and
b. Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to subsequently prepare the required adjusting journal entries to present the correct balances of the said accounts and to conduct periodic monitoring and reconciliation of subject accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the Provincial Accountant. The Municipal Accounting Office allegedly has already drawn the necessary adjusting journal entries to present the correct balances of the said accounts. Periodic reconciliation and strict monitoring of respective records of the Accounting and Treasury Offices is being observed.
observations.reason: No Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) was submitted to the Audit Team to verify the same. Discrepancies still exist as of year-end of CYs 2022 and 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC- Municipal Accountant to include information pertaining to DRRM procurement, issuance/transfer of NDRRMF and NDRRMF cash donations in the Notes to Financial Statements as required under item VII.2 of COA Circular No. 2014-002; and that the OIC-Municipal Accountant to properly account and record the procurement of goods as inventories and PPE per COA Circular No. 020-004.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Municipality. Necessary documents are being processed and will be submitted to COA once all documents have been notarized.
observations.reason: No update was given to the Audit Team in CYs 2022 and 2023. The procurement and reporting issues were not yet fully addressed.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor: a. direct the OIC- Municipal Accountant to draw journal entry vouchers to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustments and correction in the books for a fair presentation of the balance of Cash in Bank-LCCA-LBP account in the financial statements; b. augment the workforce of the Municipal Accounting Office in order to immediately locate the documents and determine the details pertaining to the unrecorded disbursements and unidentified reconciling items for proper adjustment in the Municipality’s books; and henceforth, c. strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.managementAction: The Management had reduced the reconciling items by P420,689.49, leaving an unreconciled balance of P3,583,957.35. The Acting Municipal Accountant is still in the process of identifying/locating the related documents as basis of recording the required adjustments and correction in the books. The Management is still on process of recruiting competent staff. The Management is still on process of strictly adapting with the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.reason: The Acting Municipal Accountant is still in the process of identifying/locating the related documents as basis of recording the required adjustments and correction in the books. The Management is still on process of recruiting competent applicants. Same reason as mentioned above.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to furnish the OIC-Municipal Accountant, at the start of the year a certified list of the names of the taxpayers and the corresponding amount of taxes due and collectible from them for the year to establish the correct and valid RPT and SET Receivables that can be taken up in the books of accounts. This would also provide management with accurate information needed in its decision making.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2023, the Management used the Summary of RPT of Prior Years Receivable as of January 1, 2023 as the basis of RPT/SET Receivables set-up.
observations.reason: The Management did not use the duly certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount of tax due and collectible from them for the year as the basis of RPT/SET Receivables set-up.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to retrieve the related documents/records and conduct a detailed review of the accounts and investigate/determine the causes of the unreconciled amounts; and thereafter, prepare the required adjusting journal entries in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the said accounts in the Municipality’s financial statements.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2023, the noted discrepancies rose up to P3,260,435.51 and P4,859,432.97 between the balances of the reciprocal accounts RPT Receivable as against Deferred RPT and SET Receivable as against Deferred SET, respectively. The Municipality is still in the process of reconciliation.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still on its process of reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor file the request for authority to write-off duly supported with complete relevant necessary documents to the Commission on Audit through the Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA). The request shall be supported by the following documents: a. schedule of dormant accounts by accountable officer/debtor/government entity and by account, certified by the Accountant and approved by the Head of the government entity; b. certification by the responsible officials of the entity to the effect that there are no records/documents available to validate claim; c. proof of exhaustion of all remedies to collect the receivables; and d. other justifications, like in the case of request for write-off due to loss of documents, the circumstances of the loss should be stated in the letter-request.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management. The Acting Municipal Accountant has yet to prepare the necessary documents to support their request for authority to write-off.
observations.reason: The Acting Municipal Accountant has yet to prepare the necessary documents to support their request for authority to write-off.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-GSO to: a. reconcile the net variance of P164,092,719.72 between the Detailed Statement of Financial Position and RPCPPE; b. conduct One-Time Cleansing of PPE account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006; relative to the conduct of the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account; c. prepare JEVs to effect the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account; and henceforth, d. strictly comply with Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2023, there's a net variance of P314,770,744.56 between the Detailed Statement of Financial Position and RPCPPE. The Municipality is still on its process of reconciliation of the Accounting and GSO records. The Municipality is preparing for the preliminary activities for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account.
observations.reason: No action taken by the Management. The Municipality is still on its process of reconciliation and preparing for the preliminary activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to reclassify the P4,149,670.10 semi-expandable items and effect the necessary adjustments in the books in accordance with COA Circular No. 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022.
observations.managementAction: Management stated that they will comply with the Audit Team’s audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to: a. Reconcile the difference of P13,694,071.10 noted between the Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds accounts; and b. Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to balance the two accounts and ensure the fair presentation of the Municipality’s financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management reasoned out that no supporting documents available for the previous year records and stated the need of extra effort to locate the documents needed.
observations.reason: No supporting documents available for the previous year records; need of extra effort to locate the documents needed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to withhold taxes from any payment due to suppliers/sellers pursuant to the aforecited regulations; and henceforth, remit all taxes withheld within the prescribed period to the BIR. Otherwise, any surcharges, interests and penalties that may be incurred shall be the personal liability of the municipal officials concerned who are responsible for the non-remittance of the taxes.
observations.managementAction: Management stated that they will comply with the Audit Team’s audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated our previous years’ recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Treasurer and the OIC-Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to reconcile the above subject accounts, and remit whatever balance due to the agencies concerned.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2023, tests showed the Municipality still has failed to remit in full the funds withheld due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth. Management stated that they will comply with the Audit Team’s audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Continued failure to remit in full; accounts remained unadjusted in the books as of year-end due to continued failure of the Municipal Accounting Office to determine its details.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated our previous years’ recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant to allocate enough time to verify the details of the negative (abnormal) balance in the accounts and thereafter, make the necessary adjustments to present an accurate and reliable balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management stated that they will comply with the Audit Team’s audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require immediate action from its Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) to make representation with the GSIS on the updating and/or settlement of the delinquent loan accounts totaling P2,121,666.83 of 18 municipal employees in accordance with the Revised IRR of RA No. 8291, to avoid further accumulation of penalties and surcharges by the concerned municipal employees and the unnecessary tying up of funds of the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: Management stated that they will comply with the Audit Team’s audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting Municipal Accountant and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit the lacking documentary requirements relative to the procurement of infrastructure projects in the amount of P36,980,856.93 to establish the regularity, propriety and validity of the disbursements.
observations.managementAction: Management stated that they will comply with the Audit Team’s audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the members of the Local School Board: a. Submit justification why the expenditures of P95,691.70 charged to the Special Education Fund should not be disallowed in audit; b. Refrain from allocating/utilizing the Special Education Fund to projects and activities not among those allowed charges as provided by regulations; and c. Henceforth, strictly abide with the guidelines set forth under DepEd-DBM-DILG JCs No. 1, s 2017 and 2, s 2020.
observations.managementAction: Management stated that they will comply with the Audit Team’s audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to: a. Ensure that the Collecting Officer is already covered by approved and sufficient fidelity bond before the performance of his/her duty to collect taxes for the Municipality; and b. Henceforth, strictly monitor compliance with the bonding requirements of the Municipality’s accountable officers.
observations.managementAction: Management stated that they will comply with the Audit Team’s audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to correct the malpractice of the Revenue Collecting Officer of incurring delay in the remittance of his collections.
observations.managementAction: Management stated that they will comply with the Audit Team’s audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the required remittance to the BFP of the FSIF collections not later than two working days after the transaction is made.
observations.managementAction: Management stated that they will comply with the Audit Team’s audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Chairman to comply with the submission of the PMRs to the GPPB and with the posting requirement in the Municipality’s website as required under Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in consonance with EO No. 662. s. 2007 and GPPB Circular No. 02-2020 dated May 20, 2020.
observations.managementAction: Management stated that they will comply with the Audit Team’s audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting Municipal Accountant and the BAC Secretariat to submit the lacking documentary requirements for the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects under 20 percent DF of the Municipality in the aggregate amount of P18,799,692.43 to establish the regularity, propriety and validity of the disbursements.
observations.managementAction: Management stated that they will comply with the Audit Team’s audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC and the Acting Municipal Accountant to: a. Require the concerned suppliers to post warranty securities for the procured goods already paid by the Municipality; and b. Henceforth, strictly observe the mandatory posting of warranty security to guarantee that the supplier shall perform his responsibilities pursuant to Section 62.1 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Management stated that they will comply with the Audit Team’s audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor: a. direct the Inventory Committee to expedite the completion of Municipality’s inventory of Municipality’s Local Road Networks and indicate the required details such as Road Network ID No. and description of each road component and submit thereon to the Audit Team; and b. instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-GSO to maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components and Local Road Network Property Card to substantiate the correctness of the general ledger balance for Infrastructure Assets - Road Networks account.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee prepared an inventory of Municipality’s Local Road Networks with an incomplete description of each road component. Incomplete subsidiary records for roads and road components are maintained the OIC-GSO and Acting Municipal Accountant as of year-end.
observations.reason: The Municipality had failed to expedite the completion of the inventory of Municipality’s Local Road Networks with complete details.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to immediately submit to the Audit Team all the lacking supporting documents of relative to purchase of lands to substantiate the disbursements made. Otherwise, the said transactions shall be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Accountant made a partial submission of the lacking supporting documents to the Audit Team. The Municipality is waiting for the status of report regarding Transfer Certificate of Title under the name of Municipality.
observations.reason: As of year-end the Municipality had not furnished the Audit Team the status of report regarding Transfer Certificate of Title under the name of Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor formally request from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to pass a resolution for termination of the MOA if the same is no longer implementable and for the immediate transfer the idle cash amounting to P10,031,067.33 to the active bank account of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality decided to open a checking account for the wages of the Job Order workers and allowances of Barangay Nutrition Scholars, Barangay Health Workers, Day Care Workers and street sweepers in the ensuing year. The Management had not furnished the Audit Team a letter of formal request to SB with regards to passing a resolution of the termination of MOA since the purpose was still unimplementable.
observations.reason: The Management had not furnished the Audit Team a letter of formal request to the Sangguniang Bayan with regards to passing a resolution of the termination of the MOA since the purpose was still unimplementable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. direct the Municipal Human Resource Officer (HRMO) to publish the vacancy and expedite the hiring of the mandatory positions and other prescribed positions; and b. fill out the vacant positions, as prescribed in Section 443(a) of RA No. 7160 and in accordance with existing guidelines and rules and regulations of the Civil Service Commission, for more effective administration of the said offices which in turn will benefit the Municipality as a whole.
observations.managementAction: The vacant position of the Municipality was published by the Municipal HRMO in CY 2023 but there are still unfilled plantilla position as of year-end. Management stated they are waiting for competent applicants.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2023, there were plantilla positions that remained vacant; Management is waiting for competent applicants.

observations.managementAction:
Reconciling items were already taken-up in the Accounting Ledger and Cashbook of Treasury.
Deposits totaling P95,300.00 were reflected in the general fund bank statement and not in the account of trust fund as these deposit are really for general fund. Necessary adjustments were already taken in trust fund covered by JEV# 300-23-06-015, 300-23-06-013 and 300-23-06-014.
Monthly reconciliation was made between the Accounting and Cashbook of Treasury.
observations.reason:
Reconciling items amounting to P2,044,060.55 were still unadjusted since the reported balance of CIB-LCCA in the Statement of Financial Position for period ending December 31, 2023 is the unadjusted book balance.
Discrepancies between the Accounting and Treasury records still exist as of December 31, 2023.
Reconciling Items still exist as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to:
a. Prepare the acquired reports on time and provide the assigned Supervising Auditor and/or Audit Team Leader with an updated list of all ongoing PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year, for audit validation and verification; and
b. Strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2013-004 for the timely submission of the notices of posting of PPAs signboard and the required Monthly Monitoring Report.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer had already prepared the required reports and provided the Audit Team an updated list of all ongoing PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year; best efforts were exerted to adhere with COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.reason: Due to the delays incurred, COA Circular No. 2013-004 was not yet strictly adhered.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point System to strictly monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs in accordance with the DILG endorsed GPB to ensure that the PPAs are undertaken within the target period.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Point System conducted efforts to monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs, despite concurrent function as Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator. Also, the submitted GAD AR does not reflect that PPAs were undertaken within the target period.
observations.reason: In CY 2023, the discrepancy of P301,289.00 between the GAD Fund utilization as reflected in the GAD AR and actual GAD related expenditures per accounting record indicated non-strictly implementation of GAD PPAs by the GAD Focal Person.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the Local Finance Committee to prepare the Monthly Report of Fund Utilization and Status of PPAs until the completion of the program reflecting the correct information regarding the utilization and disbursements of financial assistance as of reporting period and submit the same to the Audit Team and to other offices pursuant to item 4.4 of LBC No. 136; and
b. Instruct the concerned official to post the said reports on the Municipality’s website in order to properly disclose the related information regarding the utilization and disbursements of the said financial assistance in public.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office had already submitted all the accounts and financial reports to the Office of the Auditor. The Municipality had already required the MIS to make reports through publication and posting in LGU website and conspicuous place.
observations.reason: No update from the Office of the Auditor regarding the submission of reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit within the prescribed period all their accounts and financial reports to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to the abovementioned regulations, to facilitate timely review / post audit of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office had already submitted all the accounts and financial reports to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: Delays were still incurred in the submission of Accounts to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra efforts to conduct periodic reconciliation of their records in order to identify the cause/s of the differences between their respective records and thereafter, make the necessary adjustments to reconcile their books.
observations.managementAction: The Management had reduced the unreconciled balance by P77,106.31, leaving an unreconciled balance of P47,347.20. Reiterated in Observation No. 1, page no. 24, Part II of the Report.
No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has yet to prepare the request for write-off.

observations.managementAction:
For CY 2023, there's a net variance of P1,268,601.57 between the Detailed Statement of Financial Position and RPCPPE. Reiterated with modification in Observation No. 3, page no. 28, Part II of the Report.
No action taken by the Management.
No action taken by the Management.
No action taken by the Management.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the OIC-Provincial Accountant and Provincial Treasurer to verify the net difference of P(71,077.78), between the recorded balance of Due to LGUs account of the Municipality and its reciprocal account Due from LGUs reflected in the books of the Province;
b. Municipal Accountant to subsequently prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to present the correct balance of the said account in the Municipality’s financial statements; and
c. thereafter, conduct a periodic reconciliation of Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs accounts in the Municipality’s books with the corresponding reciprocal accounts in the Province’s books.
observations.managementAction:
As of December 31, 2023, the recorded balance of Due to LGUs account of the Municipality and its reciprocal account Due from LGUs reflected in the books still showed a net difference of P58,877.87.
No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: Same reason as mentioned above.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) to:
a. reconcile the Due to GSIS account and remit whatever balance due to the GSIS to avoid inconveniences to the concerned employees and make representation with the GSIS regarding the prior years’ unremitted balance; and
b. promptly remit in full the withheld GSIS premiums/contributions/loan amortization in compliance with the provisions of RA No. 8291.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality still has a recorded balance of P308,244.56 Due to GSIS under Trust Fund still not remitted as of year-end. No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: The balance pertains to undocumented amounts without details and dates back from CY 2003 and prior years.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the GAD Focal Person to ensure that all programs, plans and activities are properly planned, and that implementation thereof be in accordance with the approved GAD Plan and Budget to optimize the use of allocated funds, and that all issues on gender mainstreaming are duly addressed by the prepared plan, for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries; and
b. henceforth, maximize the utilization/implementation of the GAD Plan and Budget to attain its objective in accordance with Item 2.3 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by JMC No. 2016-01.
observations.managementAction:
For CY 2023, the Municipality still had unutilized funds for 20 GAD PPAs. Reiterated in Observation No. 9, page no. 44, Part II of the Report.
No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: The late approval of CY 2023 annual budget has affected the proper and immediate implementation of the GAD related activities.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the Inventory Committee to expedite the completion of the RPCPPE inventory of PPE, indicate whether the items counted are under GF, SEF or TF and submit thereon to the Audit Team; and
b. instruct the GSO to update Property Cards for all PPE accounts and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain PPE Ledger Cards for each category of assets and Real Property Ledger Cards for land to substantiate the correctness of the general ledger balances for PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: The submitted RPCPPE still does not indicate whether the items counted are under GF, SEF or TF and Updating of PCs and PPELCs is still ongoing.
observations.reason: Lack of familiarity with the rules on the necessity of identifying the inventoried items according to the funds wherein these were charged and Updating of PCs and PPELCs is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Jalajala Municipal Government adopt and implement austerity measures in its market and slaughterhouse operation in order to minimize its deficit/losses. Also, we reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Municipal officials concerned immediately consider other alternatives on how to fully utilize the market slaughterhouse to maximize its earning capacity like leasing it out to other entities for warehouse or other purposes.
observations.managementAction: The Jalajala Public Market and Slaughterhouse is still not operational as of December 31, 2023. The Management is still conducting talks/negotiations for alternative potential partners that will generate income for the Municipal Public Market.
observations.reason: Management has not implemented yet to come up with alternative measures to maximize the use of the public market structure.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. effect the immediate recording of valid reconciling items in the books of accounts to ensure the correctness of the account balances appearing in the Municipality’s financial statements at any given time; b. closely coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to cause the timely/daily submission of the disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents of the checks issued and released to the payee/suppliers; and henceforth, c. strictly adhere to the provision of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 on the preparation of the bank reconciliation statements.
observations.managementAction: The reconciling items that appear in the Bank for CY 2023 were not properly adjusted in the books of accounts. The reconciling items that appear presented in the BRS Check Issued (RCI) from the Municipal Treasurer Office.
observations.reason: Reconciling Items presented in the BRS were not properly adjusted in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical count of the entire Municipality’s PPE and correspondingly prepare and submit to the Audit Team the required report thereon; b. direct the OIC-Municipal GSO to prepare and maintain the Property Cards for all properties of the Municipality and the Property Acknowledgement Receipts for issued properties; and c. direct the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal GSO, to conduct the required reconciliation of their records with the inventory report and adjust their records, if necessary, to present the true balance of PPE accounts in the financial statements.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: d. conduct One-time Cleansing of the PPE account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020- 006; e. prepare the JEVs to effect the One-Time Cleansing; and f. strictly comply with Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction: Conducted the Physical Inventory Team had prepared and submitted the Physical Inventory Plan.
