All required accounting, adjusting and reclassification entries will be prepared, once the conduct of the physical inventories of the PPEs is complete, finalized and the reports and reconciliation of the book balances has been made, and the request to write-off of the fully depreciated PPEs, missing/non-existing and unserviceable PPEs has been approved by the Commission on Audit. The same with the recognition of the PPEs found in the station but are not included in the books. As to the titling of lands, the Municipal Assessor was instructed to prioritize the matter, including the lands where various schools are situated that needed to be donated to the Department of Education. For the PPEs that the existence, accuracy and reliability could not be ascertained due to non-inclusion in the physical inventory report, the Municipal Accountant explained that they inadvertently omitted these PPEs since these were fixed/permanent in nature, hence no need to be included in the physical counts. They commit that all the PPEs, fixed or not, will be included in the physical inventory count for the One-Time Cleansing.
observations.reason: The reconciling items and procedures for One-Time Cleansing were not fully executed; GSO/Property Office was still in the process of preparation of the required records. Physical inventory was incomplete and records not reconciled.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. coordinate with the Provincial Accountant for the reconciliation of Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs accounts so that appropriate adjustments could be made on the affected accounts for fair presentation in the Municipality’s financial statements; and b. conduct periodic reconciliation of the account balances of Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs in the Municipality’s books against their reciprocal accounts reflected in the books of the Province to avoid the accumulation of discrepancies between the two records.
observations.managementAction: The reconciling items were brought to the attention of the Provincial Accountant who is currently in the process of preparing all the supporting documents on his request for the approval of the Sangguniang Panlalawigan in writing-off old balances of Due to/From LGUs in the books of Rizal Province for proper reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts.
observations.reason: Pending approval of the Provincial Accountant’s request to write-off the old balances remaining in their books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit DVs to the Accounting Office for proper recording in the books of accounts. Otherwise, appropriate charges shall be instituted against the official/employee who is primarily responsible for the failure to render the said accounts; and, b. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the provisions of Sections 6.03 and 6.04 of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer ensured the DVs and supporting documents to the Accounting Office were transmitted to the Municipal Accounting Office on a timely manner. The Municipal Treasurer and concerned officials were instructed to ensure that all supporting documents are attached before processing for payment.
observations.reason: Delayed in the submission of DVs and supporting documents by the Municipal Treasurer; three DVs remained unsubmitted as noted in audit of municipal cash accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
a. direct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide funds for the processing of documents/requirements for TCT of land owned by the Municipality and designate a responsible official who will facilitate the transfer of title of lands and coordinate with Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor for the estimation of the amount required; and b. instruct the Municipal General Services Officer to adhere to Article 47(c) of Administrative Order No. 270 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 for ensuring that all real properties owned are titled in the name of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer had appropriated the amount required and submitted copies of Titles under the name of the Municipality to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Various lands title were yet to be transferred in the name of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to:
a. prepare the required reports on time and provide the assigned Supervising Auditor and Audit Team Leader with an updated list of all ongoing PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year, for audit validation and verification; and b. strictly comply with the cited regulations for the timely submission of the notices of posting of PPAs signboard and the required Monthly Monitoring Report.
observations.managementAction: Management ensured the required signboard were installed within ten days from the awarding of contract and issued an Executive Order designating personnel to monitor placement of signboards and timely submission of notices to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Not all required notices of posting of signboards were submitted to the Audit Team on time; backlog in posting and issues with website posting.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to: a. fully implement the GAD Plan and Budget; b. strictly monitor the implementation of the planned GAD PPAs to ensure that GAD objectives are fully attained and actual expenditures will not exceed approved budget per PPAs; and c. henceforth, strictly adhere to the guidelines of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by JMC No. 2016-01, on the localization of the magna carta for women.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted that based on their records, utilization rate was 88% and reiterated commitment to strictly adhere to gender mainstreaming policies and implement GPB for 2024.
observations.reason: Municipality used different basis for computation of utilization; Audit finding based on approved GPB amount of P19,445,200.00—management’s figures differed.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to continue exerting efforts to trace back and substantiate the old balances of the PPE accounts to provide the correct depreciation for each type of property of the Municipality and to avail the One-Time Cleansing of PPE under COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted application for One-Time Cleansing. They will prepare accounting, adjusting and reclassification entries once physical inventories are completed and request to write-off is approved by COA.
observations.reason: Pending submission of request for One-Time Cleansing; procedures for One-Time Cleansing not fully complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO-Designate to cause the immediate disposal of all the accounted unserviceable properties following the guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 92-386; to trace back and determine the details of 320 unaccounted unserviceable properties; and to draw JEVs for derecognition of disposed unserviceable properties once disposed of and record accordingly the income that could be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: One-Time Cleansing application was submitted but procedures for One-Time Cleansing were not properly executed by the Management. Ongoing actions to prepare required documents were being undertaken.
observations.reason: Pending submission of request for One-Time Cleansing; preparation of required documents not completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the responsible officials to: a. conduct a re-study and re-evaluation of the operations of the MRCF to optimize its income generating capacity; b. submit/maintain reports/records on the operations of the facility for proper monitoring; c. exhaust all available remedies to collect the long outstanding receivable from EXPERT Inc.; and d. direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory count of the properties located in the MRCF and prepare reconciliation with the accounting books, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-MENRO and MRF Supervisor were directed to re-study and re-evaluate the operations of the MRCF and regularly submit monthly reports of the operation of MRCF. Proper legal actions should be taken to collect receivable from Expert Inc. Inventory committee conducted physical count of all PPEs in MRCF.
observations.reason: No evaluation report submitted to the Audit Team; legal actions pending; some reporting and reconciliation tasks incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Service Officer (MGSO) to reconcile the discrepancy between the Detailed Statement of Financial Position and RPCPPE; b. Municipal Accountant to determine the details of the variance in their records; c. MGSO to maintain a complete record of the Municipalitys PPE; d. Municipal Accountant and MGSO conduct One-Time Cleansing of PPE account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006; and henceforth, e. strictly comply with Section 6.3 of COA Circular 2020-006.
observations.managementAction:
Management thru the OIC-GSO and the Municipal Accountant stated that they will reconstitute the Inventory Committee to facilitate the conduct and complete the physical inventory of PPE and the preparation of PIP.

Moreover, the Municipal Accountant and OIC-GSO will continue to coordinate and monitor their respective PPE records.
observations.reason: The Municipal Not focus on reconciling the discrepancies between their respective PPE records and in working together to reconcile the discrepancies; The Municipality is still in the initial phase of implementation; Not needs to reconstitute the Inventory Committee that contributes to the delay of the implementation of One-Time Cleaning of PPE account. Reiterated with modification in Observation No. 1, page no. 25, Part II of the Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned Municipal officials to properly plan the implementation of the Development Fund PPAs, ensure 100% accomplishment within their target period; direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to closely monitor and vigorously pursue the timely and full implementation of all programs and activities embodied in the 20% DF; and strictly observe the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF under Sections 3.1 and 4 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 01 dated November 4, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer discussed the predicaments encountered and came up with possible solutions for the delayed implementation of the PPAs.
observations.reason: The concerned Municipal officials encountered implementation problems and delays; monitoring was not conducted regularly and some PPAs were not prioritized and remained unimplemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to issue a demand letter to all concerned accountable officers requiring the immediate liquidation of their respective cash advances. We also reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from granting additional cash advances unless the previous ones had been already settled/liquidated; and henceforth, strictly observe the guidelines on the granting and liquidation of cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Eleven (11) demand letters were sent to concerned officers as of October 9, 2023 to enforce liquidation of their outstanding cash advances. The Municipal Accountant refrains the practice of granting additional cash advances unless the previous ones had been already settled/liquidated. Validation on the compliance of the Municipal Accountant on the guidelines disclosed that cash advances for payroll were still overlapping in CY 2023.
observations.reason: Not all accountable officers presented in Annex F of CY 2022 AAR were given demand letters to enforce liquidation of their outstanding cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to: a. exert efforts to locate and retrieve the relevant documents pertaining to the fund with no subsidiary ledger; and b. closely coordinate with the concerned National Government Agency in order to immediately return the unexpended balances of fund transfers to its source agencies as required under COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.
observations.managementAction: Unused fund of P71,500.00 was returned to the Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD) on May 26, 2023 per Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 400-2305-0080 and Official Receipt (OR) No. 9290793. The remaining balance still on the books as of December 31, 2023 amounting to P4,090,433.20 is awaiting for the formal request from the concerned agencies to return the unutilized funds.
observations.reason: Close coordination with the concerned agencies was not made for the remaining balance of P4,090,433.20.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to prepare a comprehensive and integrated LDRRMFIP in accordance with national, regional, provincial framework, and policies in close coordination with the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council and Municipal Development Council, which will be reviewed and updated annually in compliance with the above-mentioned regulation; and b. Municipal Accountant in coordination with the MDRRMO to regularly prepare the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and submit the same on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council and Local Development Council to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMFIP for Calendar Year (CY) 2023 was not prepared. The Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF was being submitted to the Audit Team on a monthly basis by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Reiterated with modifications in audit observation no. 8, page nos. 50-51.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to strictly monitor and ensure the efficient implementation of the GAD planned program, projects and activities to ensure that GAD Objectives are fully attained and actual expenditures will equate the amount of the approved budget per PPA.
observations.managementAction: The change in administration affected the implementation of the GAD PPAs which included the shortage of personnel and discrepancies in the allocated budget per PPA.
observations.reason: Reiterated with modifications in audit observation no. 12, page nos. 56-58.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to carefully analyze the transactions related to the Loans Payable account to determine if there were errors committed in recording these transactions. Thereafter, draw necessary journal entry to establish the correct balance of the said account.
observations.managementAction: Analysis of transactions is in progress. All loan payments pertaining to the Philippine Veterans Bank were accounted for.
observations.reason: Unaccounted balance still exists in the Loans Payable accounts as of December 31, 2022 amounting to P2,567,614.75.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. continue exerting efforts to locate and retrieve the relevant documents pertaining to the long outstanding receivable accounts to establish its validity and legality; b. exhaust all possible means to enforce payment from the debtor for those receivables proven to be valid; and c. request for write-off of the Dormant Receivable Accounts to the Commission on Audit, duly supported with the documentary requirements prescribed in Item 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by Management to locate and retrieve documents pertaining to the long outstanding receivable accounts. No request for write-off of the Dormant Receivable Accounts was submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Not Relevant documents pertaining to the long outstanding receivable accounts were still not yet located.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant, GSO and other concerned Offices to conduct One-Time Cleansing of PPE account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006;
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs); and henceforth, to record adjustments after completion of proper reconciliation with GSO to establish the correct balances of PPE and other affected accounts, with the presence of valid supporting documents; and
c. Strictly comply with Section 6.3 of COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1, Page Nos. 37 to 42
observations.managementAction:
a. Municipal Accountant The Municipal Services Office and other concerned Offices is currently conducting the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006; from the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with developing a Property Inventory Plan based on reconciled records from the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Inventory Committee, in preparation for the upcoming inventory.
b. Municipal Accountant The Municipal Accounting Office will prepare the necessary adjusting JEVs; and henceforth, prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the erroneous recording of the advance payments for GF and SEF.
observations.reason: Procedural guidelines in the conduct of inventory taking to implement One-Time Cleansing is yet to commence

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal GSO and the Municipal Accountant with the complete description and detailed cost and segregation of road components of the completed road projects of the Municipality for years before 2012, to facilitate the conduct of inventory, reconciliation and proper recognition of the same in the books of accounts; 
b. Municipal GSO to conduct inventory of all completed road projects of the Municipality, based on the Municipal Engineering Office thereof. An Inventory Report as of December 31, 2023 was to be submitted to this Office; and
c. Municipal Accountant, through continuous coordination between the Municipal Accountant, GSO and Engineering for the year 2023, to present the correct balances of the Local Road Networks account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The created Committee spearheaded by the Municipal Engineer in coordination with the Municipal Accountant reported the completed description and detailed cost of completed road projects of the Municipality for years before 2012, to facilitate the conduct of inventory. The Engineering and GSO are currently conducting inventory.
observations.reason: The created committee is currently conducting the inventory. The completed road projects totaling P37,837,937.46 or 25 percent was transferred to Road Networks account during year 2021. Inventory is on-going and the required documents/reports are still to be submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
 a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to verify the differences of (P422,422.55) and (P3,142,394.56) between the recorded balances of Due to and Due from LGUs accounts of the Municipality and their reciprocal accounts reflected in the books of the Province; 
 b. Municipal Accountant to subsequently prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to present the correct balances of the said accounts in the Municipality’s financial statements; and
 c. Municipal Accountant to conduct a periodic reconciliation of accounts Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs in the Municipality books with the corresponding reciprocal accounts in the books of the Province.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant made a letter dated April 12, 2023, requesting for assistance to verify the following identified balances of Due to GSIS account: • E-Card Plus to amounting to P62,971.66 since 2018 • Extra Hazardous Premiums amounting to P59,576.48 since 2012. The Municipal Accountant has been continuously coordinating with the Provincial Accountant. They already provided documents pertaining to RPT balance - Due to and Due from December 2004 amounting to P366,198.18, advance payment collected in 2015 amounting to P80.85 (GF), and advance payment collected in 2015 amounting to P115.49 under Special Education Fund (SEF). Necessary adjusting journal entries are yet to be drawn after the detailed review of the accounts.
observations.reason: Variance between the books of the Municipality and the Province of Rizal regarding the Due from/Due to LGU accounts still exists; detailed review and appropriate journal entries are pending.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
 a. Municipal Accountant to reconcile and trace the amount of P122,548.14 of Due to GSIS with the concerned government agency so that appropriate disposition on the said balances can be undertaken;
 b. Immediately return the unexpended amount of P1,040.00 to the DOH; and
 c. Initiate action to request an authority for writing-off the dormant Due to NGAs account in the amount of P49,983.82 in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant made a letter dated April 12, 2023 requesting for assistance to verify the identified balances: • E-Card Plus amounting to P62,971.66 since 2018 • Extra Hazardous Premiums amounting to P59,576.48 since 2012. The unexpended amount of P1,040.00 representing grant for municipalities during 2nd MLGP was adjusted from Due to NGAs to Trust Liabilities account thru JEV No. 300-2023-01-01 Reclassification Entry No. 1 dated January 31, 2023. Management obtained a certification from the Accountant of DOH RO IV-A that as of October 31, 2022, the Municipality has no outstanding unliquidated funds transfer. No action taken yet to request write-off for dormant Due to NGAs account.
observations.reason: No action taken yet by management to request write-off of dormant Due to NGAs; reconciliation efforts with source agency are still in progress.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
 a. Exert more efforts to locate the address of the officials/employees that are no longer connected with the Municipal Government and demand the immediate liquidation of their outstanding cash advances according to COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012. Otherwise, refer the cases to Municipal Legal Officer for appropriate legal remedies; 
 b. Initiate action to request the write-off of the dormant cash advances following procedures in COA Circular No. 2016-005; and
 c. Strictly comply with Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 in the granting, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor agreed with the audit recommendations and directed the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Administrator through memorandum to strictly adhere with the guidelines. The Municipal Accountant reported that one cash advance of the former councilor granted December 3, 2004 amounting to P3,600.00 was settled last March 2023. The OMA will continue locating officials/employees and will initiate write-off requests as appropriate.
observations.reason: The process of locating the officials/employees who are no longer connected with the Municipality is ongoing; write-off actions are yet to be initiated for dormant items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make a detailed review of the accounts and determine causes of the abnormal balances of P78,906.78 and effect necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to present the correct balances.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments are to be drawn after the detailed review of the accounts. The Municipal Accountant prepared schedules and is coordinating with concerned offices to trace and verify balances.
observations.reason: Detailed review and adjustments are pending; reconciliation in progress.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
 a. Immediately remit the balances due to the agencies to avoid incurrence of penalties, surcharges and inconveniences to the employees; and
 b. Strictly remit in full the withheld taxes, premiums and contributions and loan repayments in accordance with the regulations.
observations.managementAction: The OMA said they consistently remitted the amounts; 99 percent of taxes withheld due for CY 2022 were remitted as of date. Management will further review forwarded balances and remit appropriate amounts due.
observations.reason: Some withheld amounts remained unremitted as of cut-off; reconciliation and remittance actions are ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the HRMO to immediately fill up remaining mandatory vacant positions at the LDRRMO to ensure effective implementation of LDRRMF-funded PPAs pursuant to Section 12(b) of RA No. 10121.
observations.managementAction: No action taken yet; Municipal Mayor instructed HRMO to fill up mandatory positions. HRMO indicated steps to recruit for required positions.
observations.reason: Staffing still not fully in compliance; recruitment and filling of plantilla positions pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balances of the nonexistent/closed bank accounts in the books of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office still conducting reconciliation of account. Litigation regarding issuance of double checks, strict and careful examination and verification is required to draw the necessary adjustments to bring the account to its correct balance.
observations.reason: Due to the account’s sensitivity, as the strict and careful examination and verification is required to draw the necessary adjustments to bring the account to its correct balance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and OIC-General Services Officer to; a. reconcile the variance of P964,926,490.50 between the Post-Closing Trial Balance and RCPPE; b. conduct one-time cleansing of PPE accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to effect the same; and c. strictly comply with Section 6.3 of COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Government Service Office (GSO) is now scheduling the one-time cleansing in CY 2024.
observations.reason:
Not One-time cleansing is not yet conducted in CY 2023. The plan for the intended on-time cleansing in CY 2023 did not materialize due to alleged heavy municipal events of Hamaka Festival, Provincial Sports Meet, and other activities. Not One-time cleansing is not yet conducted, hence Section 6.3 of COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 is not yet complied.
Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1 , Page Nos. 41 to 43.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to: a. determine the details of the noted difference of P38,314,813.26 between their respective records, and subsequently prepare the necessary adjustments to present the correct balances of the Cash in Bank – LCCA in the cashbooks and in the financial statement; and b. conduct periodic reconciliation of their records as required under Section 63(F) of the LTOM.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer giving their best efforts to reconcile the difference as shown in the partial Reconciliation Report submitted on May 4, 2023.
observations.reason: Not reconciled due to the absence of supporting documents to validate the balance thereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal officials and employees with outstanding cash advances to immediately liquidated/settle their accountabilities, if warranted, impose the withholding of salaries of separated/deceased from the Municipality. Otherwise, the same shall be disallowed in audit; and b. Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the guidelines on the granting and liquidation of cash advance pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Best efforts to remind AOs and demand unliquidated cash advances were conducted by the Municipality. Not Accountable Officers with aging more than 10 years were not withheld. Also, the Municipality opted not to withhold salaries of those AO with cash advances aging less than 30 days to less than one year, but were constantly reminded instead.
observations.reason:
As of December 31, 2023, P55,841,828.83 cash advances still remained unliquidated. Moreover, in CY 2023, additional cash advances were also granted to two SDO despite the non-liquidation of their previous cash advances.
Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 6, Page Nos. 52 to 53.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, after analyzing and determining the payables without valid documentation and those that have been outstanding for two years or more, to request for an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan for the reversion of the same. Thereafter, make the necessary journal entry to revert the said outstanding obligations to Government Equity so that the Municipality can use the available funds for other priority projects to provide better services to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant confirmed that subject Accounts Payable were already due for reversal as no valid claim has been filed, administrative or judicial, to enforce payment. The Municipal Accountant failed to request for an authority with the Sangguniang Bayan for the reversion of the Accounts Payable. Thereafter, make the necessary journal entry to revert the said outstanding obligations to Government Equity so that the Municipality can use the available funds for other priority projects to provide better services to its constituents.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 8, Page Nos. 54 to 55.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the OIC-Municipal Engineer to meticulously and judiciously plan all infrastructure projects of the Municipality, comply with all the necessary requirements and coordinate with stakeholders to resolve any issues that may arise, prior to the start of the procurement activities: -Delay in the procurement process/commencement of project due to no definite timeline of implementation in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP); -Overlapping of projects; -Projects were found to be not feasible upon reassessment or there were technical problems on site; and -Issues were encountered in the preparation and approval of design or program of works.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality planned prudently government projects. In CY 2023, the Municipality planned projects; however there were admittedly challenges or issues during the implementation. -Delay in the procurement process/commencement of project due to no definite timeline of implementation in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP); -Overlapping of projects; -Projects were found to be not feasible upon reassessment or there were technical problems on site; and -Issues were encountered in the preparation and approval of design or program of works.
observations.reason:
In CY 2023, the Municipal implementation of Infrastructure Project was due to: -Delay in the procurement process/commencement of project due to no definite timeline of implementation in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP); -Overlapping of projects; -Projects were found to be not feasible upon reassessment or there were technical problems on site; and -Issues were encountered in the preparation and approval of design or program of works.
Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 12, Page Nos. 60 to 62.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to: a. ensure the optimum utilization of the 20% DF thru efficient implementation of all priority development projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic, and environmental development of the Municipality; and b. properly plan the timely procurement and implementation of all identified priority development projects under the 20% DF to guarantee the prompt accomplishment of the same.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality took some initial steps to prudently plan government projects.
observations.reason: In CY 2023, the Municipality was still unable to fully implement 14 out of 57 projects funded under 20 percent Development Fund (DF) with a total estimated cost of P70,284,040.34 as at year-end. No definite timeline of implementation in the AIP.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. make a written request from the bank for the copies of debit/credit memo and exert extra effort to retrieve other supporting documents and search for documents needed for other reconciling items and subsequently draw journal entries to record all valid reconciling items for the financial statements; and b. strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.managementAction: Written requests for copies of debit/credit memo to Land Bank of the Philippines were made however, despite written request, efforts found futile. An investigation was conducted but no documents were found. An update: reconciling items with net amount of P1,090,612.98 reflected in the BRS of bank accounts under SEF and Trust Fund were still unadjusted in the books of account of the Municipality as at year-end. Moreover, BRS for bank account under General Fund were unsubmitted within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 3, Page Nos. 45 to 48.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated ours prior years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. person/s responsible for the issuance of the checks to produce the disbursement vouchers pertaining to the subject transactions to ascertain the details and the amount due from debtors in order to prove the accuracy of the year-end balance of Other Receivable account; b. concerned official to take appropriate administrative/legal actions against the officials responsible for the non-submission or loss of the subject disbursement vouchers; and c. Municipal Legal Officer continuously monitor the case filed by the Municipality in the issue of the irregular check disbursements and demand settlement or payment for the concerned employee responsible thereof.
observations.managementAction: An investigation was already conducted by the previous administration on this matter which led to the filing of case against erring employees. A malversation case has already been filed by the Municipality against those found responsible for the non-submission or loss of the disbursement vouchers. An update regarding the status of case has already been forwarded to the Office of the Auditor on February 9, 2023. The Management committed to closely monitor the status of the case. Settlement or payment from responsible employees were not yet received by the Municipality as of December 31, 2023.
observations.reason: Only a part of the total amount is subject to the disbursement vouchers. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 3, Page Nos. 45 to 48.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor: d. submit a request for relief from property accountability and all the necessary documents for the properties razed by fire to the Audit Team to facilitate approval thereof, so that the cost of the said property will be dropped from the books of accounts; and e. concerned officials to furnish the Municipal Accountant the necessary documents needed for the derecognition of the demolished old municipal building in the books of accounts. Further, make the necessary journal entries to derecognize the demolished old municipal building.
observations.managementAction: Management is still in the process of gathering the original documents related to the properties razed by fire to support the request for the relief of property accountability. No request for relief from property accountability was received by the Audit Team as of December 31, 2023. The Municipal Accountant and OIC-General Service Office are now scheduling the one-time cleansing in CY 2024. The demolition permit and Appraisal Review Report related to the old municipal building were not submitted to the Audit Team. The Municipality planned to submit the necessary documents but as of December 31, 2023 the documents were not enough for the Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary journal entries to derecognize the old municipal building.
observations.reason: No request for relief from property accountability was received by the Audit Team as of December 31, 2023. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 4, Page No. 39.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make a representation with the concerned agencies to formally inquire whether there were unremitted balances in their records for 2014 and prior years. In case the subject Municipality has no unremitted balance for the said period, request for a certification of no-arrears; and subsequently request for approval from Sangguniang Bayan for the reversion of the said Inter-Agency Payables accounts to the Unappropriated Surplus of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: A cleanup of the Due to BIR account was already implemented by the Municipality in CY 2022. The Municipal Accountant confirmed that the Due to GSIS, Due to Pag-IBIG, and Due to PhilHealth accounts were subject to clean-up; remittances were coordinated and some were implemented.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 16, Page Nos. 70 to 72.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. conduct tax campaign initiatives to boost the collection of Real Property Tax and other taxes imposed by the Municipality; and to identify areas for improvement.
b. post notice of delinquency in places prescribed by Section 254 of RA No. 7160 and remain posted a considerable number of times so as not to defeat the purpose of the law to inform public of such tax delinquency.
observations.managementAction: The Citizen’s Charter of the Municipality was reviewed including the tax payment process as of December 31, 2022 has increased to Additional personnel was assigned handling year-round issuance of notice of tax delinquencies and tax campaign. Notice of delinquencies were being posted in all nine (9) barangays and entrance of the Municipal Hall.
observations.reason: From P65,950,499.64 increased to P69,096,023.20 Additional personnel was assigned handling year-round issuance of notice of tax delinquencies and tax campaign. Notice of delinquencies were being posted in all nine (9) barangays and entrance of the Municipal Hall. Fully Implemented as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the PPE including the Road Network System to visually check the existence and condition of the properties of the Municipality. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Office Head to request from Engineering Office detailed cost of Local Road Networks and other related information to be included in the RPCRNS.
observations.managementAction: The Property/Supply officer closely coordinated with the Municipal Engineering Office for the complete Physical Count of Local Road Network as well as the detailed cost of the Local Road Network System and RPCRNS. Notwithstanding, no inventory of Road Network System and detailed cost of the Local Road Network were submitted as of December 31, 2023, hence reiterated with modifications in Observation.
observations.reason: No inventory of Road Network System and detailed cost of the Local Road Network were submitted as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to communicate with the bank and obtain bank statements from year 1997 that could provide additional information on the investigation of the unreconciled balance and ultimately determine the cause of the unreconciled balance.
observations.managementAction: Efforts have been exerted by Management in the past but still they investigated the unaccounted reconciling items. The reconciliation has been made for the balances as of June 30 and September 21, 2023. Ultimately, the cause of the unreconciled balance is still being determined.
observations.reason: Unreconciled balance still being investigated.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated that the Municipal Officials concerned obtain the approval of COA for the sale and submit the required documents, to facilitate the review and determination of the validity/propriety of the sale.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management. Still retrieving the required document relative to the sale.
observations.reason: Management still retrieving the required documents relative to the sale.

observations.recommendation:
1.12 We recommended that Management require the:
MSWDO to furnish the accounting office of any donation received from other agencies;
Inventory Committee to submit Report of Physical Count of Inventories as required under Section 124 of Volume I of the NGAS Manual; and
MSWDO/General Services Officer/ Officers in charge for drugs and medicines, medical, dental and laboratory supplies to record the issuances based on the Requisition and Issue Slip and to provide the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued and Requisition Issue Slip to the Accounting Office to be used as basis in recording issuances of supplies and materials to the end users.

1.13 We also recommended that Management require the Municipal Accounting Office to observe the accounting policy of proper reporting and recording procedures.

We recommended that the Management:
refrain from granting additional cash advances to Accountable Officer unless the previous ones granted were fully liquidated;
demand the AO to liquidate all her cash advances with complete supporting documents after completion of the purpose; and
consider the withholding of AO’s salary until such time that the cash advances are fully settled.

We recommended that management require the Bid and Awards Committee to conduct mandatory review on the terms, conditions, specifications and cost estimates for the procurement of IT equipment through Negotiated Procurement under Two-Failed biddings and adhere with the guidelines and procedures of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 in the procurement of goods and services using the alternative mode of procurement.

We recommended and management agreed to:
strictly monitor the implementation of its infrastructure projects so that standards and specifications are strictly followed;
direct the engineering office to notify the concerned contractors to rectify and/or repair the noted deficiencies to avoid further deterioration that may render the projects useless; and
Consider giving erring contractors stern warning on the unsatisfactory quality of goods/services as this may be a ground for blacklisting.

We recommended that:
Management strictly implements the policy that once the books of the agency are already closed, the Municipal Budget Office should no longer accommodate requests for obligations. This is to facilitate the prompt preparation of year-end reports;
The Municipal Accountant transfers the unexpended balance of the MDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund under the Trust Fund Books; and
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to reconcile the utilization reports prepared by their respective offices in order to capture the accurate balance that should be transferred to the Special Trust Fund.

We recommended and management agreed to:
Enforce the LGU’s policy guidelines in assessing the necessity of hiring job order employees taking into consideration the reasonable number needed by the different offices and the appropriateness of work to be assigned;
Require the HRMO to request from the office where the job order is to be assigned a list of duties and responsibilities to be incorporated in their contract/assignment or deployment order to prevent idleness or overlapping of workload with the regular employees; and
Comply with the daily minimum wage rate prescribed by the RTWPB-CAR as a model in enforcing labor rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management:
adopt electronic payroll system through the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) of Government Banks for the payment of wages of JO workers and other employee benefits; and
instruct the Human Resource Management Office to advise all JO workers to prepare and submit the necessary requirements to include them in the automated payroll system of the LGU.

We recommended and management agreed that the municipal accountant properly compute the withholding taxes and include compensation and other benefits that are taxable in compliance with existing Rules and Regulations of the BIR.
We recommended that the LCE continuously implement GAD PPAs as shown in the GPB.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to: (a) evaluate the recording of the utilization of funds received from NGAs and the recording of the receipt and utilization of shares from tobacco excise taxes; (b) record the necessary adjusting entries ;and, (c) moving forward, ensure the proper recording of financial transactions in the books, for the fair presentation of the Due to NGAs and Government Equity accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant and GSO to complete and update the PPELC and PC, respectively, in compliance with the aforementioned provisions or, due to the time-intensive nature of this process, designate personnel from respective offices to focus exclusively on the task, to facilitate the reconciliation of both records and ensure fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that Management:
through the concerned offices, after updating the PPELC and PC, immediately conduct the reconciliation of records to identify the cause of the discrepancies between the balances of the records being maintained by the Accounting Office and the GSO or Inventory Committee;
instruct the Inventory Committee to immediately complete the physical count of all PPE of the Municipality and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the Audit Team; and
study and consider the applicability of COA Circular No. 2020-006 which provides the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of properties for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances, to come-up with a complete RPCPPE that will help both the Accounting Office and the GSO in updating and reconciling their records; thus, establishing the fair presentation of the PPE account in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management:
exert best effort to locate pertinent documents to substantiate the dormant accounts and assess collectability of the dormant accounts;
if collection is no longer feasible, undertake the appropriate actions for the proper disposition of these accounts, following the procedures outlined in COA Circular 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023; and
consider the recognition of impairment losses for those dormant accounts with no potential collection or recovery pursuant to Item 7.3 of COA Circular No.  2016-005.

1.22. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to (a)
cause the reconstitution of the Inventory Committee; (b) instruct the Inventory
Committee to (i) conduct physical inventory of all PPE of the Municipality; (ii)
submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team on or before January 31 of each year;
and (iii) reconcile the inventory reports with the accounting records; (c) Acting
Municipal Treasurer to (i) maintain Property Cards for the proper control and
monitoring of the LGU’s property and equipment; (ii) ensure that all vehicles in
active use are registered with the LTO and covered with property insurance; and
(iii) submit to the Audit Team copies of OR/CR of all motor vehicles and heavy
equipment for verification of its ownership; (d) Municipal Accountant and
Acting Municipal Treasurer to (i) consider adopting the one-time cleansing
option for PPE accounts; and (ii) in coordination with the accountable officers,
prepare IIRUP to report all unserviceable properties; and (e) Disposal
Committee to facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable PPEs in accordance
with the prescribed guidelines and procedures.

2.16. We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) Municipal
Engineer to (i) submit the Status Report of Completion, advance copies of Final
Inspection Report, Certificates of Completion and Acceptance to the Municipal
Accountant to facilitate the recording of the transfer of completed projects to
appropriate asset accounts; and (ii) report the status of projects in the Quarterly
Report on Government PPAs based on physical accomplishments; and (b)
Municipal Accountant to use Final Inspection Report, Certificates of Completion
and Acceptance as basis in recording the transfer of completed projects from CIP
to proper asset accounts for unpaid completed projects.

3.12. We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to return the unutilized funds to the
respective source agencies together with the requisite report of disbursement in
observance of COA Circular No. 94-013; and (b) Municipal Accountant to draw
journal entry to correct the year-end balances of Due to GOCCs and Due to
NGAs accounts.

4.18. We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) and BAC Secretariat to (i) ensure compliance with the
required notifications and posting requirements and other pertinent provisions
of R.A. No. 9184 and its RIRR in all procurements; (ii) henceforth submit proof
or screenshots of any error that may prevent the uploading in the PhilGEPS
website of upcoming procurement activities; and (iii) cause the submission of a
DOLE-approved construction and safety health programs and PERT/CPM of
winning bidder in every infrastructure project; and (b) Local Finance
Committee to submit Certification and evidence of posting of LGSF-SBDP
quarterly reports on its website and in three conspicuous places pertaining to (i)
the three completed projects; and (ii) any relatedly-funded projects in the
future.

5.14. We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) Municipal
Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer to (i) regularly
monitor/supervise project implementation to allow timely interventions on
concerns that may arise therefrom; (ii) expedite the installation of transformer
and water system in the Super Health Center so that intended beneficiaries can
immediately benefit therefrom; (b) optimally utilized 20% DF within the budget
year; (c) BAC to submit to the Audit Team the bidding documents that form
part of the contract for all bidded projects; and (d) Municipal Accountant to
submit all noted procurement-related documents required in COA Circular No.
2012-001 and R.A. No. 9184 and its RIRR to support the regularity of the
payments made.

6.25. We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) LDRRM Officer
to (i) in coordination with the Municipal Engineer and other officials concerned,
maximize the use of LDRRMF through facilitated implementation of the
programmed PPAs within the budget year; (ii) expedite the implementation of
the two projects that had remained unimplemented in CY 2023; (iii) submit the
monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to DILG-LGOO as
required by Section 5.1.6 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and (b) Municipal
Accountant to submit/attach in the DVs copies of APP and ITR of winning
bidders as required in COA Circular No. 2012-001 and R.A. No. 9184 and its
RIRR to support the regularity of the payments made.

7.8 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the BAC and BAC
Secretariat to (a) ensure compliance with the required notifications and posting
requirements and other pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its RIRR in all
procurements; and (b) henceforth submit proof or screenshots of any error that
may prevent the uploading in the PhilGEPS website of upcoming procurement
activities.

8.26. We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the MENRO to (a) assess
the feasibility of implementing the planned PPAs mentioned in the
Municipality’s Ten-Year SWMP, otherwise consider implementing other
programs that will improve further the waste reduction, segregation and
diversion processes; (b) in coordination with MSWMB, review and update every
year the plan as required by Section 16 of R.A. 9003; (c) in coordination with
Barangay Officials, consider the inclusion of composting operation in every
Barangay MRF and ensure that they are fully operating and functioning to help
in the diversion of biodegradable wastes as required in Section 1, Rule XI of the
IRR of R.A. No. 9003; and (d) expedite the operation of rotary composter and
mixer to help in waste reduction and diversion.

9.23. We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) BPLO to (i)
enforce the submission of business taxpayers’ certified ITR copy on or before
April 30 of each year as required in Section 2D.04 of the Revenue Code; (ii)
impose fines on businesses that are operating but failed to secure or renew their
business permits in accordance with Section 9.01 of the same Code; and (iii)
henceforth ensure that all businesses operating in the locality have valid business
permits; and (b) Municipal Treasurer to make representation with the proper
office for the hiring of additional personnel or assignment of staff who will be
trained and assigned in the examination of books of accounts of businesses.

10.20. We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the GFPS through the
GAD Focal Person to (a) administer the HGDG test when attributing costs to
GAD PPAs; (b) seek expert assistance and arrange HGDG training to
adequately equip the GFPS and other stakeholders involved in GAD planning;
(c) create a multi-year GAD Agenda as basis for the annual GAD Plans and
Budget by using the templates provided in PCW Memorandum Circular No.
2018-04 dated September 19, 2018; (d) timely submit to the Audit Team the
GAD Accomplishment Report in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-001;
and (e) review the expenses reported in the GAD Accomplishment Report prior
to its submission to DILG to identify any inconsistencies or occurrences of
duplicated charges.

11.5. We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continuously comply with the BIR rules
and regulations on the withholding and timely remittance of taxes on
compensation, value added taxes, percentage taxes, and expanded taxes from
government money payments.

12.4. We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer sustain the timely withholding and
remittance of GSIS premiums and loan amortizations of members in accordance
with Section 6(b) of R.A. No. 8291.

13.3. We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue ensuring the timely withholding
and remittance of PhilHealth premiums from members, together with the
government’s share, in accordance with R.A. No. 7875 as amended by R.A. Nos.
9241 and 10606 or the National Health Insurance Act of 2013.

14.4. We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue the timely withholding and
remittance to HDMF of the members’ contributions and loan amortization,
together with the LGU’s share, in accordance with R.A. No. 9679.

We recommended, and the City Mayor agreed to require the City Accountant to
recompute the year-end balance of all inventories using the moving average method
and draw Journal Entry Voucher to record the understatement/s to correct the
forwarded beginning balance in CY 2024.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to:
a. enforce the immediate settlement of CAs of officers and employees who are still
employed with the City Government; and
b. request for write-off/derecognition from the books all dormant CAs following the
procedures prescribed in COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.

We also recommended to enjoin all officers and employees to strictly follow the
timelines of liquidation of CAs.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
a. make the necessary journal entry to revert the said obligations without valid
claims totaling P1,928,502.63 to Government Equity; and henceforth
b. ensure that accruals of expenses are supported with valid supporting
documents pursuant to Section 46 of PD No. 1177.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Accountant to include the FS of PRDP in the preparation of the
Consolidated FS of the Municipality to properly present the account balances
pertaining to this fund;

b. Municipal Accountant to properly record the bank accounts for Mun. Gov't of Rizal
Laguna - For Professional Fee and Mun. Gov't of Rizal Laguna - For HCI Charges
by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher with a debit to CIB account and credit to Trust
Liabilities account by the same amount of P150,854.53, in accordance with
Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2015-009 to ensure that accounts are
accurately presented in the financial statements; and

c. Municipal Treasurer to evaluate the necessity of the dormant accounts and if
proven not anymore needed, secure SB resolution authorizing the closing of
dormant accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the misclassification of the PPE
items with the following proposed adjusting journal entry:

Account Title Debit Credit
Office Equipment P 747,000.00
Construction and Heavy
Equipment 10,727,233.00
Accumulated Depreciation –
Disaster Response and
Rescue Equipment 141,930.00
Disaster Response and Rescue
Equipment P 747,000.00
Other Machinery and Equipment 9,999,000.00
Motor Vehicles 728,233.00
Accumulated Depreciation – Office
Equipment 141,930.00
Total P11,616,163.00 P11,616,163.00

We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant
to ensure the recording of the Municipality’s transactions in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2015-009 for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial
statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
adjust the accounts by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher by debiting Due to NGAs
account by P12,760.23, Due to GOCCs account by P230,909.40 and Due from
Officers and Employees by P1,046.75 and crediting Trust Liabilities account by
P12,760.23, and Prior Year Adjustment account by P231,956.15 to reclassify the
misclassified accounts, and henceforth, strictly comply with COA Circular No.
2015-009 to ensure that accounts are accurately presented in the financial
statements.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
properly record the RPT/SET collections and recognize the income from RPT/SET
using the sample journal entries below, in compliance with Section 22, Chapter 3
of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and COA Circular No. 2015-009:

Account Title Debit Credit
Cash – Local Treasury XX
Discount on Real Property Tax – Basic XX
Real Property Tax – Basic Receivables XX
To record the receipt of prompt payment for RPT

Deferred Real Property Tax – Basic XX
Real Property Tax – Basic XX
To recognize the income from RPT

Cash – Local Treasury XX
Discount on Special Education Tax XX
Special Education Tax Receivables XX
To record the receipt of prompt payment for SET

Deferred Special Education Tax XX
Special Education Tax XX
To recognize the income from SET

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
maintain complete subsidiary ledgers and supporting schedules as basis in the
disclosing of the required information in the NTFS, in accordance with Paragraph
127(c) of the IPSAS I, Section 9.2 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 and Item
5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to assist the users in understanding the
financial statements and to provide them with the additional information value.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local Finance Committee to:

a. Request from the lending financial institution copies of the amortization of all
existing loans of the Municipality to determine the schedule and amount of loan
repayment; and

b. Strictly implement a plan on allocating the available cash by closely
monitoring the utilization of funds, impose cost-cutting measures, determine the
amount which has reasonably assurance of collectivity and intensify collection to
increase the Municipality’s cash balance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Treasurer to request copies of the bank’s debit memos and other
reconciling items in the amount of P1,994,177.06 and submit the same to the
Municipal Accountant as basis for the recording of adjusting entries to correct the
CIB – LCCA, pursuant to Sections 3.2 and 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011; and

b. Municipal Accountant to submit the duplicate and quadruplicate copies of the
BRS including the paid checks, original copies of debit/credit memos to the
Audit Team for verification within 10 days for receipt after the end of each month,
as prescribed under Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of granting additional or new cash
advances to the AOs with outstanding cash advances unless their previous cash
advances are fully liquidated and propose doable and effective measures to
resolve delays in liquidation; otherwise, designate additional AO who can handle
the cash advances of the Municipality.

We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to
ensure the recording of the Municipality’s transactions, in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2015-009 for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial
statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

a. MGSO to exert extra effort to locate the details and supporting documents and
determine the person/s accountable for the missing PPE items and prepare the
List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs for items described as "non-existing" or
"missing" in the RPCPPE, as required under Sections 6.3.1 and 7.3 of COA Circular
No. 2020-006, otherwise, comply the procedures provided under Section 7.11 of the
same Circular if accountability could not be established despite exhaustion of all
diligent efforts;

b. Municipal Accountant to provide proper disclosures on the Non-Existing/Missing
PPEs, in accordance with Section 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2020-006; and

c. MGSO, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant and Inventory Committee,
to strictly comply with the disposition, derecognition and disposal procedures of
all missing and unserviceable PPEs, as required under COA Circular No. 2020-
006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. MGSO to prepare the corresponding IIRUP by filling-out the inventory portion
and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor and to the Appraisal and Disposal
Committee to initiate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties, in
accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445 and the rules and procedures provided
in COA Circular No. 92-386 and COA-DBM JC No. 2024-01;

b. Appraisal and Disposal Committee to conduct an appraisal of the
unserviceable properties and submit the appraisal report to the Office of the
Auditor; and

c. Municipal Accountant to derecognize the unserviceable properties upon the
completion of the disposal.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the HRMO, in coordination
with the Municipal Budget Officer, to review the organizational structure and
staffing pattern of the Municipality taking into consideration its service
requirements and financial capability, subject to the minimum standards and
guidelines prescribed by the Civil Service Commission.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to:

a. Instruct all AOs to prepare the monthly RAAF and submit the same for
consolidation and as basis on the preparation of CRAAF and submit the CRAAF to
the Audit Team as prescribed under Sections to Sections 50 and 51 of the MNGAS
for LGUs, Volume II.

b. Re-check the entries and re-submitt to the Audit Team the CRAAF for the months
with noted discrepancies for further evaluation; and

c. Henceforth, judiciously and accurately prepare the CRAAF at the end of the month
as supported by the monthly RAAF of each AOs that handles different AFs and
submit the same to the Audit Team monthly for verification to ensure the accurate
reporting of the quantity of AFs received, issued or cancelled as of the end of the
month.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the BAC and other concerned
personnel to immediately submit the lacking documents to support the
infrastructure contract for evaluation and review; and henceforth, furnish the Audit
Team copy of perfected contracts together with its supporting documents within five
working days for technical review and evaluation, in compliance with COA Circular
No. 2009-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPMIU Head and other
concerned officials to:

a. Submit to the Audit Team the documents pertaining to the first bidding
conducted and communications with the DA PRDP PSO relative to the approval of
the rebidding for further evaluation; and

b. Closely monitor the implementation of the project and henceforth, comply with
the IMA until completion of the project for the delivery of the benefits to the
intended recipients.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, at the earliest and practicable period,
initiate the one-time cleansing of PPE balances of the Municipality by following
the guidelines and procedures on the six activities prescribed under COA Circular
No. 2020-006, to eliminate the noted discrepancy amounting to P6,199,337,581.05
as of December 31, 2023 to come up with reliable PPE balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
support the balances of PPE and Accumulated Depreciation with reliable/correct
lapsing schedules in consonance with Section 111 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to adjust the recorded PPE with acquisition
cost below the capitalization threshold of P50,000.00 by debiting Prior Period
Adjustment and corresponding Accumulated Depreciation of PPE accounts in the
