b. OIC-Municipal Accountant to draw a journal entry to adjust the Cash Local Treasury account with net discrepancy of P415,204.62, duly supported by certified reconciliation statements of Accounting and Treasury’s records; and henceforth,
c. Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with Section 181(c) of GAAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction:
OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer agreed to reconcile the books before closing every month.
Prepared the Bank Reconciliation Statement in adherence to the Circular.
observations.reason: Reconciliation for December 2023 was not prepared. Adjustments were taken-up in the books supported with JEV#100-23-01-01033S but reconciliation between Treasury and Accounting for year-end remained not fully completed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
a. coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the discrepancy between the balances of RPT and SET Receivables and the total amount of tax delinquencies;
b. prepare the necessary adjustments in the books to have the correct and reliable reported balance of the RPT and SET Receivables accounts; and henceforth,
c. strictly comply with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS I.
observations.managementAction: Coordination between the Accounting and Treasury was followed. Prepared JEV for adjustment found in the books.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of Treasury and Accounting records is still ongoing and not yet completed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
a. coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to establish the details of the difference of P1,223,013.17 and P814,176.08 between the recorded balances of the Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs accounts, respectively, per Municipality and Province’s records;
b. prepare the necessary adjusting entries to present the correct balance of the said accounts in the financial statements of the Municipality; and henceforth,
c. strictly comply with Sections 111 and 112 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Agreed to settle the differences of the recorded balances of the Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs accounts, respectively, per Municipality and Province’s records; Some adjustments were taken-up and preparing the necessary adjusting entries. Coordinated with the province to preparing the adjusting entries.
observations.reason: Reconciliation with the Province is still in process and reconciling and preparing the necessary adjusting entries is not yet completed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide funds for the processing of documents/requirements for TCT of land owned by the Municipality and designate a responsible official who will facilitate the titling of the lot; and henceforth,
b. Instruct the OIC-Municipal General Services Officer to strictly adhere to Article 47(c) of Administrative Order No. 270 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 for ensuring that all real properties owned are titled in the name of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
John Paolo Moran of MPDO was assigned and designated as the responsible person for titling of lot.
OIC-Municipal GSO is now strictly ensuring that all owned properties are titled in the name of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Municipal Engineer to provide the OIC-Municipal GSO and the OIC-Municipal Accountant with a complete description and detailed cost segregation of road components for completed road projects of the Municipality for years before 2015, to facilitate the conduct of inventory, reconciliation and proper recognition of the same in the books of accounts;
b. OIC-Municipal GSO to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to prepare the Report on the Local Road Network to facilitate the preparation of a proper inventory of the Municipality’s network system; and
c. OIC-Municipal Accountant, upon the receipt of the reports of GSO to the necessary required reports from the Municipal General Services and Engineering Offices, to make the necessary adjustments to recognize the cost of the previous years’ Local Road Network for the correct presentation of the balance of the Local Road Networks account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
Municipal Engineer has prepared the list of all roads in the Municipality with complete description and detailed cost of completed road projects for years before 2015.
OIC-Municipal GSO has prepared the Report on Local Road Network and conducted the inventory and reporting of the Municipality's road network system.
OIC-Municipal Accountant has prepared reports and some necessary adjustments were taken-up.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of Local Road Network is still on-going and some adjustments to recognize the cost of previous years' Local Road Network have not been fully implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:
a. strictly remit whatever balance due to the said agencies to avoid incurrence of penalties, surcharges and inconveniences to the employees concerned; and
b. ensure that all taxes withheld, premium contributions and loan amortizations are remitted to the respective government agencies within the period prescribed by the abovementioned regulations.
observations.managementAction:
With regards to the Pag-IBIG remittance, one of the reasons that lead to the discrepancy was that the billing was forwarded to Human Resource Management one to two months advance prior to start of deduction. Deductions are done every 15th and 30th of the month and it is remitted on or before the 15th of the succeeding month. There were instances that employee(s) apply for the renewal of their loan on the same month, the remaining loan balances are deducted to the net proceeds. Loan deductions that were already deducted during the 15th and/or 30th are refunded to employees and will be reflected in their next payroll. It is to avoid multiple deductions on the employee because the amount was already included in the computation of their loan renewal. Payments are done online thru the WEACCESS platform by Land Bank.
As for PhilHealth remittances, Electronic Premium Remittance System (EPRS) is the online platform for PhilHealth remittances and we only remit the exact amount of premiums of regular and casual employees as indicated in their EPRS.
observations.reason: On-going process of reconciliation. Amounts withheld were not fully remitted to the respective agencies as of reporting date.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. take immediate action to fully utilize the funds allocated for the implementation of HFEP in accordance with DOH Administrative Order No. 2022-006, to avoid missing out of potential future funding from the DOH;
b. require the MPDC to review and improve its planning and monitoring processes to ensure effective utilization of funds for the HEFP project and other similar initiatives in the future; and henceforth,
c. strictly adhere to the implementing guidelines for future fund transfers for specific purposes from other government agencies.
observations.managementAction:
For CY 2023, funds received for specific purpose were utilized accordingly.
MPDC has taken action and funds transfer for implementation of HFEP has been fully utilized.
Strictly followed the implementing guidelines.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to:
a. prepare the required reports on time and provide the assigned Supervising Auditor and/or Audit Team Leader with an updated list of all ongoing PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year, for audit validation and verification; and henceforth,
b. strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2013-004 and submit notices of posting of PPAs signboard and the required Monthly Monitoring Report.
observations.managementAction: Submitted a copy of the updated list of all ongoing PPAs to be implemented. Management is now submitting to the Audit Team signboard and required Monthly Monitoring report of PPAs on time.

Written policy guidelines for the hiring of JO/CoS has been established by the Human Resource Management Office with specific basis in determining daily wage.
A report has been prepared by the department heads assessing whether their respective office is understaff or overstaff. The process of assessing and reviewing the qualification of Job Order for appointment to vacant regular positions is now in force.
Strictly comply with the Sections 76 and 77 of RA No. 7160.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a new Executive Order reorganizing the GAD Focal Point System in adherence to the PCW-DILG-DBM JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by JMC No. 2016-01 to ensure the direction-setting, advocacy, planning, monitoring and evaluation, and technical advisory in mainstreaming GAD perspectives in the Municipality’s programs, projects, activities, and processes.
observations.managementAction: Submitted a copy of Executive Order No. 013, series of 2023 reorganizing the GAD Focal Point System in adherence to the PCW-DILG-DBM JMC No. 2013-01.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. OIC-Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary journal entry to revert the QRF and MF of CYs 2016 and earlier to unappropriated surplus of the General Fund;
b. Municipal Treasurer to transfer the reverted amount of P5,327,308.28 from Trust Fund to the General Fund bank account of the Municipality; and henceforth,
c. strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the reversion of unutilized amount of LDRRMF to unappropriated surplus of General Fund after five years.
observations.managementAction: Adjustment were already taken-up in the books. Municipal Treasurer has transferred the amount of P5,327,308.28 from Trust Fund to General Fund as supported with the Bank Statement.
observations.reason: Expired STF for 2017 amounting to P1,174,996.59 has still not been reverted back to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund (not implemented at that time); later action noted but earlier withheld STF reversion still pending at time of initial finding.

observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO is diligently preparing and submitting the LDRRMFIP to the OCD and DILG. The Monthly Sources and Utilization Report on time.

The OIC-Municipal Accountant coordinated with the Municipal Legal Office for the exhaust of all possible legal means to collect the dormant accounts.
Preparation of all the necessary documents to substantiate the request for write-off is still on-going.
observations.reason: Collection of the dormant accounts is still in-process. Preparation and refiling of the request for write-off not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the MSWMB convene regularly to ensure that the review of their Ten-Year SWMP will be conducted on time or as the need arises. As part of these efforts, in September 2022, the MSWMB conducted a dry-run of Waste Analysis and Characterization Study (WACS) in Barangay Mahabang Parang.
observations.managementAction: MSWMB convene regularly and conducting meetings to evaluate their Ten Year SWMP. In September 2022, the MSWMB conducted a dry-run of Waste Analysis and Characterization Study (WACS) in Barangay Mahabang Parang.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Determine the details of the noted difference of P18,622,724.04 between their respective records, and subsequently prepare the necessary adjustment to present the correct balances of the Cash in Bank – LCCA in the cashbooks and financial statements; and
b. Conduct periodic reconciliation of their records as required under Section 63(F) of the LTOM.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer conducted reconciliation of their respective records and was able to lower the discrepancy from P18,622,724.04 in CY 2022 to P364,438.86 in CY 2023.
observations.reason: A discrepancy of P364,438.86 between the accounting and treasury records was still noted as of December 31, 2023. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 3, Pages Nos. 39 to 42.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Recognize expense when incurred and report the same in the financial statements in the period to which they relate; and
b. Follow the guidelines of IPSAS 23, specifically on the recognition of revenue from non-exchange transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already recognized the expense and recognized revenue in the Trust Fund books of the Municipality for CY 2023.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to determine the details of the discrepancies between the balances of the reciprocal accounts Due to/Due from LGUs recorded in the Municipality and the Province’s records so that appropriate adjustments can be made for fair presentation of the affected accounts in the Municipality’s financial statements; and
b. Conduct periodic reconciliation of Municipality’s accounts with the Provincial accounting records to avoid the accumulation of discrepancies between the two records.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the Provincial Accountant. As per reconciliation, the amount of P227,339.00 is for adjustment in the books of the Provincial Office as supported by the issued Certification on April 22 and November 11, 2022.
The Municipal Accountant conducted regular periodic reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the OIC-Municipal Assessor, to immediately provide the Municipal Accounting Office an updated certified list of taxpayers showing the current and prior years amount due and collectible from them as basis for setting-up of RPT/SET Receivables and/or adjustment of the same accounts previously taken up in the books to present the correct balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the OIC-Municipal Assessor, provided the Municipal Accounting Office an updated certified list of tax payers showing current and prior years amount due and collectible as basis of setting-up of RPT/SET receivables in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to record the receipt and issuance of the procured various inventoriable items of the Municipality in conformity with the generally accepted accounting principles and mentioned pertinent rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already recorded the receipt and issuance of the procured inventoriable items of the Municipality in conformity with the generally accepted accounting principles in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Human Resource Management Officer to:
a. Assess the existing organization structure and staffing pattern of the Municipality so that the ideal number of plantilla items/positions could be established and develop a written policy guideline on the hiring of individuals under JO, clearly defining the conditions whereby employment of such JO personnel may be availed to the regular workforce of; and
b. Consider those qualified JO personnel for appointment to casual or vacant regular plantilla positions in the agency.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is already studying the organization structure and staffing pattern. The JO was provided to the Audit Team. The Municipality is considering JO personnel for appointment to vacant regular positions. In the current year, the number of JO personnel of the Municipality has decreased from 342 in CY 2022 to 338 in CY 2023, while six JO personnel were given permanent position in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to:
a. Ensure the optimum utilization of the 20% DF thru efficient implementation of all priority development projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic, and environmental development of the Municipality; and
b. Properly plan the timely procurement and implementation of all projects identified as priority development projects under the 20% DF to guarantee the prompt accomplishment of the same.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator had already included all PPAs chargeable to 20% DF in the yearly preparation of CY 2023 Annual Investment Plan which all said PPAs were implemented during the year. The subject PPAs under continuing appropriations were being implemented in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (MDRRMO) to prepare a comprehensive and integrated LDRRMFIP including the list of projects and activities to be funded out of the previous year’s unexpended LDRRMF in accordance with the above-mentioned regulations. Likewise, we also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to properly incorporate all activities in the Annual Investment Plan including those in the LDRRMFIP as approved by the LDRRM Council.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (MDRRMO) prepared a comprehensive and integrated LDRRMFIP including the list of projects and activities to be funded out of the previous year’s unexpended LDRRMF in CY 2023. The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator properly incorporated all activities in the Annual Investment Plan including those in the LDRRMFIP as approved by the LDRRM Council in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office along with the GAD Focal Point System to establish GAD Database with adequate and systematically gathered GAD information to include gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data which will provide the basis for gender analysis and effective gender responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Office along with the GAD Focal Point System had already established GAD Database with adequate and systematically gathered GAD information to include gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant, at the start of the year a certified list of the names of the taxpayers and the corresponding amount of taxes due and collectible to establish the correct and valid RPT and SET Receivables that can be taken up in the books of accounts. This would also provide management with accurate information needed in its decision making.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer furnished the Municipal Accountant at the start of the year a certified list of the names of the taxpayers and the corresponding amount of taxes due and collectible to establish the correct and valid RPT and SET Receivables that can be taken up in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. immediately submit the aforesaid paid disbursement vouchers together with their supporting documents to the Auditor’s Office pursuant to the above-cited regulations, for post-audit. Failure on the part of the concerned official to submit the above-mentioned documents shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of his salary until he has complied with the requirements pursuant to the above-mentioned regulations; and
b. henceforth, strictly comply with the provision of COA Circular No. 2009-006 on the submission of financial transaction documents and reports of the Municipality to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction:
The related disbursement vouchers together with their supporting documents had been submitted to the Audit Team.
For CY 2023, the Municipality had submitted the financial transaction documents and reports of the Municipality to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to:
a. ensure that project billboards showed all relevant information of the projects being implemented by the Municipality to promote public awareness and concern over the government infrastructure projects;
b. assign a specific person that will regularly update the relevant information of the projects on the posted signboards to guarantee transparency in all projects being undertaken by the Municipality; and
c. strictly comply with the guidelines set out in COA Circular No. 2013-004 on the notification to the public or publicity relating to the project.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality ensured that project billboards showed all relevant information of the projects being implemented by the Municipality to promote public awareness and concern over the government infrastructure projects. The Municipality had already assigned a specific person that will regularly update the relevant information of the projects on the posted signboards. The Municipality has complied with guidelines set out in COA Circular No. 2013-004.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Human Resource Management Officer to:
a. cease practice of hiring JO workers to perform functions that are part of the job description of the existing regular employees; b. evaluate the job description of regular employees to ascertain that the assigned duties and responsibilities are aligned with their positions and properly undertaken, to avoid the unnecessary hiring of JO workers; and c. henceforth, ensure that the hiring of JO workers is limited only to emergency or intermittent work for a short duration and for a specific piece of work pursuant to COA-DBM JC No. 2 dated October 20, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality revisited the duties and responsibilities assigned on the JO workers and ceased the functions that are part of job description of the existing regular employees. The Municipality evaluated the job description of regular employees and ensured that their assigned duties and responsibilities are aligned with their positions and properly undertaken. The Municipality has ensured that hiring of JO workers is limited only to emergency or intermittent work for a short duration and for a specific piece of work.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting documents to substantiate the disbursement made. Otherwise, the transactions shall be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had belatedly submitted the lacking supporting documents to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the Municipal Engineer to expedite the submission of the complete description and cost segregation of the road components for road projects of the Municipality; b. direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical inventory of the Road Network System of the Municipality and GSO to prepare and submit the required report thereon; c. instruct the Municipal Accountant and GSO to maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components and Local Road Network Property Card to substantiate the correctness of the general ledger balance for Local Road Networks account; d. direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the total road network system of the Municipality at year-end; and e. henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer has complied with the Audit Team’s audit recommendation. The Municipal Engineer submitted a complete description and cost segregation of the road components for road projects of the Municipality. The Municipality submitted a physical inventory of Road Network. The Municipal Accountant and the GSO started maintaining subsidiary records for roads and road components and Local Road Network Property Card. For CY 2023, the Municipal Accountant disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements the total road network system of the Municipality at year-end. The Municipality complied with the Audit Team’s audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned official to post the said reports on the Municipality’s website in order to properly disclose the related information regarding the utilization and disbursements of the aforesaid financial assistance in public.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to post the subject report on the Municipality’s website.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to (i) develop an effective tax collection campaign program by implementing stricter tax collection measures, such as, use of legal remedies to collect delinquent receivables and conduct taxpayer education program to increase awareness among the taxpayers on their obligations and responsibilities on paying taxes; and (ii) implement notice of delinquency system as prescribed in Section 254 of RA No. 7160; b. Municipal Assessor to regularly update and re-evaluate the assessment of properties and submit the updated Assessment Roll to the Municipal Treasurer so that updating of records be made and setting up of Accounts Receivable is reliable with high collectability forecast and relay the same to the Local Finance Committee for re-evaluation of target by considering the updated record of assessment and the past years’ collection performances; and c. strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 7160 for efficient and effective ways to collect revenues for the implementation of viable projects for your constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer renewed a tax collection program by implementing stricter enforcement campaign measures, conducted taxpayer education program and the Municipal Assessor drafted a comprehensive program and prepared updated Assessment Roll and submitted to the Municipal Treasurer for recognition and recording.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to increase the amount of fidelity bond of the Collecting Officer sufficient to cover her maximum accountability in accordance with the Revised Schedule of bonds, provided by the Bureau of Treasury, to protect the interest of the Municipality in case of loss resulting from unlawful use and negligence in the safekeeping of government funds; and henceforth, b. strictly comply with Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 on the requirements for fidelity bonding of accountable public officers.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer increased the fidelity bond of the collecting officer from P80,000.00 to P300,000.00 and ensured compliance with Treasury Circular No. 02-2009.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Human Resource Management Officer to:
a. establish and develop written guidelines for the hiring of JO/CoS personnel that clearly define the conditions for availing of JO/CoS personnel and specify criteria for determining a daily wage rate; b. design and implement the Municipality’s Organizational Structure and Staffing Pattern to determine the ideal number of plantilla items/positions needed; c. consider qualified JO/CoS personnel for appointment to vacant regular plantilla positions; and d. strictly comply with Sections 76 and 77 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: Organizational Structure and Staffing Pattern was implemented and appointments to plantilla positions were based on qualifications. The municipality prepared written policy guidelines and undertook evaluations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a new Executive Order re-constituting the GAD Focal Point System in adherence to the PCW-DILG-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended, to ensure direction-setting, advocacy, planning, monitoring and evaluation, and technical advisory in mainstreaming GAD perspectives in the Municipality’s programs, projects, activities, and processes.
observations.managementAction: E.O. No.14-2023 and E.O. No.16-2023 were issued reconstituting the GAD Focal Point System and establishing the Municipal GAD Office and defining roles and functions to ensure implementation of GAD PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. submit immediately to the Audit Team all the lacking supporting documents and a written explanation on the deficiencies noted in audit; otherwise, the said transactions shall be suspended in audit; and b. ensure the completeness of the supporting documents as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 before processing the payment. We also reiterated that the LDRRMF be utilized only on disaster-related PPAs pursuant to applicable laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The necessary JEV to revert the QRF CY 2015 to unappropriated surplus of the General Fund was drawn per DV No. TF23-03-015, Check No. 650832 dated 03/27/2023. The Municipal Treasurer transferred the amount to GF. Management stopped the practice of charging General Fund administrative expenses to the LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary journal entry to revert the QRF CY 2015 unappropriated surplus of the General Fund; b. Municipal Treasurer to transfer the reverted amount from the Trust Fund to the General Fund bank account; and c. strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the reversion of unutilized amount of LDRRMF to unappropriated surplus of General Fund after five years.
observations.managementAction: Journal entry was drawn and amount of P731,797.09 was reverted to GF per DV No. TF23-03-015, Check No. 650832 dated 03/27/2023. Municipal Treasurer transferred the amount to GF.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. submit immediately to the Audit Team all the lacking supporting documents and written explanation on the deficiencies noted, otherwise the said transactions shall be suspended in audit; and b. ensure the completeness of the supporting documents as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 before processing the payment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the lacking supporting documents and an explanation; concerned officers were instructed to ensure completeness of supporting documents before payment processing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the proper recording of RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based on the RPT Register/Taxpayer’s Index Card; to provide the Municipal Accounting Office the certified list of delinquent taxpayers to recognize the delinquent taxes; and to strictly comply with the regulations to ensure fair presentation of RPT/SET Receivables in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the Municipal Treasurer and prepared JEVs to set-up the RPT and SET receivables at the beginning of CY 2023. The Municipal Treasurer provided the Certified List of Delinquent Taxpayers from CY 2015-2019 to the Municipal Accountant for recognition and recording.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. MSWDO to submit the lacking supporting documents and an explanation on the non-observance of the proper procurement procedure, to substantiate the disbursements made; otherwise, the said transactions shall be suspended in audit; and b. BAC to strictly adhere to the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR on documentary and procedural requirements for all procurements.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO submitted the lacking supporting documents and explanation to the Audit Team. The BAC reviewed and ensured future procurements are compliant to RA No. 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to strictly observe the guidelines on the submission, review and approval of the Municipality’s GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Plan and Accomplishment Report provided under PCW-DILG-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01, to ensure the proposed GAD activities will respond to the GAD issues being addressed by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person adhered to the guidelines on the submission, review and approval of the Municipality’s GAD Plan and Budget and the GAD Plan and Accomplishment Report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. submit immediately the abovementioned reports/accounts and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor to enable the Audit Team to conduct the review and verification of the accounts and the supporting documents; otherwise, Notice of Suspension and Notice of Charge shall be issued for its non-submission; and b. ensure that all report DVs and its supporting documents are submitted within the prescribed period as required under the aforesaid regulations.
observations.managementAction: One accounting staff was tasked in-charge on filing, monitoring and transmitting the unsubmitted documents. The municipal accounts office reversed various CY 2019 PPAs in CY 2020 and submitted the 17 unsubmitted DVs with corresponding documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to ensure that: a. the perpetual inventory method is observed in the accounting for inventories; b. the required forms, subsidiary records and periodic reports are prepared, maintained and submitted for recognition of the expense portion of supplies and materials inventory; and c. periodic physical count of inventory on hand is conducted to ensure that any necessary adjustment in the books and/or inventory records for proper valuation.
observations.managementAction: Periodic physical count of inventory on hand was conducted and reported. The Accounting Office observed proper inventory accounting moving forward though some periodic reports were not in the prescribed format.
observations.reason: Periodic reports such as Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued were not in proper format but inventory counts were conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. make the necessary adjustments to correct the records and related balances of the Road Network Systems and its related Accumulated Depreciation and Depreciation Expense accounts; b. GSO to maintain Local Road Inventory System and the Local Road Map, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-008; and c. Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the total road network system assets as required in COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: Adjustment JEV No. 101-2020-12-00203 was made to reverse various CY 2019 PPAs. GSO was directed to maintain the Local Road Inventory and Road Network System. Municipal Accountant made disclosures in NFS for CY2020; however, full disclosure for CY2023 was not yet made.
observations.reason: Partial: Some adjustments were made but full disclosure of total road network system in NFS for later years not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the documents supporting the Prior Period Adjustments account and ensure that submission of all vouchers are supported with valid documentation.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office reversed various CY 2019 PPAs and submitted the supporting documents. Reversals were made to various CY 2019 PPAs due to absence of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the MSWMB convene regularly to ensure that the review of the Solid Waste Management Plan will be conducted on time or as the need arises.
observations.managementAction: The MSWMB convened and the Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan was undergoing updating pending the waste characterization study to be undertaken with assistance from the Rizal Provincial Government and the Department of Science and Technology.
observations.reason: Update of the Ten-Year SWMP is underway pending the waste characterization study.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the unreleased checks to revert it back to the Cash in Bank account; b. Acting Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit to the Accounting Office the schedule of unreleased checks every end of the year to serve as basis in the adjustment to revert the same to the Cash in Bank account and set up the liability account; and c. henceforth, strictly comply with the guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 96-011.
observations.managementAction: Management thru the Municipal Accountant commented that she has prepared the necessary adjusting entry as per JEV No. 101-2-23-0034 dated February 28, 2023. The Municipal Accounting Office forwarded the schedule of unreleased checks to the Treasurers Office. However, the said schedule was incomplete.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant prepared the necessary adjusting entries to record the unreleased checks per Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 101-2-23-0034 dated February 28, 2023. The schedule of unreleased checks forwarded by the Treasurers Office to the Accounting Office was incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Acting Municipal Treasurer for the conduct of a thorough review and reconciliation of the reported transactions in the Cash - Local Treasury account; b. Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjustment/s in the books to reflect the correct balance of the Cash- Local Treasury account; and c. concerned municipal officials to strictly comply with Section 181(c) of GAAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting and Treasury Office are trying to retrieve related documents and records. The Accounting Office had been conducting reconciliation and prepared the necessary adjusting entries. As of September 2023, there is still a variance that pertains to prior years transactions.
observations.reason: There is still a variance of P72,055.54 as at year-end which pertains to prior years transactions; the Accounting and Treasury Offices are still reconciling the reported transactions in the Cash - Local Treasury account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to verify the discrepancies of P12,892.46 and P143,044.18 between the recorded balances of the Due from and Due to LGUs accounts; b. prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to present the true balance of the said accounts in the Municipalitys financial statements; and c. henceforth, strictly comply with Sections 111 and 112 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the Provincial Accountant and reconciled their respective balances. As of September 2023, there is still a variance that needs further verification.
observations.reason: The discrepancy was due to timing differences and unrecorded prior period transactions which the Provincial Government will request for write-off. There is still a variance that needs further verification as of September 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balance of the affected account in the Municipalitys financial statements; b. ensure that all supplies and materials purchased by the Municipality are properly and completely accounted in the books; c. Municipal Treasurer to properly disclose in the Consolidated RAAF the complete details of the Accountable Forms still on hand at every reporting period; and d. henceforth, strictly comply with Section 120, Chapter 7 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the necessary adjusting journal entries to record the inventory of accountable forms. The Municipality prepared the necessary adjusting entries to set-up the unused supplies per JEV Nos. 101-3-23-0137, 101-11-23-0257 and 101-12-23-0281 dated March 31, 2023, November 30, 2023 and December 29, 2023, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to coordinate with the OIC-Municipal Agriculturist in monitoring the effective implementation of the Knifefish Program; b. MPDO to revisit its policy on project implementation taking into consideration the target period of implementation; and c. henceforth, strictly comply with Section 2 of PD No.1445.
observations.managementAction: The MPDO and the OIC-Municipal Agriculturist are monitoring the implementation. The MPDO revisited policies and identified problems such as typhoons and increase of water lilies in Laguna de Bay which contributed to delay of implementation.
observations.reason: There were problems such as typhoons and increase of water lilies in Laguna de Bay that contributed to delay of implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to: a. refrain from granting cash advances to the accountable officer in excess of her maximum cash accountability; b. ensure that an officer in excess of his/her maximum accountables fidelity bond is sufficient to cover his/her actual accountabilities; and c. henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 5.1 of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer refrained from granting cash advances to the accountable officer; ensured the fidelity bond coverage and complied with Treasury Circular No. 02-2009.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. the Disbursing Officer to prepare and maintain the required Cashbook; b. record and update the cash advances and liquidations on her cashbooks on a regular basis or as transaction occurs; c. ensure the correctness and accuracy of entries recorded in the Cashbooks; and d. henceforth, strictly comply with Paragraph A, Section 63 of the Local Treasury Operations Manual dated October 1, 2007.
observations.managementAction: The Disbursing Officer prepares and maintains the required Cashbook; records and updates the cash advances and liquidations on a regular basis; and ensures correctness and accuracy of entries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to: a. provide the DO with a safety vault for the proper safeguard of cash, accountable forms and other valuable records/documents in her custody; and b. henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 124 of the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines or PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The safety vault of the Disbursing Officer was provided sometime in April 2023 for the proper safeguard of cash and accountable forms.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. adopt a complete ATM payroll system for the Municipality to eliminate the risk of loss or misapplication of funds aside from promoting greater convenience, transparency, and accountability; and b. henceforth, strictly observe the provisions of Section 124 of PD No. 1445 and DBM Circular No. 2008-8 dated October 23, 2008.
observations.managementAction: As of December 31, 2023, the Municipality adopted a complete ATM payroll system.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to: a. comply with the guidelines on information and publicity on PPAs of the government agencies on the projects being undertaken by the Municipal Government by putting up the required project signboards; b. notify the Audit Team within ten days after the award of the infrastructure project or before start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboard and/or public notices are already posted; and c. henceforth, strictly comply with the guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer and Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) ensure that signboards are in placed at all times.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineering Office may had overlooked the required notification of award to the Audit Team. The Municipal Engineer and BAC ensured that signboards are in place at all times.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balance of the affected account in the Municipalitys financial statements; b. ensure that all supplies and materials received from other government agencies/sourced agencies are properly and completely accounted in the books; and c. henceforth, strictly comply with Section 63 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 120 and 121, Chapter 7 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the necessary adjusting journal entries to set-up the unused supplies per JEV Nos. 101-3-23-0137, 101-11-23-0257 and 101-12-23-0281 dated March 31, 2023, November 30, 2023 and December 29, 2023, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to: a. ensure to allocate at least five percent of the Municipalitys total annual appropriations for the implementation of GAD related activities; and b. strictly comply with the guidelines governing GAD allocation provided under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by JMC No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016.
observations.managementAction: At least five percent of the Municipalitys total annual appropriations for the implementation of GAD related activities was provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to: a. ensure that all programs, plans and activities are properly planned and that implementation thereof be in accordance with the approved GAD Plan and Budget to optimize the use of allocated funds, and that all issues on gender mainstreaming are duly addressed by the prepared plan, for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries; and b. henceforth, maximize the utilization/implementation of the GAD Plan and Budget to attain its objective in accordance with Item 2.3 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by JMC No. 2016-01.
observations.managementAction: The GAD focal person closely monitored the implementation of GAD PPAs and required the concerned offices to submit quarterly reports; however, there were PPAs that were not implemented.
observations.reason: Inadequacy of proper monitoring on implementation of GAD PPAs; some PPAs remained unimplemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (MDRRMO) to: a. refrain from appropriating/utilizing the LDRRM funds to projects and activities unrelated to disaster risk reduction and to projects and activities not related to disaster risk management; b. ensure proper allocation and utilization of the LDRRMF and its alignment with the goals and objectives to prevent/reduce the effects of calamities; and c. strictly abide with the provisions of Section 5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO refrained from utilizing the LDRRM funds to activities not related to disaster risk reduction and implemented proper disaster-related PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Finance Committee to submit the required fund utilization financial report, and the list of beneficiaries as stipulated in the guidelines; b. concerned Municipal Committee member to provide justification on the non-submission of the required supporting documents and submit the justification together with the MSWDO; c. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting documents to facilitate post audit of said transaction; and d. concerned municipal officials to ensure compliance with DBM issuances relative to Social Amelioration Programs and Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Finance Committee and MSWDO submitted the required documents to the DSWD; the Municipal Accountant and MSWDO submitted supporting documents to the Audit Team as requested.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned Municipal Officials to: a. submit copies of the contracts/purchase orders and its supporting documents within five days from issuance; b. determine the cause/s for the non-submission and institute measures to address the inability to submit the purchase orders and other contracts within the prescribed period; and c. strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Initially, the OIC-GSO was able to comply with submission of contracts/purchase orders within the prescribed period. However, due to manpower constraints, the submission of some purchase orders remained unimplemented and overlooked.
observations.reason: Due to manpower constraints, the timely submission of all contracts/purchase orders remained unimplemented; some submissions were overlooked.

observations.recommendation: a. make necessary correcting journal entries on the noted erroneous recording of the transactions; and b. exercise caution in classifying and recording transactions in order to avoid such errors.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has made the necessary correcting entries for the erroneous recording of the subject transactions per Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. GJ-100-2303-0047 dated March 31, 2023 and JEV No. 200-2304-112 dated April 30, 2023. Thereafter, caution was being exercised by the Municipal Accounting Office in recording transactions.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to: a. submit a written explanation on the difference noted between their records despite prior years audit observation on the said deficiency; and b. henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 63(F) of the Local Treasury Operations Manual and COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996. We also reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor thoroughly investigate the cause of the difference of P167,651,860.75 between their respective records and subsequently prepare the necessary adjustments to present the correct balance of the Cash in Bank - Local Currency Account in the Municipality’s financial statement.
observations.managementAction: Written explanation dated April 4, 2023 has been submitted by the Municipal Treasurer citing, among others, that the Treasury Office was working hard to reconcile its records and has considered employing additional manpower to augment the lone employee assigned in the reconciliation of its records. The reconciliation of the balances of its Cash in Bank – LCCA was still on going. Out of the total amount, the Municipal Treasury Office has identified an amount of the Municipal Cash in Bank – LCCA recorded in the Special Education Fund and Trust Fund, respectively.
observations.reason: Not An unreconciled balance still exists between the records of the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to determine the details of the discrepancies between the balances of the Due from/Due to LGUs accounts recorded in the Municipality and Province’s records so that appropriate adjustments can be made for fair presentation of the affected accounts in the Municipality’s financial statements; and henceforth, to conduct periodic reconciliation of Municipality’s accounts with the Provincial Accounting records to avoid the accumulation of discrepancies between the two records.
observations.managementAction: Coordination with the Provincial Accountant was made last August 24, 2023 by the Management to determine the details between their respective records. Necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the amount due to the Rizal Provincial Government was made and amounts due to the Rizal Provincial Government were paid.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-HRMO to: a. assess the existing organization structure and staffing pattern of the Municipality so that the ideal number of plantilla items/positions could be established and develop a written policy guideline on the hiring of individuals under JO, clearly defining the conditions whereby employment of such JO personnel may be availed of; and b. consider those qualified JO personnel for appointment to the casual or vacant regular plantilla positions in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The inaction of the Sanguniang Bayan on the request for the creation of the Human Personnel Selection Board (HRMPSB) hampered the issuance of the appointments despite the existence of 181 vacant plantilla positions as of December 31, 2022. Verbal policy guidelines on the hiring and rehiring of JO personnel was previously submitted by the HRMO. In CY 2023, Sanguniang Bayan Resolution No. 25 s. 2023 dated February 15, 2023 was made creating the HRMPBS of the Municipality and Executive Order No. 32 s. 2023 dated June 5, 2023 as its Guidelines on Hiring and Rehiring JO Personnel. As of December 31, 2023, 2,192 or 82.75% of the total workforce are JO/COS personnel based on the Manpower Complement.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. prepare and submit the required certified list of taxpayers and the tax collectible due from them, at the beginning of the year to the Municipal Accountant as a basis of recording the RPT/SET Receivable in the Municipality’s books of accounts; and b. ensure that the certified list of taxpayers and tax collectible due from them be included for the year 2023 which included the certified list of taxpayers and tax collectible due from them.
observations.managementAction: Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. GJ 100-2303-0051 and JEV No. GJ-400-2304-0021 both dated April 3, 2023 and JEV No. GJ-100-2303-0051 dated April 30, 2023 were made to record RPT and SEF tax receivable, respectively, for the year 2023 which included the certified list of taxpayers and tax collectible due from them.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to: a. conduct tax campaign initiatives to boost the collection of Real Property Tax and other taxes imposed by the Municipality; and b. post notice of delinquency in places prescribed by Section 254 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: House to house campaign have been implemented in CY 2023 and thereafter to give notices to delinquent taxpayers apart from the posting in the prescribed locations as specified in Section 254 of RA No. 7160.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and OIC-Municipal Engineer to immediately comply with the relevant requirements or resolve existing issues should these projects be continued; and henceforth, to meticulously and judiciously plan all procurement activities, comply to all necessary requirements and coordinate with stakeholders to resolve any issues, if any, prior to the start of the procurement activities.
observations.managementAction: The issues identified had been resolved thru (a) termination for convenience of the contract for the proposed renovation of the Rural Health Unit (RHU) due to the delay in the issuance of Permit to Construct from the Department of Health (DOH); (b) objection to the project was received from the Homeowner’s Association relative to the improvement of road and drainage system at Daang Tubo to Metro Royale Phase 2 project, hence withdrawn; and (c) funding for the improvement of Public Market project totaling P11,000,000.00 from the Local Government Support Fund (LGSF) was reverted to the National Treasury per JEV No. 400-2212-0301B dated December 15, 2022. The project will be re-evaluated prior to procurement and implementation in compliance with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
 a. Analyze and determine the validity of the recorded Accounts Payable of the Municipality. Otherwise, request an authority for the reversion of those payables without valid documentation to the Sangguniang Bayan;
 b. Prepare necessary journal entry to revert the Accounts Payable without valid documentation to Government Equity Accounts so that the Municipality can use the available funds for other priority projects that would uplift the living condition of its constituents; and henceforth, to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the balance of the affected accounts for the fair presentation in the financial statements; and
 c. Strictly comply with Section 4(s) of MNGAS, Volume I for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Out of the P7,399,657.92 year 2023, P513,880.00 was identified without valid claims and already reversed thru JEV No. 100-2024-05-075 dated May 2, 2024. The Municipal Accountant submitted documents related to paid accounts payable and found P34,527,981.11 as valid claims. For the remaining balances, the OMA will exert their best efforts to provide the necessary documents to prove validity and propriety.
observations.reason: P513,880.00 was already reversed thru JEV No. 100-2024-05-075. Preparation of adjusting journal entries for other items found reasonable; further submission and validation of supporting documents are on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
 a. Prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the erroneous classification of the accounts by debiting Accounts Payable and crediting the Leave Benefits Payable, Due to Officers and Employees, Due to LGUs and Other Payables accounts in the amounts indicated;
 b. Ensure that all financial transactions of the Municipality are properly recorded in the books pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; and henceforth, strictly ensure that claims are processed with complete documentation before payment.
 Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 3, Page Nos. 45 to 50
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant processed and paid P1,329,025.35 and prepared JEV No. 100-2023-03 dated March 9, 2023 to correct the remaining erroneous classification of accounts amounting to P3,235,604.09. The GSO commented that they will adhere to the recommendation and facilitate timely submission of required documents to the OMA.
observations.reason: Some transactions were adjusted and paid; however, some claims with incomplete documentation still exist and are being addressed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
 a. Send written notices to the payees to verify the existence of the stale checks;
 b. Practice sending a written notice to the payee at least one month before a check becomes stale; and
 c. Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, if any.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Treasurer regularly sends written notices to payees; replacement checks are issued upon submission of stale checks. Total stale checks amounting to P54,080.87 were adjusted to the respective payable accounts of Trust Fund thru JEV No. 2023-03-005 and JEV No. 2023-03-006 both dated March 31, 2023.
observations.reason: Replacement checks issued upon submission; adjustments made for stale checks amounting to P54,080.87. Some stale checks remain to be claimed and notices are being sent.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Engineer to:
 a. Ensure that the billboard is posted on the project site and showed all relevant information of the said project. Otherwise, deduct from the total payment the amount of the billboard;
 b. Assign a specific person who will regularly update the relevant information of the projects on the posted signboards to guarantee transparency; and
 c. Strictly comply with the guidelines set out in COA Circular No. 2013-004 on the notification to the public or publicity relating to the project.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer posted a billboard on the project site and assigned a focal person who will regularly update the relevant information on posted signboards. Some project billboards are in place though some still do not contain all required information due to varying circumstances (e.g., water saturation of soil affecting project timing).
observations.reason: Some project billboards posted at project sites do not yet contain all required information. Implementation is ongoing and being monitored.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to conduct intensive tax campaigns, employ collection strategies, and institute legal remedies to collect delinquent taxes in accordance with RA No. 7160. Also, post notices and promote advance real property tax payment acceptance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has posted in bulletin boards notices on delinquent taxpayers and acceptance of advance RPT payments for CY 2023. The Municipal Treasurer and concerned offices conducted tax campaigns and posted information in public places to encourage payments.
observations.reason: Intensive tax campaigns conducted and notices posted; however, full collection target not met for CY 2022.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
 a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit all lacking documents to facilitate post-audit of submitted disbursements; otherwise, the transactions shall be suspended in audit;
 b. Ensure that all claims are properly supported with complete documentation before processing payment; and
 c. Strictly comply with documentary requirements in the procurement of infrastructure projects prescribed in COA Circular 2012-001 and RA No. 9184 and its IRR.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted some of the lacking documents and processed payments where appropriate. The Municipality committed to ensure that future claims are properly supported with complete documentation before processing payments.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents were submitted belatedly; however, the practice of processing payments with incomplete documentation still persisted for some transactions and is being addressed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
 a. Immediately resolve all issues concerning the ownership of the land where the health center will be constructed;
 b. Revisit its policy on project implementation taking into consideration the target implementation period; and
 c. Strictly comply with guidelines on the utilization of the LGSF-SBDP and its project implementation pursuant to LBC No. 144.
observations.managementAction: Ownership issues were resolved; the subject land was transferred to the Municipality by virtue of donation supported with approved deeds. The Municipality revisited its policy on project implementation and completed the project within the prescribed period. Management complied with guidelines on LGSF-SBDP utilization.
observations.reason: Ownership issues resolved; project implemented and funds utilized per guidelines; excess returned as applicable.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
 a. Prepare reversing entries to correct the error committed in recording the advance payments of RPT; and
 b. Strictly comply with the provisions of Section 64 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I in recording the advance payments of RPT for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Prepared adjusting journal entries nos. 1 and 2, both dated January 1, 2023, to correct the erroneous recording of the advance payments for GF and SEF. Management stated it will strictly comply with Section 64 of MNGAS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Head of the Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) to:
 a. Cease designating JO workers as department/unit heads; 
 b. Consider filling plantilla positions by hiring qualified applicants or promoting deserving employees to guarantee effective implementation of PPAs; and
 c. Strictly observe Item 7.4 of COA-DBM JC No. 2, s. 2020.
observations.managementAction: Management indicated that none of the 17 JO workers were performing functions that are part of regular employees' job descriptions; they were hired because there were no regular employees. Management will adopt COA recommendations and issued memoranda directing HRMO to assess manpower and fill plantilla positions. Some JO workers were subsequently hired/promoted to plantilla where applicable.
observations.reason: Some assessments and promotions conducted; however, several JO workers remain designated in positions pending filling of plantilla items and further review.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the GSO to:
 a. Submit delivery documents of procured equipment, goods and materials to the Audit Team within 24 hours after receipt; and
 b. Strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2009-002 for Audit Team inspection.
observations.managementAction: Management acknowledged the lapse and committed to comply. Some delivery documents were submitted to COA albeit beyond the 24-hour period. The GSO was directed to comply with the 24-hour submission requirement.
observations.reason: Delivery documents were submitted belatedly; 24-hour requirement still not consistently observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LDRRMO to fast-track the implementation of PPAs funded under the LDRRMF to ensure constituents receive benefits at the soonest time possible.
observations.managementAction: Instructions were given by the Municipal Mayor to the BAC and MDRRMO through memorandum to comply with the audit recommendations and to fast-track implementation.
observations.reason: Some PPAs implemented; others remain partially or not implemented due to procurement and program scheduling issues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to vigorously pursue timely and full implementation of PPAs and to monitor/evaluate implementation to avoid delay and adjustment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Office was requested to monitor and evaluate project implementation. Management conducted quarterly LPMC meetings and planned sectoral consultations and capacity-building trainings to improve planning and monitoring.
observations.reason: Monitoring mechanisms initiated but several projects remain unimplemented or only partially implemented due to prioritization of continuing appropriations and funding constraints.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to re-align unexpended balances of continuing appropriations of completed projects to other priority projects via supplemental budget so funds can be used.
observations.managementAction: Management stated they realigned unexpended balances thru Supplemental Budget No. 2-2023 and other measures to utilize available funds for other priority projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to:
 a. Identify GAD PPAs that are responsive to gender issues; and
 b. Utilize the GAD Fund solely for PPAs that address identified gender issues as per the approved GPB.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructed the GFPS through memorandum to comply with the audit recommendations. Management identified GAD PPAs in the DILG-endorsed GPB and committed to ensure GAD fund utilization aligns with GPB.

observations.recommendation: b. Prepare the The Municipal Fully necessary Accountant already Implemented adjustment/s in their reflected in his respective books, if records by drawing warranted, to reflect joural entry to the correct balances of Cash Local Treasury with the details: • JEV No. 100- 2023-01-0136 dated January 10, 2023; • JEV Nos. 100- 2023-01-0546, 100-2023-01- 0547 and 100-2023-01-0548 dated January 31, 2023; • JEV No. 100- 2023-04-1981 and 100-2023-04-1985 dated April 13, 2023; • JEV No. 100- 2023-04-2129 dated April 20, 2023; • JEV No. 100- 2023-04-2184 dated April 25, 2023; • JEV No. 100- 2023-06-3358 dated June 27, 2023; and • JEV No. 100- 2023-06-3364 dated June 29, 2023 c. Strictly comply with Fully Section 181(c) of Implemented GAAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant submitted the Report on the Reconcilitation of Records to the Audit Team after conducting thorough review and analysis on the identified discrepancies and effected necessary adjustments in their respective records.

observations.recommendation: locate and releasing and immediately submit the disbursement vouchers (DVs) and collection reports amounting to P8,985,079.13 and P3,594,411.25, respectively, for proper recording in the Municipality’s books; and henceforth, Strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already reflected the adjustment in the books by drawing Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) and has corrected the previous practice on recording accounts payable. In addition, the letter stated that the Management will make necessary actions in order to implement the recommendation regarding the submission of notice of delivery within 24 hours.
observations.reason: The identified bank and book reconciling items are not yet taken up/recorded in the books up to date.

observations.recommendation: b. OIC-Municipal Necessary Not Non-recording of Accountatnt to conduct public Implemented prepare the necessary auction of unserviceable Journal Entry Vouchers; motor vehicles, subject for disposal and henceforth, and writing-off for recognize the cost of the the conduct of One- former of the Time Cleansing of Accountant receive the PPE accounts. report and the required documents and reports pursuant to the pertinent provisions of the Circular.

b. Municipal GSO to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to prepare the Report on the Local Road Network (RLRN) in proper format as subject for the Municipal Engineer to prepare the Report on the Local Road Network (RLRN) in proper format as subject for the Municipal Engineer to prepare the Report on the Local Road Network (RLRN) in proper format as subject for the Municipal Engineer to prepare the Report on the Local Road Network (RLRN) in proper format as subject for the Municipal Engineer to prepare the Report on the Local Road Network (RLRN) in proper format as subject for the Municipal Engineer to prepare the Report on the Local Road Network (RLRN) in proper format as subject for the Municipal Engineer to prepare the Report on the Local Road Network (RLRN) in proper format as subject for the Municipal Engineer to prepare the Report on the Local Road Network (RLRN) in proper format as subject for the Municipal Engineer to prepare the Report on the Local Road Network (RLRN) in proper format
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer will furnish the GSO a copy of the detailed cost segregation of Local Road Network. The GSO is yet to prepare the RLRN based on the actual inventory and coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Office for the Road ID No. and furnish the same to Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Necessary documents for detailed cost recognition and reconciliation not yet submitted to the former of the Circular.

observations.recommendation: b. OIC-Municipal Accountant, subsequently prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to present the correct balances of the said accounts in the Municipality’s financial statements; and henceforth, OIC-Municipal Accountant to conduct a periodic reconciliation of accounts Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs in the Municipality books with the corresponding reciprocal accounts in the books of the Province.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Provincial Accountant have been continuously coordinating to reconcile the Due from/Due to LGU accounts.
observations.reason: No action taken by the Provincial Government of Rizal to reconcile the Due from/Due to LGU accounts.

observations.recommendation: b. Prepare the necessary journal entry to revert the said outstanding obligations to the unappropriated surplus pursuant to Section 98 of PD No. 1445 so that the Municipality can use the available funds for other priority projects.
observations.managementAction: An authority from Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to process the write-off of the dormant accounts was requested by Management and the Municipal Accountant already forwarded the request letter for the SB to give authority to revert the outstanding balance into equity.

observations.recommendation: b. Effect the necessary adjustments in the Municipality’s books to present the correct balance of the said account.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant effected the necessary adjustments and prepared the JEVs.

observations.recommendation: b. Municipal Agriculturist to conduct close and regular monitoring of the status of the remaining existing breeding stocks, so as transfer, birth and death of animals could be fully accounted for and provide the OIC-Municipal Accountant of the monitoring report and the periodic appraisal of breeding stocks; and c. Concerned officials to exert efforts in finding relevant documents such as report of the farmers/beneficiaries for the investigation conducted and action taken by the Municipality on the matter to support the request from relief of accountability to be filed to Commission on Audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Agriculturist has monitoring and is coordinating with the Provincial Veterinarian for certification on deaths reported and will request the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) for authority to write-off if necessary.

observations.recommendation: b. Immediately return to the Source Agency the unexpended balance of fund amounting to P609,685.00 which was received during the term of former Congresswoman. Otherwise, initiate action to request for an authority to write-off the said dormant account in accordance with the requirements and procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reverted the unexpended funds after coordination and the Social Welfare and Development Officer exerted effort to collect the balances of the beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: b. Strictly remit in full the withheld taxes, premium contributions and loan amortizations withheld from employees’ compensation to the respective government agencies in accordance with the cited regulations. Preparation of working paper for withheld amounts was done and adjustments will be effected if required.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant commented that he will continuously coordinate and request a certification of no pending payments to support that there are no outstanding balances from the concerned agencies. He added that necessary adjustment in the books will be made after further analysis and reconciliation on the profile of accounts of employees so that the same will no longer appear in the books.
observations.reason: Variances are due to renewal of loans of the employees.

observations.recommendation: b. Strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-002 for the Audit Team to conduct an inspection of deliveries at the most appropriate time and manner, so that the Municipality could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies that may be noted thereon.
observations.managementAction: Immediate preparation of notice of delivery was made by the Procurement and Supply Section of GSO after acceptance of delivered items and copies of delivery documents were furnished to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: a. Implementing Division to check the Memorandum of Agreement if they are still on the timeline and coordinate with the Source Agency if necessary and coordinate with the concerned Offices and agencies to fast track the implementation of the PPAs; and b. OIC-Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the OIC-Municipal Treasurer, to remit to the BTR/Source Agencies all the unused balance of funds pursuant to the cited regulations, so that the amount could be immediately used for other projects/programs of the National Government and to relieve the Municipality from any accountability/liability for the said unused funds. Should there be a need for the unspent funds, the Municipality should request from the source agencies for authority/approval to use the excess amounts for other purposes or projects.
