observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC, with the assistance of the GFPS, to establish a complete and updated GAD database, and conduct gender analysis to serve as a basis for addressing gender issues and concerns in GAD planning and budgeting for the ensuing years; and b. Local Sanggunian to formulate GAD Code or ordinances that will support the Municipality’s efforts in promoting, protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights, economic empowerment and gender-responsive governance towards the attainment of gender equality and women’s empowerment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor directed the MPDC and the GFPS-TWG Chairperson through Memorandum Order No. 64, series of 2021 for the establishment of complete and updated GAD database. The Municipal Mayor requested the Sanggunian Bayan to furnish the GFPS-TWG a copy of existing Municipal Ordinance No. 2015-028 entitled “An Ordinance Enacting the Gender and Development Code of the Municipality of Rizal” for ready reference in the program implementation.

observations.recommendation:
Municipal Accountant
To record the remaining items of the relief goods as Inventory as stated below:

Welfare Goods for
 Distribution		149,461.90
Drugs and Medicines for Distribution		106,530.00
	                           
  Prior Period Adjustments          255,991.90	

To record purchases under the Inventory account  on the basis of the Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR) and other supporting documents such as Purchase Orders (POs), Delivery Receipts a/or Supplier’s Invoice.

To record expenses on the basis of the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) together with the supporting RIS.

Municipal Treasurer as the General Services Officer

To consolidate the RIS in the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued and submit the same to the Office of the Municipal Accountant for proper recording of its issuances in order to reflect the correct Inventory and Expense balances.

Requisitioning Office/Departments
To refrain from attaching RIS in the disbursement vouchers as one of the supporting documents for such shall only be used upon issuance of a particular item carried in stocks.

Focal Person assigned to Distribution of Relief Goods

To prepare RIS upon issuances for distribution to end-users/beneficiaries of relief goods for proper control and monitoring thereof.

Municipal Treasurer as the General Services Officer

To prepare and maintain an inventory list of all procured items before repacking of goods and corresponding Stock Cards for purposes of check and balance. Likewise, submit an explanation/justification on the noted variances.

Municipal Accountant
 6.14.3To prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for all procured relief goods out of appropriations.

The Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and officials concerned to ensure that financial assistance extended to the barangays for the implementation of MRF be supported by the above-mentioned documentary requirements to provide basis for  monitoring the proper use of funds.

  Further recommended that the Municipal Accountant make sure that fund transfers be recorded in the Due from LGUs account instead of Subsidy so that the municipality can monitor the implementation of the proposed PPAs.

Municipal Planning and Development Officer:

To specify the details of the identified priority projects under the 20% Development Fund including project location, specific implementation schedule, and physical target to avoid generic and lump-sum projects.

Require the component barangays to submit their project proposal with complete details before the completion of the Annual Investment Plan.

Initiate the preparation of necessary documents (e.g. detailed engineering) to support cost estimate of projects funded under the 20% Development plan before the budget preparation.

Develop and recommend other procedures to enhance the current practice regarding the giving of financial assistance/aid to barangays under the 20% Development Fund. That any projects/programs relative thereto partakes the nature of capital expenditures.

We That management to monitor the implementation of MRF for Barangays granted with financial assistance. Moreover, require the preparation of POW as one of the requirements in releasing financial assistance in order to ensure appropriate structure with enough corresponding appropriation. Also to prepare MOA on the financial resources incorporating therein the prohibition of construction any structures in a privately-owned lot to avoid future legal issues.

To cause the submission of required Mayor’s Permit in compliance of the above-quoted provision.
Post all   procurement of goods and supplies related to COVID-19 relative to the Procurement Projects undertaken in the GPPB Online Portal the necessary data as enumerated in the GPPB Circular issuances. These data or information shall be used by the GPPB and its Technical Support Office to comply with the reportorial requirements under the Bayanihan Act.  

Ensure that the form/content of the Omnibus Sworn Statement (OSS) be in accordance with the afore-cited GPPB Circular and contains the name of the procurement undertaken and must be an original copy. This OSS is required in lieu of the

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

Enforce collection and settlement of the long outstanding receivables and cash advances through issuance of demand letters;
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit a sworn Certification for unliquidated cash advances, and other receivables with no available documents but are not yet considered dormant as defined under COA Circular 2016-005; and
Initiate the preparation and filing of the request of authority to write-off accounts with the COA in accordance with the aforementioned Circular.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a reconciliation report of the reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustments, if any.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will take up the necessary adjustment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity require the BAC, through its Secretariat, to post the Notice of Award and the Notice of Proceed in the PhilGEPS and agency website within three (3) days and fifteen (15) days, respectively, to be compliant with the regulations contained in the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Management will comply with the auditor’s recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to the Management:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly signed certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible every beginning of the year for recording purposes;

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to record the RPT/SET receivables based on the certified list from the Office of the Treasurer and ensure that the recorded receivables are based on actual reference documents; and 

Intensify tax collections thru the formulation of strategies to improve collection of real property taxes and encourage the involvement of barangay officials in the tax campaign to reach out to a larger area.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant give utmost priority in analyzing and determining the composition of the long outstanding Receivables. Otherwise, comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 in requesting for the write-off of the long outstanding receivables.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct complete periodic physical inventory count of property, plant and equipment by type, which shall be made annually and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to be submitted to the office of the COA Auditor not later than January 31 of each year. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should maintain Property, Plant & Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) for each category of assets. The General Services Officer should properly classify/identify the PPEs thru the maintenance of property cards to ascertain its accuracy and existence and reconcile any difference with that of the records of the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended the following to the Management:

To require the Municipal Engineer to: (i) monitor the simultaneous implementation of projects by the same contractor to guard against unwarranted delays or substandard quality of work; (ii) recommend for the imposition of liquidated damages on the delays that were not justified; and

To Scrutinize the contractor’s manpower and equipment on all the projects to be awarded and not on an individual project only in cases where the latter is the lowest bidder in several projects which have the same or overlapping implementation period. This is in order to require the contractor to augment its manpower and/or equipment to be utilized in the project awarded to it or to disqualify it from obtaining several award or cotract in consonance with the provisions of the Item 23.6 of the 2016 RIRR OF RA 9184.

We recommended that the Management secure copies of the MOA/TA from the Source Agencies and furnish the COA Audit Team copies the same to determine whether fund transfers are properly utilized and the requirements for projects implementation and reporting are strictly complied with.
observations.managementAction: The Management filed the MOA but were not submitted to the auditor. Also, there was no MOA given to some funds received that were automatically credited to the bank account. The Management will furnish the auditor the MOA/TA. Copies of Memorandum of Agreement/Trust Agreement of fund transfers from the different National Government Agencies were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to properly classify the amount due within twelve months after the reporting date as part of the current liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.reason: The current portion of Loans Payable was disclosed only in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer and all concerned department heads to meticulously and judiciously prepare the Annual Procurement Plan in accordance with Section 7 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and ensure that all necessary information required in the APP and its amendments, if any, are reflected therein to serve as a planning and control tool for efficient procurement.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed with the audit observation and will take actions to comply with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: The APP still lacked necessary information as the GSO used the same format of the previous year’s APP and was not able to include the other required information.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Executive Committee and GAD Technical Working Group to provide technical assistance to the Local Planning and Development Offices (LPDO) to establish and maintain GAD Database and ensure that it is operational. Likewise, provide the funds necessary for its establishment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality prioritized other activities and programs. The Municipality prioritized activities related to the COVID-19 Pandemic during CY 2020.
observations.reason: The MSDWO prioritized their activities related to the COVID-19 Pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the GAD Focal Point System, strictly observe the prescribed timelines for GAD Planning and Budgeting, as provided in the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016 to facilitate timely preparation and submission to the Provincial Planning and Development Office and the subsequent review and endorsement by the DILG Provincial Office.
observations.reason: CY 2020 GPB was not submitted to the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) for review and consequently to the Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) for final review and endorsement to the Local Government Unit (LGU) because poor planning and inefficiency in the timely preparation of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Inventory Committee start the reconciliation of those property items included in the inventory report with the records being maintained by the Accounting and Property/Treasurer’s Office since its existence and condition have already been established

Likewise, the Inventory Committee should exert their best efforts to complete the required physical inventory-taking of properties owned by the LGU and update the RPCPPE with the data of those properties not included in the previous inventory report.

After the updated RPCPPE have been made in accordance with the prescribed format, reconciliation thereof shall be made with that of the accounting unit’s report to establish reliability and fair valuation of the PPE accounts.

Lastly, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant maintain Property Card and Equipment Ledger Cards, respectively, for easy determination, identification and reconciliation of records.

We recommended that management follow the accounting and reporting guidelines in the recognition of the various components of the Road Network as stated in COA Circular No. 2015-008 in order to ensure that the Road Network System of the municipality is properly identified, valued and accounted for in the books.

We recommended that management to direct the Local Disasters Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to properly implement the LDRRMP and optimize the utilization of its LDRRMF to lessen the socioeconomic and environmental impacts of disasters in the community and disaster risks, including projected climate risks, and to enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the community. 

Relative to the issue of lack of manpower, the municipality could tap the services of other regular employees within the agency that can support and help the implementation of enlisted PPAs.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that management prioritize the establishment of a permanent evacuation center intended to be fully disaster-ready at all times in accordance with the set-forth standards under DILG Memorandum Circular 122 series of 2018.

We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendations that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare and submit the Procurement Monitoring Reports in its prescribed format for approval and submission by the Municipal Mayor to the Government Procurement Policy Board within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Solid Waste Management Board to:

 Effectively and efficiently implement the solid waste management programs/projects/activities embodied in the SWMP especially on the establishing of collection system for all types of wastes from household. 

 To take appropriate action in making efficient use of the different      facilities and equipment expended by the agency.

We recommend that the municipality provides the garbage collectors with sufficient and necessary trainings or seminars to enhance their knowledge in the proper handling and disposal of solid waste.

We recommend that the management provide personal protective equipment to all collectors and personnel directly dealing with solid waste and require the concerned personnel to attend the necessary trainings as mandated under Section 23 of RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Property Officer to facilitate the submission of the updated list of inventory of insurable properties with the GSIS so that the necessary insurance coverage for all the assets can be applied with the GSIS General Insurance Fund.

We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Agriculturist to coordinate the source agency to secure/obtain needed documents as stated in the preceding paragraphs in order to have sufficient basis in implementing and monitoring of project. Also, the management can take other actions for the full utilization of the equipment by encouraging the farmers to invest in corn production. Further, the accounting can effect necessary recording in the books of the municipality.

 Likewise, the management shall take essential actions in maintaining the equipment/project to avoid rapid physical deterioration of the facility due to its non-utilization.

That the Municipal Assessor conduct re-assessment and regular tax mapping to all real properties located in the municipality in order to determine and identify the proper and correct classification of every real property, the property owner and other related to the present structure and projection of all properties which shall be then the basis in the computation of estimated real property tax. 

With this, the estimated tax collectibles is more accurate and may result to a reasonable tax collection efficiency projection that will ultimately quantify performance indicator in its goal to improve tax administration.

 Intensify tax collection strategies in order to collect the estimated RPT and SET receivables in order to generate additional funds to be used in implementing other programs, projects and activities.

1.6 We recommended and Management agreed that:
a) The Municipal Accounting Office prepare the necessary adjusting
entries to reclassify the items previously recognized as PPE to their
proper Inventory accounts and recognize the corresponding expense.
b) The General Service Office issue ICS instead of ARE or the PAR to
establish accountability on items issued.

2.6 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to: (a) prepare
a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and take up necessary
adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all reconciling items
are resolved and/or adjusted on a timely basis; and (b) perform
constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter error and
omissions.

3.5 We recommended that fuel expenses with the nature of financial
assistance be charged to the General Fund appropriation and return
back to the BGCM fund the said amount with corresponding adjusting
entries.

4.8 We recommended that Management adhere to the provisions of the Price
Act Law in times of State of Emergency and State of Calamity and
likewise, strictly follow pertinent laws, rules and regulations on the
procurement of goods.

5.7 We recommended that the Accounting Office coordinate with the Treasury
Office to determine reasons for the long outstanding checks that already
became stale for proper adjustment in the books.

6.4 We recommended that Management adhere with the provisions of COA
Circular NO. 75-6 to safeguard government property against unauthorized
use and/or expenses.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) Municipal Treasurer to formally request the reduction of the PCF to an amount that closely approximates the average monthly requirement for petty expenses; (b) PCF Custodian to ensure the every payment out of the PCF are adequately documented; and (b) Municipal Accountant to immediately apply Imprest system in handling the Petty Cash Fund in compliance with Section 84, Volume I of the GAAM for LGUs.
During the exit conference, it was informed that the use of Imprest Sytem is already in place as of the first quarter of CY 2021.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to: (a) coordinate with former accounting officials or staff who may have knowledge of the details of receivables that were recognized prior to his assumption to office; (b) based on competent documents, issue demand letters to all debtors whose accounts had already fallen due; (c) as warranted, recognize impairment of receivables in accordance with IPSAS 29; and (d) ascertain if conditions are met for the writing off of receivable accounts, and take appropriate action in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Heads of Offices of Accounting and Treasury to (i) complete and reconcile their respective PPE records;  (b) Municipal Treasurer to consider designating a staff who will be in-charge of maintaining PPE records; (c) Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of physical inventory of PPEs and render  the corresponding RPCPPE; and (d) Disposal Committee to (i) facilitate disposal of unserviceable PPE in accordance with the prescribed guidelines and procedures.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Engineer to ensure that the six unimplemented projects as of year-end are completed as planned; (b) Local Development Council to explore ways on how to align future development projects with the demands of the times with  requisite consideration of applicable regulations; and (c) Bids and Awards Committee to determine the most appropriate mode of procurement to facilitate the procurement of the Municipality’s development projects even in this time of COVID-19 pandemic.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant and the Human Resource and Management Officer to provide the list and duly signed authority of employees who reported to work during pandemic; (b) Head of LDRRMO and Municipal Accountant to explain the inconsistencies in the reported share of  LGU employees in the meals purchased out of the LDRRMF; and (c) Head of LDRRMO and Municipal Budget Officer to ensure the equitable dispensation of similar assistance to the people of the community and that government fund is efficiently and optimally utilized.

We recommended and the LCE concurred that the GAD Focal Point System, in coordination with the Local Planning Development Office, (a) create a GAD database to include gender statistics and other relevant sex-disaggregated data, as support to the evidence-based GAD planning, programming and policy making; and (b) timely submit to the Audit Team the GAD Accomplishment Report; and (c) henceforth strictly comply with the provisions of PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01 and COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the officer/s concerned who were responsible for the receipt, utilization, distribution and inventory of cash and in-kind donations to: (a) comply with Section II.1 (a) of the COA Circular No. 2020-09 by accounting all donations received during the quarantine period; and (b) to submit all the required reports and supporting documents for the donations received from all donors.

We recommended and the LCE concurred to instruct the: (a) Accountant to (i) properly account all COVID-19-related transactions; (ii) submit the minimum documentary requirements for the paid-for meals and snacks; and (b) BAC Secretariat to post the required procurement data for all emergency purchases under the R.A. No. 11469 to promote transparency and accountability.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the: (a) Municipal Treasurer to refrain from drawing cash advances in excess of the approved payroll amount;  (b) MSWDO to (i) fully examine the master list of SAP beneficiaries against the SAP payroll to detect erroneous payment and/or documentary deficiencies; (ii) make necessary actions and corresponding adjustments to the liquidation report; and (iii) call the payees concerned to explain the differences noted in the specimen signatures and inconsistencies in personal information, and take appropriate actions to ensure that the SAP fund was used as intended; and (c) Municipal Accountant to ensure that timely liquidations of funds to source agencies are made.
We recommended and the LCE committed to continue its satisfactory observance of BIR regulations on the withholding and remittance of taxes.
We recommended and the LCE agreed to continuously adhere to the requirements of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor assured the sustained observance of the National Health Insurance Act by withholding and timely remitting all contributions withheld to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation.
We recommended and the LCE agreed to continue adhering to the requirement of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009.
We recommended and the LCE concurred to require all those named liable in the Notice of Disallowance to refund the amount disallowed.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant give utmost priority in analyzing and determining the composition of the long outstanding Inter-Agency Receivables. Otherwise, comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 in requesting for the write-off of the long outstanding receivables.

Require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly signed certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible every beginning of the year for recording purposes; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to record the RPT/SET receivables based on the certified list from the Office of the Treasurer and ensure that the recorded receivables are based on actual reference documents.

We recommended that the Management:
Enforce collection and settlement of the long outstanding receivables and cash advances through issuance of demand letters;
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit a sworn Certification for unliquidated cash advances, and other receivables with no available documents but are not yet considered dormant as defined under COA Circular 2016-005; and
Initiate the preparation and filing of the request for authority to write-off accounts with the COA in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit the collections daily and intact, if practicable, and strictly implement controls over collections and remittances to avoid misappropriations, misuse and/or concealment of irregularities in the handling of cash; and
Instruct the Collectors to religiously observe daily remittance of all their collections, if practicable, together with the issued official receipts, and preparation and submission of the corresponding RCDs to the Treasurer.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant exert more effort in identifying the discrepancies in the balances of the reciprocal accounts, “Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds”, and to effect the necessary adjustments to bring the affected accounts into agreement.

We recommended that the Management advice the Accounting Office to reconcile the difference between the Statement of Cash Flows – General Fund and the Reconciliation of Net Cash Flow in order to present fairly the financial statements and adhere with Section 379 of the Local Government Accounting Manual.

We recommended that the Management:
Submit justification in including the construction of Two-Storey Police Outpost in the utilization of BGCM funds; and
Comply with the provisions of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125 dated April 07, 2020 to address and respond to the COVID-19 emergency.

We recommended that the BAC observe Section 22.1 of IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and justify their failure to conduct the pre-bid conference in the procurement of infrastructure projects goods with an approved budget of One Million (P1,000,000.00) or more.

We recommended that the Management, with the assistance of the Municipal Social Welfare Office, establish specific guidelines in the granting of financial assistance for uniformity and fairness to avoid possible misuse of government funds.

We recommended that the Management stop the practice of advance payment in full for services not yet rendered or goods not yet delivered and strictly comply with the provisions of Section 88 of PD 1445, Section 1 of the Office of the President Memorandum No. 48. and other rules and regulations pertaining to the disbursement of government funds.

We recommended that the Management:
Ensure that the procurement of all relief goods be supported with distribution list of recipients and other supporting documents in the alternative, if the list is not practicable; and
Strictly comply with specific provisions of PD 1445 to safeguard the funds of the Municipality especially in this time of pandemic.

We recommended that the Management require the General Services Officer to maintain a Requisition and Issue Slip for the issuance of all inventories of the Municipality in compliance with Section 189, Volume I of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs to prevent loss and/or wastage of resources of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management require the concerned officials to include or accomplish all the necessary and relevant information required in the Purchase Order so that the supplier will deliver the goods/items in accordance with the terms and conditions of the contract and to attach and accomplish completely an Acceptance and Inspection Report to show whether the goods/items were actually accepted and inspected.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly ensure that payments for fuel, oil and lubricants are duly supported with properly and completely filled up driver’s trip ticket and billing statements from the supplier for proper evaluation and reasonableness of fuel consumption.

We recommended that the Management:
Submit a written justification on the purchase of the high-end cellular phones otherwise, refund such costs; and
Prepare a correcting entry and submit the corresponding JEV on the classification of the said purchased cellular phones to present fairly the affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management:
Submit legal justification in increasing the Pag-IBIG contributions – Government Share for the regular employees instead of monthly contribution of only P100.00 per employee;
Make representation with the HDMF/Pag-IBIG regarding the excess payments; and
Strictly comply with the provision of RA 9679 in observing the maximum limit of PAG-IBIG contribution government share at P100.00 per month per official/employee.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Accountant to:
a. prepare and maintain PPELC for the properties recorded under the Special
Education Fund;
b. prepare a lapsing schedule to compute for the depreciation expenses of all the
recorded PPE and draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the computed amount of
depreciation;
c. draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the misclassified PPE according to its
classification, specification and nature; and
d. draw Journal Entry Voucher to drop the small value property items from the books
by debiting Prior Years’ Adjustment and Accumulated Depreciation of the respective
PPE, and by crediting the respective PPE accounts.

Additionally, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require an annual
physical inventory of all the properties of the Municipality to be conducted by a
committee consisting of representative from the Office of the Municipal Mayor,
Municipal GSO or Municipal Treasurer and Supply Accountable Officer of the
department or office concerned.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Accountant to properly classify the amount due within twelve months
after the reporting date as part of the current liabilities in the Statement of
Financial Position.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of supplies and materials as
inventory following the perpetual inventory method and recognize as
expense when it is already consumed or distributed. Also, maintain a
Supply Ledger Card (SLC) for each inventory item; and
b. General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain stock cards for supplies and
materials purchased and received based on its Acceptance and Inspection
Report (AIR).

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the Trust Liabilities-LDRRMF
subsidiary ledgers per year of transfer and disclose the amount and details of
the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF in the Notes to the Financial
Statements pursuant to Sections 5.1.12 and 5.1.16 of the COA Circular No. 2012-
002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to submit all the lacking
documents to the Auditor immediately to support and justify the payments for
procured infrastructure projects and supplies and materials, for review and
evaluation of the Audit Team; and
b. Municipal Health Officer to ascertain that the requisition for medicines
conform with the Philippine National Drug Formulary (PNDF) Volume I, prior
to procurement, and strictly require from the suppliers the Certificate of
Product Registration (CPR) from the Bureau of Food and Drugs (BFAD and the
General Services Officer to submit the Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR)
to the COA within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of the items delivered
and strictly comply with the COA Circular Nos. 92-386.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Inventory Committee, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to
maintain an Inventory of all Local Roads and prepare Report on the
Physical Count of the Road Network System;
b. General Services Officer to prepare and submit the Report on Local Road
Network at the end of the accounting period; and
c. Municipal Accountant to prepare a lapsing schedule to compute for the
depreciation expenses of all the recorded Road Network Components and
draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the computed amount of
depreciation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to prepare
and submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF duly
reconciled with the accounting records and certified correct by the Municipal
Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month as required
under Section 5.1.5 of the COA Circular No. 2012-002 and the Inventory List of
all procured, donated relief goods, supplies, materials and Disaster Response
and Rescue Equipment at the end of the year in compliance with COA Circular
No. 2014-02.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the General
Services Officer and all concerned department heads to meticulously and
judiciously prepare the Annual Procurement Plan in accordance with Section 7
of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and ensure that all necessary information
required in the APP and its amendments, if any, are reflected therein to serve
as a planning and control tool for efficient procurement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer
and BAC Secretariat to:
a. prepare the monthly utilization reports of BGCM and post the same in
LGU’s website and in at least three conspicuous public places in the
locality at the end of every month; and
b. comply with the posting in the GPPB online portal website as required by
the GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and GPPB Resolution No. 19-2020.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRM Officer to
prepare the 1) Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances
for In-Kind Donations; 2) Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash
Donations; 3) Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of Donated
Relief Goods; and 4) Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of
Donated PPE; and submit the same to the NDRRMC, through the OCD, copy
furnished the Audit Team within ten (10) working days after the end of each
quarter pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2014-002 and 2020-009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer
to specifically allocate one percent (1%) of Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA)
and the members of the Municipal Council for the Protection of Children (MCPC)
to prepare Local Development Plan for Children and Local Investment Plan for
Children, and maximize utilization of the allocated LCPC Fund to identified
PPAs provided for in DILG MC No. 2012-120 dated July 4,2012 to ensure full
strengthening and implementation of the LCPC.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure strict compliance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations on the monitoring, utilization, implementation of COVID-19 related PPAs and reporting of the status of funds. Likewise, we also recommended that the:
BAC to: (i) ensure that all procurements for COVID-19 related PPAs are included in the revised AIPs approved by the local sanggunian, and see to it that all procurements are strictly in accordance with the modes of procurement indicated therein; (ii) submit all the originals/authenticated copies of the lacking documents and proper justifications for non-compliance with the provisions of 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and other COVID-19 related issuances on the procurement of COVID-19-related PPAs to arrive at a decision in audit; and  (iii) henceforth strictly comply with the provisions of the said Revised IRR to establish the validity and regularity of the procurement process and award of contracts/POs; and
Municipal Accountant to: (i) immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking disbursement vouchers and other lacking supporting documents for audit, to arrive at a decision in audit; (ii) check the completeness and propriety of supporting documents prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 and other COVID-19 related issuances before processing claims for payments to establish the legality, validity and propriety of the disbursements; (iii) ensure that purchases of relief goods for distribution are recorded under their proper inventory accounts and that the appropriate expense accounts shall be recognized only upon its issuance; and (iv) prepare the necessary JEV for the proper recording of funds received from BGCM for fair and accurate presentation of the affected accounts in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: (a) Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all PPEs and Inventories of the Municipality, prepare and submit the corresponding RPCI and RPCPPE in accordance with the prescribed format and with complete details, and henceforth ensure that said physical count are strictly conducted annually; (b) Accounting Unit to prepare and maintain updated PPE Ledger Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards for each inventory account; and (c) Designate-GSO to: (i) maintain Stock Cards; and (ii) prepare and submit duly accomplished Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), together with the required supporting documents to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable items.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
Municipal Accountant to: (i) analyze and review the outstanding staled checks and prepare the necessary JEV for the adjustments to present the correct account balances of the Cash in Bank account; (ii) strictly monitor the grant and liquidation of cash advances to ensure that no additional cash advance is granted to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made; (iii) cause the immediate settlement of cash advances to avoid accumulation thereof; and (iv) analyze the unreconciled balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts, prepare a reconciliation statement therein and make the necessary adjustments in the books for fair and accurate presentation of the account balances in the financial statements; and
Municipal Treasurer to: (i) send written notices to the payees one month before the check becomes stale, to inform all payees holding the staled checks to return the same for cancellation and issuance of check replacements, and henceforth ensure that all checks issued are properly monitored, to avoid the accumulation of staled checks. Cancelled staled checks shall be reported and attached to the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) at the period of cancellation; and (ii) withhold the payment of salaries of concerned Officers/Employees, if necessary, to enforce liquidation of their outstanding cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC and the TWG to ensure that all procurement activities undertaken by the Municipality are strictly in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, particularly during the bid evaluation and post-qualification phase, for proper evaluation and assessment of the eligibility as to legal, technical and financial capacities of the prospective bidders.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
BAC to (i) immediately submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team to arrive at a decision in audit, and henceforth ensure that perfected contracts are properly and completely documented before submitting the same to the Audit Team; (ii) invite, in addition to the representative of the COA, at least two (2) qualified observers with knowledge, experience and expertise on the procurement at hand, in all stages of the procurement process, five (5) days before the activity; (iii) post in the PhilGEPS website and in any conspicuous places within the premises of the LGU copies of the Notices of Award, Approved Contracts and Notices to Proceed within the prescribed period; (iv) ensure that the submitted technical and eligibility  requirements were original or certified true copies; (v) submit copies of bid proposals of the other/losing bidders and attendance sheets duly signed by those who attended the pre-procurement  conferences; and (vi) submit perfected contracts, together with all supporting documents, within five (5) working days after its approval;
The Municipal Engineer to closely monitor the project implementation and ensure that construction schedules are strictly followed to ascertain that projects are implemented efficiently and effectively in accordance with the approved plans and specifications within the contract duration; and
Municipal Accountant to (a) immediately submit the required supporting documents and proper justification for the deficiencies cited to be able to arrive at a decision in audit and henceforth, ensure that claims are duly supported with complete and proper documentation before processing claims for payments to establish the validity and propriety of transactions; and (b) ensure that accounting forms are properly accomplished and complete with essential information.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the MDRRM Council:                    (a) prepare the LDRRMFIP which shall include Continuing Appropriations under LDRRMF; (b) ensure the optimal utilization of the MDRRMF, including the unexpended balances from previous years, specially, during times of calamities to enhance the Municipality’s resilience and capacity to reduce vulnerabilities when exposed to hazards and calamities; and (c) refrain from utilizing the fund for purposes outside the mandate of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that purchases of relief items, supplies and materials are recorded under the appropriate Inventory accounts and recognize the appropriate expense accounts only upon its issuance. The inventory accounts shall be properly monitored using the Supplies Ledgers Card (SLCs) pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-002.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to: (a) instruct the MSWMB and MESWMO Designate to immediately submit the revised SWMP and coordinate with the DENR to fast track the review of the SWMP for the eventual approval of the National Solid Waste Management Commission; and (b) henceforth hasten the implementation of all Solid Waste Management-related programs/project/activities in order to enforce proper sorting, segregation, composting, recycling and disposal of waste materials.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the: (a) MPDC to plan the procurement and implementation of the identified priority projects for the optimal utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund to spur development, provide jobs to constituents and to achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU so that the intended users/beneficiaries may enjoy the immediate benefits derived from said development projects; and (b) ensure that all PPAs to be included in the expenditure program for 20 percent Development Fund are strictly in compliance with DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 dated November 4, 2020.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the GAD Focal Point Person, strictly observe the prescribed timelines for GAD Planning and Budgeting, as provided in the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016, to enable its timely submission to the PPDO to check its alignment with the priorities of the Province and then to the DILG Provincial Office for final review and approval.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to monitor and remit promptly the amount Due to BIR and adhere strictly to the provisions of Sections 4.114(c) and 2.58(a)(2) of BIR Revenue Regulations 2-98, as amended by Section 5 of Revenue Regulations 17-2003, to avoid incurrence of interests and/or penalties for delayed remittance.

We recommended for the settlement of the audit suspension within 90 days from receipt of the Notice of Suspension, pursuant to Section 9.4 of the Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts (RRSA). The head of the agency is responsible for ensuring that the requirements of transactions suspended in audit are complied with.

1.10 We recommended the following to the management:
Members of the Inventory Committee:
To fast tract the reconciliation of those property items included in the inventory report with the records being maintained by the Accounting and Property/Treasurer’s Office since its existence and condition have already been established. As provided under COA Circular 2020-005, the reconciliation shall be completed within ten (10) days from rendition of the RPCPPE by the Inventory Committee.
To exert their best efforts in completing the required physical inventory taking of properties owned by the LGU and update the RPCPPE with the data of those properties not included in the previous inventory report as the detailed procedures on how to conduct which are specified above.
Further, after the updated RPCPPE have been made in accordance with the prescribed format, reconciliation thereof shall be made with that of the accounting unit’s report to establish reliability and fair valuation of the PPE accounts within ten (10) days from the submission of completed RPCPEE.
Moreover, those missing PPES established during the physical count, demand letters are to be issued to those AO’s and cause the conduct of an investigation to determine who are responsible for the loss, among others.
Municipal Treasurer and Accountant
To maintain Property Card and Equipment Ledger Cards, respectively, for easy determination identification and reconciliation of records.

2.9 We recommended that management follow the accounting and reporting guidelines in the recognition of the various components of the Road Network as stated in COA Circular No. 2015-008 in order to ensure that the Road Network System of the municipality is properly identified, valued and accounted for in the books.

To enhance its inventory control mechanism, we recommended to Management the following:
Require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to retrieve all disbursement vouchers related to the above inventories in order to establish itemized records of the said inventories. Based thereto, validate who were the end-users thereof and require the submission of RIS and the same prepare the SSMI summarizing all the receipts and issuances thereof.
Accounting Office to prepare the JEV to derecognize from the books the inventories on the basis of the SSMI submitted by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.
Require the Accounting and Treasurer’s Office to maintain SLC and SC, respectively, for each item of stocks on the basis of the disbursement vouchers and to update and reconcile records, regularly.

4.10 We recommended the following to the management:
Municipal Accountant
To record the remaining items of the relief goods as Inventory as stated below:
Welfare Goods for Distribution		149,461.90
Drugs and Medicines for Distribution		106,530.00
Prior Period Adjustments       255,991.90
To record purchases under the Inventory account  on the basis of the Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR) and other supporting documents such as Purchase Orders (POs), Delivery Receipts a/or Supplier’s Invoice.
To record expenses on the basis of the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) together with the supporting RIS.
Municipal Treasurer as the General Services Officer
To consolidate the RIS in the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued and submit the same to the Office of the Municipal Accountant for proper recording of its issuances in order to reflect the correct Inventory and Expense balances.
Requisitioning Office/Departments
To refrain from attaching RIS in the disbursement vouchers as one of the supporting documents for such shall only be used upon issuance of a particular item carried in stocks.

5.7 We recommended that management to direct the personnel concerned to provide necessary data on the transactions presented as to minimum information such as  list of names of who received the food packs, its corresponding quantity for every meal and other related information as mentioned in the preceding paragraphs in order to establish proper liquidation on the disbursements incurred and to present adequate information that can establish the actions taken that transpired during the distribution of food packs.

We recommended the following to the management:
Focal Person assigned to Distribution of Relief Goods
To prepare RIS upon issuances for distribution to end-users/beneficiaries of relief goods for proper control and monitoring thereof.
Municipal Treasurer as the General Services Officer
To prepare and maintain an inventory list of all procured items before repacking of goods and corresponding Stock Cards for purposes of check and balance. Likewise, submit an explanation/justification on the noted variances.
Municipal Accountant
6.14.3 To prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for all procured relief goods out of appropriations.

7.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and officials concerned to ensure that financial assistance extended to the barangays for the implementation of MRF be supported by the above-mentioned documentary requirements to provide basis for  monitoring the proper use of funds.
7.8 Further recommended that the Municipal Accountant make sure that fund transfers be recorded in the Due from LGUs account instead of Subsidy so that the municipality can monitor the implementation of the proposed PPAs.

9.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:
To exert their best efforts in retrieving and locating the disbursement vouchers of the concerned debtors/employees who have long outstanding cash advances in order to validate the schedule enumerating the names and amounts of the said cash advances.
Conduct thorough review, with the involvement of other concerned personnel, and analyze the details of receivables to ascertain their correctness, validity and collectability and send demand letters to persons who have known address to facilitate collections;
Prepare request for write-off to the Commission on Audit the account balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected duly supported with certified relevant documents pertaining to the accounts pursuant with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

10. 8 We recommended that management to monitor the implementation of MRF forBarangays granted with financial assistance. Moreover, require the preparation of POW as one of the requirements in releasing financial assistance in order to ensure appropriate structure with enough corresponding appropriation. Also to prepare MOA on the financial resources incorporating therein the prohibition of construction any structures in a privately-owned lot to avoid future legal issues.

11. 9 We recommended to the members of the BAC the following:
To cause the submission of required Mayor’s Permit in compliance of the above-quoted provision.
Post all   procurement of goods and supplies related to COVID-19 relative to the Procurement Projects undertaken in the GPPB Online Portal the necessary data as enumerated in the GPPB Circular issuances. These data or information shall be used by the GPPB and its Technical Support Office to comply with the reportorial requirements under the Bayanihan Act.
Ensure that the form/content of the Omnibus Sworn Statement (OSS) be in accordance with the afore-cited GPPB Circular and contains the name of the procurement undertaken and must be an original copy. This OSS is required in lieu of the performance and warranty securities required for Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act.

We recommended that:
a. the GSO designate/ Municipal Treasurer cause the transfer of donated properties to the concerned agencies by accomplishing the required transfer documents and submit copy of the same to the accounting office for the necessary derecognition of said properties.
b. the GSO designate/ Municipal Treasurer cause the immediate disposal of all unserviceable properties which are beyond economic repair using the required format.
c. the Office of the Municipal Engineer furnish the accounting office a copy of the road network inventory as basis for reclassification in the books of accounts .
d. the General Services Officer designate/ Municipal Treasurer maintain and update property cards for all PPEs and regularly renew their corresponding Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment (ARE)/Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR).
e. management require the inventory committee to conduct annual physical inventory of all municipal properties and submit a report using the prescribed format of the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) as basis for the reconciliation of records between the accounting and GSO.

ARE/PAR were
renewed,
however, property
ledger cards were
not yet fully

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to disclose the details of the LDRRMF and Road Networks account using the pro-forma disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements provided in COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2015-008 starting CY 2020.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record the necessary reclassification entries in the books of the LGU to reflect the correct balance of the affected liability accounts .
observations.managementAction: Adjusted in the books in June 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record the necessary adjustment in the books of the LGU in accordance with COA Circular No 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: Adjusted in the books on June 30, 2020.

Job order was institutionalized
The deployment of Job Orders were duly assessed by the Administrative Office.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the MESWMB coordinate with the barangays relative to their plans and programs in compliance with RA No. 9003 to identify the needs in order to effectively implement solid waste management programs of the Municipality and consider the clustering of barangays to address the lack of fund in putting up their MRF.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: Not all barangays have established MRF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit copies of contracts to the audit team within 5 days after perfection in accordance with pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with our audit recommendations
observations.reason: Contracts for the procurement of goods through alternative mode of procurement were not submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the 

(a) Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer and Municipal Assessor to (i) exert diligent effort to locate the titles of the other lands listed in the Summary of Owned Lands prepared by the Office of the Assessor; 




(ii) take immediate action to register and secure the title of the subject parcels of land in the name of the municipality; and 

(iii) regularly reconcile the accounting and assessor’s records vis-à-vis the ownership documents kept at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to ensure the reliability of the reported land assets of the municipality; and  

(b) Municipal Budget Officer to allocate funds for expenses that may be incurred in the titling of these lands.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer/ Property Officer and the Municipal Assessor already started coordinating with Registry of Deeds during the first quarter of CY 2020
observations.reason:
Not Implemented -– The restrictions in movement due to the pandemic hindered the management to complete the coordination with Registry of Deeds on titling of untitled lands.

Partially Implemented





Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Local Development Council, the Sangguniang Bayan and the Municipal Budget Officer to continue adhering with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-01 and use the 20% Development Fund solely for development projects and programs that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures with the end view of attaining desirable socio economic and environmental management outcomes.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:

(a) refrain from using DRRMF on PPAs which are not directly supportive of disaster risk management; and 

(b) continue adhering with the guidelines and minimum standards as enumerated under the Disaster Preparedness Volume 2 pursuant to R.A. No. 10121.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor concurred to

(a) implement the ordinance imposing charges/penalties on violators of related SWM regulations, especially on its “No segregation, no collection” policy; and 

(b) continue the advances already achieved in solid waste management.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the GAD Focal Person: to 

(a) continue preparing and implementing GAD Budget and Plan pursuant to the pertinent laws and regulations on GAD; and

 (b) monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs to ensure adherence to the milestones and timelines set forth in the GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to perform the necessary accounting of the fiscal transactions of the municipality’s economic enterprises pursuant to R.A. No. 7160 and Manual on NGAS for LGUs, with the end view of promoting accountability and transparency in their operation.
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor committed to continue its satisfactory observance of BIR regulations on the withholding and remittance of taxes.
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to continuously adhere to the requirements of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor assured the sustained observance of the National Health Insurance Act by withholding and timely remitting all contributions withheld to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation.
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to continue adhering to the requirement of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Prepare and submit monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements of all its depository bank accounts, take up reconciling items, and effect adjustment in the GL to ensure the correct balance of the Cash in Bank accounts; and 

Exert more time and effort to trace, verify and reconcile regularly the book balances of the Cash in Bank account against the bank records including reconciliation with the cashbook maintained by the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reconciled the balance through the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management comply strictly with the prescribed controls over PPE as provided for in pertinent laws, rules and regulations and require the following:
The Inventory Team/Committee to conduct an inventory of all the properties of the Municipality and reconcile the related accounting and property records. Discrepancies and/or deficiencies, if any, should be analyzed/investigated. Likewise, submit a Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the Audit Team concerned as required under Section 66 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System.

The General Services Officer to renew the Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment every three years or every time there is a change in accountability to establish who is in actual possession or custody of all government properties in accordance with Section 56, Vol. II of the New Government Accounting System Manual.

The Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to maintain Property, Plant & Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) for each category of property, plant and equipment and post all transactions promptly from source documents and reconcile their records regularly in order to establish the accuracy of balance of each PPE account presented in the financial statements as of a certain date.
observations.managementAction: Management created an Inventory Committee, which conducted a physical inventory of the municipal properties and equipment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
Require the Liquidating Officer to maintain a separate cashbook for each fund  and deposit the proceeds accruing to each fund in their respective depository bank accounts in compliance with Sections 309 and 310 of RA No. 7160;
Reconcile the book balances with the cash on hand daily and with the Accounting Office; and
Instruct the Liquidating Officer to properly fill up the entries required in recording in the cashbook and to foot and close the books at the end of each month.
observations.managementAction:
Management started to maintain a separate cashbook for each fund and deposits each fund in their respective depository bank accounts.

Management reconciled the Report of Collections monthly with the assigned bookkeeper.

The Liquidating Officer properly filled up the required entries in the cashbook, and footed and closed the books at the end of each month.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:

Stop the practice of selling CTC receipts to the barangays, instead authorize them to act as deputized collectors in compliance with Section 334(a) of RA No. 7160;
Monitor the CTC receipts issued to barangays and require them to remit the collections therefrom; and 
Submit a duly accounted list of receipts to reconcile with the receipts presented during the cash examination for verification.
observations.managementAction: The Management has prepared the list of receipts to reconcile with the receipts presented during the cash examination for final verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Liquidating Officer certify their cashbooks as to the correctness and completeness of the recorded transactions at month-end after the last entry.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer is now certifying as to the correctness and completeness of the recorded transactions at the end of the month after the last entry in the cashbook.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer always update his cashbooks by recording all cash advances daily in order to reflect/establish the accountability at a given date.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer is already updating his cashbooks by recording all cash advances daily.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the monthly Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms and his own monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms. The collecting officers are also required to prepare their Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms on a monthly basis. The CRAAF, together with all the RAAFs, should be submitted to the Office of the Auditor not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month pursuant to Section 51 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume II.
observations.managementAction:
Weekly report of collections for accountable forms of every collecting officer has been prepared and the same will be remitted to the Liquidating Officer for verification and correction.

All collectors are advised to prepare their monthly consolidated report of accountability for Accountable forms as basis for the Liquidating Officer to monitor and the same copy will be submitted to the Office of the Auditor

All collections for the week received from all collecting officers will be deposited to the corresponding depository bank of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountable Officers properly observe the immediate renewal of fidelity bond before its expiration as required by the abovementioned regulation. In the absence of their fidelity bond, they should not be allowed to collect funds for the government.
observations.managementAction: The Treasury Office immediately processed the Fidelity bonds of each Accountable Officer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

Instruct the Field Collectors to remit their collections daily to the Liquidating Officer, who will prepare the Report of Collections and submit the same to the Municipal Treasurer; and

Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit the collections daily and intact to avoid possibility of theft, mishandling and misuse.
observations.managementAction: The Liquidating Officer will collect weekly all collections of every collector and immediately remit to the Municipal Treasurer for deposit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Liquidating Officer religiously reconcile the recorded amounts reflected in her cashbook to avoid the possibility of occurrence of shortage or overage in compliance with Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: The Liquidating Officer made sure that all collections received are remitted to the Municipal Treasurer to avoid discrepancies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management ensure that all contract agreements be supported with complete documentary requirements prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012 – 001. Further, we recommended that the lacking documents above mentioned be submitted immediately to facilitate the review of the procurement.
observations.managementAction: The BAC submitted the said lacking documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: 

Submit the lacking documents and ensure that all claims against government funds are supported with all the required documentations;
Sign/certify the computation of the terminal leave benefits; and
Refrain from processing and approving payments with incomplete documents to avoid suspension /disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will submit the lacking documents and no longer process/approve payment with incomplete documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

Require the submission of the DTR, Application for leave and other supporting documents to ascertain the correctness of salaries and allowances paid to each employee; and
Effective immediately, instruct the HRMO to undertake the payroll preparation for proper internal control procedure and instruct the Treasury Office to properly turn-over said task to the HRMO.
observations.managementAction: The Management required the submission of DTRs and applications for leave from the employees. The preparation of payroll was immediately turn-over to the HRMO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the payroll deduction be limited following the order of preference provided in the GAA and ensure that an employee’s net take home pay shall not be less than the minimum allowed take home pay of P5,000.00 as provided under Section 47 of the General Provisions of GAA of 2020.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee:
Submit justification on declaring the lone bidder eligible on the foregoing infrastructure projects despite insufficient NFCC as per Section 23.4.2.6 of IRR of RA No. 9184; and 

Carefully examine/verify/validate all the statements and documents submitted by the bidder with the Lowest/Single Calculated Bid to ensure compliance with and its responsiveness to all the requirements and the conditions as specified in the Bidding Documents.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Immediately transfer the unexpended balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE totaling to P1,703,982.57 to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability – DRRM” as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-002; and

Subsidiary Ledgers of the unutilized DRRMF to the Special Trust Fund be maintained.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant immediately transferred the unutilized Balances of Quick Response Fund and Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund – Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (DRRMF-MOOE) to the Special Trust Fund and maintained a Subsidiary Ledger of unutilized DRRMF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management: 

Direct the MDRRM Council to prepare the LDRRMF Investment Plan for CY 2020 in consonance with Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular 2012-002;

Review and update annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan to incorporate the PPAs to be funded by the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years; and 

Strengthen the capabilities of its Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office to enable it to perform its mandated functions under Section 4, Rule 6, IRR of R.A. 10121, especially in the formulation of an LDRRM Investment Plan, as guide in the utilization of the LDRRMF and proper monitoring of the fund.
observations.managementAction: The Management already prepared a LDRRMF Investment Plan inculcating all the recommendations of the Auditor

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management immediately initiate action to enforce settlement of audit suspensions pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office submitted documents evidencing the settlement of the suspension.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to the officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances and demand the immediate settlement thereof; and stop granting another cash advance to officers and employees with unliquidated cash advances. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should not process the cash advance of officials other than  for official travel and strictly comply with provisions of Section 339 of RA 7160 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 24, 2012.
observations.managementAction:
The Management no longer grants cash advance to employees with unliquidated cash advance. And cash advances other than travelling expenses are granted to employees who are properly bonded.

Various cash advances remained unliquidated.  
However, percentage of liquidation of previous years’ cash advances that remained outstanding was very minimal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose the noted deficiencies in the Notes to Financial Statements of the Municipality as required by IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The disclosure requirements were not fully observed.  Only some noted deficiencies in CY 2018 were disclosed in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to Management:

To instruct the Field Collectors to remit their collections daily to the Liquidating Officer, who will prepare the Report of Collections and submit the same to the Municipal Treasurer;

To instruct the Liquidating Officer and the Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of using their collections to pay various payrolls and/or other expenses of the LGU; and

To require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit the collections daily and intact to avoid possibility of theft, mishandling and misuse of the amount collected.
observations.managementAction:
All collectors were instructed to prepare the report of collections to be submitted to the Municipal Treasurer. 

Collections by the Field Collectors were not remitted daily to the Liquidating Officer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to the Management:

To require the Municipal Treasurer to liquidate his cash advances immediately after the pay period;

To instruct the Accounting Office to strictly observe the granting of cash advances only if previous cash advances are properly liquidated and process the same within two (2) to three (3) days before the pay period; and

To require the Accounting Office to strictly monitor the liquidation of cash advances granted to the Municipal Treasurer and to submit status of cash advances to the COA on a quarterly basis.
observations.managementAction:
Cash advance is not granted to any official or employee unless previous cash advance has been liquidated.

Various cash advances still remained unliquidated at year end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit all the required supporting documents. Likewise, it is recommended that the Management require the Accountant to ensure that all the Disbursement Vouchers are supported with the documentary requirements, as enumerated in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 24, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant scrutinized the supporting documents attached to every disbursements before payments are made.  However, verification disclosed that some disbursements are not supported with complete documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management refrain from procuring goods and services on a reimbursement basis, except petty purchases to be paid out from the Petty Cash Fund.
observations.managementAction:
The Management limited the purchase of goods and services on reimbursement basis.

Some purchases of goods or services were still made through reimbursement basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately transfer the unexpended balance of the DRRMF to a special trust fund under the account “Trust Liability – DRRM” as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-002. Consequently, the account shall be used to record transfers of the agency’s unutilized QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years, receipts of NDRRMF, DRRMF from other LGUs and other sources. We further recommend that subsidiary ledgers for the transfer of the agency’s unutilized DRRMF to the special trust fund be maintained.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant has transferred the CY 2018 unexpended balance of the DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund and prepared and maintained the subsidiary ledger for the transfer of fund.

The unexpended balance of the QRF and DRRM-MOOE for CY 2019 were not transferred to a Special Trust Fund under the account Trust-Liability-DRRM.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer fast track the submission of the Solid Waste Management Plan to the Environment Management Bureau to be forwarded to the National Solid Waste Management Commission and ensure that the programs and activities included in the plan are consistent with the National Solid Waste Management Framework.
observations.managementAction: The Management had just submitted their Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan (ESWMP) to Environment Management Bureau Regional Office No. 02 last March 29, 2019 for them to review and submit said Plan to the National Solid Waste Management Commission.

The Municipal Accountant assured full compliance with the audit recommendations.
The Municipal Accountant explained that his office has no records of the details of PPE under the SEF when he assumed the position but he assured that the PPELC will be prepared and maintained. Also, the Management mentioned that they already created an Inventory Committee to expedite the physical inventory and reconciliation of PPE records.
observations.reason:
None Not Implemented
The Municipal Accountant explained that he had already prepared and maintained Subsidiary Ledgers for each fund transfers and the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books were already made. However, the Municipality diverted their attention on the COVID-19 Pandemic which happened in CY 2020 hence, all of its personnel were busy handling the pandemic.
This was reiterated under Part II, Pages 21 to 25 of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality of Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to record all the valid reconciling items to reflect the correct year-end balances of the Cash in Bank - LCCA.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already drawn a Journal Entry Voucher to record all the valid reconciling items to reflect the correct year-end balances of the Cash in Bank-LCCA.

The Petty Cash Custodian liquidated the Petty Cash fund and returned the unused cash to the Municipal Treasurer and returned the unused cash to the Municipal Treasurer at the end of the year thus, overstating both the PCF and the expense account by the amount of unrecorded expense. The Petty Cash Custodian had liquidated the Petty Cash fund and returned the unused cash to the Municipal Treasurer at the end of the year and the Municipal Accountant drawn a JEV to record the expenses accounting entries and credited the petty cash accounts. The Municipal Treasurer had prepared and maintained the cashbook for PCF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. make necessary adjustments/corrections in the books to present the correct balance of the affected accounts; and
b. exercise due care in the recording and classifying the financial transactions of the Municipality to ensure that the balances of the reciprocal accounts are the same at all times.
