observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already made the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books. The Municipal Accountant ensured that all transactions were recorded properly to ensure that the balances of the reciprocal accounts are the same at all times.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. provide and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for each fund transfer;
b. exert extra effort to locate the documents and information on the details of the various fund transfers from different NGAs amounting to P69,793.36; and
c. prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to correct the classification of receipts from Philippine Coconut Authority and Laguna Lake Development Authority to their proper account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared and maintained Subsidiary Ledgers for each fund transfers and drawn a JEV to correct the classification of receipts from PCA and LLDA to their proper account.
observations.reason: The gathering of information on the details of the various fund transfers is on-going. The gathering of information on the details of the various fund transfers is not yet done due to numerous workloads of the accounting office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. draw journal entry for the transfer of the unexpended balance of the Quick Response Fund and the transfer of the unexpended balance of CY 2019 to the Special Trust Fund of the Trust Fund; and
b. prepare and maintain the Trust Liabilities-LDRRMF subsidiary ledgers per year of transfer and disclose the details of the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statements pursuant to Sections 5.1.12 and 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already drawn a JEV to record the transfer of the unexpended balance of the Quick Response Fund and the transfer of the unexpended balance of CY 2019. The Municipal Accountant had prepared and maintained the Trust Liabilities-LDRRMF subsidiary ledgers.
observations.reason: The transfer of the unexpended balance of CY 2020 was not transferred to the Special Trust Fund. The Municipal Accountant was not able to disclose the amount and details of the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statements. The required Subsidiary Ledgers that will be the basis for disclosure are not yet prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose the required information in the Notes to Financial Statements to assist the users in understanding the financial statements and to provide them with the additional information value.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant disclosed the required information in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately to the Auditor the duly approved disbursement vouchers of the above transactions totaling P2,935,269.17 to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit. Henceforth, ensure that all payments/disbursements are supported with duly approved disbursement vouchers and supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has submitted the duly signed disbursement vouchers and ensured that all payments are supported with duly approved disbursement documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to immediately submit all the lacking documents to the Auditor to support and justify the payments of infrastructures projects and to refrain from approving payments of disbursements with incomplete documentation. We also recommended that the official driver of the vehicle submit the serially numbered and duly accomplished drivers’ trip tickets for payment of gasoline totaling P607,952.70 to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit and strictly implement the guidelines on the use of government vehicles stated in COA Circular No. 77-61 in order to minimize wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption and to properly utilize the government motor transportation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has recovered and had submitted the lacking documents. The Municipal Accountant submitted the lacking documents and the officer-in-charge of the vehicle submitted the serially numbered driver's trip tickets for payment of gasoline and ensured that issuance of driver's trip tickets is controlled by the officer-in-charge at the office of the Municipal Mayor.

The Officer-in-Charge of the MSWDO submitted their justification letter on granting the financial assistance to the seven beneficiaries totaling P33,000.00. The Officer-in-Charge of the MSWDO submitted their justification letter on granting the financial assistance. The MSWDO submitted a draft of policies on the grant of financial assistance containing the eligibility and the uniform amount to be granted for each type of assistance to the Committee on Social Welfare. The Sangguniang Bayan had enacted ordinances providing guidelines on granting financial assistance. The Municipal Accountant ensured the completeness of the required supporting documents.
observations.reason: The submitted draft of policies on the grant of financial assistance is still subject for approval of Committee on Social Welfare.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials to:
a. explain in writing why the implementation of the supplemental project was not simultaneously implemented with the construction of the NCDC;
b. explain why the Municipality was not able to seek the concurrence of the ECCD Council prior to the final payment to the contractor; and
c. request from the Sangguniang Bayan the appropriate budget for the construction of playground for the National Child Development Center to fully comply with the remaining conditions of the MOA to avoid possible withdrawal of additional grants and assistance from ECCD Council.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer submitted the supplemental justification letter on why the implementation of the supplemental project was not simultaneously implemented with the construction of the NCDC and the Municipality was not able to seek the concurrence of the ECCD Council prior to the final payment to the contractor. The required playground for the National Child Development Center was already complied by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the barangay officials to strictly implement the Ordinances on waste management within their respective barangays by making the community aware of the ordinances and the sanctions for non-compliance.
observations.managementAction: The barangay officials started to enforce the Municipal Ordinances on waste management and implemented the ecological solid waste management systems within their respective barangays and made the community aware of the ordinances and the sanctions for non-compliance.
observations.reason: The construction of MRF in Barangay Poblacion was not yet implemented due to lack of location.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the concerned officials stop the practice of charging to the 20 percent Development Fund projects and expenses which are not included in Section 3 and are not allowed in Section 4 of the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1, respectively. Moreover, fully adhere with the provisions of the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in identifying the projects to be appropriated and implemented against the 20 percent DF.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to adhere with the provisions of the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in identifying the projects to be appropriated and implemented against the 20 percent DF. In the acquired land, two projects were constructed such as National Early Childhood Care and Development Center and a new Multi-purpose Hall named as Rizal Centennial Civic Center. Likewise, the Rizal Municipal Police Department and Bureau of Fire Protection Stations were constructed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to:
a. enforce collection of unpaid rentals and surcharges to unpaid rentals and otherwise cancel the contract of lease to avoid further loss from uncollectible receivables; and
b. strictly observe the pertinent provisions in the Municipal Revenue Code on the regular payment of monthly rentals and imposition of penalties and sanctions for late payment. Also ensure execution/renewal of contract yearly.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to strictly observe the pertinent provisions in the Municipal Revenue Code. The designated Market Administrator had managed the execution if the contract lease between the Municipality and all stallholders.
observations.reason: The delinquent stallholders only paid the partial amount of their unpaid rentals.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: (a).Designate-GSO and  Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all PPEs and Inventories of the Municipality, prepare and submit the corresponding RPCI and RPCPPE, and henceforth ensure that said physical count is strictly and timely conducted every year; (b) Designate-GSO and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of RPCPPE and RPCI with  the corresponding ledger balances;          (c) Designate-GSO to: (i).maintain Property Cards and Stock Cards; and (ii) fast track the preparation of Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), together with the required supporting documents to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable items; and (d) Accounting Unit to maintain an updated PPE Ledger Cards (PPELCs) and prepare Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs) for each inventory account.
observations.managementAction:
The Inventory Committee conducted physical count and submitted the RPCPPEs.

Designate-GSO and Municipal Accountant has already reconciled RPCPPE with the corresponding ledger balances.

IIRUP as of December 31, 2020 was submitted to the audit team upon request on May 4, 2021.
observations.reason:
RPCPPE was submitted late due to failure to pay attention with the guidelines in the preparation and submission of RPCPPE and letter of the Audit Team requesting the submission of the said report.

RPCI was not submitted for audit due to the failure of the GSO to conduct the physical count of all inventories as of CY 2020.

The Designate-GSO and Municipal Accountant still failed to maintain proper and complete perpetual inventory records such as Stock Cards, PPELCs and SLCs for all the properties of the Municipality due to Accounting Unit was still updating the PPELCs while the  Designate-GSO inadvertently disregarded the preparation of SLCs. 

Unserviceable properties has increased from P18,395,985.98 to P20,948,397.10 and were still not disposed due to the absence of necessary details required for proper verification of properties included in the said report and failure to create Disposal Committee.

The observations were reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
In view thereof, we recommended that the:

Municipal Accountant: (i).analyze and review the outstanding stale checks and prepare the necessary JEV for the adjustments to present the correct account balances of the Cash and Cash Equivalents and Accounts Payable; (ii) strictly monitor the grant and liquidation of cash advances to ensure that no additional cash advance is granted to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made; (iii) cause the immediate settlement of cash advances to avoid accumulation thereof for the accurate presentation of the related expenses and receivables account; and         (iv) analyze the unreconciled balances of the Due from and Due to Other Funds accounts, prepare a reconciliation statement therein and make the necessary adjustments in the books for the affected funds for fair and accurate presentation of the account balances in the financial statements; and

Municipal Treasurer: (i) send written notices to the payees one (1) month before the check becomes stale as required in Section 59 of the NGAS Manual. Cancelled stale checks shall be reported and attached to the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) at the period of cancellation; and            (ii) withheld the payment of salaries of concerned AOs to enforce liquidation of their outstanding cash advances.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (i) cause the return of the unexpended balances of the Due to NGAs totaling P552,811.06 to the respective Source Agencies for proper presentation of the Due to NGAs account in the Financial Statements and/or make representations with the source agency for the possible reprogramming of the unexpended balances to other PPAs; and (ii) impose liquidated damages and/or institute appropriate legal actions/charges against the contractor who abandoned the projects amounting to P4,800,000.00, and henceforth ensure that projects funded out of grants from the NGAs are completed in accordance with the approved plans within the target date of completion.
observations.managementAction:
Stale checks totaling P21,140.00 were already replaced and issued to payees.

Advances to Officers and Employees as at December 2019 amounting to P49,896.76 were already settled in CY 2020.

Out of the unused balances of Due to NGAS as of CY 2019 amounting to P552,811.06, P322,702.60 were already remitted to the Source Agencies.
observations.reason:
Thirty-six (36) stale checks totaling P327,837.31 were still not cancelled and remained unadjusted in the books due to the failure of the Municipal Treasurer to contact/inform payees to return the staled checks for cancellation and replacement and to monitor the issuance of check to avoid the accumulated of staled checks.

Cash advances granted during CY 2020 totaling P54,592.07 remained unliquidated as at year-end due to failure of the Accounting Unit to inform/send demand letters to Officers and Employees for the settlement of their unliquidated cash advances.

The balances of reciprocal accounts, Due from and Due to Other Funds as at December 31, 2020 totaling P79,422.72 and P82,818.94, respectively, were not reconciled, having a difference of P(3,396.22) due to failure of the Accounting Unit to analyze the unreconciled balances, prepare reconciliation and 
make the necessary adjustments in the books.
 
The Management still failed to file necessary actions against the contractor who abandoned the projects totaling P4,800,000.00.

Hence, this observation is reiterated in the Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the: (a) MPDC for the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund to spur development, provide jobs to constituents and to achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU so that the intended users/beneficiaries may enjoy the immediate benefits that may be derived from the said development projects. Also, ensure that all projects included in the expenditure program for 20% Development Fund are strictly in compliance with DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017; and (b).consider the hiring of additional staff for the Engineering Department, if warranted, to fast track the preparation of the Program of Works, Project Designs and Cost Estimates to avoid delays in the procurement activities and project implementation.

We also recommended the Municipal Accountant to: (a).immediately submit the required supporting documents to the Audit Team to be able to arrive at a decision in audit; (b).ensure that claims are duly supported with complete and proper documentation before processing for payment to establish the validity and propriety of transactions; and (c).ensure that accounting forms are properly accomplished and complete with essential information.
observations.managementAction:
Several unsubmitted documents in previous audit observation were already submitted by the management in CY 2020.

After the receipt of AOM in CY 2020 by the Accounting Unit, most of the documentary requirements/accounting such as PR, PO and IAR were filled-up with essential information.
observations.reason:
Due to COVID-19 pandemic, implementation of PPAs funded under the 20% DF, GF and LDRRMF were put on hold and concentrated more on the COVID-19 PPAs to contain/curtail the spread of infections among the constituents.

Disbursements totaling P6,147,331.59 were still not supported with complete and proper documentations.

The observations were reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the MDRRM Council:             (a).ensure the optimal utilization of the MDRRMF, including the unexpended balances from previous years, specially, during times of calamities to enhance the Municipality’s resilience and capacity to reduce vulnerabilities when exposed to hazards and calamities; and (b) refrain from utilizing the fund for purposes outside the mandate of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (a) ensure that purchases of relief items, supplies and materials are recorded under the appropriate Inventory accounts and recognize the appropriate expense accounts only upon its issuance. The inventory accounts shall be properly monitored using the Supplies Ledgers Card (SLCs) pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-002; (b) immediately submit the required supporting documents to the Audit Team to be able to arrive at a decision in audit, and henceforth ensure that claims are duly supported with complete and proper documentation before processing for payment to establish the validity and propriety of transactions; and (c) ensure that accounting forms are properly accomplished and complete with essential information.
observations.managementAction:
The amount of P10,983,062.18 or an overall financial delivery rate of 73.25 percent  was expended out of the CY 2020 current and continuing appropriations and grants from LGU and other sources totaling P14,994,551.38.

Several documentary requirements such as PR and IAR were already properly filled-up by the Accounting Office.
observations.reason: The Management did not submit explanation for the deficiencies cited, hence this is reiterated in the Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to: (a) include the President of the Association of Barangay Councils in the Municipality, the Chairperson of the Sangguniang Kabataan Federation and designate each representative from the recycling industry and manufacturing or packaging industry in the composition of the MESWMB; and (b) instruct the designated MESWMO to make representations and coordination with the DENR to fast track the review of the SWMP for the eventual approval of the National Solid Waste Management Commission .

We also recommended that the designated MESWMO coordinate with the Engineering Office and Bids and Awards Committee for the immediate implementation of the project, Construction of Materials Recovery Facilities for 11 barangays within the Municipality, to enforce proper sorting, segregation, composting and recycling of waste materials.
observations.managementAction: The composition of the MESWMB was already compliant with the requirements of Section 5, Rule IV of IRR Ra No. 9003.
observations.reason:
The revised SWMP was not yet submitted to the Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR)-Environment Management Bureau (EMB) for review and was still not approved by the NSWMC due to delayed incorporation of changes/revisions in the existing SWMP. Also, the project, Construction of Materials Recovery Facilities for 11 barangays totaling P1,100,000.00 was still on-going as of December 31, 2020, due late implementation of the said project thus, the construction of MRF started only in the 4th quarter of CY 2020.

The observations were reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC and the TWG to ensure that all procurement activities undertaken by the Municipality are strictly in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, particularly during the bid evaluation and post-qualification phase, for proper evaluation and assessment of the eligibility as to, legal, technical and financial capacities of the prospective bidders.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

BAC and its TWG to immediately submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team to arrive at a decision in audit, and henceforth ensure that perfected contracts are properly and completely documented before submitting the same to the Audit Team; and

Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking supporting documents in the claims for payment of the projects, and henceforth ensure that all succeeding payments are supported with complete and proper documentations as required under Sections 9.1.3.1 and 9.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 before processing claims for payment.
observations.managementAction: Several documents were already submitted by the Management.
observations.reason: Procurement of goods were still not made strictly in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 due to the continued disregard of the concerned employees with the recommendation thus, reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC and the TWG to ensure that all procurement activities undertaken by the Municipality are strictly in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, particularly during the bid evaluation and post-qualification phase, for proper evaluation and assessment of the eligibility as to, legal, technical and financial capacities of the prospective bidders. 

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

BAC and its Technical Working Group (TWG) to immediately submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team to arrive at a decision in audit, and henceforth ensure that perfected contracts are properly and completely documented before submitting the same to the Audit Team;

Municipal Engineer to: (i).prepare the ABC in the format prescribed under DPWH Department Order No. 197 s. 2016; and (ii) closely monitor the project implementations and ensure that projects are implemented in accordance with the approved plans and specifications and completed within the contract duration, otherwise, impose liquidated damages therein; and

Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking supporting documents in the claims for payments, and henceforth ensure that all succeeding payments are supported with complete and proper documentations as required under Sections 9.1.3.1 and 9.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 before processing claims for payment.
observations.managementAction: Several documents were already submitted by the Management.
observations.reason: Procurement of infrastructure projects were still not made strictly in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 due to the continued disregard of the concerned employees with the recommendation thus, reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:  (a).immediately submit the required supporting documents to the Audit Team to arrive at a decision in audit, and henceforth ensure that all claims are duly supported with complete and proper documentations before processing for payment to establish the validity, regularity and propriety of the transactions;       (b) ensure that accounting forms are properly accomplished and complete with essential information; and (c) submit the lacking 27 DVs to facilitate verification of accounts, and henceforth ascertain prompt and complete submission of financial accounts and reports 10 days after the end of each month.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide a valid and legal basis for the grant of the monthly educational allowance, otherwise, refrain from granting or authorizing such payments.
observations.managementAction:
Several Disbursement Vouchers and documentary requirements were already submitted by the Management.

Several documentary requirements/accounting forms were already properly filled-up with essential information by the Accounting Office.
observations.reason: Claims for payment of disbursements totaling P37,325,300.97 were paid despite incomplete and improper documentations while  77 DVs totaling to P5,559,937.35 were not submitted for audit due to continuous disregard of the Municipal Accountant and other concerned personnel to comply with the Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, and the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act (RA) No. 9184 thus, reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management that both the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant conduct quarterly reconciliation of cashbook balances and the books of account of the LGU to facilitate early detection of errors and immediate correction thereof in order to establish correct and accurate balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
	
Analyze transactions to ensure proper recording and classifying of accounts;

Reclassify the accounts payable and effect necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the transactions in their appropriate/correct accounts as discussed above; and

Ensure that the preparations of the Financial Statements of the LGU comply with the requirements of PPSAS 1, COA Resolution No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 and P.D 1445.

We recommended that 
management require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Intra-agency accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds to promptly identify any discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and be able to effect the necessary adjustments for reconciliation.

We recommended management to direct:

The Municipal Treasurer to:

Establish the balances of the 15% share of the OBO from CY 2019 and earlier years and transfer these balances to its TF bank account.

Prepare a separate Report of Collections for subsequent shares of the OBO and deposit directly to its TF account; and

Show in the Official Receipt the breakdown of the shares of the LGU, OBO and DPWH from the collections of building permit fees and other charges.

 The Municipal Accountant to:

Make the necessary adjustments to correct the erroneous recording and transfer of the 15% share of the OBO to TF as follows:

GF Books:

Prior Period Adjustment			xxxx
	Cash in Bank			xxxx

                 TF Books

Cash Local Treasury		xxxx
	Trust Liabilities		xxxx

Record the 15% share of the OBO in the TF books as Trust Liabilities. A separate Subsidiary Ledger is to be maintained for this purpose/fund.

 The Municipal Engineer to prepare a Program of Work for approval by the Local Chief Executive and submit to the DPWH Secretary thru the National Building Code Development Office (NBCDO) for the utilization of the 15 percent share in accordance with the procedural guidelines set forth by JMC No. 001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to follow-up/ make representation to the NSWMC as to the status of the submitted SWMP in order to find out if there are still necessary requirements or lacking documents needed for the approval of the said SWMP.
We have recommended that the Accounting Office coordinate with the Treasury Office to determine reasons for the long outstanding checks that already became stale for proper adjustment in the books.

We have recommended that the MESWMB coordinate with the barangays relative to their plans and programs in compliance with RA No. 9003 to identify the needs in order to effectively implement solid waste management programs of the Municipality and consider the clustering of barangays to address the lack of fund in putting up their MRF.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to inform the  MENRO to monitor the MRF of all barangays.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the Management:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly signed certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible every beginning of the year for recording purposes;

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to record the RPT/SET receivables based on the certified list from the Office of the Treasurer and ensure that the recorded receivables are based on actual reference documents; and 

Intensify tax collections thru the formulation of strategies to improve collection of real property taxes and encourage the involvement of barangay officials in the tax campaign to reach out to a larger area.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant give utmost priority in analyzing and determining the composition of the long outstanding Receivables. Otherwise, comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 in requesting for the write-off of the long outstanding receivables.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct complete periodic physical inventory count of property, plant and equipment by type, which shall be made annually and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to be submitted to the office of the COA Auditor not later than January 31 of each year. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should maintain Property, Plant & Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) for each category of assets. The General Services Officer should properly classify/identify the PPEs thru the maintenance of property cards to ascertain its accuracy and existence and reconcile any difference with that of the records of the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that the Management secure copies of the MOA/TA from the Source Agencies and furnish the COA Audit Team copies the same to determine whether fund transfers are properly utilized and the requirements for projects implementation and reporting are strictly complied with.
observations.managementAction: The Management filed the MOA but were not submitted to the auditor. Also, there was no MOA given to some funds received that were automatically credited to the bank account. The Management will furnish the auditor the MOA/TA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the: 

(a) concerned office or Inventory Committee, to complete the physical count of the assets of the LGU and to render the corresponding report; 

(b) MTO to maintain and update records on accountability on PPEs; and 

(c) reconciliation of records of MTO and Municipal Accountant to settle any discrepancy.
observations.reason:
Lack of staff hampered the completion of the physical count by the Inventory Committee.

Due to incomplete physical count, the MTO was not able to update their records.
 
No updated records maintained by the MTO. Reiterated in Observation No. 4, Part II, this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to record all the property acquired out of Trust Fund in accordance with the provision of Section 104 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I. In addition, furnish the Municipal General Services Officer with the subject Journal Entry Vouchers in order to include those property in the property records of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant did not record the projects funded by BUB. The Municipal Accountant explained that he is still verifying the correct amount of the projects to be recorded in the General Fund.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant explained that he is still verifying the correct amount of the projects to be recorded in the General Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
 trace the period or date of acquisition of the abovementioned property and compute the depreciation base on the estimated useful life of assets provided under COA Circular 2003-007; and
 take up the prior years’ and current year’s depreciation as Prior Years Adjustment to be closed to Government Equity.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is still looking for documents to trace the PPE were acquired. No accounting entry was yet recorded.
observations.reason: He explained that no documents are available to show the date when the subject PPE were acquired.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to provide sufficient disclosure of the LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Proper disclosure was not provided. Subsidiary Ledgers that will be the basis for the required disclosure were not prepared.
observations.reason: For CY 2019, the Municipal Accountant was not able to provide sufficient disclosure as the subsidiary ledgers were not prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer close the Municipality’s account with FCB. We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer account the prior years’ service charge given by FCB to the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor advised the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to prioritize the task recommended by COA and avoid the same transaction that may violate any rules and regulations as stated.
observations.reason: FCB is the nearest depository bank to the LGU, thereby planning to request the DOF/BSP for conduit bank of LBP, the depository bank of the LGU, particularly in the deposit of local collections to comply with the COA rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor insure all government properties with the Property Insurance Fund. We also recommend the OIC-GSO to register and ensure full insurance coverage of the Municipal Owned Vehicles.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor already instructed the GSO to perform the task immediately to ensure the municipal government properties entitled for insurance to GSIS to protect the interest of the Municipality and to follow the laws as mandated regarding Property Insurance.
observations.reason: Appraisal of Properties from GSIS not yet implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor facilitate the titling of those real properties acquired in prior years, Henceforth, the Municipality through the Municipal Treasurer, GSO and the Municipal Accountant should observe strictly the rules and regulations regarding the acquisition of real properties.
We also advised that the Municipal Accountant support the general ledger controlling account with complete and updated SL and RPLC to effectively control and monitor any movement in the Land Account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor ordered the involved offices to complete the list of all real properties owned by the Municipality of Rizal with status as to tenurial instruments and ownership.
observations.reason: Ongoing validation and verification to documents the  real properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor stop the granting of retirement privileges without legal basis and demand refund of the retirement privilege amounting to P330,000.00 because to allow the payee to retain the cash retirement incentive would amount to his unjust enrichment to the prejudice of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal mayor asked for proper advice from the Provincial Legal Office and ordered the offices involved to completely submit all copies documents related to the released fund for proper transmittal to Provincial Legal Office.
observations.reason: The documents for transmittal to the Provincial Legal Office is still to be extracted.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendations that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare and submit the Procurement Monitoring Reports in its prescribed format for approval and submission by the Municipal Mayor to the Government Procurement Policy Board within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester.

We reiterateed our prior years’ recommendation that management prioritize the establishment of a permanent evacuation center intended to be fully disaster-ready at all times in accordance with the set-forth standards under DILG Memorandum Circular 122 series of 2018.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Property Officer to facilitate the submission of the updated list of inventory of insurable properties with the GSIS so that the necessary insurance coverage for all the assets can be applied with the GSIS General Insurance Fund.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee prepare and submit the Procurement Monitoring Report for the 1st Semester of CY 2017 to GPPB website on July 14, 2017; submit the CY 2018 Annual Procurement Plan-Common-Used Supplies and Equipment to DBM on November 15, 2017 and prepare the APCPI for CY 2016 during the 1st Quarter of CY 2017. The same shall be done on each ensuing semesters and years thereafter.

We recommended compliance of AO No. 70, s. 2003 dated April 14, 2003 for the creation of Internal Audit Service and its implementing guidelines under DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-4 dated March 22, 2004 for the guidelines on the organization and staffing of Internal Auditing Services.

We  recommended that:
the GSO designate/Municipal Treasurer cause the transfer of donated properties to the concerned agencies by  accomplishing the required transfer documents and submit copy of the same to the accounting office for  the necessary derecognition of said properties.
the GSO designate/Municipal Treasurer  cause the immediate disposal of all unserviceable properties which are beyond economic repair using the required format.
the Office of the Municipal Engineer   furnish the accounting office a copy of the road network inventory as basis for  reclassification in the books of accounts .
the General Services Officer designate/ Municipal Treasurer  maintain and update property cards for all PPEs and regularly renew their corresponding Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment (ARE).
management require the inventory committee to conduct  annual physical inventory of all municipal properties and submit a report using the prescribed format of the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE)  as basis for the reconciliation of records between the accounting and GSO.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to disclose the details of the LDRRMF and Road Networks account using the pro-forma disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements provided in COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2015-008 starting CY 2020 .
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant  agreed to record the necessary reclassification entries in the books of the LGU to reflect the correct balance of the affected liability accounts .
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record the necessary adjustment in the books of the LGU in accordance with  COA Circular No 2015-009.

We recommended that the project engineers assigned to projects strictly accord with Section 477 (b) (3) of the Local Government Code to ensure that projects are implemented in accordance with plans and specifications.
We recommended further that management resolve the remaining technical issues to avoid further deterioration of the projects causing unnecessary loss of government funds.

Thus, we recommended that they prepare a realistic budget allocation for Gender and Development (GAD) programs, activities and projects that will allow minimal and acceptable cost variance upon implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Prepare and submit monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements of all its depository bank accounts, take up reconciling items, and effect adjustment in the GL to ensure the correct balance of the Cash in Bank accounts; and
Exert more time and effort to trace, verify and reconcile regularly the book balances of the Cash in Bank account against the bank records including reconciliation with the cashbook maintained by the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Management:
Enforce collection and settlement of the long outstanding receivables and cash advances through issuance of demand letters;
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit a sworn Certification for unliquidated cash advances, and other receivables with no available documents but are not yet considered dormant as defined under COA Circular 2016-005; and
Initiate the preparation and filing of the request of authority to write-off accounts with the COA in accordance with the aforementioned Circular.

We recommended that the Management comply strictly with the prescribed controls over PPE as provided for in pertinent laws, rules and regulations and require the following:
The Inventory Team/Committee to conduct an inventory of all the properties of the Municipality and reconcile the related accounting and property records. Discrepancies and/or deficiencies, if any, should be analyzed/investigated. Likewise, submit a Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the Audit Team concerned as required under Section 66 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System.
The General Services Officer to renew the Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment every three years or every time there is a change in accountability to establish who is in actual possession or custody of all government properties in accordance with Section 56, Vol. II of the New Government Accounting System Manual.
The Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, for each class of property, plant and equipment and post all transactions promptly from source documents and reconcile their records regularly in order to establish the accuracy of balance of each PPE account presented in the financial statements as of a certain date.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a reconciliation report of the reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustments, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
Require the Liquidating Officer to maintain a separate cashbook for each fund  and deposit the proceeds accruing to each fund in their respective depository bank accounts in compliance with Sections 309 and 310 of RA No. 7160;
Reconcile the book balances with the cash on hand daily and with the Accounting Office; and
Instruct the Liquidating Officer to properly fill up the entries required in recording in the cashbook and to foot and close the books at the end of each month.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
Stop the practice of selling CTC receipts to the barangays, instead authorize them to act as deputized collectors in compliance with Section 334(a) of RA No. 7160;
Monitor the CTC receipts issued to barangays and require them to remit the collections therefrom; and
Submit a duly accounted list of receipts to reconcile with the receipts presented during the cash examination for verification.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Liquidating Officer certify their cashbooks as to the correctness and completeness of the recorded transactions at month-end after the last entry.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer always update his cashbooks by recording all cash advances daily in order to reflect/establish the accountability at a given date.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the monthly Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms and his own monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms. The collecting officers are also required to prepare their Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms on a monthly basis. The CRAAF, together with all the RAAFs, should be submitted to the Office of the Auditor not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month pursuant to Section 51 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that the Accountable Officers properly observe the immediate renewal of fidelity bond before its expiration as required by the abovementioned regulation. In the absence of their fidelity bond, they should not be allowed to collect funds for the government.

We recommended that the Management:
Instruct the Field Collectors to remit their collections daily to the Liquidating Officer, who will prepare the Report of Collections and submit the same to the Municipal Treasurer; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit the collections daily and intact to avoid possibility of theft, mishandling and misuse of the amount collected.

We recommended that the Liquidating Officer religiously reconcile the recorded amounts reflected in her cashbook to avoid the possibility of occurrence of shortage or overage in compliance with Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommended that the Management ensure that all contract agreements be supported with complete documentary requirements prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012 – 001. Further, we recommended that the lacking documents above mentioned be submitted immediately to facilitate the review of the procurement.

We recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity require the BAC, through its Secretariat, to post the Notice of Award and the Notice of Proceed in the PhilGEPS and agency website within three (3) days and fifteen (15) days, respectively, to be compliant with the regulations contained in the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Submit the lacking documents and ensure that all claims against government funds are supported with all the required documentations;
Sign/certify the computation of the terminal leave benefits; and
Refrain from processing and approving payments with incomplete documents to avoid suspension /disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the submission of the DTR, Application for leave and other supporting documents to ascertain the correctness of salaries and allowances paid to each employee; and
Effective immediately, instruct the HRMO to undertake the payroll preparation for proper internal control procedure and instruct the Treasury Office to properly turn-over said task to the HRMO.

We recommended that the payroll deduction be limited following the order of preference provided in the GAA and ensure that an employee’s net take home pay shall not be less than the minimum allowed take home pay of P5,000.00 as provided under Section 47 of the General Provisions of GAA of 2020.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee:
Submit justification on declaring the lone bidder eligible on the foregoing infrastructure projects despite insufficient NFCC as per Section 23.4.2.6 of IRR of RA No. 9184; and
Carefully examine/verify/validate all the statements and documents submitted by the bidder with the Lowest/Single Calculated Bid to ensure compliance with and its responsiveness to all the requirements and the conditions as specified in the Bidding Documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Immediately transfer the unexpended balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE totaling to P1,703,982.57 to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability – DRRM” as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
Subsidiary Ledgers of the unutilized DRRMF to the Special Trust Fund be maintained.

We recommended that the Management:
Direct the MDRRM Council to prepare the LDRRMF Investment Plan for CY 2020 in consonance with Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular 2012-002;
Review and update annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan to incorporate the PPAs to be funded by the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years; and
Strengthen the capabilities of its Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office to enable it to perform its mandated functions under Section 4, Rule 6, IRR of R.A. 10121, especially in the formulation of an LDRRM Investment Plan, as guide in the utilization of the LDRRMF and proper monitoring of the fund.
We recommended that the Management immediately initiate action to enforce settlement of audit suspensions pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct, through the
Municipal Treasurer, the (a) Collecting Officers to (i) religiously remit their
collections on time; (ii) discontinue using multiple Official Receipt booklets
simultaneously; and (iii) avoid any alterations/erasures on the accountable
forms issued; (b) Cashier II to (i) deposit all collections and remittances intact
daily or not later than the next banking day; (ii) stop utilizing municipal
collections to encash checks; (iii) facilitate the updating of the fidelity bond
coverage of all AOs that would commensurate their actual maximum cash
accountability; (iv) prepare and submit the monthly CRAAF; and (v) secure
special order or designation for the three mentioned accountable officers.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the (a)
Municipal Accountant to immediately apply imprest system in handling PCF;
and (b) PCF Custodian to reduce the fund to an amount that closely
approximates the actual monthly requirement for petty expenses.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the (a)
Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer and Municipal Assessor to (i) exert
diligent effort to locate the titles of the other lands listed in the Summary of
Owned Lands prepared by the Office of the Assessor; (ii) take immediate
action to register and secure the title of the subject parcels of land in the name
of the municipality; and (iii) regularly reconcile the accounting and assessor’s
records vis-à-vis the ownership documents kept at the Municipal Treasurer’s
Office to ensure the reliability of the reported land assets of the municipality;
and (b) Municipal Budget Officer to allocate funds for expenses that may be
incurred in the titling of these lands.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the (a)
Municipal Treasurer to (i) maintain Property Cards to closely monitor the
acquired assets of the municipality in compliance with Section 119 of LGU
NGAS Manual, Volume I; (ii) exert diligent efforts in locating the missing
Certificate of Registration (CRs) to establish ownership over the motor
vehicles and/or in the event of lost CRs, secure a notarized affidavit of loss and
make a representation to the Land Transportation Office for the issuance of a
duplicate CR; and (iii) immediately register with the LTO in the name of the
Municipality of Rizal the vehicles which CRs are still under the name of a
private person/entity; and (b) Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to
regularly reconcile their records in order to immediately detect or correct
discrepancies or deficiencies, if any.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Local
Development Council, the Sanggunian Bayan and the Municipal Budget
Officer to continue adhering with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-01 and use the
20% Development Fund solely for development projects and programs that
partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures with the end view of
attaining desirable socio economic and environmental management outcomes.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to (a) refrain from using
DRRMF on PPAs which are not directly supportive of disaster risk
management; and (b) continue adhering with the guidelines and minimum
standards as enumerated under the Disaster Preparedness Volume 2 pursuant
to R.A. No. 10121.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor concurred to (a) implement the
ordinance imposing charges/penalties on violators of related SWM
regulations, especially on its “No segregation, no collection” policy; and (b)
continue the advances already achieved in solid waste management.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the GAD Focal
Person: to (a) continue preparing and implementing GAD Budget and Plan
pursuant to the pertinent laws and regulations on GAD; and (b) monitor the
implementation of GAD PPAs to ensure adherence to the milestones and
timelines set forth in the GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to perform the necessary accounting of the fiscal transactions of
the municipality’s economic enterprises pursuant to R.A. No. 7160 and
Manual on NGAS for LGUs, with the end view of promoting accountability
and transparency in their operation.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor committed to continue its
satisfactory observance of BIR regulations on the withholding and remittance
of taxes.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to continuously adhere to
the requirements of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor assured the sustained observance
of the National Health Insurance Act by withholding and timely remitting all
contributions withheld to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to continue adhering to
the requirement of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law
of 2009.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor concurred to require all those
named liable in the Notice of Disallowance to refund the amount disallowed.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. record all the property acquired out of Trust Fund using the appropriate PPE accounts
in accordance with the COA Circular No. 2015-009. In addition, furnish the Municipal
General Services Officer with the subject Journal Entry Vouchers in order to include
those property in the property records of the Municipality;
b. strictly adhere on the proper recording of disbursements and liquidations of the fund
transfers from NGAs by debiting the appropriate expense or asset accounts on the
disbursements of fund and debiting Due to NGAs only upon liquidation of the fund.
c. prepare and maintain PPELC for the properties recorded under the Special Education
Fund and make adjusting entries to correct the erroneous recording of Disaster
Response Equipment and the incorrect charging of depreciation expense;
d. prepare a lapsing schedule to compute for the depreciation expenses of all the
recorded PPE and draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the computed amount of
depreciation;
e. draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the misclassified PPE according to its
classification, specification and nature; and
f. draw Journal Entry Voucher to drop the small value property items from the books by
debiting Prior Years’ Adjustment and Accumulated Depreciation of the respective
PPE, and by crediting the respective PPE accounts.

Additionally, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require an annual physical
inventory of all the properties of the Municipality to be conducted by a committee
consisting of representative from the Office of the Municipal Mayor, Municipal GSO or
Municipal Treasurer and Supply Accountable Officer of the department or office
concerned.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
draw Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to record all the valid reconciling items to
reflect the correct year-end balances of the Cash in Bank-LCCA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
a. the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to refrain from using the
Petty Cash Fund for payment of recurring and non-petty expenses by
limiting the charges to the fund up to P1,000.00 only per voucher pursuant
to Section 48 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
b. the Petty Cash Custodian to liquidate the Petty Cash fund and return the
unused cash to the Municipal Treasurer at the end of the year and the
Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher by debiting
expenses and Cash-Local Treasury accounts and crediting Petty Cash Fund
account;
c. the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher at the beginning
of the year to set-up the Petty Cash Fund by debiting Petty Cash Fund and
crediting Cash in Bank; and
d. the Petty Cash Custodian to maintain a separate Cashbook to record the PCF
transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. make necessary adjustments/corrections in the books to present the correct
balance of the affected accounts; and
b. exercise due care in the recording and classifying the financial transactions
of the Municipality to ensure that the balances of the reciprocal accounts are
the same at all times.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. provide and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for each fund transfer;
b. exert extra effort to locate the documents and information on the details of
the various fund transfers from different NGAs amounting to P69,793.36; and
c. prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to correct the classification of receipts from
Philippine Coconut Authority and Laguna Lake Development Authority to
proper account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
properly classify the amount due within twelve months after the reporting date
as part of the current liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. draw journal entry for the transfer of the unexpended balance of the Quick
Response Fund and LDRRMF-Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses
to the Special Trust Fund of the Trust Fund books; and
b. prepare and maintain the Trust Liabilities-LDRRMF subsidiary ledgers per
year of transfer and disclose the amount and details of the unexpended
balances of the LDRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statements pursuant
to Sections 5.1.12 and 5.1.16 of the COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
disclose the required information in the Notes to Financial Statements to assist
the users in understanding the financial statements and to provide them with
the additional information value.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
submit immediately to the Auditor the duly approved disbursement vouchers of
the above transactions totaling P2,935,269.17 to avoid suspension or
disallowance in audit. Henceforth, ensure that all payments/disbursements are
supported with duly approved disbursement vouchers and supporting
documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and
Municipal Accountant to immediately submit all the lacking documents to the
Auditor to support and justify the payments of infrastructures projects and
refrain from approving payments of disbursements with incomplete
documentation.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the official driver of the
vehicle to submit the serially numbered and duly accomplished drivers’ trip
tickets for payment of gasoline totaling P607,952.70 to avoid
suspension/disallowance in audit and strictly implement the guidelines on the use
of government vehicles stated in COA Circular No. 77-61 in order to
minimize wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel
consumption and to properly utilize the government motor transportation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the Officer-in-Charge of the Municipal Social Welfare and
Development Office and other concerned officials to justify or explain in
writing the basis for the grant of financial assistance to the seven
beneficiaries totaling P33,000.00;
b. require the MSWDO to formulate policies on the grant of financial assistance
containing the eligibility requirements and the uniform amount to be given
for each type of financial assistance in conformity with DSWD MC N0. 11
series of 2019;
c. request the Sangguniang Bayan to enact an Ordinance that would serve as
the official guidelines in the granting of medical, burial and educational
assistance in order to promote uniformity in identifying the qualified
beneficiaries and the amount to be granted;
d. require the MSWDO to establish monitoring measures in granting of financial
assistance; and
e. require the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the required
supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer
and all concerned department heads to meticulously and judiciously prepare
the Annual Procurement Plan in accordance with Section 7 of the Revised IRR
of RA No. 9184 and ensure that all necessary information required in the APP
and its amendments, if any, are reflected therein to serve as a planning and
control tool for efficient procurement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer and
other concerned officials to:
a. explain in writing why the implementation of the supplemental project was
not simultaneously implemented with the construction of NCDC;
b. explain why the Municipality was not able to seek the concurrence of the
ECCD Council prior to the final payment to the contractor; and
c. request from the Sangguniang Bayan to appropriate budget for the
construction of playground to fully comply with the remaining conditions of
the MOA to avoid possible withdrawal of additional grants and assistance
from ECCD Council.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the barangay officials to
strictly enforce the Municipal and Barangay Ordinances on the implementation
of the ecological solid waste management systems within their respective
barangays by making the community aware of the ordinances and the sanctions
for non-compliance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: (a) Designate-GSO
and Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all PPEs and
Inventories of the Municipality, prepare and submit the corresponding RPCI and
RPCPPE, and henceforth ensure that said physical count is strictly and timely
conducted every year; (b) Designate-GSO and Municipal Accountant to reconcile
the balances of RPCPPE and RPCI with the corresponding ledger balances;
(c) Designate-GSO to: (i) maintain Property Cards and Stock Cards; and (ii) fast
track the preparation of Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property
(IIRUP), together with the required supporting documents to facilitate the disposal
of unserviceable items; and (d) Accounting Unit to maintain an updated PPE
Ledger Cards and prepare Supplies Ledger Cards for each inventory account.

a. Municipal Accountant: (i) analyze and review the outstanding stale
checks and prepare the necessary JEV for the adjustments to present the
correct account balances of the Cash and Cash Equivalents and Accounts
Payable; (ii) strictly monitor the grant and liquidation of cash advances
to ensure that no additional cash advance is granted to any official or
employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or
a proper accounting thereof is made; (iii) cause the immediate settlement
of cash advances to avoid accumulation thereof for the accurate
presentation of the related expenses and receivables account; and
(iv) analyze the unreconciled balances of the Due from and Due to Other
Funds accounts, prepare a reconciliation statement therein and make the
necessary adjustments in the books for the affected funds for fair and
accurate presentation of the account balances in the financial statements;
and
b. Municipal Treasurer: (i) send written notices to the payees one (1) month
before the check becomes stale as required in Section 59 of the NGAS
Manual. Cancelled stale checks shall be reported and attached to the
Report of Checks Issued (RCI) at the period of cancellation; and
(ii) withheld the payment of salaries of concerned AOs to enforce
liquidation of their outstanding cash advances.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (i) cause the return of the
unexpended balances of the Due to NGAs totaling P552,811.06 to the respective
Source Agencies for proper presentation of the Due to NGAs account in the
Financial Statements and/or make representations with the source agency for the
possible reprogramming of the unexpended balances to other PPAs; and (ii) impose
liquidated damages and/or institute appropriate legal actions/charges against the
contractor who abandoned the projects amounting to P4,800,000.00, and henceforth
ensure that projects funded out of grants from the NGAs are completed in
accordance with the approved plans within the target date of completion.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the: (a) MPDC for the
optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund to spur development, provide jobs
to constituents and to achieve the desired socio-economic development and
environmental outcomes of the LGU so that the intended users/beneficiaries may
enjoy the immediate benefits that may be derived from the said development
projects. Also, ensure that all projects included in the expenditure program for 20%
Development Fund are strictly in compliance with DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017; and (b) consider the hiring of
additional staff for the Engineering Department, if warranted, to fast track the
preparation of the Program of Works, Project Designs and Cost Estimates to avoid
delays in the procurement activities and project implementation.
We also recommended the Municipal Accountant to: (a) immediately submit
the required supporting documents to the Audit Team to be able to arrive at a
decision in audit; (b) ensure that claims are duly supported with complete and
proper documentation before processing for payment to establish the validity and
propriety of transactions; and (c) ensure that accounting forms are properly
accomplished and complete with essential information.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the MDRRM Council:
(a) ensure the optimal utilization of the MDRRMF, including the unexpended
balances from previous years, specially, during times of calamities to enhance the
Municipality’s resilience and capacity to reduce vulnerabilities when exposed to
hazards and calamities; and (b) refrain from utilizing the fund for purposes outside
the mandate of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25,
2013.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (a) ensure that
purchases of relief items, supplies and materials are recorded under the appropriate
Inventory accounts and recognize the appropriate expense accounts only upon its
issuance. The inventory accounts shall be properly monitored using the Supplies
Ledgers Card (SLCs) pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-002; (b) immediately
submit the required supporting documents to the Audit Team to be able to arrive at
53
a decision in audit, and henceforth ensure that claims are duly supported with
complete and proper documentation before processing for payment to establish the
validity and propriety of transactions; and (c) ensure that accounting forms are
properly accomplished and complete with essential information.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to: (a) include the President of the
Association of Barangay Councils in the Municipality, the Chairperson of the
Sangguniang Kabataan Federation and designate each representative from the
recycling industry and manufacturing or packaging industry in the composition of
the MESWMB; and (b) instruct the designated MESWMO to make representations
and coordination with the DENR to fast track the review of the SWMP for the
eventual approval of the National Solid Waste Management Commission .
We also recommended that the designated MESWMO coordinate with the
Engineering Office and Bids and Awards Committee for the immediate
implementation of the project, Construction of Materials Recovery Facilities for 11
barangays within the Municipality, to enforce proper sorting, segregation,
composting and recycling of waste materials.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC and the TWG
to ensure that all procurement activities undertaken by the Municipality are strictly
in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184,
particularly during the bid evaluation and post-qualification phase, for proper
evaluation and assessment of the eligibility as to, legal, technical and financial
capacities of the prospective bidders.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. BAC and its TWG to immediately submit the lacking documents to the
Audit Team to arrive at a decision in audit, and henceforth ensure that
perfected contracts are properly and completely documented before
submitting the same to the Audit Team; and
b. Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking supporting
documents in the claims for payment of the projects, and henceforth
ensure that all claims are duly supported with complete, proper and duly
accomplished documents, as required under Sections 9.1.3.1 and 9.1.1 of
COA Circular No. 2012-001 before processing for payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC and the TWG
to ensure that all procurement activities undertaken by the Municipality are strictly
in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184,
particularly during the bid evaluation and post-qualification phase, for proper
evaluation and assessment of the eligibility as to, legal, technical and financial
capacities of the prospective bidders.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. BAC and its Technical Working Group (TWG) to immediately submit
the lacking documents to the Audit Team to arrive at a decision in audit,
and henceforth ensure that perfected contracts are properly and
completely documented before submitting the same to the Audit Team;
b. Municipal Engineer to: (i) prepare the ABC in the format prescribed
under DPWH Department Order No. 197 s. 2016; and (ii) closely monitor
the project implementations and ensure that projects are implemented in
accordance with the approved plans and specifications and completed
within the contract duration, otherwise, impose liquidated damages
therein; and
c. Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking supporting
documents in the claims for payments, and henceforth ensure that all
succeeding payments are supported with complete and proper
documents as required under Sections 9.1.3.1 and 9.1.1 of COA
Circular No. 2012-001 before processing claims for payment;

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to: (a) immediately submit the required supporting documents to the
Audit Team to arrive at a decision in audit, and henceforth ensure that all claims
are duly supported with complete and proper documentations before processing for
payment to establish the validity, regularity and propriety of the transactions;
(b) ensure that accounting forms are properly accomplished and complete with
essential information; and (c) submit the lacking 27 DVs to facilitate verification of
accounts, and henceforth ascertain prompt and complete submission of financial
accounts and reports 10 days after the end of each month.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide a valid and legal
basis for the grant of the monthly educational allowance, otherwise, refrain from
granting or authorizing such payments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the GAD Focal Point
System, strictly observe the prescribed timelines for GAD Planning and Budgeting,
as provided in the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and amended by
PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016 to facilitate
timely preparation and submission to the Provincial Planning and Development
Office and the subsequent review and endorsement by the DILG Provincial Office.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) thru, the Municipal Accountant, regularly serve written notice to accountable officers and employees to demand settlement of their outstanding cash advances; (bi) consider withholding the of salaries of those employees still connected with the Municipality who refuse to settle their accounts without justifiable reason; and (c) institute the necessary legal action against those who failed to settle their accountabilities, if warranted.
The Municipal Accountant exert efforts to locate documents and previous records to facilitate reconciliation and/or determination of the causes of the unreconciled and negative cash advance balances of various AOs and thereafter effect the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts, as appropriate
In all instances, the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant must ensure that the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 are strictly observed in the granting and liquidation of cash advances and see that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year.
Management has not submitted official response to this observation as of the date of this report.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to complete the annual physical inventory of PPE, reconcile the results of the count with the property and accounting records, and submit to the Audit Team the RPCPPE in the prescribed format, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I. All discrepancies between physical and book inventories must be investigated, cleared and reconciled immediately.  If necessary, written explanation should be required from accountable and responsible person. The Inventory Committee should likewise ensure that physical inventory is undertaken annually and reports thereon are submitted accordingly.
The Property Officer and the Municipal Accountant should be required to reconstruct and thereafter maintain the property cards and PPE ledger cards, respectively, in the forms prescribed in the said Manual. Also, the Property Officer should ensure that all issued PPE for official use of officials and employees are covered by ARE which shall be renewed every January of the third year after issue.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the PPE schedule pertaining the understated amounts so that complete amount will be reflected in the financial statements.
We also recommended that the OIC-GSO a) create a database of all properties of the Municipality with the corresponding acquisition costs and property numbers, prefereably similar to those indicated in the subsidiary ledgers of the Municipal Accountant; b) write a letter of demand to the previous custodian of unreturned PPE totaling P912,000.00; c) coordinate with the Inventory Committee to specifically identify and have an inventory of the unserviceable and fully depreciated PPE items and thereafter prepare and submit the IIRUP to the Audit Team together with the transportation equipment’s checklist, pictures and in-house appraisal values to facilitate request for proper disposal.
The GSO commented that there is negligence on their part as to: poor records management, mismanagement of property inventory (lack of property index cards), non-compliance to property insurance law, failure to established archival and records management with respect to records of offices and departments, absence of property inventory committee and there is no regular General Services Officer that is qualified to manage or administer the multi-millions worth of properties owned by the LGU. However, she added that she has decided to structure the General Service Office as part of my leadership approach being the Officer In-charge of the office.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the OIC-GSO, (a) secure insurance coverage for all insurable properties of the Municipality with the Property Insurance Fund of the GSIS to safeguard its assets and protect the interest of the Municipality; and (b) register with the LTO all serviceable motor vehicles in compliance with R.A. No. 4136 or the Land Transportation and Traffic Code  and secure full insurance coverage for all Municipality-owned vehicles.
The GSO commented that there is negligence on their part as to: poor records management, mismanagement of property inventory (lack of property index cards), non-compliance to property insurance law, failure to established archival and records management with respect to records of offices and departments, absence of property inventory committee and there is no regular General Services Officer that is qualified to manage or administer the multi-millions worth of properties owned by the LGU. However, she added that she has decided to structure the General Service Office as part of my leadership approach being the Officer In-charge of the office.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor (i) ensure the optimum utilization of the 20% Development Fund by promptly implementing the priority development projects  as reflected in its duly approved local development plans and Annual Investment Program, in accordance with Section 5 of DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1; (ii) ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the projects to maximize the developmental benefits of the intended beneficiaries; and (iii) consider realigning or reprogramming the unutilized/unimplemented PPAs to other viable development projects;
Municipal Engineer (i) require the concerned project engineer, officers and employees to be prudent in overseeing the timely implementation of PPAs under their responsibility; (ii) assist the MPDC in identifying those projects not yet completed or started subject for possible realignment or reprogramming; (iii) provide complete information on the status of the projects funded by the 20% DF together with all the necessary details; and (iv) provide satisfactory explanation as to why the projects were not yet completed or implemented despite the considerable lapse of time; and
MPDC (i) ensure close coordination with the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant for proper monitoring of the implementation of the projects funded by the 20% DF and provide all the necessary information regarding the status of project implementation for timely preparation and submission of the Status Report of Government PPAs to the Audit Team; (ii) ensure that the information provided in the Status Report of Government PPAs are complete before submitting it to the Audit Team; (iii) conduct intensive review of the continuing projects funded under the 20% DF to determine whether the PPAs are still part of the vital needs of the Municipality otherwise reprogram them to other priority PPAs; and (iv) continue to identify development projects that are doable and more beneficial to the constituents
Management has not submitted official response to this observation as of the date of this report.
