We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council to (i) include the unexpended balance of LDRRMF from previous balances in the preparation of LDRRMFIP with appropriate consideration of the minimum standard set forth by the DILG; and (ii) set realistic and specific schedule of implementation for each programs, projects and activities in the LDRRMFIP to maximize the utilization of LDRRMF.
Management has not yet commented on this audit observation as of the date of this report.

We recommended that the:
MGSO take charge of the custody, storage and monitoring of small items and issue the needed quantity of such items upon the request of MDRRMO or MSWDO or other offices concerned with the use of Inventory Custodian Slips (ICSs) and ensure that copy of the ICS is forwarded to Municipal Accountant for proper recording;
Municipal Accounting Office, MGSO and MSWDO staff revisit COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 and comply with the required records, reports and registries to be prepared and maintained for DRRM Funds to achieve transparency and accountability in the use of disaster relief aid; and
Municipal Accountant ensure proper disclosures pertaining to the information on DRRMF on the Notes to the Financial Statements as prescribed by Item No. 7 of COA Circular No. 2014-002.
Management has not yet submitted their comments on this audit observation as of the date of this report.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System-Technical Working Group (GFPS-TWG) improve in establishing clear baselines and realistic estimates in budget preparation for PPAs translated from the demands/commitments identified in the GAD agenda. The GFPS-TWG should also provide justification for all variances in the GAD AR to facilitate proper evaluation of GAD PPAs.
Management has not yet submitted their comments on this observation as of the date of this report.

Recommendations:
1.10 We recommended that the Inventory Committee to start the reconciliation of those property items included in the inventory report with the records being maintained by the Accounting and Property/Treasurer’s Office since its existence and condition have already been established.
1.11 Likewise, the Inventory Committee to exert their best efforts to complete the required physical inventory taking of properties owned by the LGU and update the RPCPPE with the data of those properties not included in the previous inventory report.
1.12 After the updated RPCPPE have been made in accordance with the prescribed format, reconciliation thereof to be made with that of the accounting unit’s report to establish reliability and fair valuation of the PPE accounts.
1.13 Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant maintain Property Card and Equipment Ledger Cards, respectively, for easy determination of identification and reconciliation of records.

Recommendation:
2.9 We recommended that management follow the accounting and reporting guidelines in the recognition of the various components of the Road Network as stated in COA Circular No. 2015-008 in order to ensure that the Road Network System of the municipality is properly identified, valued and accounted for in the books.

Recommendation:
3.5 We recommended to the management that both the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant conduct quarterly reconciliation of cashbook balances and the books of account of the LGU to facilitate early detection of errors and immediate correction thereof in order to establish correct and accurate balances in the financial statements.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to:
Analyze the transactions to ensure proper recording and classifying of the accounts;
Reclassify the accounts payable and effect necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the transactions in their appropriate/correct accounts as discussed above; and
Ensure that the preparations of the Financial Statements of the LGU comply with the requirements of PPSAS 1, COA Resolution No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 and P.D 1445.

Recommendation:
5.8 We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Intra-agency accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds to promptly identify any discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and be able to effect the necessary adjustments for reconciliation.

Recommendations:
We recommended management to direct:
6.8 The Municipal Treasurer to:
Establish the balances of the 15 percent share of the OBO from CY 2019 and earlier years and transfer these balances to its TF bank account.
Prepare a separate Report of Collections for subsequent shares of the OBO and deposit directly to its TF account; and
Show in the Official Receipt the breakdown of the shares of the LGU, OBO and DPWH from the collections of building permit fees and other charges.
6.9 The Municipal Accountant to:
Make the necessary adjustments to correct the erroneous recording and transfer of the 15 per cent share of the OBO to TF as follows:
GF Books:
Prior Period Adjustment			xxxx
Cash in Bank					xxxx
TF Books
Cash Local Treasury			xxxx
Trust Liabilities				xxxx
Record the 15 percent share of the OBO in the TF books as Trust Liabilities. A separate Subsidiary Ledger is to be maintained for this purpose/fund.
6.10 The Municipal Engineer to prepare a Program of Work for approval by the Local Chief Executive and submit to the DPWH Secretary thru the National Building Code Development Office (NBCDO) for the utilization of the 15 percent share in accordance with the procedural guidelines set forth by JMC No. 001.

Recommendations:
7.5 We recommended that management to direct the Local Disasters Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to properly implement the LDRRMP and optimize the utilization of its LDRRMF to lessen the socioeconomic and environmental impacts of disasters in the community and disaster risks, including projected climate risks, and to enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the community.
7.6 Relative to the issue of lack of manpower, the municipality could tap the services of other regular employees within the agency that can support and help the implementation of enlisted PPAs.

Recommendation:
8.8 We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that management prioritize the establishment of a permanent evacuation center intended to be fully disaster-ready at all times in accordance with the set-forth standards under DILG Memorandum Circular 122 series of 2018.

Recommendation:
9.5 We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendations that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare and submit the Procurement Monitoring Reports in its prescribed format for approval and submission by the Municipal Mayor to the Government Procurement Policy Board within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester.

Recommendation:
11.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to follow-up/ make representation to the NSWMC as to the status of the submitted SWMP in order to find out if there are still necessary requirements or lacking documents needed for the approval of the said SWM

Recommendation:
12.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Property Officer to facilitate the submission of the updated list of inventory of insurable properties with the GSIS so that the necessary insurance coverage for all the assets can be applied with the GSIS General Insurance Fund.

Recommendations:
13.8 We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Agriculturist to coordinate the source agency to secure/obtain needed documents as stated in the preceding paragraphs in order to have sufficient basis in implementing and monitoring of project. Also, the management can take other actions for the full utilization of the equipment by encouraging the farmers to invest in corn production. Further, the accounting can effect necessary recording in the books of the municipality.
13.9 Likewise, the management to take essential actions in maintaining the equipment/project to avoid rapid physical deterioration of the facility due to its non-utilization.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the management:
14.8 That the Municipal Assessor to conduct re-assessment and regular tax mapping to all real properties located in the municipality in order to determine and identify the proper and correct Real Property classification of every real property, the property owner and other related to the present structure and projection of all properties which shall be then the basis in the computation of estimated real property tax.
14.9 With this, the estimated tax collectibles is more accurate and may result to a reasonable tax collection efficiency projection that will ultimately quantify performance indicator in its goal to improve tax administration.
14.10 Intensify tax collection strategies in order to collect the estimated RPT and SET receivables in order to generate additional funds to be used in implementing other programs, projects and activities.
We have recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant reclassify the completed projects to their proper asset account.

consider the repair of unserviceable properties still needed, provided, the cost to be incurred is within 30% of their economic value to avoid further deterioration, wear and tear and possible loss of parts.
observations.managementAction:
Issued memorandum to all departments to turn-over all unserviceable properties to the GSO designate.

The GSO informed the audit team that the inventory and appraisal for unserviceable properties is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant facilitate the immediate return of the unutilized balance of PDAF amounting to ₱45,000.00 to the national government thru the Bureau of Treasury in compliance with SC decision in GR No. 208566.

We have recommended that the Bids and Award Committee (BAC) prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report in its prescribed format for approval by the Municipal Mayor and submission to the Government Policy Board within the time frame required under Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We have recommended that Management require the BAC to prepare the municipality’s APP in accordance with GPPB Circular No. 07-2015 to ensure that all the necessary information required are reflected therein for its continuous relevance and usefulness as a planning and control tool.
We recommended that concerned municipal officials/employees submit Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis as required by COA Circular 2012-002.
We recommended that management submit copies of contracts to the audit team within 5 days after perfection in accordance with pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted only perfected contracts that were procured through public   bidding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to the officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances and demand the immediate settlement thereof; and stop granting another cash advance to officers and employees with unliquidated cash advances. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should not process the cash advance of officials other than  for official travel and strictly comply with provisions of Section 339 of RA 7160 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 24, 2012.
observations.managementAction:
The Management no longer grant cash advance to employees with unliquidated cash advance. And cash advances other than travelling expenses are granted to employees who are properly bonded.

Various cash advances were still remained unliquidated.  However, percentage of liquidation of previous years’ cash advances that remained outstanding was very minimal.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 3

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose the noted deficiencies in the Notes to Financial Statements of the Municipality as required by IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The disclosure requirements were not fully observed.  Only some noted deficiencies in CY 2018 were disclosed in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the cited auditing and accounting laws, rules and regulations.  Submit the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents to the Auditor within the prescribed period to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The management identified the said disbursement vouchers and submitted the same to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the Management:

To require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly use his cash advances for payment of expenses specifically identified as the purpose for granting his cash advances;

To require the Municipal Treasurer to stop accommodating additional payrolls and other expenses, such travelling expenses, reimbursement of meals and snacks, and fuel, oil, and lubricants, submitted to him for payment, and refund any excess of his cash advances immediately after the pay period; and

To require the Accounting Office to verify the payrolls and other disbursement vouchers submitted for liquidation and ensure that only those expenses pertaining to the purpose of the cash advances be allowed and accepted for recording in the General Journal.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer only used his cash advances for the payment of salaries of regular employees.
The Municipal Accountant only granted cash advances, except for travel to employees, who are properly bonded.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the Management:

To immediately purchase a safety vault or steel cabinet with vault, where collections can be kept before it will be deposited to the bank and ensure that the purchase of the said vault is in accordance with the provisions of Republic Act 9184; and

To provide additional security features in the Office of the Municipal Treasurer such as additional door locks and window grills.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer already purchased safety vault for the safekeeping of all collections, which are subject for deposit.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to Management:

To instruct the Field Collectors to remit their collections daily to the Liquidating Officer, who will prepare the Report of Collections and submit the same to the Municipal Treasurer;

To instruct the Liquidating Officer and the Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of using their collections to pay various payrolls and/or other expenses of the LGU; and

To require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit the collections daily and intact to avoid possibility of theft, mishandling and misuse of the amount collected.
observations.managementAction:
All collectors were instructed to prepare the report of collections to be submitted to the Municipal Treasurer. 

Collections by the Field Collectors were not remitted daily to the Liquidating Officer.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to Management:
To instruct all Field Collectors to remit intact, on a daily basis, all their collections to the Liquidating Officer;

To instruct the Liquidating Officer to prepare the Report of Collections and to remit intact all the collections to the Municipal Treasurer; and

To require the Municipal Treasurer, who is properly bonded as a Collecting Officer, with the highest maximum amount of accountability, to personally deposit the collections to avoid exposing the cash to risk of non-recovery in case of loss.
observations.managementAction: Liquidating Officer remits her collections to the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Treasurer will only deposit the collections.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to Management:

To stop the practice of allowing and/or maintaining pre-signed checks in the custody of the Municipal Treasurer; and

To observe proper planning in the preparation of vouchers with complete supporting documents and its corresponding checks, considering the availability of the authorized signatories of checks.
observations.managementAction: Pre-signed checks were not allowed as per instruction of the Municipal Mayor to the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the Management:

To require the Municipal Treasurer to liquidate his cash advances immediately after the pay period;

To instruct the Accounting Office to strictly observe the granting of cash advances only if previous cash advances are properly liquidated and process the same within two (2) to three (3) days before the pay period; and

To require the Accounting Office to strictly monitor the liquidation of cash advances granted to the Municipal Treasurer and to submit status of cash advances to the COA on a quarterly basis.
observations.managementAction: Cash advance is not granted to any official or employee unless previous cash advance has been liquidated.
observations.reason: Various cash advances still remained unliquidated at year end.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to Management:

To instruct the Municipal Treasurer to require all LGU employees to sign all copies of the payrolls as they receive their salaries;

To require the Municipal Treasurer to report and record in his cashbook only the amount of salaries actually received by the LGU employees, and refund immediately any unclaimed salaries by some employees; and

To require the Accounting Office to strictly check the completeness of signatures of LGU employees in the payrolls submitted by the Municipal Treasurer for his liquidation, and to consider only the salaries actually received by the employees as expense in the General Journal.
observations.managementAction: All payrolls were duly signed by LGU employees.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to Management:

To remove the function of assigning DV numbers from the Municipal Treasurer and transfer it to the Accounting Office to ensure the completeness of recording of the LGU’s transactions;

To require the Municipal Treasurer to properly record transactions in his cashbook, to include only his cash advances and corresponding liquidations, and all his collections and its corresponding deposits, to provide a clear reporting of his cash accountability; and

To instruct the Municipal Accountant to take the lead in monitoring the recording of all transactions of the LGU, to be given emphasis on the granting of cash advances to various LGU employees, and to initiate reconciliation with the Municipal Treasurer on a regular basis.
observations.managementAction: The assigning of Dv numbers is only being done by the Accounting Office.  Cash advances given to employees are being monitored monthly and reconciliation of book balances maintained by the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer are done quarterly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit all the required supporting documents. Likewise, it is recommended that the Management require the Accountant to ensure that all the Disbursement Vouchers are supported with the documentary requirements, as enumerated in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 24, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant scrutinize the supporting documents attached to every disbursements before payments are made.  However, verification disclosed that some disbursements are not supported with complete documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management refrain from procuring goods and services on a reimbursement basis, except petty purchases to be paid out from the Petty Cash Fund.
observations.managementAction:
The Management limited the purchase of goods and services on reimbursement basis.

Some purchases of goods or services were still made through reimbursement basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately transfer the unexpended balance of the DRRMF to a special trust fund under the account “Trust Liability – DRRM” as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-002. Consequently, the account shall be used to record transfers of the agency’s unutilized QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years, receipts of NDRRMF, DRRMF from other LGUs and other sources. We further recommend that subsidiary ledgers for the transfer of the agency’s unutilized DRRMF to the special trust fund be maintained.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant has transferred the CY 2018 unexpended balance of the DRRMF-MOOE to Special Trust Fund and prepared and maintained the subsidiary ledger for the transfer of fund.

The unexpended balance of the QRF and DRRM-MOOE for CY 2019 were not transferred to a Special Trust Fund under the account Trust-Liability-DRRM.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer fast track the submission of the Solid Waste Management Plan to the Environment Management Bureau to be forwarded to the National Solid Waste Management Commission and ensure that the programs and activities included in the plan are consistent with the National Solid Waste Management Framework.
observations.managementAction: The Management had just submitted their Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan (ESWMP) to Environment Management Bureau Regional Office No. 02 last March 29, 2019 for them to review and submit said Plan to the National Solid Waste Management Commission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU submit the GAD Accomplishment Report for the team to evaluate its conformity with the approved plan and if directed towards the realization of our country’s commitment, plan, and policies on women empowerment, gender equality, and gender development.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted the GAD accomplishment report for CY 2018 and CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MDRRMO to continue the implementation of the remaining LDRRM projects/programs/activities in order to guarantee the attainment of the Municipality's objective of mitigating the impacts of disaster/calamities towards building a disaster-resilient communities.
observations.reason: Ineligible expenses amounting to ₱1,000,000.00 were charged to the fund. See Observation No. 6, Part II, this report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:

(a) instruct the BAC members and the Municipal Engineer to properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to pave the way for their timely implementation and completion; and 

(b) ensure that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time to arrest possible delays.

observations.reason: No training/seminar was attended by the members of the GFPS during the year. Reiterated in Observation No. 8, Part II of this report

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive will:

instruct the MSWMB to: i) continue to involve all the Barangay Officials in the strict implementation of the existing environmental laws; and 

ii) strengthen the Information and Education Campaign (IEC) especially on “proper waste segregation”, “no segregation, no collection policy” among citizens; 

b) exercise strong political will to implement the laws on preserving and protecting the environment as mandated under Republic Act No. 9003.
observations.reason:
Waste segregation at source is still not being implemented.

Waste segregation at source is still not being implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the: 

(a) the OIC- Human Resource Management Officer to clearly establish the need in augmenting the workforce per Department before hiring Job Order personnel; and 

the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the supporting documents, such as accomplishment reports for services rendered, were attached to the disbursements vouchers to establish validity and propriety of payment.  

Moreover, management should maximize the benefits of hiring Job Orders by properly identifying the needs of each Department so that the proper number of Job Orders possessing the right qualifications could be established.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive follow-up on the review of the contracts:

(a) follow-up on the review of the lease contract; and 

(b) draw the lease contract for all existing market stallholders ensuring the application of the new competitive rates, hence, increasing their income from rentals from market stalls.

We recommended and management agreed to instruct the Municipal Engineer to require from their contractors the issuance of warranty security on all completed projects that will cover the entire defect liability period.
We recommended and management concurred to continue in complying with BIR regulations by remitting all taxes withheld to the BIR.
We recommended and management agreed to continuously adhere to the requirements of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997.
We recommended and management agreed to comply with the National Health Insurance Act by withholding and remitting all contributions withheld to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation.
We recommended and management concurred to continue adhering to the requirement of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009, unremitted
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the:
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant maintained the PPELC except for PPE under SEF.
Property cards for PPE were not maintained by the Municipal Treasurer.
The Local Chief Executive (LCE) is planning to reorganize the Inventory Committee. Inventory Committee who will do the physical inventory and reconciliation of PPE records.
observations.reason:
According to the Municipal Accountant, no records pertaining to PPE were available.
Due to lack of manpower and volume of work, the Municipal Treasurer was not able to maintain property cards.
It was only in CY 2020 that the Municipality was able to create an Inventory Committee who will do the physical inventory and reconciliation of PPE records.
The Municipality diverted their attention on the Lambanog Casualty which happened in December 2019 hence, all of its personnel were busy handling the calamity.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
 consider the cost and complete description of road network assets to be recorded in the general ledger and Local Road Network Ledger Card; and
 disclose the information in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the format prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction:
A JEV was prepared to take up the recording of Road Network Assets.
Verification of CY 2019 showed that some projects funded by BUB were taken up in the General Fund.
observations.reason: A JEV was already prepared taking into account the beginning balance of the local road network and its component in the preparation of System Journal Entry Voucher.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to recognize the total value of the land and record the amount due to the seller, as shown below:
Debit: Land P7,000,000.00
Credit: Accounts Payable P7,000,000.00
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already drawn a JEV to record the land.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to record in the books of accounts the parcels of land acquired by the Municipality as reported by the Municipal Assessor’s Office by drawing the following accounting entries:
Debit: Land P918,000.00
Credit: Government Equity P918,000.00
observations.managementAction: A JEV was drawn to record the land.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to undertake the required initial actions as enumerated above to facilitate the transfer and processing of land titles.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality are still gathering documents for the processing of land titles.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer explained that numerous documents were needed for processing of land titles.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately provide and maintain complete subsidiary ledgers for the special accounts in compliance with Section 313 of RA No. 7160 and Section 110 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, and submit to the Audit Team the financial statements and trial balances with the necessary supporting schedules of each special account.
observations.managementAction: Financial Statements of Economic Enterprise were submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant was not able to maintain Subsidiary Ledgers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant/BAC Secretariat to:
 immediately submit the documents needed to support the disbursements for the said projects; and
 ensure that all disbursements are supported with complete documentary requirements before processing/approving future disbursements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was able to submit the complete plans and documents.
observations.reason: Verification of CY 2019 showed that some documents were complete before DVs were processed. However, unintentionally, they were not able to attach it to the DVs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to:
 a. submit the required attachments of the Municipality’s Annual Accomplishment Report as provided under Section 4.1.C.8 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, such as: (a) brief summary of the reported program or project, (b) copies of reported policy issuances, and (c) recommendations;
 b. coordinate with the Municipal Planning and Development Office to establish a centralized GAD database to have a source of reliable information necessary for its GAD planning, programming and policy formulations. Meanwhile, utilize the existing database, planning and data-gathering tools and management information system to generate gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information from primary and secondary sources, such as surveys and studies; and
 c. furnish the Audit Team a copy of GAR within five working days from the end of January of the ensuing year, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-001.
observations.managementAction:
Required attachments for the Municipality’s Annual Accomplishment Report were submitted except for HGDG results.
GAD AR was submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council to maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF to strengthen the Municipality’s ability in reducing disaster risk and enhancing its disaster preparedness and response capabilities.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2019, the Municipality utilized P3,046,714.91 or 81.16 percent out of the P3,753,639.10 appropriation for LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MSWMB to review and update the Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan of the Municipality every two years or as the need arises in compliance with Section 4 (g), Rule VI of the IRR of RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality updated the Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan. Additional budget needs to be appropriated for the review of Solid Waste Characterization Study (WACS).
observations.reason: The Municipality is still conducting studies on some data needed to update the Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Barangay Officials to look for conducive locations for the Material Recovery Facilities of all barangays, in compliance with Section 1, Rule XI of the IRR of RA No. 9003 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2009-168.
observations.managementAction: Two (2) MRFs are already present. Other programs/projects are being prioritized by some Barangays. Also, lack of location for the construction of MRF in Barangay Poblacion.
observations.reason: Two (2) MRFs are already present. Other programs/projects are being prioritized by some Barangays. Also, lack of location for the construction of MRF in Barangay Poblacion.

observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee conducted physical count and submitted the RPCPPEs.
observations.reason:
The Inventory Committee conducted partial physical count only on selected properties while no physical count was conducted for all inventories of the Municipality.

The Designate GSO and the Municipal Accountant still failed to maintain updated and complete property records. Thus, reconciliation between records could not be facilitated.

The Designate GSO still failed to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), together with the required supporting documents to facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable items.

The schedule of PPEs submitted by the Municipal Accountant were still not reconciled with the PPE ledger balances with discrepancies totaling P136,123,463.77.


The observation was reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: (a).ensure strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 and the CSC Memorandum Circular No. 12 s. 2012 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to avoid accumulation of outstanding cash advances; (b).issue demand letters to concerned officials/employees requiring the immediate settlement of their remaining outstanding cash advances, otherwise consider withholding the payment of their salaries to enforce settlement thereof; (c).refrain from granting additional cash advances unless the previous cash advance given is first settled and proper accounting thereof is made in accordance with Section 4.1.2 of COA Circular N. 97-002; (d).ensure that only permanently appointed officials and employees are granted cash advances; and (e) make the necessary adjustments on the reclassification of the Due from Officers and Employees account totaling P138,773.17 to Advances to Officers and Employees account for fair and accurate presentation of accounts in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.managementAction:
Several employees were able to fully settle/liquidate their outstanding cash advances in CY 2019 thus, decreasing the balance of the Due from Officers and Employees account.

The outstanding cash advances decreased by P89,876.76 or 64.76%.
observations.reason:
Cash advances for travel amounting to P49,896.76 which has been outstanding for 30 days to more than two (2) years remained unliquidated as of December 31, 2019 due to the continued disregard of concerned Municipal employees with the recommendations. 

Cash advances were still granted although previous cash advances were not yet settled and/or accounted for.

The observation was reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) through the Municipal Development Council (MDC), ensure that the 20% Development Fund are fully utilized to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the LGU so that the intended users/beneficiaries may enjoy the immediate benefits derived from said development projects; and (b) consider hiring additional staff in the Engineering Department if warranted, to fast track the preparation of the Program of Works and the conduct of site surveys so as not to delay the procurement activities and project implementation. 


Likewise, the Municipal Accountant was advised to: (a).immediately submit the required supporting documents to be able to arrive at a decision in audit, and henceforth ensure that claims are duly supported with complete and proper documentation before processing for payment to establish the validity and propriety of the transactions; (b) ensure that accounting forms are properly accomplished, complete with the essential information/data for proper referencing and better appreciation of the transactions; and (c) submit immediately the two lacking disbursement vouchers and/or payroll for audit, and henceforth assure timely and complete submission of accounts ten days after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already submitted the two (2) lacking disbursement vouchers and payroll.
observations.reason:
The Municipality still failed to fully utilize the 20% Development Fund registering an overall financial delivery rate of 28.12% only. Out of the 70 projects funded out of the current and continuing appropriations, only 33 projects or 47.14% were implemented or P17,340,972.30 disbursements.

Several disbursements were still not supported with complete and proper documentation.

The observation was reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat and Municipal Accountant submitted some of the lacking documents
observations.reason:
Procurement of goods and infrastructure projects were still not made strictly in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 due to the continued disregard of the concerned employees with the recommendations thus, reiterated in Part II of this Report.

Likewise, the Management still has not imposed legal actions on the contractor who abandoned three (3) projects out of DA funds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the LDRRM Council: (a) ensure the optimal utilization of the LDRRM Fund including the unexpended prior year’s balance as incorporated in the LDRRMF Investment Plan to enhance the resilience of the Municipality and its constituents should disaster and calamities arise in the future; and (b) refrain from disbursing the Fund for purposes outside the mandate of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013. 

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: (a).refrain from recording purchases of goods and supplies as outright expense instead such shall be accounted under the inventory accounts which shall be properly monitored using SLCs pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-002; and (b) coordinate with the MDRRM Office for the proper inventory and reconciliation of the relief goods on hand as of December 31, 2018 and prepare the necessary adjustments for the correct presentation of the related inventory and expense accounts.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality has already reprogrammed the unexpended prior years’ balances of the LDRRMF. The Accounting Office coordinated with the LDRRM Officer regarding the distribution list of relief goods.

Moreover, the Management was able to utilize almost all of the current appropriations for the LDRRMF, registering a 99.95% financial delivery rate.
observations.reason:
The Municipality only utilized 43.84% of the continuing appropriations. Moreover, the prescribed 70% and 30% allocations for the Mitigation Fund and QRF were not observed.
 
The MDRRMF totaling P89,394.00 was still utilized in disbursements which could not be categorized as disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness activities.

Relief items and other supplies and inventories totaling P2,205,458.16 were still recorded as outright expense instead of the proper Inventory account.

The observation was reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide appropriation for the completion of the Municipality’s 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan so that the programs/projects/activities embodied therein such as the establishment of Materials Recovery Facilities (MRF) and the implementation of mixed wastes and residuals’ collection including proper disposal thereof can be implemented accordingly pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 9003. 

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor, thru the Local Sanggunian, initiate the creation of an ordinance that will restrict/halt the practice of open burning of wastes in compliance with Section 48 of RA No. 9003 to avoid various health problems acquired through inhalation of fine particles produced during incineration and to avoid long-term adverse effects on the environment
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality already provided budget for the completion of the Solid Waste Management Plan and allocated budget for MRFs in 11 Barangays. 

Also, in CY 2020, they have already allotted budgets specifically for SWM.

Moreover, the Sangguniang Bayan already issued a Resolution prohibiting incineration or open burning of solid waste materials.
observations.reason:
SWMP was not yet approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission (NSWMC).

Construction of 11 units Materials Recovery Facilities in all barangays within the Municipality amounting to P1,100,000.00 was not yet utilized.

The observation was reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Accountant and other officials concerned to make representations with the bank to furnish the Municipality with copies of debit and credit memos to facilitate recordings of reconciling items in the book of accounts for the fair presentation of the Cash in Bank – LCCA account in the financial statements
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to record all valid reconciling items immediately after the preparation of the Bank Reconciliation statements pursuant to Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 to forestall the accumulation of unadjusted reconciling items.
c. Determine the details of Bank service charge of P 22,800.00 on February 9, 2017 and personally hold the person responsible for such charges;
d. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the documents supporting the inter fund transfers to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording in the books.
observations.managementAction:
Recorded transactions in the books from Municipal Accounting Office.
As to date there is ongoing validation and verification of necessary documents for recording and preparation of Bank Reconciliation statements.
Determined bank charges reflected on February 9, 2017, pending appropriate action.
observations.reason: Lack of proper coordination or proper communication among concerned officers and no direct order from LCE which strictly require the Municipal Accountant to regularly submit all the necessary reports on time per COA rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer send written notice to the concerned payees at least one month before a check becomes stale. Moreover, we required the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments in the books for the long outstanding/ stale checks in accordance with section 59 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Made the necessary adjustments in the books regarding the staled checks.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert more efforts to prepare and submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements of prior year’s accounts for five (5) depository accounts.
observations.managementAction: Submitted monthly Bank reconciliation Statements for the year CY 2019.
observations.reason:
The BRSs for the following years:
FY 2002-2005
FY 2006-2010
FY 2011-2014 are yet to be prepared.

observations.managementAction:
Municipal Treasurer assigned personnel to do reconciliation of cash book and SLs every month.
All pertinent documents regarding RCI are transmitted properly to Municipal Accountings Office weekly as recommended.
.Municipal Treasurer ensured the following:
a. The proper recording of RCI with essential details;
b. The procedure for deposit of all collections are followed accordingly.
