The Municipal Accountant ensured the following: 
a. transmit copies of SLs, Bank Statements and BRS for Municipal Treasurers’ reference.
b. Monitored the assigned personnel in recording the Cash Receipts Journal weekly.
c. Issued certification on balances of all Accountable Officers that maintain a cashbook as well as provide details of the SLs beginning balances based on existing records.
observations.reason: Collections deposit cannot be done on a daily basis because the LBP is in Puerto Princesa City, thus deposit is made whenever the MT or any staff travel to the City.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor thru the Municipal Accountant send demand letter to concerned Accountable Officers, who are still connected with the Municipality, to liquidate their cash advances and henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the negative balances in the RUCA and if warranted, facilitate the processing of payment of over refund/over liquidation made by six (6) accountable officers/ employees.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructed the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the serving of demand letters to accountable officers and comply with the rules and regulations prescribed by COA.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant sent demand letters to 70% of the total concerned officers and employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor through the Municipal Accountant gather all the available information and documents and send demand letters to the concerned individuals and NGO/POs to enforce collection of receivables.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor gave strict reminder and monitoring to the Municipal Accountant pertaining to the collection of the said receivables.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is still on the process of gathering the data concerning the livelihood assistance given to various individuals and NGOs/POs to enforce collection of the said receivables.

observations.managementAction:
1. The GSO  assigned respective personnel to complete the physical count of properties with proper recording to reconcile with the Municipal Accountant records’
2. GSO identified the unreturned items with full details and will summon the concerned party as required.
d. The Municipal Accountant  assigned personnel to record the PPE in the subsidiary ledgers on a per piece basis.
observations.reason: Lack of Personnel in GSO, Municipal Engineering and Municipal Accountant Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant keep and maintain complete Subsidiary Ledgers which should contain the details of all PPE pursuant to Section 114 of the Manual on NGAS, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant  assigned personnel to record the PPE in the subsidiary ledgers on a per piece basis.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Create an Inventory Committee who will conduct inventory of the local roads within the Municipality and submit the corresponding reports to the Audit Team for evaluation and verification; and
b. Enjoin the concerned Department Heads’ compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008.
We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant adequately disclose the Road Network System in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor created an inventory team for Local Road and Road Network System.
observations.reason: The Municipal is still validating and verifying records for disclosure of the Road Network System in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adjust the accumulated depreciation of the affected PPE accounts in the SLs.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant fully accounted the accumulated depreciation in the books.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor: 

a. Maximize the utilization of the 20% DF by speeding up the implementation of the prioritized programs and activities funded out of the 20% DF as per Annual Investment Program and that closer coordination among the officials involved in planning, execution and monitoring be undertaken to achieve the desired level of outcome. 
b. Instruct the MPDO to immediately look into the unimplemented developmental projects and revised the Approved Budget for the Contract, if warranted, to compensate for the inflation of construction materials. We advised the MPDO to properly plan the implementation stage of the PPAs to ensure prompt project completion. 
c. Reprogram the excess balances of all completed and discontinued projects and unprogrammed fund amounting to Php 2,441,950.18 and Php 26,723,328.20, respectively, to finance development PPAs identified to be funded under the 20% DF in CY2019.
observations.managementAction: Implemented various PPAs funded under CY 2018 20% DF.
observations.reason:
CY 2018 20% DF was still not fully utilized.
No proposed prioritized project from the MPDO and the identification of cost of the involved projects is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer reconcile their reports to determine the cash deficiency of the fund; (b) Municipal Treasurer explain the incurrence of cash deficit of the fund; and (c) Municipal Mayor direct the Local Finance Committee to investigate and identify the cause of the deficit so that remedial measures can be implemented.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor advised the concerned departments for review and reconciliation of the said budget deficit and report the result immediately to identify the underlying cause.
observations.reason: Still waiting for the validated reports from concerned departments.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the: 

a. Municipal Accountant prepare necessary accounting entries to recognize the unexpended balances of LDRRMF for the years 2015, 2017 and 2018 in the books for both General and the Trust Funds and reclassify the balance of the Other Payables account to Trust Liabilities- DRRMF account;
b. MDRRMO, the Municipal and the Treasurer facilitate the immediate transfer of unexpended balance of LDRRMF totaling P14,869,167.84 to Special Trust Fund bank account and present evidence thereof; and 
c. Municipal Mayor, though the Municipal Budget Officer, submit budget proposal for the reverted fund of P6,000,000.00.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant recorded the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF and the corresponding cash were transferred during the 1st quart of CY2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the inventory committee, property custodian and accounting unit diligently conduct the physical count of inventory items and property, plant and equipment, prepare the RPCI and RPCPPE, respectively, and reconcile their respective records to address any discrepancies thereof.

b. Recognized Local Road Networks in the Books of Accounts are Unreliable due to Incomplete Documentation

We recommended that Management, through the following heads of offices of the LGU furnish the Audit Team with the following documents to support the validity of the recognized amount of Road Networks totaling to ₱28,936,022.59, net of accumulated depreciation:

Municipal Accountant – Registries of Public Infrastructures, working paper on the distribution of costs for road components, working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement costs, subsidiary records for road and road components and lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each road and road components at the end of the year;

Municipal Treasurer (designated MGSO) – Local Road Inventory and Road Map and present complete Local Road Network Property Ledger Card for all roads and road components;

Municipal Engineer – Report on the complete description and cost segregation of road and road components which was the basis of the Municipal Accountant for the recognition of the Road Networks in the books of accounts at year-end; and

Inventory Committee – Report on the Physical Count of the Public Infrastructures of the LGU including the Road Network System. 

c. Real Estate owned by the Municipality were not Properly Valued; Inadequate Supporting Documents to Prove Ownership

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor register in the name of the Municipality of Rizal the parcels of land owned by the LGU, under the Torrens Title System. Likewise, the municipality appropriate funds needed to secure the TCTs of the real estate.



It was further recommended that an appraisal committee be created to conduct assessment of the values of the donated parcels of land to facilitate recording in the books of account.

We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist conduct physical inventory of animals owned by the municipality.

Moreover, reconcile existing stocks against accounting records and the accounting unit accordingly adjust the balances in the books of accounts.
We recommended that management stop the practice of charging RATA to MOOE and see to it that for future transactions, it will be part of the Personal Services subject to the 55% PS limitation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Solid Waste Management Board to:

a. Remove the wastes/garbage scattered in the Municipal MRF and recycle and/or dispose them accordingly following the disposal procedures provided in the Solid Waste Management Plan;

b. Involve closely the Barangays and its constituents in the implementation of the strategies of the LGU’s SWM Plan, as well as continuously intensify information campaign for the strict compliance of the segregation at source and collection of solid wastes; and

c. Take appropriate actions for the collection of garbage of the LGU and make efficient use of the different facilities and equipment expended by the government for the said purpose.

The LDRRMF Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) did not include projects and activities for the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years totaling ₱9,656,191.49.

We recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) and other concerned officers include the formulated projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended balances of LDRRMF of previous years in the LDRRMFIP under a separate caption for the comprehensive formulation and implementation of disaster risk reduction and management activities.

Only ₱694,627.93 or approximately five percent (5%) out of the total appropriation of ₱12,978,219.05 for LDRRMF was expended during the year which may affect the carrying-out of the purpose for which the fund was created.

We recommended that management, through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office:

Monitor the utilization of the LDRRMF focusing on programs, projects and activities (PPAs) requiring the highest priority in terms of urgency and/or impact on the four thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management; and Ensure efficient and effective implementation of all programmed projects identified during the budget year to optimize the utilization of the fund and to warrant that goals and objectives or reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities be achieved.

Non-Establishment of a Resilient Evacuation Center

We recommended that management prioritize the establishment of a permanent evacuation center intended for disaster preparedness and response in accordance with the set-forth standards provided in DILG Memorandum Circular 122, series of 2018.

We recommended the GAD Focal Point Person override the monitoring and evaluation for the full implementation of the enlisted GAD activities for CY 2018 that were still unimplemented by the LGU.Moreover, it was recommended that management see to it that for the ensuing years, full implementation of the GAD Plan and Budget be purposefully achieved.

We recommended the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC), and other concerned officials to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004, and regularly prepare and submit to the Office of the Audit Team Leader the Quarterly Report on Government PPAs.
We recommended that the management require the AO to properly and completely record the transactions in the cashbook to reflect the correct balances at the end of the period.

We recommended that the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF be prepared and submitted within the required period for the timely evaluation of the implementation and monitoring of the disaster risk reduction management projects, programs and activities of the LGU.


We recommended that the members of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (MDRRMC) ensure that the programs, projects and activities to be included in the LDRRMFIP shall cover the priority projects and activities that are included or related to the DRRM activities as declared under Section 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-002. Be guided with the provisions of Section 5.0 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the LDRRMC conduct assessment on the PPAs under the LDRRMF as guide in the prioritization of particular areas that the expected beneficiaries need most to have smooth and efficient implementation of the targeted programs or projects.
observations.reason: Management still had low implementation of projects, plan and activities for DRRM as reiterated in Observation No. 5, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that concerned municipal officials/employees submit Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis as required by COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Management assured the audit team that starting this year, the required report be submitted as prescribed in the COA Circular No. 2012-002.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive thru the Municipal Planning Development Officer (MPDO) strategize their planning activities to ensure timely implementation of programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: During the exit conference, management gave an assurance to strategize on how to fast track implementation to avoid delay.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 4, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately reconcile the Cash in Bank accounts appearing in the books of accounts at year-end against the balances per bank statements and those confirmed by the depository banks and make the necessary adjustments to present the correct balance of Cash in Bank account at any given date.
observations.managementAction: The Management will prepare Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements to determine the reconciling items.
observations.reason: No Bank Reconciliation Statements submitted to the Office of Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the personnel concerned be required to immediately liquidate their cash advances. We also recommend strict adherence to the aforementioned provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445 and the specific provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 to ensure proper granting, utilization of cash advances.
observations.reason: Reiterated with modification in part II of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate give utmost priority in analyzing and determining the composition of these long outstanding Receivables. Otherwise, comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 in requesting for the write – off of the long outstanding receivables for which the LGU could not support its validity and propriety and could not establish rightful claims.
observations.managementAction: The Management did not file a request for write off.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended management to:

a.	Direct the Municipal Accountant adopt the perpetual inventory method for supplies and materials;

b.	Provide budget for storage for the supply requirements, otherwise, if no available storage area is available we recommend to establish control procedures to regulate the issuance to the end-users within a month; and

c.	A central procurement system should be introduced by establishing the actual needs of each section offices as documented in the Annual Procurement Plan.
observations.managementAction: Supplies and Materials are still treated as outright expenses

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management enforce the immediate settlement of the suspended transactions by the submission of complete supporting documents required in the Notices of Suspension. Otherwise, the appropriate Notice of Disallowance shall be issued immediately as the prescribed period to settle the suspended transactions have already lapsed. Likewise, we recommend that Management should strictly enforce the settlement or payment of the long outstanding disallowance amounting to ₱91,512.35.

We recommended the Management comply with the provisions of IRR of RA 1021 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 and instruct the LDRRM Officer to submit the report within the required period as prescribed in Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The Monthly report on the Sources and Utilization of the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (DRRMF) were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountable Officers submit regularly the required monthly report of accountability for Accountable Forms and the Report of Collections pursuant to Section 50 and 51 of Volume II of NGAS Manual and Section 64 of P.D 1445 to ensure proper accounting of collection and deposits. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer is required to submit the CRAAF duly supported with the individual RAAF of all collecting officer not later than 5th day of the ensuing month.

We recommended the General Service Officer to regularly submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels with the accomplished Drivers Trip Ticket for each vehicle/equipment to support the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the Auditor for the verification in the audit of fuel, oil and lubricants.

We recommended that the LGU GFPS to set-up and maintain GAD database and develop GAD Codes in order to adhere to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular Nos. 2013-01 and 2016-01 in the implementation of GAD programs, projects and activities.

We recommended that Management:

a.	Submit the identified lacking documents to support the public bidding conducted and strictly observe the documentary and procedural requirements of the Procurement Law to preclude the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance.

b.	Comply with the standard format of bidding documents as prescribed under Government Procurement and Policy Board (GPPB) Resolution No. 06-2010 dated December 17, 2010 and supply correct and complete information thereof to maintain reliability and integrity thereof.

c.	Require the contractor to post a Performance Security prior to the signing of contract in adherence to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

d.	Impose deduction of retention money from progress billings or provision of irrevocable standby letter of credit until 50% work completion and a satisfactory work is proven.

e.	Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the supporting documents of the disbursement vouchers are complete to prove the validity and legality of the claims and to avoid the suspension/disallowance of the transactions.

We   recommended that Management cause the immediate submission of the required documents to justify the legality or validity of the disbursements. Also, for future disbursement of funds, the required supporting documents should accompany the Disbursement Voucher before payment and to comply with the proper disbursement procedures to avoid suspension /disallowance in audit.

We recommended that Management:

Strictly comply with the procedural requirements in resorting to Negotiated Procurement as a result of Two Failed Biddings prescribed by the procurement law to ensure that the principles of transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency, and economy in the procurement process are carried out.

Submit the identified lacking documents to support the procurement activities conducted and strictly observe the documentary requirements of the Procurement Law and other related COA Issuances to preclude the occurrence of irregular transactions, to wit:

Proof of posting of invitation or request for submission of price quotation in the PhilGEPS website, website of the procuring agency, if available, and at any conspicuous place reserved for this purpose in the premises of the procuring agency for a period of seven calendar days,
Performance and warranty securities,
Proof of posting of Notice of Award in the PhilGEPs website, the website of the procuring entity concerned, if available, and at any conspicuous place the premises of the procuring entity,
Approved Procurement Plan,
Statement of the prospective bidder that it is not blacklisted or barred from bidding by the Government or any of its agencies, offices, corporations or LGUs,
Sworn affidavit of the bidder that it is not related to the HOPE by consanguinity or affinity up to the third civil degree, 
BAC Resolution recommending award of contract to Lowest Calculated Responsive Bid (LCRB)
Inspection and acceptance report signed "Inspected by" by the authorized agency inspector and signed "Accepted by" by the authorized end-user to whom the item was delivered or the property officer if item is for stock
Agency's offer for negotiations with selected suppliers, contractors or consultants, and
Evidence of invitation of observers in all stages of the negotiation.

We recommended and management agreed to instruct the Municipal Engineer to require from their contractors the issuance of warranty security on all completed projects that will cover the entire defect liability period.
observations.managementAction: Management was given personal warranties by the contractors and after sales warranty by suppliers.
observations.reason: Warranty securities should still be required as per R.A. 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant to maintain the PPELC for each category of property; b. Municipal Treasurer to maintain property cards for property, plant and equipment, and work animals to account for the receipt and disposition of the same; c. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra efforts to reconcile the accounting and property records; and d. Inventory Committee to complete the yearly physical inventory of property, plant and equipment and to submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year.
observations.managementAction: No actions were taken by the management.
observations.reason: PPELC for each category of property were still not maintained by the Municipal Accountant. Property cards were still not maintained by the Municipal Treasurer. Records of all the real properties of the Municipality were not updated. Some PPE accounts were still not provided with depreciation. Physical inventory by the Inventory Committee was not completed as of December 31, 2018, thus, RPCPPE was not prepared and submitted. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1, page 23.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to record all the property acquired out of Trust Fund in accordance with the provision of Section 104 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I. In addition, furnish the Municipal General Services Officer with the subject Journal Entry Vouchers in order to include those property in the property records of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: No actions were taken by the management.
observations.reason: Projects from BuB and various NGAs were still not recorded and accounted for in the books of accounts of the Municipality. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 4, page 27.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately provide and maintain complete Subsidiary Ledgers for the special accounts, in compliance with Section 313 of RA No. 7160 and Section 110 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, and submit to the Audit Team the financial statements and trial balances with the necessary supporting schedules of each special account.
observations.managementAction: No actions were taken by the management.
observations.reason: Special accounts with subsidiary ledgers were still not provided by the management. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 8, page 33.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality, through the Solid Waste Management Board, develop a 10-year comprehensive Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan that will ensure the long-term management of solid waste in compliance with the requirements of RA No. 9003 to protect public health and the environment.
observations.reason: The Management still failed to develop their 10-year comprehensive Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan due to lack of funds and/or appropriations to support the implementation of the Programs, Projects and Activities (PPAs) for Solid Waste Management thus, reiterated in the Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the         (a) BAC strictly comply with the requirements of RA No. 9184 and its IRR as regards the implementation of infrastructure projects; and (b) the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of supporting documents before processing claims for payments pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.reason: Procurement for infrastructure projects was not made strictly in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 due to the continued disregard of the concerned employees with the recommendations thus, reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Government of Rizal, through the (a) Municipal  Planning and Development Officer and the Municipal Engineer ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund by being more aggressive in implementing the priority projects embodied in the approved local development plan to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes in the Municipality;    (b) Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant          (i) reconcile their records to present the correct SCBAA for the year ended December 31, 2017; and (ii) verify their records with regard to the difference of P3,069,913.09 between the cash balance and the budget records.  If there is indeed excess cash balance, the amount could be appropriated for other priority projects as may be identified by the Local Development Council; (c) Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant coordinate with each other in the production of the 15 disbursement vouchers/payrolls to facilitate audit thereof; and (d) Municipal Accountant review the regularity and completeness of the supporting documents prescriber under COA Circular No. 2012-001 before processing claims for payment to establish the validity, regularity and propriety of the disbursements.
observations.reason: The 20% Development Fund were still not optimally utilized registering an overall financial delivery rate of only 39.45%. Only three (3) out of the target 14 PPAs were implemented in CY 2018, not to mention the remaining 29 unimplemented projects from previous years’ due to lack of personnel in the Engineering Department. Moreover, disbursements were still made despite incomplete documentations. This observation is reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management require the AO to properly and completely record the transactions in the cashbook to reflect the correct balances at the end of the period.
observations.reason: Cash count conduct ed in CY 2019 revealed that the AO is not regularly updating its cash book due to time constraints and numerous workloads.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended balances of LDRRMF of previous years be included in the LDRRMFIP under a separate caption for comprehensive formulation and implementation of disaster risk reduction and management activities.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II – Detailed Observations and Recommendations portion of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended compliance of AO No. 70, s. 2003 dated April 14, 2003 for the creation of Internal Audit Service and its implementing guidelines under DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-4 dated March 22, 2004 for the guidelines on the organization and staffing of Internal Auditing Services.
observations.reason: The Local Government Unit is uncertain to create an Internal Audit Service because of the qualification of the personnel and funding constraints. The Municipal Accountant serves as the internal auditor of the LGU, and satisfactorily delivers the necessary functions thereon.

observations.recommendation:
1.7 We have recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant reclassify the
completed projects to their proper asset account as follows:
Debit Credit
Road Networks ₱14,335,217.70
Water Supply Systems 19,338,947.70
Other Structure 300,000.00
CIP – Infrastructure Assets ₱33,974,165.40

2.5 We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. create an inventory Committee to conduct an inventory of the local roads
under the jurisdiction of the municipality and render the Report on the
Physical count of the Road Network System, and
b. require all offices concerned to meet and come up with the complete
description and cost segregation of road projects as basis in recording their
cost or valuation in the books of the municipality.

3.5 We have recommended that the Accounting Office coordinate with the Treasury
Office to determine reasons for the long outstanding checks that already became
stale for proper adjustment in the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to the officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances and demand the immediate settlement thereof; and stop granting another cash advance to officers and employees with unliquidated cash advances. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should not process the cash advance of officials other than  for official travel and stricly comply with provisions of Section 339 of RA 7160 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 24, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant give utmost priority in analyzing and determining the composition of the long outstanding Receivables. Otherwise, comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 in requesting for the write-off of the long outstanding receivables.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct complete periodic physical inventory count of property, plant and equipment by type, which shall be made annually and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to be submitted to the office of the COA Auditor not later than January 31 of each year. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should maintain Property, Plant & Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) for each category of assets. The General Services Officer should properly classify/identify the PPEs thru the maintenance of property cards to ascertain its accuracy and existence and reconcile any difference with that of the records of the Municipal Accountant.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose the noted deficiencies in the Notes to Financial Statements of the Municipality as required by IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the cited auditing and accounting laws, rules and regulations.  Submit the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents to the Auditor within the prescribed period to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.

To immediately purchase a safety vault or steel cabinet with vault, where collections can be kept before it will be deposited to the bank and ensure that the purchase of the said vault is in accordance with the provisions of Republic Act 9184; and
To provide additional security features in the Office of the Municipal Treasurer such as additional door locks and window grills.

To stop the practice of allowing and/or maintaining pre-signed checks in the custody of the Municipal Treasurer; and
To observe proper planning in the preparation of vouchers with complete supporting documents and its corresponding checks, considering the availability of the authorized signatories of checks.

To require the Municipal Engineer to: (i) monitor the simultaneous implementation of projects by the same contractor to guard against unwarranted delays or substandard quality of work; (ii) recommend for the imposition of liquidated damages on the delays that were not justified;
To Scrutinize the contractor’s manpower and equipment on all the projects to be awarded and not on an individual project only in cases where the latter is the lowest bidder in several projects which have the same or overlapping implementation period. This is in order to require the contractor to augment its manpower and/or equipment to be utilized in the project awarded to it or to disqualify it from obtaining several award or cotract in consonance with the provisions of the Item 23.6 of the 2016 RIRR OF RA 9184.

We recommended that the Management secure copies of the MOA/TA from the source agencies and furnish the COA Audit Team copies the same to determine whether fund transfers are properly utilized and the requirements for projects implementation and reporting are strictly complied with.

We recommended that the Management submit all the required supporting documents. Likewise, it is recommended that the Management require the Accountant to ensure that all the Disbursement Vouchers are supported with the documentary requirements, as enumerated in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 24, 2012.
We recommended that the Management refrain from procuring goods and services on a reimbursement basis, except petty purchases to be paid out from the Petty Cash Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately transfer the unexpended balance of the DRRMF to a special trust fund under the account “Trust Liability – DRRM” as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-002. Consequently, the account shall be used to record transfers of the agency’s unutilized QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years, receipts of NDRRMF, DRRMF from other LGUs and other sources. We further recommend that subsidiary ledgers for the transfer of the agency’s unutilized DRRMF to the special trust fund be maintained.

We recommended that the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer fast track the submission of the Solid Waste Management Plan to the Environment Management Bureau to be forwarded to the National Solid Waste Management Commission and ensure that the programs and activities included in the plan are consistent with the National Solid Waste Management Framework.

We recommended that the LGU submit the GAD Accomplishment Report for the team to evaluate its conformity with the approved plan and if directed towards the realization of our country’s commitment, plan, and policies on women empowerment, gender equality, and gender development.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a) concerned office or Inventory Committee, to complete the physical count of the assets of the LGU and to render the corresponding report; (b) MTO to maintain and update records on accountability on PPEs; and (c) reconciliation of records of MTO and Municipal Accountant to settle any discrepancy.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MDRRMO to continue the implementation of the remaining LDRRM projects/programs/activities in order to guarantee the attainment of the Municipality's objective of mitigating the impacts of disaster/calamities towards building a disaster-resilient communities.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the BAC members and the Municipal Engineer to properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to pave the way for their timely implementation and completion; and (b) ensure that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time to arrest possible delays.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Government Unit will (a) ensure that the expenses incurred for the programs and activities have properly authorized appropriations; (b) provide the required trainings/seminars to the members of GFPS to aid them in the proper discharge of their duties and responsibilities; and (c) create M&E team who will assess the overall effectiveness and efficiency of their gender mainstreaming efforts.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive will (a) instruct the MSWMB to: i) continue to involve all the Barangay Officials in the strict implementation of the existing environmental laws; and ii) strengthen the Information and Education Campaign (IEC) especially on “proper waste segregation”, “no segregation, no collection policy” among citizens; b) exercise strong political will to implement the laws on preserving and protecting the environment as mandated under Republic Act No. 9003.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) the OIC- Human Resource Management Officer to clearly establish the need in augmenting the workforce per Department before hiring Job Order personnel; and (b) the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the supporting documents, such as accomplishment reports for services rendered, were attached to the disbursements vouchers to establish validity and propriety of payment.  Moreover, management should maximize the benefits of hiring Job Orders by properly identifying the needs of each Department so that the proper number of Job Orders possessing the right qualifications could be established.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive follow-up on the review of the contracts (a) follow-up on the review of the lease contract; and (b) draw the lease contract for all existing market stallholders ensuring the application of the new competitive rates, hence, increasing their income from rentals from market stalls.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Market Supervisor to (a) send out demand letters to market lessees requesting for the updating of the rental payments on market stalls occupied by them; and (b) inform the lessees of the provisions under the Revenue Code of 2017 which will be included in the Contact of Lease, particularly on the (i) qualifications of the lessees; (ii) prompt payments of rentals and (iii) the penalties and surcharges to be paid during violations of the provisions under the said code and the penalties and surcharges to be imposed on late payments of rental of market stalls.

We recommended and management agreed to instruct the Municipal Engineer to require from their contractors the issuance of warranty security on all completed projects that will cover the entire defect liability period.
We recommended and management concurred to continue in complying with BIR regulations by remitting all taxes withheld to the BIR.
We recommended and management agreed to continuously adhere to the requirements of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997.
We recommended and management agreed to comply with the National Health Insurance Act by withholding and remitting all contributions withheld to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation.
We recommended and management concurred to continue adhering to the requirement of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009, unremitted
We recommended and management agreed to require all those named liable in the Notice of Disallowance to refund the amount disallowed.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor
require the:
a) Municipal Accountant to maintain the PPELC for each category of
properties;
b) Municipal Treasurer to maintain property cards for PPE to account for the
receipt and disposition of the same;
c) Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra efforts to
reconcile the accounting and property records; and
d) Inventory Committee to complete the yearly physical inventory of PPE and
to submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. consider the cost and complete description of road network assets
provided by the Municipal Engineer in the preparation of Journal Entry
Voucher taking into account beginning balance of the local road network
and its component in the general ledger and Local Road Network Ledger
Card; and
b. disclose the information in the Notes to Financial Statements in
accordance with the format prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
draw a Journal Entry Voucher to recognize the total value of the land and
record the amount due to the seller, as shown below:
Particulars Debit Credit
Land P7,000,000.00
Accounts Payable P7,000,000.00

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that Municipal Mayor
direct the Municipal Accountant to record all the property acquired out of Trust
Fund in accordance with the provision of Section 104 of MNGAS for LGUs,
Volume I. In addition, furnish the Municipal General Services Officer with the
subject Journal Entry Vouchers in order to include those property in the
property records of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
record in the books of accounts the parcels of land acquired by the
Municipality as reported by the Municipal Assessor’s Office by drawing the
following accounting entries:
Particulars Debit Credit
Land P918,000.00
Government Equity P918,000.00

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. trace the period or date of acquisition of the abovementioned property and
compute the depreciation base on the estimated useful life of assets
provided under COA Circular 2003-007; and
b. take up the prior years’ and current year’s depreciation as Prior Years
Adjustment to be closed to Government Equity.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to
undertake the required initial actions as enumerated above to facilitate the
transfer and titling of the aforementioned parcels of land in the name of the
Municipality to protect its interest from third party claims and illegal
dispositions.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor
direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately provide and maintain complete
subsidiary ledgers for the special accounts in compliance with Section 313 of
RA No. 7160 and Section 110 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, and submit to
the Audit Team the financial statements and trial balances with the necessary
supporting schedules of each special account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant/BAC Secretariat to:


















• immediately submit the documents needed to support the
disbursements for the said projects; and
• ensure that all disbursements are supported with complete
documentary requirements before processing/approving future
disbursements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to:
a. submit the required attachments of the Municipality’s Annual
Accomplishment Report as provided under Section 4.1.C.8 of PCW-DILG-
DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, such as: (a) brief summary of the reported
program or project, (b) copies of reported policy issuances, and (c) actions
taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and recommendations;
b. coordinate with the Municipal Planning and Development Office to
establish a centralized GAD database to have a source of reliable
information necessary for its GAD planning, programming and policy
formulations. Meanwhile, utilize the existing database, planning and data-
gathering tools and management information system to generate gender
statistics and sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information
from primary and secondary sources, such as surveys and studies; and
c. furnish the Audit Team a copy of GAR within five working days from the
end of January of the ensuing year, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-001.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that the Municipal
Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to provide sufficient disclosure of the
LDDRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction Management Council to maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF to
strengthen the Municipality’s ability in reducing disaster risk and enhancing its
disaster preparedness and response capabilities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MSWMB to review and
update the Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan of the Municipality every
two years or as the need arises in compliance with Section 4 (g), Rule VI of the
IRR of RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Barangay Officials to look
for conducive locations for the Material Recovery Facilities of all barangays, in
compliance with Section 1, Rule XI of the IRR of RA No. 9003 and DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2009-168.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: (a) ensure strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 and the CSC Memorandum Circular No. 12 s. 2012 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to avoid accumulation of outstanding cash advances; (b) issue demand letters to concerned officials/employees requiring the immediate settlement of their remaining outstanding cash advances, otherwise consider withholding the payment of their salaries to enforce settlement thereof; (c) refrain from granting additional cash advances unless the previous cash advance given is first settled and proper accounting thereof is made in accordance with Section 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002; (d) ensure that only permanently appointed officials and employees are granted cash advances; and (e) make the necessary adjustments on the reclassification of the Due from Officers and Employees account totaling P138,773.17 to Advances to Officers and Employees account for fair and accurate presentation of accounts in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) through the Municipal Development Council (MDC), ensure that the 20% Development Fund are fully utilized to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the LGU so that the intended users/beneficiaries may enjoy the immediate benefits derived from said development projects; and             (b) consider hiring additional staff in the Engineering Department, if warranted, to fast track the preparation of the Program of Works and the conduct of detailed engineering so as not to delay the procurement activities and project implementation.
Likewise, the Municipal Accountant was advised to: (a) immediately submit the required supporting documents to be able to arrive at a decision in audit, and henceforth ensure that claims are duly supported with complete and proper documentation before processing for payment to establish the validity and propriety of the transactions; (b) ensure that accounting forms are properly accomplished, complete with the essential information/data for proper referencing and better appreciation of the transactions; and (c) submit immediately the two lacking disbursement vouchers and/or payroll for audit, and henceforth assure timely and complete submission of accounts ten days after the end of each month.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the LDRRM Council: (a) ensure the optimal utilization of the LDRRM Fund including the unexpended prior year’s balance as incorporated in the LDRRMF Investment Plan to enhance the resilience of the Municipality and its constituents should disaster and calamities arise in the future; and (b) refrain from disbursing the Fund for purposes outside the mandate of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: (a) refrain from recording purchases of goods and supplies as outright expense instead such shall be accounted under the inventory accounts which shall be properly monitored using SLCs pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-002; and (b) coordinate with the MDRRM Office for the proper inventory and reconciliation of the relief goods on hand as of December 31, 2018 and prepare the necessary adjustments for the correct presentation of the related inventory and expense accounts.

In view thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide appropriation for the completion of the Municipality’s 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan so that the programs/projects/activities embodied therein such as the establishment of Materials Recovery Facilities (MRF) and the implementation of mixed wastes and residuals’ collection including proper disposal thereof can be implemented accordingly pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 9003.
Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor, thru the Local Sanggunian, initiate the creation of an ordinance that will restrict/halt the practice of open burning of wastes in compliance with Section 48 of RA No. 9003 to avoid various health problems acquired through inhalation of fine particles produced during incineration and to avoid long-term adverse effects on the environment.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Accountant and other
officials concerned to make representations with the bank to furnish the
Municipality with copies of debit and credit memos to facilitate recording of
reconciling items in the book of accounts for the fair presentation of the Cash
in Bank – LCCA account in the financial statements;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to record all valid reconciling items
immediately after the preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statements
pursuant to Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 to forestall the accumulation of unadjusted reconciling items;
c. Determine the details of the bank service charge of ₱22,800.00 on February 9,
2017 and personally hold the person responsible for such charges; and
d. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the documents supporting the
interfund transfers to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording in the
books.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer send
written notice to the concerned payees at least one month before a check becomes
stale. Moreover, we required the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary
adjustments in the books for the long outstanding/stale checks in accordance with
Section 59 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
Municipal Accountant to exert more efforts to prepare and submit the Bank
Reconciliation Statements of prior years’ accounts for five (5) depository
accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer
close the Municipality’s account with FCB. We reiterated our recommendation
that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer account the prior
years’ service charge given by FCB to the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
a. Reconcile her cashbook against the SLs of the Municipal Accountant for both
the Cash in Local Treasury and Cash in Bank accounts every end of the
month;
b. Submit the RCI to the Municipal Accountant weekly with all checks issued
including cancelled and voided checks arranged chronologically, and
unissued/unreleased checks properly marked as “Unclaimed” in the report;
c. Strictly observe the proper numbering of the RCI containing the fund code,
year, month, and series number which should be one series a year;
d. Deposit intact all collections weekly or as soon as the collections reach
₱10,000.00; and
e. Prepare the Report of Collections and Deposits summarizing all collections
received and deposits made together with the validated deposit slips indicating
thereat the collector and source of collections and submit the same to the
Municipal Accountant for recording in the books;
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Furnish the Municipal Treasurer with the copies of SLs, Bank Statements and
BRS for her reference in reconciling her cashbooks with accounting records;
b. Record in the Cash Receipts Journal all deposit of collections based on the date
it was deposited;
c. Issue a certification on the balances of all Accountable Officers that maintain
a cashbook for their reference in the reconciliation of their cashbook against
the SLs; and
d. Provide details of the SLs beginning balances based on existing records.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor thru the
Municipal Accountant send demand letter to concerned Accountable Officers,
who are still connected with the Municipality, to liquidate their cash advances
and henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the negative
balances in the RUCA and if warranted, facilitate the processing of payment of
over refund/over liquidation made by six (6) accountable officers/employees.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the Municipal
Accountant gather all the available information and documents and send demand
letters to the concerned individuals and NGOs/POs to enforce collection of
receivables.

We reiterated our recommendation that the:
a. GSO and the Municipal Engineer complete the physical count of properties of the
Municipality and submit the Report on Physical Count of PPE in the prescribed
form with information on acquisition costs;
b. GSO and the Municipal Accountant reconcile the Report on the Physical Count of
PPE, Property Cards, PPE Ledger Cards, and accounting records, taking into
consideration the noted deficiencies in audit, and furnish the Audit Team with the
said report;
c. GSO (i) create a database of all properties owned by the Municipality with the
corresponding acquisition costs and property numbers, preferably similar to those
indicated in the subsidiary ledgers of the Municipal Accountant; (ii) write a letter
of demand to the previous custodian of unreturned PPE totaling ₱912,000.00; and
(iii) submit an IIRUP with transportation equipment’s checklist, pictures and in-
house appraisal values; and
d. Municipal Accountant record the PPE in the subsidiary ledgers on a per piece
basis.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant keep and maintain
complete Subsidiary Ledgers which should contain the details of all PPE
pursuant to Section 114 of the Manual on NGAS, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor insure all government
properties with the Property Insurance Fund. We also recommend the OIC-GSO
to register and ensure full insurance coverage of the Municipality owned vehicles.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor facilitate the
titling of those real properties acquired in prior years. Henceforth, the
Municipality through the Municipal Treasurer, GSO and the Municipal
Accountant should observe strictly the rules and regulations regarding the
acquisition of real properties.
We also advised that the Municipal Accountant support the general ledger
controlling account with complete and updated SL and RPLC to effectively
control and monitor any movement in the Land account.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Create an Inventory Committee who will conduct inventory of the local roads
within the Municipality and submit the corresponding reports to the Audit Team
for evaluation and verification; and
b. Enjoin the concerned Department Heads’ compliance with the requirements
of COA Circular No. 2015-008.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant adequately disclose
the Road Network System in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adjust the accumulated
depreciation of the affected PPE accounts in the SLs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor stop the granting of
retirement privileges without legal basis and demand refund of the retirement
privilege amounting to ₱330,000.00 because to allow the payee to retain the cash
retirement incentive would amount to his unjust enrichment to the prejudice of
the Municipality.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the inventory committee, property custodian and accounting unit diligently conduct the physical count of inventory items and property, plant and equipment, prepare the RPCI and RPCPPE, respectively, and reconcile their respective records to address any discrepancies thereof.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the inventory committee, property custodian and accounting unit diligently conduct the physical count of inventory items and property, plant and equipment, prepare the RPCI and RPCPPE, respectively, and reconcile their respective records to address any discrepancies thereof.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Assessor register in the name of the Municipality of Rizal the parcels of land owned by the LGU, under the Torrens Title System. Likewise, the municipality appropriate funds needed to secure the TCTs of the real estate.
It was further recommended that an appraisal committee be created to conduct assessment of the values of the donated parcels of land to facilitate recording in the books of account.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist conduct physical inventory of animals owned by the municipality.
Moreover, reconcile existing stocks against accounting records and the accounting unit accordingly adjust the balances in the books of accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management stop the practice of charging RATA to MOOE and see to it that for future transactions, it will be part of the Personal Services subject to the 55% PS limitation.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Solid Waste Management Board to:
Remove the wastes/garbage scattered in the Municipal MRF and recycle and/or dispose them accordingly following the disposal procedures provided in the Solid Waste Management Plan;
Involve closely the Barangays and its constituents in the implementation of the strategies of the LGU’s SWM Plan, as well as continuously intensify information campaign for the strict compliance of the segregation at source and collection of solid wastes; and
Take appropriate actions for the collection of garbage of the LGU and make efficient use of the different facilities and equipment expended by the government for the said purpose.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) and other concerned officers include the formulated projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended balances of LDRRMF of previous years in the LDRRMF Investment Plan under a separate caption for the comprehensive formulation and implementation of disaster risk reduction and management activities.
Management Comment:
During the exit conference, the MDRRMO presented to the Audit Team its proposed LDRRMF Investment Plan for its previous years’ unexpended balances for CY 2019, embodying therein balances of CY 2013-2018.
Only ₱694,627.93 or approximately five percent (5%) out of the total appropriation of ₱12,978,219.05 for LDRRMF was expended during the year which may affect the carrying-out of the purpose for which the fund was created.
Recommendations:
We recommended that management, through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office:
Monitor the utilization of the LDRRMF focusing on programs, projects and activities (PPAs) requiring the highest priority in terms of urgency and/or impact on the four thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management; and
Ensure efficient and effective implementation of all programmed projects identified during the budget year to optimize the utilization of the fund and to warrant that goals and objectives or reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities be achieved.
Recommendation:
We recommended that management prioritize the establishment of a permanent evacuation center intended for disaster preparedness and response in accordance with the set-forth standards provided in DILG Memorandum Circular 122, series of 2018.

Recommendations:
We recommended the GAD Focal Point Person override the monitoring and evaluation for the full implementation of the enlisted GAD activities for CY 2018 that were still unimplemented by the LGU.
Moreover, it was recommended that management see to it that for the ensuing years, full implementation of the GAD Plan and Budget be purposefully achieved.

Recommendations:
We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant submit immediately the remaining unsubmitted reports and other financial documents for calendar year 2018;
The Municipal Accountant be cautious of the deadlines of the preparation  and submission of the required financial reports, schedules and documents in order to avoid delays; and
Management consider hiring additional personnel for the Accounting Office and ensure proper division of workload amongst them and/or require accounting personnel to render overtime: Provided, however, that they will not exceed the PS Limitation.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC), and other concerned officials to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004, and regularly prepare and submit to the Office of the Audit Team Leader the Quarterly Report on Government PPAs.

We recommended that the BAC and TWG evaluate and validate thoroughly the documents submitted by the bidder to ensure its responsiveness to procurement rules and regulations before awarding the contract as set under the revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184
observations.managementAction: Bidding documents submitted by the bidder were already evaluated thoroughly by the BAC and TWD
observations.reason: The audit team conducted sampling examination on the submitted bidding documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the BAC agreed to perform their functions to ensure that the municipality abides by the standards set forth under R.A. 9184 and it shall prepare a Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) as a tool for LGU’s project monitoring.
observations.managementAction: The BAC  already performed their functions in accordance with the standards
observations.reason: Failed to prepare PMR as reiterated in Observation    No. 10, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management release the amount of retention fee only upon final acceptance by the LGU pursuant to the provision Sec. 6.2 Annex “E” of IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Management already implemented the recommendation
observations.reason: No retention fee disbursed without final acceptance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that for succeeding budget preparation, the Local School Board prepare the annual budget for SEF based on the provision of RA No. 7160 and DEPED/DBM/DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.
observations.managementAction: Management prepared annual budget in accordance with laws and standards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LSB refrain from charging SEF expenditures not in accordance with the provisions of RA 7160 and the DECS/DBM/DILG Joint Circulars. Likewise, assistance for athletes or DepEd is transferred directly to the funds of the school for proper liquidation and monitoring.
observations.managementAction: Management stopped disbursing expenses not in accordance with laws.
observations.reason: SEF expenditures were in accordance w/ law.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management terminate the contracts of defaulting contractors pursuant to Section III.A.2 of the revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and consider implementation of the same projects if determined still necessary in order that the LGU’s objectives on rural development will attained.
observations.managementAction: Management already terminated contracts of defaulting contractors

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Solid Waste Management Board prepare and formulate the yearly plans and programs to support the activities in the attainment of its targets and laid down in the Municipality’s 10 year SWMP and include in the yearly plans and programs the formal establishment and frame of reference of MRF in every barangay, segregation at source, segregated collection, and other activities that will ensure the protection of public health and environment.
observations.managementAction: Management included in their plan the establishment of MRF in every barangay.
observations.reason: Management already prepared yearly plans and programs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management, through the Local Planning Development Council, submit GAD Accomplishment Report and GAD Data base that were used as their basis in the attribution of GAD plan and budget not later than January 31 of the ensuing year to have basis in assessing if the LGU’s GAD budget is gender responsive and aligned with the National government’s commitment on ensuring gender responsive governance.
observations.reason: Management already submitted GAD accomplishment report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management pay wages of job order personnel based on their daily rates and the approved number of days as stated in their DTRs.
observations.reason: Job Order employees were already paid on a daily rate basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management refrain from paying incentive to PNP or other employees without a duly approved municipal ordinance or resolution.
observations.reason: Management already stopped giving incentives to PNP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC ensure compliance with the guidelines and documentary requirements of Negotiated Procurement as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001.
We recommended that management submit copies of contracts to the audit team within 5 days after perfection in accordance with pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted perfected contracts on procurement of infrastructure projects only.
observations.reason: Contracts for the procurement of goods were not submitted to the audit team

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly avoid piecemeal purchases and/or splitting of requisitions or purchase orders.
observations.reason: Splitting of requisitions was already stopped by management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the budget office obligate expenses only to their proper accounts and refrain from window dressing the Statement of Appropriation, Allotments and Obligations.

We also recommended that the Budget Officer present to the Local Chief Executive the real picture of the approved budget and obligations incurred; and recommend for the augmentation of other funds only when necessary.
observations.reason: There were already budget augmentations issued by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting Office and General Services Office maintain complete records of all it properties, enforce an annual physical count of properties and prepare and submit a report thereon in compliance with the provisions of the Manual on New Government Accounting System for LGUs, otherwise Section 122 of PD 1445 shall be imposed.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office will help the General Services Office to conduct physical count of all Property, Plant and Equipment and will prepare a RPCPPE and maintain PPELC for each category of property, plant and equipment. They will also provide inventory tags to all the property, plant and equipment counted by indicating information such as property number, description, date acquired, acquisition cost and the present status and condition of such properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management immediately submit for audit all subject DVs together with the related supporting documents, to preclude the issuance of Notice of Disallowance and/or Suspension, as the case may be.

We recommend that Management submit the identified lacking documents to support the public bidding conducted and strictly observe the documentary and procedural requirements of the Procurement Law to preclude the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Disallowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer open a separate bank account through an approval resolution by the Sangguniang Bayan for Special Education Fund to proper monitor the funds and for better determination of the balances for each fund. We also recommend that detailed subsidiary ledgers be maintained for each type of fund to properly and accurately record the transactions involved.

We recommended that Management assess the present situation of tobacco farming in the Municipality and identify the immediate needs of the farmers. After which, devise programs, projects and activities (PPAs) that could address the issues and concerns identified and include such PPAs in the Annual Investment Program with due consideration on the prescribed documentary and procedural requirement of the Department of Budget and Management.
observations.managementAction: The Management distributed some amount to the tobacco farmers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to maintain a separate financial statements/subsidiary ledgers for the special accounts in the General Fund Proper to ensure adequate information of the individual sub code accounts maintained under the General Fund Proper.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers to support the general ledger balances to ensure fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended the liquidating officer and all collectors maintain an individual cashbook to record their daily collections as prescribed by existing regulations. The use of the cashbook will facilitate monitoring of their accountabilities as of a given date.

We recommended that Management immediately submit for audit all subject DVs together with the related supporting documents, as the corresponding Notice of Disallowance for the amount involved will be issued for non-submission thereof.
observations.managementAction: Management partially submitted the subject DVs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management strictly follow the guidelines on the entitlement, release, use, reporting and audit of Confidential Fund (CF) and/or Intelligence Fund (IF) prescribed in COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

We recommended and management concurred to require the:

(a) concerned office or Inventory Committee, if any, to complete the physical count of the assets of the LGU and to render the corresponding report;



(b) Municipal Treasurer’s Office (MTO) and Accounting Office to update and regularly reconcile their property records; and 

(c) MTO to maintain and update records on accountability on PPEs.  



Moreover, we advise Management to:

(a) address the reported inadequacy of personnel to facilitate the physical count of and reporting on PPEs; and 




(b) require proper turnover of accountabilities in case of change of accountable officers.
observations.managementAction:
Physical count of inventories and PPE was conducted, however, final reports are still being reconciled from previous records.

Records for 2018 tran-sactions were now being kept.  Reconciliation of previous records are still being done.

Management informed the inventory committee that they could request assistance to various department during conduct of inventory.

Management complied.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor will instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:

(a) prepare a Certified List of Taxpayers that shall be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in the setting up of RPT and SET Receivables for CY 2018; and 

(b) prepare the list of delinquencies as at end of CY 2017 and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for the updating of individual tax payer’s account. 

Meanwhile, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor act on the reported inadequacy of personnel at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to facilitate distribution of notices with the end view of intensifying tax collections.
observations.managementAction:
Certified List of Taxpayers are being updated.


List of delinquencies are still being updated.


MTO were instructed to seek assistance if warranted.
observations.reason:
Records are still being validated.


List still needs to be validated.


Although there were additional personnel tasked to assist, due to the voluminous records to reconcile, it is still on-going as of to date.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed that the Members of the Local School Board will:

(a) ensure that future plans and budgets for SEF are consistent with DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 and other pertinent rules that may be issued thereafter; and 

(b) refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute.

We recommended and management concurred that they will desist paying more than the maximum allowable contribution of the employer in the monthly amount of P100.00 per employee as prescribed in the HDMF Circular No. 274.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management concurred that the Local Chief Executive instruct:

(a) the OIC- Human Resource Management Officer to clearly define in the contract of service the duties and responsibilities of Job Order Personnel to avoid confusion and duplication of duties; and 

(b) the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the supporting documents, such as accomplishment reports for services rendered, were attached to the disbursements vouchers to establish validity and propriety of payment.
observations.managementAction:
Contract of services drawn clearly define the duties and responsibili-ties to be carried out.

Payments are only made upon submission of the required supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to: 

(a) fill up the mandatory position for the three staff of the MDRRMO to handle the administration, training, research and planning, and operations and warning, to provide adequate support to DRRM activities; and 

(b) require the concerned MDRRM Officer Assistant to comply with the reporting guidelines on the preparation and regular submission to the COA Auditor of the Report on the Sources and Utilization of Funds prepared by the Municipal Accountant, for proper and timely evaluation.
observations.managementAction:
Management already filled up the mandatory position.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management concurred that the Local Chief Executive would: 

(a) provide the required training interventions for the GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee and Technical Working Group to aid them in the proper discharge of their functions; and 

(b) instruct said committee and group to review the GAD Plan and Budget to determine programs, projects, and activities that are responsive to gender gaps or issues faced by their women and men employees, as well as their clients and constituents.
observations.managementAction:
Concerned officials attended GAD Trainings during the year.


Thorough review of PPAs for GAD were done by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation:

(a) convene the LCPC; 


(b) direct the (i) LCPC to formulate their PPAs for the implementation of R.A. No. 9433 based on the Comprehensive Municipal Juvenile Intervention Program provided by the JJWC; and (ii) Budget Officer to appropriate through attribution 1% of its Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for these PPAs; and 

(c) designate a duly licensed social worker that shall recommend interventions and monitor children’s program and projects in the locality.
