Management review PPAs that could be directly and indirectly charged to LCPC.



Executive Order for designation of the DSWDO were already issued.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and management agreed to: 

(a) instruct the MSWMB to involve closely the Barangay officials in the strict implementation on the existing environmental laws; 

(b) intensify the information campaign on the “no segregation, no collection policy” among the residents; and 

(c) exercise strong political will to implement the laws on preserving and protecting the environment as mandated under Republic Act No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: Management held meetings with Barangay officials to urge them to have active roles in the implementa-tion of the Solid Waste Management Plan.
observations.reason:
Segregation at source were still not done on all barangays.


Strict implementation of the existing environmental laws and penalties for non-observance were not done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to be approved by the Local Chief Executive before the same is submitted to the Government Procurement and Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: BAC was able to prepare the PMR, however, it was not submitted online to GPPB for validation.
observations.reason: Reports should be submitted online to the GPPB as required in RA9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed to continue the intensified tax collection in their collection process in Tax Revenue in order to have an additional fund that will aid them in providing greater service to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: Management conducted tax campaign during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management concurred to continue in complying with BIR regulations by remitting all taxes withheld to the BIR.
observations.managementAction: Taxes were withheld and remitted within the required period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management concurred to continue adhering to the requirements of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997.
observations.managementAction: Management complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:  Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects as basis in the preparation and maintenance of property and ledger cards and proper charging of depreciation for depreciable components of the road networks system;  Require the Municipal Treasurer to start the preparation and maintenance of Local Road Network Property Cards upon receipt of the required data from the Municipal Engineer as required under COA Circular 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015; and  Direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose information in accordance with the prescribed format of the subject COA Circular.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer was able to provide the Municipal Accountant with the list of road components. The Municipal Treasurer started the preparation and maintenance of Local Road Network Property Cards.
observations.reason: Information pertaining to the Local Road Network System of the Municipality were not disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to undertake the initial steps required to facilitate the transfer and titling of the aforementioned parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to protect its interest from third party claims and illegal dispositions.
observations.managementAction: The management had already undertaken initial actions for the transfer and titling of the six parcels of land in the name of the Municipality.
observations.reason: The aforementioned parcels of land were still not covered with titles in the name of the Municipality as of year-end. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 7, page 32.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method in accounting for inventory items, in compliance with Section 114 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Perpetual method in accounting for inventory items was adopted by the management in accounting for inventory items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to carefully check the entries made in the Subsidiary Ledgers to determine the causes of the discrepancy, make the necessary correcting entries to bring both accounts in agreement and to provide necessary disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Correcting entries were drawn by the Municipal Accountant to address the discrepancy.
observations.reason: The discrepancy was addressed.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to restore and retain the account Due from NGAs totaling P43,713.90 in the books of accounts of the Municipality, until the authority to write off the dormant account is granted by the Commission on Audit. Thereafter, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor file a request for the authority to write-off the dormant account, Due from NGAs, in the total amount of P43,713.90 in the books of accounts of the Municipality, in accordance with the guidelines and procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The accounts were restored in the books of accounts of the Municipality.
observations.reason: The Management is still obtaining the necessary documents to support the claim for write-off of the dormant accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to release the Barangay’s share from RPT and CTC within five days after the end of each quarter as mandated under Section 271 (d) of LGC and Section 15 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Shares of Barangays from RPT and CTC within five days after the end of each quarter was released.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:  Bids and Award Committee (BAC) to require the submission of documents already prescribed in Appendix A of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 before the issuance of Notice of Award (NOA) by Head of the Procuring Entity;  Municipal Accountant to facilitate the immediate submission of the lacking documents listed in Annex A to support the validity and propriety of the transactions;  Municipal Accountant to ensure that the expenses payments for telephone expenses are attached with the corresponding statement of accounts or bills as required by COA Circular No. 2012-001;  Municipal Accountant to ensure that payments for Representation Expenses are attached with sufficient documents that will prove that these expenses were incurred for official meetings/seminars/conventions such as, but not limited to, pictures, minutes of the meetings, attendance and certification from the officials concerned; and  Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to immediately submit the documents needed to support the disbursements for four Infrastructure Projects.
observations.managementAction: Documents were submitted. Telephone expenses were supported with statement of accounts. Representation Expenses were supported with sufficient documents. The Municipal Engineer submitted the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit immediately trial balances and financial statements to the Office of the Auditor, in compliance with Section 70 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Trial balances and financial statements were prepared and submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local GAD Focal Point Team (LGFPT) and the GAD Focal Point Coordinator to establish a centralized database to have a source of reliable information necessary for its GAD planning, programming and policy formulations. Meanwhile, utilize the existing database, planning and data-gathering tools and management information system to generate gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information from primary and secondary sources, such as surveys and studies. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Chairperson of the GAD Focal Point System, Technical Working Group to maintain the required GAD database, in compliance with Section 37.D of Rule VI of the IRR of RA No. 9710 to ensure that GAD programs/activities/projects are addressing the gender-related issues in the locality.
observations.managementAction: The management was able to start the establishment of GAD database.
observations.reason: Centralized GAD Database was not maintained. GAD database was maintained by each office. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 10, page 35.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:  Municipal Accountant to provide sufficient disclosure of the LDDRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements; and  Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office to submit the Report on the Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund.
observations.managementAction: The utilization report for CY 2018 was submitted by the Management. The Management committed to provide the required disclosures.
observations.reason: Required disclosures were still not provided in the Notes to Financial Statements. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 11, page 38.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor: a. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Social Welfare Officer to properly appropriate and utilize the one per cent of the IRA for the LCPC, respectively, in compliance with RA No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120. b. Advise the LCPC to prepare and submit immediately the following reports to the Office of the Auditor:  Council’s Annual Work and Financial Plan;  Local Development Plan for Children;  Local Investment Plan;  Local Code for Children;  Local State of Children’s Report;  Local Investment Plan for Children; and,  Accomplishment Reports
observations.managementAction: The Management was able to appropriate and utilize the one per cent allotment for the LCPC. The required reports were submitted by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor: a. Extend the full implementation of the proper segregation of solid wastes from the household level (segregation at source) up to the sanitary landfill; b. Manage efficiently the residual wastes as well as the sanitary landfill; c. The SWMB of the Municipality to be formed or amended to comply with the requirements provided under Section 12 of RA No. 9003; d. Encourage and support the establishment of Multi-Purpose Environment Cooperatives or Associations that will help promote the implementation of RA No. 9003; and e. Coordinate MSWMB and BSWMB to ensure that all barangays are provided with MRFs that are properly maintained and fully operated.
observations.managementAction: The SWMB of the Municipality was already present and amended to comply with the requirements of RA No. 9003. Multi-Purpose Environment Cooperative and/or association were already present in the Municipality. The MSWMB was able to manage solid waste operations.
observations.reason: Only ten Barangays were provided with MRFs, thus waste segregation at source was not fully implemented. Not all barangays have MRFs. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 14, page no. 41.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the         (a) Inventory Committee conduct physical count of all the properties of the Municipality to validate the existence thereof and prepare the RPCPPE, copy furnish the Municipal Accountant to facilitate reconciliation with accounting records; and             (b) General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant maintain complete property cards and PPELC, respectively, as required by the Manual on NGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee conducted physical count and submitted the required RPCPPEs.
observations.reason:
The Inventory Committee conducted partial physical count on selected properties of the Municipality excluding properties per Unit/Department.

The Designate GSO and the Municipal Accountant still failed to maintain an updated and complete property records. Thus, reconciliation between the two records could not be facilitated.

The observation was reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the         (a) Municipal Mayor (i) cause the immediate settlement of cash advances to avoid accumulation thereof; and (ii) direct the Municipal Treasurer to suspend the payment of salaries of concerned AOs to enforce liquidation of their cash advances, in case of unjustified failure to settle their accountabilities; and (b) Municipal Accountant strictly monitor the grant and liquidation of cash advances to ensure that no additional cash advance is granted to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made.
observations.managementAction: Several employees were able to fully settle/liquidate their outstanding cash advances in CY 2018 thus, decreasing the balance of the Due from Officers and Employees account.
observations.reason: The outstanding cash advances decreased by P26,043.25 or a meager 15.80% only. Cash advances for travel amounting to P138,773.17 which has been outstanding since CY 2017 still remained unliquidated as of December 31, 2018 due to the continued disregard of concerned Municipal employees with the recommendations thus, reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Accountant, require the concerned barangays to        (a) submit the lacking supporting documents on the transfer of funds; and (b) liquidate the fund transfer by submitting the accomplishment reports and other related supporting documents to ensure that the funds were used for its intended purpose. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2012-001 and 2004-008 on the documentation and recording of fund transfers.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has already provided and submitted to the Audit Team the necessary liquidation documents for the fund transfers to three (3) barangays.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant failed to submit the necessary liquidation documents for the fund transfer to one (1) barangay.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the:        (a) Municipal Accountant record procurement of medical supplies, drugs and medicines for operations as inventories and to credit the same account for issuance to end-users, transfers or other disposal as provided for in COA Circular No. 2015-009; and (b) Requisitioning officer/s certify the conformity of drugs and medicines with the essential drug list as contained in the Philippine National Drug Formulary to ensure that only those drug and medicines with approved therapeutic efficiency are procured and delivered to the general public.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Health Officer, as the requisitioning officer, has already provided certifications that the acquired drugs and medicines conform to the standards of Philippine National Drug Formulary.

Likewise, the Municipal Accountant recorded the purchases of drugs and medicines under the Inventories account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GSO to prepare and submit the Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption and Official Travels and ensure that the driver’s trip tickets are serially numbered and properly accomplished for proper control and monitoring of fuel consumption and use of government motor vehicles.
observations.managementAction: Driver’s trip tickets were prepared and accomplished, accompanied with travel orders for the respective drivers.
observations.reason: Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption and Official Travels were still not prepared since fuel consumption reports were only prepared for every purchase of fuel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Mayor (a) through an executive order, establish the LCPC that will coordinate with and assist the Municipality for the adoption of a comprehensive plan on delinquency prevention and oversee its proper implementation; and (b) allocate at least 1% of the IRA for child protection program as required under Section 15 of RA No. 9344. We also recommended for the strict observance of the rules and regulations with regard to the allocation of budget and utilization of the 1% LCPC fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has already created/ established the Local Council for the Protection of Children. Also, the one percent (1%) of the IRA was already allocated for LCPC programs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the         (a) Municipal Mayor seek authority from the DILG Secretary if the Municipality intends to purchase motor vehicles of any kind and regardless of funding source in compliance with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-155 dated December 17, 2014;       (b) BAC strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR as regards procurement of goods such as motor vehicles; and (c) General Services Officer      (i) require the supplier to issue sales invoice and delivery receipt upon delivery of the goods for proper documentation; and       (ii) prepare and issue the Property Acknowledgement Receipt to the end-user for proper monitoring of  accountability over the service vehicle.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality did not acquire any service vehicle for CY 2018 thus, implementation with regards to seeking authority from the DILG could not be validated. However, in similar transactions, Sales Invoices and Delivery Receipts from the suppliers were already attached to the claims for payment. Likewise, PAR were prepared and issued to the end-user.
observations.reason: Procurement for similar transaction such as, procurement of heavy equipment was not made strictly in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 due to the continued disregard of the concerned employees with the recommendations, thereby reiterated in the Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the         (a) Local School Board strictly comply with the provisions of Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 in the prioritization of programs, projects and activities that should be included in the LSB Budget to ensure  utilization of the SEF only for the specific purposes prescribed by law; (b) Municipal Mayor, through close coordination with the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer, observe the general principles of imprest system in handling cash by seeing that transactions are paid in check except those expenditures that may be paid out of cash advance as provided for under COA Circular No. 97-002;                  (c) Disbursing Officer refrain from transferring cash advance to other accountable officer let alone employees of other national government agencies in compliance with Section 4.1.6 of  COA Circular No. 97-002;        (d) Municipal Treasurer ensure that checks are drawn only on duly approved disbursement voucher and payrolls pursuant to Section 4(5) of PD No. 1445; and (e) Municipal Accountant          (i) review the completeness of supporting documents before processing claims for payment; and (ii) send letters to the concerned accountable officers to submit the liquidation reports for the amount of P120,000.00 extended as financial assistance.
observations.managementAction: Expenditures are within the mandate of the SEF program and most of the  disbursements were supported with complete documentations.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant failed to follow-up from the concerned accountable officers the submission of required liquidation documents for the financial assistance amounting to P120,000.00. As such, liquidation reports were still not submitted to the Audit Team thus, could not be verified

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the:       (a) Managements, through the LDRRM Officer, cause the optimum utilization of the LDRRMF to ensure that basic rescue and response equipment are procured in order to enhance the disaster resilience of communities and to institutionalize arrangements and measures for reducing disaster risks; (b) Municipal Accountant (i) prepare and maintain separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations for LDRRMF for QRF, MOOE and Capital Outlay to facilitate monitoring of its appropriations, expenditures and balances to provide reliable and real - time information for decision making purposes to improve the administration and utilization of the LDRRMF; and (ii) comply with Section 5.1.16 of COA Circular 2012-002 by providing disclosure on the amount and details of the unexpended LDRRMF balance in the Notes to Financial Statements; and          (c) LDRRMO prepare and submit, through the LDRRMC and the LDC, the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF and other disaster risk reduction and management resources to be certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, to the COA Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, copy furnish the OCD.
observations.managementAction:
The Management was able to increase the financial delivery rates from the previous 26.98% to 57.88% in CY 2018.

Moreover, the Management was able to implement 19 out of the 23 target PPAs for CY 2018.
observations.reason: LDRRM Funds, particularly that of the 70% Mitigation Fund were still not optimally utilized for CY 2018, several projects were still not implemented and expenses totaling P237,761.00 which do not contribute to the level of preparedness of the Municipality in emergency situations were still charged against the funds due to the disregard of the concerned employees with the recommendations thus, reiterated in the Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, with the help of the Municipal Accountant, exert efforts to enforce the settlement of suspensions and disallowances by sending letters to the concerned persons responsible/liable requiring them to settle their accountabilities within the prescribed period provided under the RRSA.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted to the Audit Team supporting documents for the settlement of several audit suspensions.
observations.reason: The assigned personnel is still in the process of collating, retrieving and reconciling documents and reports regarding the unsettled audit suspensions and disallowances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: Direct the Inventory Committee to prioritize the completion of the physical inventory of all the LGU’s assets, prepare RPCPPE for each type of property, plant and equipment, reconcile the results thereof with the records of the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, and submit to COA for review and verification; and Immediately investigate, clear and reconcile all discrepancies between physical count and accounting and property records. If necessary, written explanations shall be required from accountable and responsible persons.
observations.reason:
The LGU did efforts in the conduct of inventory, however, the same was unfinished and the RPCPPE thereof was not made.
Reiterated in Part II – Detailed Observations and Recommendations portion of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management through the following heads of offices of the LGU furnish COA with the following documents as stated in the aforementioned COA Circulars: Municipal Accountant – Registries of Public Infrastructures of ₱27,102,246.46, and ₱349,000.00 classified by category of property, plant and equipment and recognized 50% of the applicable requirement; Municipal Treasurer – Local Road Inventory and Road Map and present a complete Local Network Property Ledger Card for all roads and its components as prescribed in Annex E of COA Circular 2015-008; Municipal Engineer – Report on the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects which was submitted to the Municipal Accountant as basis of recording Road Networks in the books of accounts at year-end; and Inventory Committee – Report on the Physical Count of the Public Infrastructures of the LGU including Road Network System.
observations.reason:
The LGU has recognized 100% of its Public Infrastructures to the PPE accounts; however, proper documentation as basis for the recognition is not provided.
Unfinished inventory-taking which resulted no Report of Physical Count.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: Require the AO to liquidate her outstanding cash advance immediately as soon as the purpose for which it was given has been served and refrain from granting another cash advance before liquidating first the previous one. Pay salaries/wages of its officers and employees and job order personnel thru payroll system for every office or department to prevent additional expenses of the LGU and lessen workload of the employees concerned.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer provide an explanation as to whether projects was not completed on its target completion date together with the variation orders and the contractors notices. We recommended that the LGU must direct all concerned to see to it that all transactions like procurements and/or projects must not be paid by the local government if not supported with complete documentations in order to avoid suspension of the same.

We recommended that management refrain from utilizing downloaded funds in the Trust Fund account of the LGU outside of the scope or purpose for which these funds were created. The Municipal Accountant should establish a control and monitoring system in the management of downloaded funds in the Trust Fund account of the LGU and ensure that counterpart funds in the General Fund are transferred to Trust Fund prior to implementation of projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, being the immediate and primary responsible for all financial and operational transactions, ensures that laws, rules and regulations shall be strictly complied. We further recommended that the members of the Sangguniang Bayan strictly comply with the process/procedures under DILG Memorandum Circular 2013-70 and COA Circular 2007-001 especially on accreditation of CSOs.

We recommended that the municipality return/refund the unutilized balances of the fund transfers to the source National Government Agencies to fully liquidate the same, thus, reduce the liability of the LGU.

We recommended the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain separate books of accounts as well as the financial statements/reports for each of the economic enterprises created and maintained by the LGU as prescribed in Section 110 of the NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I. The financial reports will provide vital and sufficient information to the end-users as regards the assets, liabilities and equity of each of the economic enterprises and to draw decisions therefrom. Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer maintain cashbooks for each economic enterprise of the municipality where to record the receipts and disbursements of the entities. It was further recommended that the LGU adhere to the foregoing provisions in the creation and maintenance of special accounts in the general funds as well as the proper recording of the transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: Direct the Municipal Treasurer to duly execute all contracts of lease for all existing occupants and submit to COA copies within thirty (30) days from receipt hereof; Designate a market administrator from among the personnel in the Office of the Municipal Treasurer with the primary responsibility of making sure that all laws, rules and regulations pertaining to the operation, maintenance and upkeep of the municipal market be strictly followed so as to prevent further problems or violations to be incurred; and Direct all LGU officials and employees concerned to strictly impose all provisions of its municipal revenue code.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and other concerned offices to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 on publicity of projects/programs/activities as amended under COA Circular No. 2016-003 and disseminate the same to all department heads or concerned offices. We also recommended that a focal person must be designated to review and consolidate the reports submitted by each department before submission to COA in hard and soft copies.
observations.reason:
The Consolidated Report on Government PPAs for Quarter Ending March 31, 2018 was submitted on June 21, 2018; however, succeeding quarters have not been provided with the report.
Reiterated in Part II – Detailed Observations and Recommendations portion of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management immediately submit the remaining financial reports for CY 2017.
observations.reason:
Delays have been incurred in the preparation and submission of financial reports.
Reiterated in Part II – Detailed Observations and Recommendations and Recommendations portion of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) adhere to the provisions of RA 7160 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017, dated January 19, 2017 in the preparation of SEF Annual Budget. Any issues or conflict arising from its implementation and cases not covered by the provision thereof shall be submitted to the DepEd for resolution and in consultation with the LGU. We also recommended that the LSB stop charging/budgeting of allowances, office equipment and purchase of various school supplies not in accordance with the above cited regulations and instead utilize the funds for the needs of other schools. Henceforth, incurrence of similar expenditures in the future may be suspended and/or disallowed in audit.

We recommended that in the succeeding fiscal years, the municipality shall include sufficient appropriation in the Annual Budget for the implementation of the programs of the Local Council for the Protection of Children as required under RA 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

We recommended that: The Accounting Department, in collaboration with the Treasury Department, perform immediate identification and thorough analysis of the recorded trust liabilities and reconcile with the available cash balances in the Trust Fund books; and The Municipal Accountant should also perform periodic reconciliation of the trust receipts/transfers coming into the possession of the local treasurer with due regard on the proper utilization and charges against the trust fund for control and monitoring purposes.

We recommended that management: Require the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the reconciliation of the difference between the Due to and Due from Other Funds accounts and take-up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to bring the two accounts in agreement; Require the Municipal Accountant to direct the concerned personnel in charge of the different funds to simultaneously prepare Journal Entry Vouchers when Due To and Due from Other Funds accounts are involved so that errors can be immediately detected and corrected; and Perform regular review and evaluation of transactions to detect errors and omissions.

We recommended that the projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended balances of LDRRMF of previous years be included in the LDRRMFIP under a separate caption for comprehensive formulation and implementation of disaster risk reduction and management activities. We recommended that the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF be prepared and submitted within the required period for the timely evaluation of the implementation and monitoring of the disaster risk reduction management projects, programs and activities of the LGU. We recommended that the members of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (MDRRMC) ensure that the programs, projects and activities to be included in the LDRRMFIP shall cover the priority projects and activities that are included or related to the DRRM activities as declared under Section 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-002. Be guided with the provisions of Section 5.0 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.reason:
Only Reports for the Months of January, February, March and April were prepared and submitted. The reports for the succeeding months has yet received by the auditors.
Reiterated in Part II – Detailed Observations and Recommendations portion of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the members of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (MDRRMC) ensure that the programs, projects and activities to be included in the LDRRMFIP shall cover the priority projects and activities that are included or related to the DRRM activities as declared under Section 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-002. Be guided with the provisions of Section 5.0 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to look into the -requirements necessary for the approval of the Municipality’s Solid Waste Management Plan as basis for the implementation of their solid waste activities.
observations.reason: ESWM remains unapproved as of December 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the: Municipal Accountant to check and verify the completeness of supporting documents attached to the vouchers; and Personnel officer conduct a thorough review and inventory of all job orders and provide specific duties and responsibilities for all hires based on the staffing requirements of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Property Officer to facilitate the submission of the updated list of inventory of insurable properties with the GSIS so that the necessary insurance coverage for all the assets can be applied with the GSIS General Insurance Fund.
observations.reason: Physical inventory of the insurable properties is still on-going and the report thereof, as basis for the insurable amount, has yet submitted by the property officer and/or inventory committee.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee prepare and submit the Procurement Monitoring Report for the 1st Semester of CY 2017 to GPPB website on July 14, 2017; submit the CY 2018 Annual Procurement Plan-Common-Used Supplies and Equipment to DBM on November 15, 2017 and prepare the APCPI for CY 2016 during the 1st Quarter of CY 2017. The same shall be done on each ensuing semesters and years thereafter.
observations.reason:
No submission and postings of PMR to the GPPB Website as of December 31, 2018.
On-going compliance of the required reports as mentioned in the audit finding.

observations.recommendation: Prioritize the issue and exert effort to take necessary steps to acquire Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) as proof of ownership and furnish the Auditor copies of the TCTs. Appropriate funds for the titling of the lot to avoid future conflict and claims of ownership by third party.
observations.reason:
Several parcels of land owned by the LGU already have copies of TCTs. However, some deficiencies were noted on the correctness of the valuation of the Land account in the books of the municipality.
A separate audit observation is embodied in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that concerned municipal officials/employees submit Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis as required by COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Management assured the audit team that starting this year, the required report be submitted as prescribed in the COA Circular No. 2012-002.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly avoid piecemeal purchases and/or splitting of requisitions or purchase orders.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, thru the Municipal Planning Development Officer (MPDO), strategize their planning activities to ensure timely implementation of programs, projects and activities.

We recommended that Management observe strict implementation of the municipality’s local disaster program aimed at addressing the community disaster preparedness and understanding of disaster contingency planning at all levels, thus, enhancing the capacities of the municipality and its constituents to effectively understand, prepare, respond and recover from the impacts of natural and man-made disasters.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented prior years programs and activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the GAD focal person to submit promptly the GAD Accomplishment Report within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding audit year as stated in Section V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated May 18, 2014.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Accomplishment report was not submitted within set deadline
