observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year recommendation that the Local Chief Executive:

 require the conduct of a complete physical count of its properties, prepare the report thereon and reconcile its results with the records maintained at the Accounting Office; and 
 
monitor its progress, by setting a timetable within which to finish the count.
observations.managementAction:
Management would conduct an actual physical inventory of its assets.




Management would set up a plan to comply with the physical count of the assets.
observations.reason:
Management was not able to conduct a complete physical inventory count of their assets.


Management failed to render a report.

observations.managementAction:
Management will minimize the payroll costs of SWM.


Management should prioritize the PPAs charged to 20% DF.
observations.reason: Management failed to significantly minimize the labor payroll charged to 20% DF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management stop the practice of paying expenditures which are not included in the priority expenses chargeable against SEF as provided under Section 1 of R.A. 5447, Section 272 of R.A. 7160 and Deped-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01, s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998, No. 01-A, March 14, 2000, No. 01-B, dated June 25, 2001.
observations.managementAction: Management will prioritize the expenses charged to SEF.
observations.reason: Management continued to grant requests on expenses not allowed to be charged to SEF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality allocate at least 1% of their annual IRA for child protection program as required under RA No. 9344 and to create their LCPC and formulate and implement a comprehensive plan specifically to strengthen, develop and protect children in the Municipality.
observations.reason: Oversight of Management to provide 1% of IRA for Child Protection Program in the 2017 annual budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Solid Waste Management Board develop a comprehensive Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan (ESWMP) that will ensure the long-term management of solid waste, as well as integrate the various solid waste management programs and strategies of its barangays through consultations with the various sectors of the community. Ensure the viability and effective implementation of solid waste management programs embodied in the ESWMP and monitor its implementation.
observations.reason:
The SWMB is still on the process  of  collecting  and
processing data for the preparation of ESWMP to ensure its successful implementation and that the effect will directly benefit the environment and the people of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to keep and maintain a complete subsidiary ledger of all accounts and facilitate a monthly reconciliation of the Subsidiary Ledgers against the General Ledger controlling account.
We also recommended the Municipal Accountant to record the First Consolidated Bank (FCB) account in the Cash in Bank - Local Currency, Current Account.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Accountant and other officials concerned to make representations with the bank to furnish the Municipality with copies of debit and credit memos to facilitate recording of reconciling items in the book of accounts for a fair presentation of the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements.
We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to submit the documents supporting the transactions to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to immediately transmit the identified unrecorded disbursements to the Municipal Accountant to facilitate recording thereof in the book of accounts.
We recommended Ms. Drilon and Ms. Bungalso to submit the respective Disbursement Vouchers of their cash advance together with the liquidation documents to the Municipal Accountant within thirty (30) days from receipt hereof, otherwise the Municipal Mayor is hereby instructed to issue an order for the suspension of their salaries in accordance with the mentioned regulations and Section 5.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
Henceforth, strict compliance by the Municipal Treasurer with regards to the transmittal of the check disbursements and its supporting documents within the prescribed period to the Municipal Accountant must be enjoined and strictly monitored. The Municipal Treasurer must impose administrative disciplinary action against the Disbursing Officer/s who fails to submit a complete report of disbursement including its supporting documents.
We also recommended the Management to improve the agency’s record keeping and tracking system to ensure that every bank transaction and all other financial information are properly documented and duly recorded in the books.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer and Special Disbursing Officer to submit all the mentioned RDs together with the supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant for recording.
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to forward the same to the Audit Team for post audit, otherwise the transaction will be suspended/disallowed in audit.
In case of failure to render his/her accounts within thirty (30) days from receipt hereof, we recommended the Municipal Mayor to suspend the salaries of the concerned accountable officers and file administrative disciplinary action in accordance with the mentioned regulations.

We recommended the Management to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and strictly adopt the “no liquidation, no cash advance” policy.
We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to temporary relieve the Municipal Accountant and the concern Disbursing Officer/s of their regular duties, for 15 days upon receipt hereof, to give them ample time to comply with the below-mentioned requirements in relation to Section 104 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to: a) Revised the Report of Unliquidated Cash Advances taking into consideration the needed information presented above especially on the breakdown of cash advances made by the Disbursing and Special Disbursing Officers, and submit the same to the Audit Team for evaluation; b) Submit the supporting documents of the unsupported adjustments made to the Advances to Special Disbursing Officer and Advances for Operating Expenses Accounts amounting to ₱4,187,708.11 and ₱12,756,970.00, respectively; c) Reclassify the cash advances made by the Special Disbursing Officers from Advances to Officers and Employees account to Advances to Special Disbursing Officer; d) Include in the checklist of documents the noted deficiencies and implement the same in the processing of cash advances and liquidations; e) Process only the travels of JO and COS with justifiable reasons and indorsement from their Department Head in compliance with DILG MC No. 2004-130, otherwise the same will be disallowed in audit; f) Discontinue the practice of partial liquidation of cash advances and direct the concern accountable officers to settle their accounts immediately especially for the Special Disbursing Officers; g) Submit a copy of the Accountant’s Advice, duly received by the Municipality’s depository bank, to the Municipal Auditor, and refrain from issuing Accountant’s Advice containing the cash advance of the accountable officer unless the previous cash advance DV was forwarded by the Municipal Treasurer to the Accounting for recording; h) Prospectively, return to the DO all LR/RDs that are not included in the cash advance list. Likewise, return to the accountable officer all liquidations with photocopied Obligation Request; and i) Claims of travelling expenses should be within the allowable days of the Travel Order; any additional days or extension of travel shall be for the personal expense of the employee.
We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to: a) Transmit daily to the Municipal Accountant the Report of Checks Issued together with all supporting documents; b) Direct the previous and current Disbursing Officer/s to settle their accountabilities within the prescribed period provided by COA Circular No. 97-002; c) Segregate the cash advances of the DO per type of expenses – Personal Services, MOOE, Special Activities, payroll of job order or contract of service personnel and other petty operating expenses; d) Provide and attach a list of expenses to be paid to the cash advance DV; e) Attach an Obligation Requests duly certified by the Municipal Budget Officer to the cash advance DV while the original copy will be attached to the liquidation documents; f) Liquidation documents should conform to the cash advance list; and g) Forward the softcopies of the RD and RCI to the Municipal Accountant.
We recommended the Municipal Budget Officer to: a) Specifically indicate in the Obligation Request the type of activity, expense to be paid and other necessary details such as travel dates and place to ensure that the payment will be made for the intended purpose; and b) Issue a certified copy of the Obligation Request in relation to our recommendation to the Municipal Treasurer as attachment to the cash advance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is in the process of determining causes of negative balances; some adjustments already made in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to serve a written notice to the concerned accountable officers on their outstanding cash advances as mandated by Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2012-004. Settlement of the accountable officer’s outstanding account is hereby enjoined within 15 days upon receipt hereof.
We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to suspend the salaries of the concerned accountable officers upon failure to settle their obligations.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following: a. Maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund by speeding up the implementation of the prioritized programs, projects and activities funded out of the 20% DF as per Annual Investment Program and that closer coordination among the officials involved in planning, execution and monitoring be undertaken to achieve the desired level of outcome; b. Instruct the Municipal Planning Development Officer to immediately look into the unimplemented developmental projects as embodied in the Annual Investment Program. Identify causes of non-implementation and develop feasible and specific solutions to effectively implement developmental projects; c. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to be very prudent, meticulous, conscientious and precise in evaluating the timely and perfect implementation of each and every project. Likewise, he should also conduct regular inspection of the projects so that accomplishment of the contractor is monitored and evaluated based on the required accomplishment against the number of days stipulated in the contract; d. Should there have been delays incurred in the project implementation, Liquidated Damages must be imposed from the contractors concerned. Such amount shall be computed and deducted from any money due or which may become due the contractor under each contract and/or collect such liquidated damages from the retention money or other securities posted by the contractor, whichever is convenient to the Municipality, in accordance with Item 8.3, Annex “E” of the Revised I.R.R. of R.A. 9184; e. Reappropriate the unexpended balance of the completed projects of the 20% DF to finance development PPAs identified to be funded under the 20% DF for the ensuing year.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to immediately forward the mentioned documents to the auditor for post audit. The Municipal Accountant should ensure that transactions recorded in the books are properly documented.

We recommended the HRMO to submit the required documentary requirements and deduct the 5 days annual forced/mandatory leave for the second claim, and direct the former Mayor to refund the amount received both for the first claim.
We recommended the Municipal Budget Officer to submit an explanation on the funding source of the claims.
observations.reason: With Notice of Disallowance Nos. 17-138-100-(16) and 17-139-100-(16), both dated October 24, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Management strictly adhere to the Procurement Law; b. Municipal Accountant return all liquidations of the Disbursing Officer that is not covered by an original Obligation Request/s and those that is not included in the original list of expense/s to be paid out of the cash advance; c. Municipal Budget Officer indicate the nature of transaction in the Obligation Request to determine the appropriateness of the claim; d. Municipal Treasurer make the necessary claims payable through checks in accordance with Section 93 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.reason: For Disallowance

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to mandate the Municipal Engineer to immediately submit a complete list of all on-going PPAs for year 2016 and the ensuing year, containing all necessary information relative thereto, otherwise, he should be subject to disciplinary action in accordance with Section 4.0 of the same Circular.
We recommended the management to impose liquidated damages to the defaulted Contractors in compliance with Section 68 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to create an Inventory Committee and enjoin the concerned department heads’ compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 and submit the corresponding reports to the Audit Team for evaluation and verification.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary entry to remove the inventoriable items from the PPE. Further, we recommended the Municipal GSO to issue an Inventory Custodian Slip to the Accountable Officer, who is the actual custodian of these items. The Municipal GSO should make separate database/lists of these items for monitoring purposes.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the cashbook of all bank accounts of the Municipality against the Subsidiary Ledgers of the Municipal Accountant. We also recommended the Municipal Treasurer to require all Accountable Officers to reconcile their respective records with the subsidiary ledgers of the Accounting Office.
The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant should appoint one focal person per office, who will be tasked to monitor and do the reconciliation of the two records. Both the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant must identify the discrepancies between the cashbook and the general ledger so that necessary adjustments can be effected soonest to bring the two records in agreement.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the concerned payees at least one month before a check becomes stale. Moreover, we required the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books for the long outstanding/stale checks in accordance with Section 59, Volume I of the NGAS Manual.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method in accounting for inventory items, in compliance with Section 114 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: No actions were taken by the Management. Reiterated in Observation No.4 under Part I of this report.
observations.reason: Perpetual Inventory Method was still not adopted by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the JEV to record and account for all the property and equipment acquired out of Trust Fund, and to furnish the Municipal Treasurer with the subject JEV in order to include those properties in the property records of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: No actions were taken by the Management. Reiterated in Observation No. 5 under Part II of this report.
observations.reason: Projects from BUB and various NGAs were still not recorded and accounted for in the books of accounts of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain complete Local Road Network Property Cards; require the Municipal Accountant to record the beginning balance of the local road network in the General Ledger and its component in the Local Road Network Ledger Card and to disclose the required information on Local Road Network System in the Notes to Financial Statements; and require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: No actions were taken by the Management. Reiterated in Observation No. 2 under Part II of this report.
observations.reason: The beginning balance of the local road network in the general ledgers was still not recorded. Moreover, the total Local Road Network System was still not disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements. Complete description and cost segregation for road projects were still not provided to the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately provide and maintain complete Subsidiary Ledgers for the special accounts in compliance with Section 313 of RA No. 7160 and Section 110 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, and submit to the Audit Team the financial statements and trial balances with the necessary supporting schedules of each special account.
observations.managementAction: No actions were taken by the Management. Reiterated in Observation No. 8 under Part II of this report.
observations.reason: Complete subsidiary ledgers for the Special accounts were still not provided and maintained by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
5. We recommended that the BAC and TWG evaluate and  validate  thoroughly
the documents submitted by the bidder to ensure its responsiveness  to
procurement rules and regulations before awarding the contract as  set
under the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

7. We recommended and the BAC agreed to perform their functions to ensure
that the municipality abide by the standards set forth under R.A. 9184
and it shall prepare a Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) as  a  tool
for LGU’s project monitoring.

6. We recommended that management release the amount  of  retention  fees
only upon final acceptance by the LGU pursuant to the  provision  Sec.
6.2 Annex “E” of IRR of RA 9184.

7.  We  recommended  that  the  LSB  refrain  from  charging   from   SEF
expenditures not in accordance with the provisions of RA 7160 and  the
DBM/DepEd/DILG Joint Circulars.  Likewise, assistance for athletes  or
DepEd be transferred directly to the funds of the  school  for  proper
liquidation and monitoring.

8. We recommended that the  Accounting  Office  and  General  Services
        Office maintain complete records of all it properties,  enforce  an
        annual physical count of properties and prepare and submit a report
        thereon in compliance with the provisions  of  the  Manual  on  New
        Government Accounting System for LGUs, otherwise Section 122 of  PD
        1445 shall be imposed.

7. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately  reconcile
        the Cash in Bank accounts appearing in the  books  of  accounts  at
        year-end  against  the  balances  per  bank  statements  and  those
        confirmed  by  the  depository  banks  and   make   the   necessary
        adjustments to present the correct balance of Cash in Bank  account
        at any given date.

5. We recommended  that  the  officials  and  employees  concerned  be
        required to immediately liquidate  their  cash  advances.  We  also
        recommend strict adherence  to  the  aforementioned  provisions  of
        Section 89 of PD 1445 and the specific provisions of  COA  Circular
        No. 97-002 to ensure proper granting, utilization of cash advances.

8. We recommended that  the  Municipal  Accountant  -  Designate  give
        utmost priority in analyzing and  determining  the  composition  of
        these long outstanding  Receivables.  Otherwise,  comply  with  the
        provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 in requesting for the write-
        off of the long outstanding receivables for which the LGU could not
        support its validity and propriety and could not establish rightful
        claims.

9. We recommended that Management immediately  submit  for  audit  all
        subject  DVs  together  with  the  related   supporting   documents
        otherwise the issuance of Notice of Disallowance and/or  Suspension
        is necessary, as the case may be.

10. We  recommended  that  Management  submit  the  identified  lacking
        documents to support the  public  bidding  conducted  and  strictly
        observe  the  documentary  and  procedural  requirements   of   the
        Procurement Law to preclude the issuance of  Notice  of  Suspension
        and/or Disallowance.

7. We recommended Management to:

        a. direct the Municipal Accountant adopt  the  perpetual  inventory
           method for supplies and materials;

        b.  provide  budget  for  storage  for  the  supply   requirements,
           otherwise,  if  no  available  storage  area  is  available   we
           recommend  to  establish  control  procedures  to  regulate  the
           issuance to the end-users within a month; and

        c.  a  central  procurement  system   should   be   introduced   by
           establishing  the  actual  needs  of  each  section  offices  as
           documented in the Annual Procurement Plan.

7. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer open  a  separate  bank
        account through an approved resolution by the Sangguniang Bayan for
        Special Education Fund to proper monitor the funds and  for  better
        determination of the balance for each fund. We also recommend  that
        detailed subsidiary ledgers be maintained for each type of fund  to
        properly and accurately record the transactions involved.

6. We recommended that Management enforce the immediate settlement  of
        the suspended transactions by the submission of complete supporting
        documents required in the Notices  of  Suspension.  Otherwise,  the
        appropriate Notice of Disallowance shall be issued  immediately  as
        the prescribed period to settle  the  suspended  transactions  have
        already lapsed.  Likewise,  we  recommend  that  Management  should
        strictly enforce the settlement or payment of the long  outstanding
        disallowance amounting to P91,512.35.

8. We recommended that Management  assess  the  present  situation  of
        tobacco farming in the  Municipality  and  identify  the  immediate
        needs of the farmers. After which, devise  programs,  projects  and
        activities (PPAs)  that  could  address  the  issues  and  concerns
        identified and include such PPAs in the Annual  Investment  Program
        with due consideration on the prescribed documentary and procedural
        requirements of the Department of Budget and Management

5. We recommended that Management comply with the provisions of IRR of
        RA 1021 and COA  Circular  No.  2012-002  and  instruct  the  LDRRM
        Officer  to  submit  the  report  within  the  required  period  as
        prescribed in Sec. 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

7. We recommended that the Accountable Officers submit  regularly  the
        required Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms and
        the Report of Collections and Deposits pursuant to Section  50  and
        51 of Volume II of NGAS Manual and Section 64 of P.D 1445 to ensure
        proper  accounting  of  collections  and  deposits.  Likewise   the
        Municipal Treasurer was required to submit the CRAAF duly supported
        with the individual RAAF of all collecting officer not  later  than
        the 5th day of the ensuing month.

6. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  maintain  a  separate
        financial  statements  and  subsidiary  ledgers  for  the   special
        accounts in the General Fund Proper to ensure adequate  information
        of the individual sub code accounts maintained  under  the  General
        Fund Proper.

5. We recommended the General Service Officer to regularly submit the
        Monthly Report of Official Travels with  the  accomplished  Drivers
        Trip Ticket for  each  vehicle/equipment  to  support  the  Monthly
        Report of Fuel Consumption to the Auditor for verification  on  the
        audit of fuel, oil and lubricants.

5. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare  and  maintain
        subsidiary ledgers to support the general ledger balances to ensure
        fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

5. We recommended that the  liquidating  officer  and  all  collectors
        maintain an individual cashbook to record their  daily  collections
        as prescribed by existing regulations. The use of the cashbook will
        facilitate monitoring of their respective accountabilities as of  a
        given date.

9. We recommended that  the  LGU  GFPS  to  set-up  and  maintain  GAD
        database and  develop GAD Codes  in order to adhere to PCW-DILG-DBM-
        NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular Nos.  2013-01  and  2016-01  in  the
        implementation of GAD programs, projects and activities.

We recommended and management concurred to require the (a) concerned office or Inventory Committee, if any, to complete the physical count of the assets of the LGU and to render the corresponding report; (b) Municipal Treasurer’s Office (MTO) and Accounting Office to update and regularly reconcile their property records; and (c) MTO to maintain and update records on accountability on PPEs.  Moreover, we advise Management to (a) address the reported inadequacy of personnel to facilitate the physical count of and reporting on PPEs; and (b) require proper turnover of accountabilities in case of change of accountable officers.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor will instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer to (i) prepare a Certified List of Taxpayers that shall be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in the setting up of RPT and SET Receivables for CY 2018; and (ii) prepare the list of delinquencies as at end of CY 2017 and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for the updating of individual tax payer’s account. Meanwhile, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor act on the reported inadequacy of personnel at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to facilitate distribution of notices with the end view of intensifying tax collections.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Members of the Local School Board will (a) ensure that future plans and budgets for SEF are consistent with DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 and other pertinent rules that may be issued thereafter; and (b) refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute.

We recommended and management concurred that they will desist paying more than the maximum allowable contribution of the employer in the monthly amount of P100.00 per employee as prescribed in the HDMF Circular No. 274.

We recommended and management concurred that the Local Chief Executive instruct (a) the OIC- Human Resource Management Officer to clearly define in the contract of service the duties and responsibilities of Job Order Personnel to avoid confusion and duplication of duties; and (b) the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the supporting documents, such as accomplishment reports for services rendered, were attached to the disbursements vouchers to establish validity and propriety of payment.

We recommended and management agreed to (a) fill up the mandatory position for the three staff of the MDRRMO to handle the administration, training, research and planning, and operations and warning, to provide adequate support to DRRM activities; and (b) require the concerned MDRRM Officer Assistant to comply with the reporting guidelines on the preparation and regular submission to the COA Auditor of the Report on the Sources and Utilization of Funds prepared by the Municipal Accountant, for proper and timely evaluation.

We recommended and management concurred that the Local Chief Executive would (a) provide the required training interventions for the GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee and Technical Working Group to aid them in the proper discharge of their functions; and (b) instruct said committee and group to review the GAD Plan and Budget to determine programs, projects, and activities that are responsive to gender gaps or issues faced by their women and men employees, as well as their clients and constituents.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor (a) convene the LCPC; (b) direct the (i) LCPC to formulate their PPAs for the implementation of R.A. No. 9433 based on the Comprehensive Municipal Juvenile Intervention Program provided by the JJWC; and (ii) Budget Officer to appropriate through attribution 1% of its Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for these PPAs; and (b) designate a duly licensed social worker that shall recommend interventions and monitor children’s program and projects in the locality.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and management agreed to (a) instruct the MSWMB to involve closely the Barangay officials in the strict implementation on the existing environmental laws; (b) intensify the information campaign on the “no segregation, no collection policy” among the residents; and (c) exercise strong political will to implement the laws on preserving and protecting the environment as mandated under Republic Act No. 9003.

We recommended and management agreed to instruct the Municipal Engineer to require from their contractors the issuance of warranty security on all completed projects that will cover the entire defect liability period.

We recommended and management agreed that Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to be approved by the Local Chief Executive before the same is submitted to the Government Procurement and Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the management agreed to continue the intensified tax collection in their collection process in Tax Revenue in order to have an additional fund that will aid them in providing greater service to its constituents.

We recommended and management agreed to require all those named liable in the Notice of Disallowance to refund the amount disallowed and to settle immediately those requirements stated in the Notice of Suspensions to avoid its eventual maturing into disallowance.

We recommended that the (a) Inventory Committee conduct physical count of all the properties of the Municipality to validate the existence thereof and prepare the RPCPPE, copy furnish the Municipal Accountant to facilitate reconciliation with accounting records; and (b) General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant maintain complete property cards and PPELC, respectively, as required by the Manual on NGAS for LGUs.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Mayor (i) cause the immediate settlement of cash advances to avoid accumulation thereof; and (ii) direct the Municipal Treasurer to suspend the payment of salaries of concerned AOs to enforce liquidation of their cash advances, in case of unjustified failure to settle their accountabilities; and (b) Municipal Accountant strictly monitor the grant and liquidation of cash advances to ensure that no additional cash advance is granted to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Accountant, require the concerned barangays to (a) submit the lacking supporting documents on the transfer of funds; and (b) liquidate the fund transfer by submitting the accomplishment reports and other related supporting documents to ensure that the funds were used for its intended purpose. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2012-001 and 2004-008 on the documentation and recording of fund transfers.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant record procurement of medical supplies, drugs and medicines for operations as inventories and to credit the same account for issuance to end-users, transfers or other disposal as provided for in COA Circular No. 2015-009; and
Requisitioning officer/s certify the conformity of drugs and medicines with the essential drug list as contained in the Philippine National Drug Formulary to ensure that only those drug and medicines with approved therapeutic efficiency are procured and delivered to the general public.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GSO to prepare and submit the Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption and Official Travels and ensure that the driver’s trip tickets are serially numbered and properly accomplished for proper control and monitoring of fuel consumption and use of government motor vehicles.

We recommended that the Municipality, through the Solid Waste Management Board, develop a 10-year comprehensive Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan that will ensure the long-term management of solid waste in compliance with the requirements of RA No. 9003 to protect public health and the environment.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor (a) through an executive order, establish the LCPC that will coordinate with and assist the Municipality for the adoption of a comprehensive plan on delinquency prevention and oversee its proper implementation; and (b) allocate at least 1% of the IRA for child protection program as required under Section 15 of RA No. 9344. We also recommended for the strict observance of the rules and regulations with regard to the allocation of budget and utilization of the 1% LCPC fund.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Mayor seek authority from the DILG Secretary if the Municipality intends to purchase motor vehicles of any kind and regardless of funding source in compliance with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-155 dated December 17, 2014; (b) BAC strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR as regards procurement of goods such as motor vehicles; and (c) General Services Officer (i) require the supplier to issue sales invoice and delivery receipt upon delivery of the goods for proper documentation; and (ii) prepare and issue the Property Acknowledgement Receipt to the end-user for proper monitoring of  accountability over the service vehicle.

We recommended that the (a) BAC strictly comply with the requirements of RA No. 9184 and its IRR as regards the implementation of infrastructure projects; and (b) the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of supporting documents before processing claims for payments pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the:
Managements, through the LDRRM Officer, cause the optimum utilization of the LDRRMF to ensure that basic rescue and response equipment are procured in order to enhance the disaster resilience of communities and to institutionalize arrangements and measures for reducing disaster risks;
Municipal Accountant (i) prepare and maintain separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations for LDRRMF for QRF, MOOE and Capital Outlay to facilitate monitoring of  its appropriations, expenditures and balances to provide reliable and real-time information for decision making purposes to improve the administration and utilization of the LDRRMF; and (ii) comply with Section 5.1.16 of COA Circular 2012-002 by providing disclosure on the amount and details of the unexpended LDRRMF balance in the Notes to Financial Statements; and
LDRRMO prepare and submit, through the LDRRMC and the LDC, the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF and other disaster risk reduction and management resources to be certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, to the COA Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, copy furnish the OCD.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, with the help of the Municipal Accountant, exert efforts to enforce the settlement of suspensions and disallowances by sending letters to the concerned persons responsible/liable requiring them to settle their accountabilities within the prescribed period provided under the RRSA.

We recommended the Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of
physical inventory count of the remaining PPE of the Municipality. We also
recommended the GSO and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Report on the
Physical Count of PPE, Property Cards, Property Ledger Cards, and accounting
records, taking into consideration the noted deficiencies in audit, and furnish the
Audit Team with the said report.

We also recommended the GSO create a database of all properties owned by the
Municipality with the corresponding property numbers.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to insure all government properties
with the Property Insurance Fund.

We recommended the Management to immediately transfer the titles of
land that are still in the name of its previous owners to the Municipality of Dr.
Jose P. Rizal. In case those properties were not yet alienable and disposable, we
recommended the Management to submit a copy of the tax declaration duly
certified by the Municipal Assessor. Henceforth, the Management should observe
strictly the rules and regulations regarding the acquisition of real properties.

We also advised the Municipal Accountant to support the general ledger
controlling account with complete and updated recording of real property
acquired by the Municipality to effectively control and monitor any movement in
the Land account.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Treasurer, the
Municipal Accountant and other officials concerned to make representations
with the bank to furnish the Municipality with copies of debit and credit memos
to facilitate recording of reconciling items in the book of accounts for a fair
presentation of the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to submit the documents
supporting the transactions to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the former
Senior Bookkeeper and demand for the turn-over of all documents under his
custody as transmitted by the Disbursing Officer.

Henceforth, strict compliance by the Municipal Treasurer with regard to the
transmittal of the report of disbursements and its supporting documents within
the prescribed period to the Municipal Accountant must be enjoined and strictly
monitored. The Municipal Treasurer must impose administrative disciplinary
action against the Disbursing Officer/s who fails to submit a complete report of
disbursement including its supporting documents.

We also recommended the Management to improve the agency’s record
keeping and tracking system to ensure that every bank transaction and all other
financial information are properly documented and duly recorded in the books.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the
concerned payees at least one month before a check becomes stale. Moreover, we
required the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the
books for the long outstanding/stale checks in accordance with Section 59, Volume
I of the NGAS Manual.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements of prior years’
accounts.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the bank for the
prompt printing of bank statements. Further, it is suggested that the Municipal
Treasurer utilize the Land Bank i-access option for monitoring and printing of bank
transactions, which will then be forwarded to the Municipal Accountant for timely
reconciliation of the bank depository accounts.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the cashbook of all
bank accounts of the Municipality against the Subsidiary Ledgers of the
Municipal Accountant. We also recommended the Municipal Treasurer to require
all Accountable Officers to reconcile their respective records with the subsidiary
ledgers of the Accounting Office.

The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant should appoint one focal
person per office, who will be tasked to monitor and do the reconciliation of the
two records. Both the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant must
identify as soon as possible the discrepancies between the cashbook and the
general ledger so that necessary adjustments can be effected.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to forward the necessary
documents to the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the recording of the two bank
accounts.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Treasurer to close the
Municipality’s account with FCB. We recommended the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to account the prior years’ service charge given by FCB to the
Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation to the Management to strictly comply
with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and strictly adopt the “no
liquidation, no cash advance” policy.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to review the negative balances
in the RUCA and if warranted, facilitate the processing of payment for the over
refund by those eight (8) accountable officers/ employees.

We reiterated our recommendation to the Municipal Accountant to serve a
written notice to the concerned accountable officers on their outstanding cash
advances as mandated by Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2012-004. Settlement of
the accountable officer’s outstanding account is hereby enjoined within 15 days
upon receipt hereof.

We also reiterated our recommendation to the Municipal Mayor to suspend
the salaries of the concerned accountable officers upon failure to settle their
obligations.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to compel the real property
owners to pay the undercollection, otherwise she will be held personally liable.
The Municipal Treasurer should direct all collectors to strictly comply with the
provisions of the LGC on granting discount to real property owners.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to submit the subsidiary
ledgers to facilitate the monthly reconciliation of the Subsidiary Ledgers against
the General Ledger controlling account.

We reiterated our recommendation to the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Maximize the utilization of the 20% DF by speeding up the implementation of
the prioritized programs, projects and activities funded out of the 20% DF as
per Annual Investment Program and that closer coordination among the
officials involved in planning, execution and monitoring be undertaken to
achieve the desired level of outcome.
b. Instruct the Municipal Planning Development Officer to immediately look into
the unimplemented developmental projects as embodied in the Annual
Investment Program. Identify causes of non-implementation and develop
feasible and specific solutions to effectively implement developmental projects.
c. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to be very prudent, meticulous, conscientious
and precise in evaluating the timely and perfect implementation of each and
every project. Likewise, he should also conduct regular inspection of the
projects so that accomplishment of the contractor is monitored and evaluated
based on the required accomplishment against the number of days stipulated
in the contract.
d. Impose liquidated damages to contractors, should there be a delay in the
project implementation of the project. Such amount shall be computed and
deducted from any money due or which may become due the contractor under
each contract and/or collect such liquidated damages from the retention
money or other securities posted by the contractor, whichever is convenient to
62
the Municipality, in accordance with Item 8.3, Annex “E” of the Revised I.R.R.
of R.A. 9184.
e. Reappropriate the unexpended balance of the completed projects of the 20%
DF to finance development PPAs identified to be funded under the 20% DF
for the ensuing year.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Budget
Officer, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their
records on the 20% DF and provide justification/explanation on the noted
discrepancy.

We recommended the Inventory Committee to reconcile their report
against the Subsidiary Ledgers of Local Road Network Systems maintain by the
Municipal Accountant. Likewise, the Municipal Engineer and GSO should submit
the road map of the Municipality for our reference in audit.

We recommended the Management to fully comply and implement all the
audit findings contained in the AAR of CYs 2016 and 2015, and inform this Office
on the status of the mentioned findings.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Direct the Inventory Committee  to prioritize the completion of the physical inventory of all the LGU’s assets, prepare RPCPPE for each type of property, plant and equipment, reconcile the result thereof with the records of the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, and submit to COA for review and verification; and
Immediately investigate, clear and reconcile all discrepancies between physical account and accounting and property records.  If necessary, written explanations shall be required from accountable and responsible persons.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management:
Require the AO to liquidate her outstanding cash advance immediately as soon as the purpose for which it was given has been served and refrain from granting another cash advance before liquidating first the previous one.
Pay salaries/wages of its officers and employees and job order personnel thru payroll system for every office or department to prevent additional expenses of the LGU and lessen workload of the employees concerned.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management require the AO to properly and completely record the transactions in the cashbook to reflect the correct balances at the end of the period.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer provide an explanation as to whether projects was not completed on its target completion date together with the variation orders and the contractors notices.
We recommended that the LGU must direct all concerned to see to that all transactions like procurements and/or projects must not be paid by the local government if not supported with complete documentations in order to avoid suspension of the same.
The Municipal Mayor, being the immediate and primary responsible for all financial transactions and operations entered into by the municipality, ensures that laws, rules and regulations shall be strictly complied.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management refrain from utilizing downloaded funds in the Trust Fund account of the LGU outside of the scope or purpose for which these funds were created.  The Municipal Accountant should establish a control and monitoring system in the management of downloaded funds in the Trust Fund account of the LGU and ensure that counterpart funds in the General Fund are transferred to Trust Fund prior to implementation of projects.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, being the immediate ad primary responsible for all financial and operational transactions ensures that laws, rules and regulations shall be strictly complied. We further recommend that the members of the Sangguniang Bayan strictly comply with the process/procedures under DILG Memorandum Circular 2013-70 and COA Circular 2007-001 especially on accreditation of CSOs.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the municipality return/refund the unutilized balances of the fund transfers to the source National Government Agencies to fully liquidate the same, thus, reduce the liability of the LGU.

Recommendations:
We recommended the management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain separate books of accounts as well as the financial statements/reports for each of the economic enterprises created and maintained by the LGU as prescribed in Section 110 of the NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I. The financial reports will provide vital and sufficient information to the end-users as regards the assets, liabilities and equity of each of the economic enterprises and to draw decisions therefrom.
Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer maintain cashbooks for each economic enterprise of the municipality where to record the receipts and disbursements of the entities.
It is further recommended that the LGU adhere to the foregoing provisions in the creation and maintenance of special accounts in the general funds as well as the proper recording of the transactions.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to duly execute all contracts of lease for all existing occupants and submit to COA copies within thirty (30) days from receipt hereof;
Designate a market administrator from among the personnel in the Office of the Municipal Treasurer with the primary responsibility of making sure that all laws, rules and regulations pertaining to the operation, maintenance and upkeep of the municipal market be strictly followed so as to prevent further problems or violations to be incurred; and
Direct all LGU officials and employees concerned to strictly impose all provisions of its municipal revenue code.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator (MPDC) and other concerned offices to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 on publicly of projects/programs/activities as amended under COA Circular No. 2016-003 and disseminate the same to all department heads or concerned offices. We also recommend that a focal person must be designated to review and consolidate the reports submitted by each department before submission to COA in hard and soft copies.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management immediately submit the remaining financial reports for CY 2017.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) adhere to by the provisions of RA 7160 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017, dated January 19, 2017 in the preparation of SEF Annual Budget. Any issues or conflicts arising from its implementation and cases not covered by the provision thereof shall be submitted to the DepEd for resolution and in consultation with the LGU.
We also recommended that the LSB to stop charging/budgeting of allowances, office equipment and purchase of various school supplies not in accordance with the above cited regulations and instead utilize the funds for the needs of other schools. Henceforth, incurrence of similar expenditures in the future may be suspended and/or disallowed in audit.

Recommendation:
We recommended that in the succeeding fiscal years, the municipality shall include sufficient appropriation in the Annual Budget for Implementation of the programs of the Local Councils for the Protection of Children as required under RA 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-120.

Recommendation:
We recommended that:
a. The Accounting Department, in collaboration with the Treasury Department, perform immediate identification and thorough analysis of the recorded trust liabilities and reconcile with the available cash balances in the Trust Fund books; and
b. The Municipal Accountant should also perform periodic reconciliation of the trust receipts/transfers coming into the possession of the local treasurer with due regard on the proper utilization and charges against the trust fund for control and monitoring purposes.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the reconciliation of the difference between the Due to and Due from Other Funds accounts and take-up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to bring the two accounts in agreement;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to direct the concerned personnel in charge of the different funds to simultaneously prepare Journal Entry Vouchers when Due to and Due from Other Funds accounts are involved so that errors can be immediately detected and corrected; and
c. Perform regular review and evaluation of transactions to detect errors and omissions.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the members of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (MDRRMC) ensure that the programs, projects and activities to be included in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) shall cover the priority LDRRMF projects and activities that are included or related to the DRRM activities as declared under Section 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-002. Be guided with the provisions of Section 5.0 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-01.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to look into the requirements necessary for the approval of the Municipality’s Solid Waste Management Plan as basis for the implementation of their solid waste activities.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management direct the:
Municipal Accountant should check and verify the completeness of supporting documents attached to the vouchers; and
Personnel Officer conduct a thorough review and inventory of all job orders and provide specific duties and responsibilities for all hires based on the staffing requirements of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:

a) the Municipal Accountant to maintain Property, Plant and
Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) for each category of properties;

b) the Municipal Treasurer to maintain property cards for property,
plant and equipment and work animals to account for the receipt
and disposition of the same;

¢) the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to exert
extra efforts to reconcile their records;

d) the Inventory Committee to complete the yearly physical inventory
of property, plant and equipment and to submit the Report on the
Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment not later than
January 31 of each year;

e) the Municipal Treasurer to update the records of real properties of
the Municipality and furnish the same to the Municipal Accountant
for proper recording in the books of accounts; and

f) the Municipal Accountant to provide accumulated depreciation for
all depreciable assets of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

e direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant
and the Municipal Treasurer with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects as basis in the
preparation and maintenance of property and ledger cards and
proper charging of depreciation for depreciable components of the
road networks system;

e require the Municipal Treasurer to start the preparation and
maintenance of Local Road Network Property Cards upon receipt of
the required data from the Municipal Engineer as required under
COA Circular 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015; and

¢ direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose information in accordance
with the prescribed format of the subject COA Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to exert extra efforts in facilitating the
transfer and titling of the six parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to
protect the interest of the Municipality from third party claims and illegal
disposition.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to adopt the Perpetual Inventory
Method in accounting for inventory items, in compliance with Section 114 of the
MNGAS for LGUs, Volume L

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that Municipal
Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to record all the property acquired out
of Trust Fund in accordance with the provision of Section 104 of MNGAS for
LGUs, Volume I In addition, furnish the Municipal General Services Officer
with the subject Journal Entry Vouchers in order to include those properties in
the property records of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to carefully check the entries made in the Subsidiary Ledgers to
determine the causes of the discrepancy, make the necessary correcting entries
to bring both accounts in agreement and to provide the required disclosures in
the Notes to Financial Statements.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal
Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to restore and retain the account Due
from NGAs totaling P43,713.90 in the books of accounts of the Municipality,
until the authority to write off is granted by the Commission on Audit.

Accordingly, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor file a request for
authority to write-off the dormant account, Due from NGAs, in the total amount
of P43,713.90, in accordance with the guidelines and procedures prescribed
under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal
Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately provide and maintain
complete Subsidiary Ledgers for the special accounts, in compliance with
Section 313 of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 110 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume
1, and submit to the Audit Team the financial statements and trial balances with
the necessary supporting schedules of each special account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Treasurer to release the barangay’s share from RPT and CTC within five days
after the end of each quarter as mandated under Section 271 (d) of LGC and
Section 15 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume L.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

¢ Bids and Award Committee (BAC) to require the submission of
documents prescribed in Appendix A of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA
No. 9184 before the issuance of Notice of Award by Head of the
Procuring Entity;

* Maunicipal Accountant to facilitate the immediate submission of the
lacking documents to support the validity and propriety of the
transactions;

¢ Municipal Accountant to ensure that the payments for telephone
expenses are attached with the corresponding statement of accounnts
or bills as required by COA Circular No. 2012-001;

¢ Municipal Accountant to ensure that payments for Representation
Expenses are attached with sufficient documents that will prove that
these expenses were incurred for official meetings/seminars/conven-
tions such as, but not limited to, pictures, minutes of the meetings,
attendance and certification from the officials concerned; and

® Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to immediately
submit the documents needed to support the disbursements for four
Infrastructure Projects enumerated in Annex J.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare and submit immediately trial balances and financial
statements to the Office of the Auditor, in compliance with Section 70 of the
MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

record receipts of DRRM funds as Trust Liability account in the Trust Fund books in accordance with Section 5.2.1 of COA Circular 2012-002.; and

effect the following adjusting entry to correct the account balances at year end:
PYA                      1,849,000.00
Due to NGAs        9,555,000.00
            Trust Liability – DRRM   11,404,000.00
observations.managementAction:
Management
adjusted the accounts in third quarter of CY 2017

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

strictly abide with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002, NDRRMC/DBM/DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 and NDRRMC/DILG/DBM/CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 in the utilization of the LDRRMF;

refrain from charging expenses not related to disaster risk and reduction activities; and

prepare detailed LDRRMFIP and include in separate caption list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF.
We recommended that management ensure all projects implemented are in accordance with the duly approved investment plan and corresponding budget, authorized through an appropriation ordinance.

We recommended that:
 
the BAC ensure pre-procurement conference is conducted for procurement of goods costing ₱2 million and above to effectively set out the details of the procurement which includes the technical specifications and to advertise procurement opportunities in a newspaper of general nationwide circulation to attract a greater number of prospective bidders.
 
The BAC ensure compliance with the guidelines and documentary requirements of Negotiated Procurement as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction:
Pre-procurement conference were conducted for succeeding for all procurement with ABC ₱2 M and up.




In the exit conference, the BAC assured to strictly follow the requirements for negotiated procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit copies of contracts to the audit team within 5 days after perfection in accordance with pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Management started to submit some perfected contracts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee invite observers in all stages of the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Notices for the  scheduled time and date to bid were furnished to the Office of the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting department start establishing the Special Accounts in the General Fund and its corresponding subsidiary ledger to conform with the requirements provided for under the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units.
observations.managementAction: Accountant already started maintaining Special Accounts for 20% DF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management adhere strictly with applicable provisions of the Omnibus Election Code to avoid the incurrence of unlawful expenditures

We recommended that the budget office obligate expenses only to their proper accounts and refrain from window dressing the Statement of Appropriation, Allotments and Obligations.

We also recommended that the Budget Officer present to the Local Chief Executive the real picture of the approved budget and obligations incurred; and recommend for the augmentation of other funds only when necessary.

We also recommended that the Budget Office regularly reconcile his reports with the Accounting Office to avoid discrepancy.
observations.managementAction: The Budget Officer still failed to obligate expenses in their proper accounts.
