observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that management direct the concerned personnel to immediately settle their respective outstanding cash advances to comply with Section 89 of PD No. 1445; otherwise, effect the withholding of their salary equivalent to the unsettled amount, if they are still in the active service and institute appropriate legal action against those officials and employees with unsettled accountability.   Likewise, locate the other officials who were transferred to other agency/ies and demand the immediate liquidation thereof.
observations.managementAction:
Management was able to reduce the unliquidated balance to P42,200.00 as of year-end.





Management were still trying to communicate with previous employees with unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason:
Management still failed to demand from employees the liquidation of their prior years’ cash advances.



Management was not able to communicate and demand liquidation to some employees with unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to: 

require the concerned accountable officers to remit their collections intact and within the prescribed period in accordance with existing rules and regulations;
 
investigate the causes of infractions and subject the erring accountable officers to appropriate administrative sanctions; and

require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to enhance her reviewing functions over the flows of accountable forms and ensure that daily collection reports by collecting officers are extensively checked and verified to eliminate the non-reporting of official receipts and non-remittance of collections.
observations.managementAction:
Municipal Treasurer remit the collections as soon as conveniently possible.


Accountable officers were given verbal reprimand.


Municipal Treasurer reviewed daily collection reports.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to be more careful in handling petty cash to ensure that all replenishments are fully accounted and supported by proper vouchers to avoid double replenishment; and 
 
instruct the Municipal Accountant and the ICO-Municipal Treasurer  to strengthen mechanism in recording petty cash transactions of the  Municipality in order to  avoid such lapses.
observations.managementAction:
Municipal Treasurer were careful in the handling of the petty cash


Municipal Accountant were more vigilant in recording petty cash transactions

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year recommendation that the Management refrain from using DRRMF for programs/projects/activities which are not directly related with disaster preparedness, mitigation, and response and rescue operations to have sufficient funds for calamity purposes.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to prioritize the projects charged to DRRMF.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year recommendation that the MSWMB: 
involve closely the Barangay officials in the strict implementation on the existing environmental laws; 

intensify the information campaign on the “no segregation, no collection policy and the “no-littering policy” among the residents; and 


exercise strong political will to implement the laws on preserving and protecting the environment as mandated under Republic Act No. 9003.
observations.managementAction:
Municipality have started to involve the Barangay officials


Municipality continued the campaign by working closely with Barangay officials


Municipality committed to implement the laws as mandated under R.A. No. 9003.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year audit recommendations that:

the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) assist the Sangguniang Bayan in the formulation of GAD Code that will support the LGU’s efforts in promoting, protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights, women’s economic empowerment and gender-responsive governance; and

b.  the Municipal Mayor ensure that GFPS structures and compositions will be in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction:
Management will formulate GAD Code.





Management will comply.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year recommendation that the GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee and Technical Working Group: 

review the GAD Plan and Budget to determine programs, projects, and activities that are responsive to gender gaps or issues faced by their women and men employees, as well as their clients and constituents.; and 

utilize the GAD Fund only for programs, projects, and activities that would address the identified gender issues in the locality based on PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01
observations.managementAction:
Management review and ensure the PPAs are responsive to gender issues.




Management identified the expenses chargeable to GAD
observations.reason:
There were PPAs charged to GAD which were not gender issues

There were some PPAs that are not related to gender issues.

observations.recommendation:
We  recommended  that the municipality strictly follow  COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 regarding the information and publicity on the PPAs of the Government Agencies to promote good governance through transparency and accountability to the people and to enable this Office to comply with the submission of the Quarterly Report of the PPAs to the Regional Director for monitoring compliance. 
Replace the existing tarpaulins posted in the particular areas where the projects are located.

Follow strictly the design and format of the tarpaulin signboards as prescribed in Section 2.2.3 of the circular and avoid displaying  or affixing pictures associated with the political leaders of the implementing agency.
observations.managementAction:
Management have complied.





Management replaced the tarpaulins.


Tarpaulins displayed were the correct design and format as prescribed

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

enforce the strict implementation of municipal ordinances relevant to the operation of three wheeled public conveyances; and  

consider the necessity of hiring additional traffic personnel to man the critical or accident-prone areas and provide the necessary communication equipment and facilities for improved information dissemination.
observations.managementAction:
Management will enforce strictly the relevant ordinances


Management considered the hiring of additional personnel
observations.reason:
Management should be more forceful in implementing ordinances


Management were not able to provide necessary communication equipment and improved information dissemination.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management continue remitting the due to GSIS in  compliance with Section 3.4 of R.A. 8291.
observations.managementAction: Management continued to comply

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management continue remitting the withheld taxes from different sources in compliance with the regulations set by the BIR.
observations.managementAction: Management continued to comply

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management direct the Inventory Team to conduct complete physical count of all PPE and require the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to maintain updated PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively, to facilitate its reconciliation with the RPCPPE. Should there be any discrepancy between these records, the same should be properly examined to facilitate adjustment of the affected PPE account balances for its fair presentation in the Statement of Financial Position.

We also recommended that Management prepare the IIRUP for unserviceable properties for eventual disposal to decongest the space occupied by these assets and generate additional income therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The GSO maintained property cards for all PPE procured by the Municipality since her assumption in Office in CY 2016.
observations.reason:
The physical count of all PPE is still on going.                                                         

PPE before her designation as GSO had no property cards and her Office is still in the process of preparing them.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement to the Auditor together with copies of bank statements, debit/credit memos, cancelled and paid checks and all other supporting documents necessary to facilitate its timely verification; and (b) supply the necessary information, nature, and description of reconciling items for better appreciation and to determine its proper disposition.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the Bank Reconciliation Statements regularly and provided sufficient description of reconciling items including supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the immediate liquidation of cash advances totaling P87,774.47.  We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) strictly monitor and fully observe the rules and regulations in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances; and (b) see to it that no additional cash advance is granted to officials/employees unless their previous cash advances are fully settled and that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of each year.
observations.managementAction: Accountable officers had settled cash advances totaling P68,415.72 out of  last year’s balance of        P 87,774.67. Management committed to issue   demand letters to those AOs with outstanding balances.
observations.reason: Failure of the Municipal Accountant to strictly monitor the grant and liquidation of cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 for the proper accounting and control of fuel consumption. We also recommended that the concerned drivers prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels to COA to fill up/provide relevant information on the Driver’s Trip Ticket.
observations.managementAction: Required all drivers to fill up relevant information on the Driver's Trip Ticket.
observations.reason: Failure of the GSO to monitor issuance of fund and preparation of Monthly Report of Official Travels by the drivers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC faithfully comply with the requirements of RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR to ensure competition, efficiency and transparency in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The BAC conducted public biddings and tried to observe the rules and regulations on the procurement of goods and on the posting of the Notice of Award, Contract Agreement and Notice to Proceed in the PhilGEPS website, among others.
observations.reason: No comments were given by management/BAC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strictly observe the provisions of RA 9184 and its Revised IRR in the procurement of goods to promote efficiency, competition and transparency in the procurement process. Comply with the provisions of Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 on the complete documentation of disbursements to establish their validity, legality and propriety.
observations.managementAction: The Management requested the supplier for the postponement of the delivery of goods because the cage and pig pen constructed by the recipients are not yet completed. The supplier commenced delivery on July 12, 2016. Management also submitted the Bid                            Supplement Abstract;                                               Master List of Tobacco Farmers; and the letter of                                      DBM for the said project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC and the Municipal Engineering Office ensure that detailed engineering activities are conducted prior to bidding and award of infrastructure projects. We also recommended that the BAC strictly comply with the requirements of RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR to enhance efficiency and transparency of government procurement. We further recommended that all claims against government funds be supported with complete documentation to establish the validity and propriety of the disbursements.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the following documents:                                    *Certificate of Site Inspection                                              *BAC Resolution recommending the approval of award for the opening of  Local Access Road-Good Morning Hills,Aguas-Manoot, Rizal                                        *Plans/Drawings and Technical Specifications                                      *Certification from BAC Secretary that this contract was posted at any conspicuous places.                                         *Construction S-Curve

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board (a) prioritize the expenditures chargeable against the SEF in accordance with Sections 100 and 272 of RA No. 7160; and (b) see that the SEF is utilized solely for priority activities that contribute to the attainment of its objectives for the benefit of public elementary and high school students.
observations.managementAction: The non-priority expenditures was significantly reduced from last year’s P269,250.95 to P83,820.00 this year while payment out of cash advances was reduced from P214,000.00 to P178,000.00 this year.
observations.reason: Lack of funds from the national government forced them to use the SEF to finance school activities especially travelling expenses incurred by poor but deserving students in various competitions .

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the optimal utilization of the LDRRM Fund to enhance the resilience of the constituents should disaster and calamities arise in the future.  We also recommended that the LDRRM Council decide on the use of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF which shall be incorporated in the LDRRMF Investment Plan.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to maximize utilization of LDRRM funds and will revise LDRRM – Annual Investment Plan to include the unexpended balance of prior years.
observations.reason: For CY 2017, overall utilization rate of the LDRRMF was still low at 26.93%. Other observations relevant to the utilization of the Fund were also noted in the audit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended for the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

We also recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator ensure that the programs, projects and activities under the 20% Development Fund are specifically itemized to facilitate proper monitoring and evaluation.  Identify, first and foremost, the programs and projects that must be prioritized for inclusion and consideration in the Annual Investment Plan.  For those which are inconsistent with the governing rules and regulations, initiate amendments to address the needs of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Management prepared a more itemized and detailed AIP for 2017.  The Local Development Council also identified and prioritized in the AIP the PPAs that contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental     management outcomes of the Municipality.
observations.reason: The overall utilization of the 20% Development Fund was still low at 31.02% due to non-implementation of other programs, projects and activities. Non-implementation of other PPAs due to  lack of technical personnel to prepare detailed engineering works such as Program of Works.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor exert efforts to enforce the settlement of suspensions and disallowances. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly monitor the settlement of suspensions and disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Management assigned   personnel to consolidate/ reconcile documents/ reports to facilitate  the settlement of suspensions and disallowances.
observations.reason:
The assigned personnel is still in the process of consolidating    and 
 reconciling documents and reports regarding the unsettled audit  suspensions and disallowances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to: a) create an Inventory Committee that will be responsible for the conduct of the physical inventory count and furnish the Audit Team with the said report; b) require the Municipal Accountant to maintain Subsidiary Ledgers and reclassify the PPE; and c) require the GSO to reconcile with the Municipal Accountant records.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted the Physical Inventory Count.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 1.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to submit the necessary Journal Entry Vouchers and supporting documents to establish the legality and propriety of those entries/adjustments, otherwise, reverse the undocumented entries.
Henceforth, strict compliance and observance of highest standards of honesty is enjoined and see to it that all claims against government funds are supported with complete documents in compliance with Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445.
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to permanently relieve the Senior Bookkeeper of his duty in the maintenance of General Ledgers and preparation of Financial Statements. Assign the Financial Statement preparation to the Municipal Accountant, so that he can personally attest to the accurateness of the Municipality’s Financial Statements. Likewise, implement job rotation on the employees as a good practice of internal control.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant partially reversed those unsupported entries in February 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to fast track the completion of the Bank Reconciliation Statements.
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to forward the original copies of the bank statements and cleared checks to the Audit Team as reference in audit.
We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the bank for the prompt printing of bank statements. Further, it is suggested that the Municipal Treasurer utilize the Land Bank access option for monitoring and printing of bank transactions, which will then be forwarded to the Municipal Accountant for timely reconciliation of the bank depository accounts.
We also recommended the Municipal Treasurer to refrain from withholding the bank statements and cleared checks that may have come into her custody. Likewise, she should also forward all cancelled checks to the Municipal Accountant, who will also forward it to the auditor.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was able to submit the BRS for the Trust Fund accounts of CYs 2015 and 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the remaining balance of the 20% DF to its separate bank account.
We also recommended the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their books on the prior 20% DF that remained in the bank account of GF-Proper and submit the said report to the Audit Team for validation.
Henceforth, practice the transfer of the entire amount of 20% DF to its separate bank account as released by the DBM and validated deposit slip should be attached in the disbursement voucher of 20% DF transfer.
observations.managementAction: The Management fully implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to submit a justification/s on the non-implementation of the mentioned programs under the Trust Fund.
We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to immediately inform the Municipal Mayor and Accountant on the nature of deposits and fund transfers coming from other government agencies to facilitate the timely implementation of its intended purpose.
We recommended the Municipal Accountant/Treasurer to submit a copy of the Official Receipts of the said fund transfer to the Trust Fund account of the Municipality. We advised the Municipal Accountant to separately reflect all grants and donations from other government agencies in the Cash Receipts Journal and indicate the payor name to immediately identify the source of fund.
observations.managementAction: The Management fully implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to require a Board of Director’s Resolution before the transfer of fund to provide bases for monitoring of fund utilization and to ensure that funds were properly used for its intended purpose.
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to initially take up the fund transfers in the books as Due from LGUs and subsequently be recorded as Subsidy - Others after liquidation was submitted and evaluated. The Municipal Accountant should immediately submit the liquidation documents covering the transaction for CY 2016 to the Audit Team for post audit.
We also recommended the ABC Secretary to submit all of the minutes of the Board of Director’s Resolutions covering the mentioned disbursements for validation. He should submit a certify copy of the Fidelity Bond Documents of the Liga Treasurer and Liga’s Bank Statement in accordance with Section 505 of the Local Government Code.
observations.managementAction: The Management fully implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to issue the accountable forms to bonded accountable officers duly authorized to issue the same. We also recommended the Municipal Treasurer to recover all Accountable Form #51 issued to Ms. Gleizle C. Magbanua.
We recommended the MSWDO to strictly adhere to the rules and regulations in the granting and utilization of funds release to NGOs/POs. We also recommended the MSWDO to refrain from entering into a MOA with NGOs/POs as the same is not among her listed powers and duties enumerated in Section 483 of the Local Government Code.
Henceforth, we recommended the Municipal Accountant to initially take up the release of grants to NGOs/POs as Due from Non-Government Organizations/People's Organizations and subsequently recorded as Donations upon submission of the liquidations and required reports.
observations.managementAction: The Management fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation last year that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the asset. The Management should assess the useful life of the assets to determine the related amortization or any impairment in its cost to reflect its carrying amount.
observations.managementAction: The Management implemented reclassification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to record the depreciation for those PPE and ensure recognition of the monthly depreciation for all the PPE of the Municipality for fair presentation in the financial statement.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to apply the change in accounting policy to determine the impact on the Financial Statements and consequently disclose it to the Notes to Financial Statements. We also recommended the Municipal Accountant to fill out the acquisition dates of the PPE for the determination and accuracy of accumulated depreciation as at year-end.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the previous SDO to reconcile her accountabilities against the records of the Municipal Accountant and settle any remaining balances.
We recommended the SDO to transmit her cashbook to the Audit Team for post audit.

We recommended the Management to increase the fidelity bond of the accountable officers to ensure full bond coverage of their cash advances, otherwise grant cash advances within their maximum accountability.
We recommended the Management to strictly abide by the provisions laid down by the Local Government Code and COA Circular No. 98-002.
We recommended the Management to fully comply and implement all the audit findings contained in the AAR of CY 2015, and inform this Office on the status of the mentioned findings.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to make the necessary improvements to the dumpsite to conform to the standards prescribed in R.A. 9003 and its IRR.
We recommended the MENRO to forward the proposed budget of these improvements to the Municipal Mayor for indorsement to the Sangguniang Bayan.

We recommended the Management to introduce improvement on the facilities in the dumpsite taking into consideration the noted discrepancies between the SWMP and the actual facilities constructed.
We also recommended the Sangguniang Bayan to appropriate funds for the improvement of the Municipality’s dumpsite.
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to make the necessary corrective measures and devise a way to conform to the mentioned regulations to avoid penalties imposed by Section 49 of the Act.

We recommended the MENRO to properly label the garbage bins according to the type of wastes to be collected.
We recommended the Licensing Division of the Municipality to mandate all business establishments to provide properly labelled garbage bins before the issuance of Mayor’s Permit.

We recommended the MDRRMO to submit all the mandatory reports for CY 2016 for the determination of the utilization and unexpended portion of the fund.
We also recommended the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Audit Team a copy of her turnover documents to the newly appointed MDRRMO for our reference in audit.

We recommended that management effect the transfer of its Public Infrastructure accounts to the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts of the LGU pursuant to the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards and COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following department heads for the compliance with the requirements of the COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015:

a. Municipal Accountant

1.  Prepare the Journal Voucher to record the beginning balance of the local road    
network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card (Annex D of the Circular), respectively;	

2. Support the JV with the Registry of the Public Infrastructures for the Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the 	determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available 	cost per registry;

3.  Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and 

4. Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

b. Municipal General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer

1.	Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and 
      

2. Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components. (Annex E of the Circular)




c. Municipal Engineer

1.	Provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Property Officer to facilitate the submission of the updated list of inventory of insurable properties with the GSIS so that the necessary insurance coverage for all the assets can be applied with the GSIS General Insurance Fund.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee prepare and submit the Procurement Monitoring Report for the 1st semester of CY 2017 to GPPB website on July 14, 2017; submit the CY 2018 Annual Procurement Plan-Common-use Supplies and Equipment to DBM on November 15, 2017 and prepare the APCPI for CY 2016 during the 1st quarter of CY 2017. The same shall be done on each ensuing semesters and years thereafter.

We recommended that the municipal mayor will require the Municipal Planning and Development Council to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund so that all its programmed development projects could be implemented immediately to ensure that intended beneficiaries avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.

We have recommended the municipality to formulate more realistic and doable socio-economic programs and projects and prioritize their implementation according to the needs of the constituency to avoid constant reprogramming and reversion. The Executive Committee must ensure the decisions of the council to faithfully carry out and implement so that the public may receive the entire benefits that they may be able to derive from the projects.

Require coordination between the Planning, Engineering, Treasury, Accounting, DSWD, DILG, and other concerned divisions/offices to fast track the implementation of BUB projects especially so that these projects are intended to provide benefits to the municipality’s grassroots population.

Require the BUB focal person to regularly monitor the status of proposed BUB projects as to funding and implementation.

We recommended to the municipality that all construction/improvements, repairs and maintenance of municipal properties/facilities have to be supported with estimate of work, labor costs, drawings/plans specifications and program of work to prevent wastage of materials and losses of government resources.

We recommended that management should:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) Fund and submit to the COA Auditor thru the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) every 15th day of the month.

b. LDRRM Officers must strictly comply with the accounting and reporting requirements stipulated in RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002, dated September 12, 2012, to ensure that the financial information is appropriately taken up in the books and properly presented in the financial statements with adequate disclosures.

We recommended that the municipality ensure strict compliance to the provisions of Republic Act 9003 in proper segregation, collection, transport, storage, treatment and disposal of solid waste. Ensure the implementation of segregation at source of solid waste, enforcement of “no segregation, no collection’ policy, creation of Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) in every barangay, and the development of disposal facility in the municipality.

We recommended that other basic services/ priority expenditures enumerated in Section 272 of the LGC should be considered in allocating funds of SEF so as not to deprive the constituents of the benefits from the said funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor will direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit the amount of P236,118.17 to the Bureau of Internal Revenue and submit to the Office of the Auditor the proof of its remittance as soon as possible.

Strengthen the GAD Focal Point System and build capabilities of the agency on GAD thru trainings.

Require the GAD Focal Point System to conduct gender analysis to be able to incorporate the programs/activities/projects (PAPs) that address gender issues in the GAD Plan and budget as well as mainstreaming in the agency operations so that the formulation, development, submission, implementation, monitoring, evaluation and accounting of results of the Municipality’s annual GPB and GAD Accomplishment Reports will be in order.

We recommend compliance of AO No. 70, s. 2003 dated April 14, 2003 for the creation of Internal Audit Service and its implementing guidelines under DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-4 dated March 22, 2004 for the guidelines on the organization and staffing of Internal Auditing Service.

Prioritize the issue and exert effort to take necessary steps to acquire Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) as proof of ownership and furnish the Auditor, copy of the TCT.


b. Appropriate funds for the titling of the lot to avoid future conflict and claims of ownership by third party.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Vouchers to adjust the Cash in Bank-LCCA and Accounts Payable due to stale checks. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to make the necessary adjustment of the cashbook balance and to notify the payees for replacement of the stale checks.
observations.managementAction: Necessary JEVs were drawn to adjust the Cash in Bank-LCCA and the Municipal Treasurer made the necessary adjustment in his cashbook.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to restore and retain the account Due from NGAs totaling P43,713.90 in the books of accounts of the Municipality, until the authority to write off is granted. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor file a request for the authority to write-off the dormant account, Due from NGAs, in the total amount of P43,713.90, in accordance with the guidelines and procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Management partially complied with the audit recommendation. A request to write-off the dormant account was already filed by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: A request to write-off the dormant account was already filed by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to send its staff of Inventory Unit to training/seminar on Supply and Property Management; The Municipality was able to start the physical inventory of the PPE of the Municipality. Moreover, RPCPPE was not yet prepared nor submitted. The Municipal Treasurer was able to update the records of the real property of the Municipal. No actions were taken by the Management. Reiterated in Observation No. 1 under Part II of this report.
observations.reason: Records of the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer were not fully reconciled since the physical count of all the PPE of the Municipality was not completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra efforts to account and record all parcels of land owned by the Municipality of Rizal and to reconcile their records based on the available documents. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to initiate action for the titling of the parcels of land owned by the Municipality to protect its interest against third party claims and illegal disposition. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to initiate the appropriation of available funds for the cost of titling of the parcels of land covered only by Certificates of Tax Declaration named after the Municipality of Rizal. Likewise, we also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and Municipal Assessor to look into the possibility for the revaluation of the parcels of land owned by the Municipality in accordance with the policies and guidelines on the measurement of cost after recognition prescribed under Paragraphs Nos. 43, 45 and 51 of PPSAS 17.
observations.managementAction: Budget for the titling of parcels of land were already appropriated in the Municipality’s 2018 budget. The management explained that necessary actions were initiated for the revaluation of the parcels of land owned by the Municipality.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2018, the values of the parcels of land were still the same.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to make the appropriate classification of the PPE accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 prescribing the Revised Chart of Account for Local Government Units.
observations.managementAction: The Management was able to comply with this recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the Monthly Report of Checks Issued, Cash Disbursements, and the Report of Collections and Deposits within the time required under Section 6.03 of COA Circular No. 95-006. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit immediately trial balances and financial statements to the Office of the Auditor, in compliance with Section 70 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Monthly Reports of Checks Issued, Cash Disbursements and the Report of Collections and Deposits were already submitted within the required time frame. Paid disbursement vouchers and other documents evidencing the transactions were submitted within ten days from the date of receipt of said documents. Monthly Trial Balances and Financial Statements were not submitted by the Municipality.
observations.reason: Monthly Trial Balances and Financial Statements were not submitted by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strengthen the LGU GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) by enhancing and creating the GFPS TWG as well as its Secretariat and completing the composition of the GFPS Execom. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local GAD Focal Point Team (LGFPT) and the GAD Focal Point Coordinator to establish a centralized GAD database to have a source of reliable information necessary for its GAD planning, programming and policy formulations. Meanwhile, utilize the existing database, planning and data-gathering tools and management information system to generate gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information from primary and secondary sources, such as surveys and studies. Further, we recommended that the Municipality maximize the utilization of the GAD Fund for the purposes for which it was created.
observations.managementAction: The composition of the Municipality’s GAD Focal Point Team was already compliant with the requirement of the MCW-IRR. Some offices were able to provide GAD database separately. Despite the partial compliance, the latter were still unable to finish the establishment of a centralized GAD database. More than 95% of the Fund was utilized by the Municipality.
observations.reason: Despite partial compliance the latter were still unable to finish the establishment of a centralized GAD database.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and other concerned official to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned Municipal officials to see to it that the development projects and programs to be implemented are in accordance with the Section 2.3 of the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 and partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.
observations.managementAction: The Management ensured that 20% Development Fund is utilized for projects that will help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes in the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended balance of LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities — Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund” of the Trust Fund books, maintain its corresponding subsidiary ledgers and make full disclosure of the details of the unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Unexpended balance of LDRRMF was transferred to the Special Trust Fund under the account Trust Liabilities - DRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the head of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Office to: a. extend the full implementation of the proper segregation of solid wastes materials from the household level (segregation at source) up to the sanitary landfill; and b. efficiently manage the residual wastes and minimize the volume of waste being dumped in sanitary landfill. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor form or establish a Solid Waste Management Board in accordance with the requirements provided under Section 12 of RA No. 9003. We likewise recommended that the Municipal Mayor encourage and support the establishment of Multi-Purpose Environment Cooperatives or Association that will help promote the implementation of RA No. 9003. Further, we also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the coordination of MSWMB and BSWMB to ensure that all barangays are provided with MRFs that are properly maintained and fully operated.
observations.managementAction: The Management explained that they are very strict in the implementation of waste segregation at source. Management also explained that as of this writing, they are on the process of establishing a new Solid Waste Management Board. There is continuous rehabilitation of the MRF of the Municipality. No additional MRF was constructed in CY 2017.
observations.reason: Despite the efforts of the Municipality, the NGOs, manufacturing and recycling industry were still not represented in the Solid Waste Management Board and nine barangays of the Municipality were still not provided with MRF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the budget office obligate expenses only to their proper accounts and refrain from window dressing the Statement of Appropriation, Allotments and Obligations .

We also recommended that the Budget Officer present to the Local Chief Executive the real picture of the approved budget and obligations incurred; and recommend for the augmentation of other funds only when necessary.

We also recommended that the Budget Office regularly reconcile his reports with the Accounting Office to avoid discrepancy.
observations.managementAction: The Budget Officer still failed to obligate expenses in their proper accounts

observations.recommendation:

Ensure that disbursements be properly supported with an appropriation ordinance;

Require that all savings from LDRRMF – MOOE be transferred to special trust fund account and any savings from the CO shall be available for use in the implementation of the LDRRMFIP; and

Henceforth, strictly follow the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the use of LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction:
Management committed the same deficiency.

Management failed to transfer savings to trust fund liability accounts.

Management failed to implement proper usage of savings on LDRRMF – MOOE and CO
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 2, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly comply with the required submission of contracts to the Audit Team within five (5) days from their issuance or execution to ensure timely review as to the legality and propriety prior to their payment.

observations.recommendation: We strictly recommended that Management immediately transfer the unexpended balance of the DRRMF to a special trust fund under the account “Trust Liability – DRRM” as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Management did not implement the audit transfer the unexpended balance of LDRRMF. Furthermore, the unexpended/unobligated balance of QRF for CYs 2015 and 2014 totaling P833,563.49 were not reported as of year-end.

Travels during the year have been regulated that only important travels have been allowed and approved.

observations.recommendation: In order to effectively implement the rules and regulations of RA 9710 otherwise known as the “Magna Carta of Women” and to fully achieve the objectives thereof, the Municipality through its GAD Focal Point System should endeavor to prepare and submit the necessary reports needed by the PCW.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator ensure that the priority development programs, projects and activities to be implemented during the year under the 20% Development Fund are specifically specified/ itemized to facilitate proper monitoring and evaluation. Identity, first and foremost, the programs and projects that must be prioritized for inclusion and consideration in the Annual Investment Plan. For those which are inconsistent with the governing rules and regulations, initiate amendments to address the needs of the constituents as far as tangible projects and programs are concerned.

We also recommended that Management limit the use of the 20% Development Fund for projects that contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures in accordance with the guidelines provided under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 to improve the socio-economic condition of constituents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to
introduce improvement on the
facilities in the dumpsite taking into
consideration the noted discrepancies
between the SWMP and the actual
facilities constructed.

The Sangguniang Bayan should
appropriate funds for the improvement
of the Municipality’s dumpsite.
observations.managementAction:
MENRO commented the
following:

a) There were eight (8) dump trucks distributed to barangays namely: Bunog,
Traan, Punta Baja, Campong Ulay Ransang, Candawaga, Culasian and
Panalingaan during the time of then Mayor Samson 1. Degillo. The remaining
three (3) barangays namely: Taburi, Latud and Canipaan were not given dump
trucks because these barangays were not yet connected to the national
highway that time.

b) The LGU has allocated £200,000.00 for the garbage bins and signage.

c) Only 2 material recovery facilities have been established which are situated at
the Public Market and Pinagar, Campong Ulay

SLF is almost complete and may commence anytime should the Power Shovel
(Back Hoe) is available.

There is a new watchman assigned in the disposal facility.

The space in between barbed wires will be improved by inserting additional 2
to 3 barbed wires line. This will be done in early possible time upon arrival of
the requested materials. Alternative fencing materials locally available in the
site such as hamboo, buho or small round woods may be considered to be used.

There is an existing shallow well at the site. A new jetmatic pup will be
installed to provide clean water for washing and cooking food. Drinking water
will be taken from the safe source located at the nearby nature spring.

Kitchen, comfort room, office space and solar power shall be included in the
budget proposal for CY 2018 or in supplemental budget in CY 2017.

Padlock and other safety devices will be installed in the building. Facility
watchman is already available. Additional one shall be requested to guard the
site for a time span of 24/7.

The maintenance bay for vehicles, heavy equipment and other equipment shall
be put in place as soon as the existence of those becomes available in the site.

There is a shallow well in the site which will be developed into pumped station
for wash bay.

Water sample will be gathered for examination in the nearest DENR or PCSD
testing laboratory to confirm if there is leachate contamination in the stream or
creek.

The closed dumpsite in Pinagar disposal facility is more than 50 meters away
from any bodies of water.

The four (4) segregation floors are situated right immediately at the front of the
concrete composting chamber (Figure 17). The concrete floor has concrete low
sidings purposely to avoid spread out of waste during unloading of garbage
from the dump truck and during segregation activities.

h) The LGU is now implementing the use of SLF for waste management.
Different facilities are constructed in order to properly manage the residual
waste such as: Residual Waste Retaining Area, Leachate Collection Chamber
or Septic Tanks and Leachate Treatment Pond with Leachate Well.

i) Proper training for waste collectors will be requested through the Sanitary
Inspector. Likewise, safety equipment will be provided to them.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in
Observation No.
30

        Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on  a  monthly  basis  as
        required by COA Circular 2012-002.

We recommended that management ensure all projects implemented are  in

We recommended that Management strictly  avoid  piecemeal  purchases
        and/or splitting of requisitions or purchase orders.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee invite  observers
        in all stages of the procurement process.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive,  thru  the  Municipal
       Planning  Development  Officer  (MPDO),  strategize  their  planning
       activities to ensure timely implementation of programs, projects and
       activities.

We recommended that the Accounting department start establishing the
